12. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_48.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_48.rs\n//!\n//! Event-Driven Infrastructure for EduPulse Live\n//! ============================================\n//! This module encapsulates the **Outbox \u2192 Broker** integration used across the\n//! platform.  Each bounded context persists `DomainEvent`s to its local\n//! PostgreSQL **outbox** table as part of the same transaction that mutates\n//! aggregates.  A dedicated background task (see [`EventDispatcher`]) then\n//! reliably publishes those events to the message broker (NATS) and marks them\n//! as dispatched, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery while preserving\n//! transactional integrity.\n//!\n//! The implementation follows DDD, Repository, and Service Layer patterns and\n//! demonstrates:\n//!   \u2022 Database integration with `sqlx`\n//!   \u2022 Async, streaming message publication with `nats`\n//!   \u2022 Robust error handling and observability (`thiserror`, `tracing`)\n//!   \u2022 Support for GDPR erase requests through an explicit `UserEraseRequested`\n//!     event visible to downstream services.\n//!\n//! # Public API\n//! * [`DomainEvent`]: Trait implemented by every event emitted by the domain.\n//! * [`EventEnvelope`]: Serializable wrapper containing metadata & payload.\n//! * [`EventOutboxRepository`]: Abstract persistence port.\n//! * [`PgOutboxRepository`]: Postgres-backed implementation.\n//! * [`NatsPublisher`]: Message broker adapter.\n//! * [`EventDispatcher`]: Orchestrates fetch \u2192 publish \u2192 acknowledge loop.\n\nuse std::{\n    sync::Arc,\n    time::{Duration, SystemTime},\n};\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse futures::{stream, StreamExt};\nuse nats::asynk::{self, Connection};\nuse serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, QueryBuilder};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tokio::{select, task::JoinHandle, time::sleep};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Domain Event Abstractions                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/// Marker trait implemented by *all* domain events.\n///\n/// Implementations **must** be serializable (`Serialize + DeserializeOwned`) and\n/// versioned via the `EVENT_VERSION` constant to facilitate schema evolution.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait DomainEvent:\n    Send + Sync + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + 'static + std::fmt::Debug\n{\n    /// Logical type discriminator, e.g. `\"lesson.published.v1\"`.\n    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str;\n\n    /// Semantic version of the event's JSON schema.\n    const EVENT_VERSION: u8 = 1;\n\n    /// Globally unique identifier used for idempotency by consumers.\n    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n\n    /// Aggregate root identifier the event originated from.\n    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n\n    /// Timestamp (UTC) at which the event occurred.\n    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n}\n\n/// Example event emitted when a lesson is published.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LessonPublished {\n    pub event_id: Uuid,\n    pub lesson_id: Uuid,\n    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl DomainEvent for LessonPublished {\n    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str = \"lesson.published.v1\";\n\n    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.event_id\n    }\n\n    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.lesson_id\n    }\n\n    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n        self.occurred_at\n    }\n}\n\n/// Event raised when a user requests GDPR account erasure.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct UserEraseRequested {\n    pub event_id: Uuid,\n    pub user_id: Uuid,\n    pub requested_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl DomainEvent for UserEraseRequested {\n    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str = \"user.erase_requested.v1\";\n\n    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.event_id\n    }\n\n    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.user_id\n    }\n\n    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n        self.requested_at\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Event Envelope & Serialization                    */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/// Wrapper persisted to the outbox and exchanged via NATS.\n///\n/// We store payload as raw JSON (`payload_json`) to avoid polymorphism issues\n/// in the DB layer while still enabling typed deserialization by consumers.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct EventEnvelope {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    pub event_type: String,\n    pub event_version: u8,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Raw JSON representation of the event.\n    pub payload_json: serde_json::Value,\n}\n\nimpl EventEnvelope {\n    /// Create a new envelope from a concrete [`DomainEvent`] implementation.\n    pub fn from_event<E: DomainEvent>(event: &E) -> serde_json::Result<Self> {\n        Ok(Self {\n            id: event.event_id(),\n            aggregate_id: event.aggregate_id(),\n            event_type: E::EVENT_TYPE.to_string(),\n            event_version: E::EVENT_VERSION,\n            occurred_at: event.occurred_at(),\n            payload_json: serde_json::to_value(event)?,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                          Repository: Outbox Storage                       */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum OutboxError {\n    #[error(\"Database error: {0}\")]\n    Db(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    #[error(\"Serde error: {0}\")]\n    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventOutboxRepository: Send + Sync {\n    /// Persist a new domain event within an existing DB transaction.\n    async fn add_event<E: DomainEvent + Send + Sync>(\n        &self,\n        tx: &mut sqlx::Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n        event: &E,\n    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError>;\n\n    /// Fetch a *batch* of events that have not yet been dispatched.\n    async fn fetch_pending(\n        &self,\n        limit: i64,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, OutboxError>;\n\n    /// Mark the given event IDs as dispatched.\n    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, ids: &[Uuid]) -> Result<(), OutboxError>;\n}\n\n/// PostgreSQL implementation of [`EventOutboxRepository`].\npub struct PgOutboxRepository {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PgOutboxRepository {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventOutboxRepository for PgOutboxRepository {\n    #[instrument(skip(self, tx, event))]\n    async fn add_event<E: DomainEvent + Send + Sync>(\n        &self,\n        tx: &mut sqlx::Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n        event: &E,\n    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError> {\n        let env = EventEnvelope::from_event(event)?;\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO outbox_events (\n                id,\n                aggregate_id,\n                event_type,\n                event_version,\n                occurred_at,\n                payload_json\n            )\n            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n            \"#,\n            env.id,\n            env.aggregate_id,\n            env.event_type,\n            env.event_version as i16,\n            env.occurred_at,\n            env.payload_json,\n        )\n        .execute(&mut **tx)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n    async fn fetch_pending(\n        &self,\n        limit: i64,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, OutboxError> {\n        let raw = sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT id,\n                   aggregate_id,\n                   event_type,\n                   event_version,\n                   occurred_at,\n                   payload_json\n            FROM outbox_events\n            WHERE dispatched_at IS NULL\n            ORDER BY occurred_at\n            LIMIT $1\n            \"#,\n            limit\n        )\n        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        let mut events = Vec::with_capacity(raw.len());\n        for row in raw {\n            events.push(EventEnvelope {\n                id: row.id,\n                aggregate_id: row.aggregate_id,\n                event_type: row.event_type,\n                event_version: row.event_version as u8,\n                occurred_at: row.occurred_at,\n                payload_json: row.payload_json,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(events)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self, ids))]\n    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, ids: &[Uuid]) -> Result<(), OutboxError> {\n        if ids.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let mut qb: QueryBuilder<Postgres> = QueryBuilder::new(\n            \"UPDATE outbox_events SET dispatched_at = NOW() WHERE id IN (\",\n        );\n        let mut separated = qb.separated(\", \");\n        for id in ids {\n            separated.push_bind(id);\n        }\n        separated.push_unseparated(\")\");\n\n        qb.build().execute(&self.pool).await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Message Broker Integration                        */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum PublishError {\n    #[error(\"NATS error: {0}\")]\n    Nats(#[from] nats::Error),\n    #[error(\"Serialization error: {0}\")]\n    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), PublishError>;\n}\n\npub struct NatsPublisher {\n    conn: Connection,\n    subject_prefix: String,\n}\n\nimpl NatsPublisher {\n    pub async fn connect(\n        nats_url: &str,\n        jwt: Option<String>,\n        prefix: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Result<Self, PublishError> {\n        let conn = if let Some(token) = jwt {\n            asynk::Options::with_jwt(token, |nonce| async move {\n                // TODO: Provide signature callback for NATS auth if required.\n                Ok(vec![])\n            })\n            .connect(nats_url)\n            .await?\n        } else {\n            asynk::connect(nats_url).await?\n        };\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            conn,\n            subject_prefix: prefix.into(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn subject_for(&self, event_type: &str) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}.{}\", self.subject_prefix, event_type)\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n    #[instrument(skip(self, event))]\n    async fn publish(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n        let subject = self.subject_for(&event.event_type);\n        let data = serde_json::to_vec(event)?;\n        self.conn.publish(subject, data).await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                             Event Dispatcher                              */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum DispatchError {\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Outbox(#[from] OutboxError),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Publish(#[from] PublishError),\n}\n\n/// Repeatedly polls the outbox and hands events to the broker.\n///\n/// Spawn via [`tokio::spawn`]; it terminates when the passed `shutdown`\n/// [`tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver`] is signalled.\npub struct EventDispatcher<R: EventOutboxRepository, P: EventPublisher> {\n    repo: Arc<R>,\n    publisher: Arc<P>,\n    batch_size: i64,\n    poll_interval: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl<R, P> EventDispatcher<R, P>\nwhere\n    R: EventOutboxRepository + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        repo: Arc<R>,\n        publisher: Arc<P>,\n        batch_size: i64,\n        poll_interval: Duration,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            repo,\n            publisher,\n            batch_size,\n            poll_interval,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self, shutdown_rx), name = \"event_dispatch_loop\")]\n    pub fn start(\n        self,\n        mut shutdown_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<()>,\n    ) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            loop {\n                select! {\n                    _ = shutdown_rx.recv() => {\n                        info!(\"EventDispatcher received shutdown signal\");\n                        break;\n                    }\n                    _ = self.tick() => {}\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Single poll \u2192 publish \u2192 ack iteration.\n    async fn tick(&self) {\n        match self\n            .repo\n            .fetch_pending(self.batch_size)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(events) if events.is_empty() => {\n                sleep(self.poll_interval).await;\n            }\n            Ok(events) => {\n                let ids: Vec<Uuid> = events.iter().map(|e| e.id).collect();\n                debug!(count = events.len(), \"Publishing events\");\n\n                // Publish concurrently but limit concurrency to avoid backpressure.\n                let results = stream::iter(events)\n                    .map(|e| async {\n                        if let Err(err) = self.publisher.publish(&e).await {\n                            error!(error = %err, \"Failed to publish event\");\n                            Err(err)\n                        } else {\n                            Ok(())\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .buffer_unordered(8)\n                    .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                    .await;\n\n                if results.iter().all(Result::is_ok) {\n                    if let Err(err) = self.repo.mark_dispatched(&ids).await {\n                        error!(%err, \"Failed to mark events as dispatched\");\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    warn!(\"Some events failed to publish; will retry later\");\n                }\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                error!(%err, \"Failed to fetch pending events\");\n                sleep(self.poll_interval).await;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         Module Initialization Helper                      */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/// Build all dependencies and start dispatcher.\n///\n/// Example usage (in `main.rs`):\n///\n/// ```rust,no_run\n/// #[tokio::main]\n/// async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n///     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n///\n///     let (_shutdown_tx, shutdown_rx) = tokio::sync::broadcast::channel(1);\n///     let handle = module_48::init_and_spawn(\"postgres://...\", \"nats://localhost\", None, shutdown_rx).await?;\n///\n///     // ...\n///\n///     // On ctrl-c\n///     _shutdown_tx.send(())?;\n///     handle.await?;\n///     Ok(())\n/// }\n/// ```\npub async fn init_and_spawn(\n    pg_dsn: &str,\n    nats_url: &str,\n    nats_jwt: Option<String>,\n    shutdown_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<()>,\n) -> Result<JoinHandle<()>, anyhow::Error> {\n    let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n        .max_connections(8)\n        .connect(pg_dsn)\n        .await?;\n\n    let repo = Arc::new(PgOutboxRepository::new(pool));\n\n    let publisher = Arc::new(\n        NatsPublisher::connect(nats_url, nats_jwt, \"edupulse\")\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?,\n    );\n\n    let dispatcher = EventDispatcher::new(\n        repo,\n        publisher,\n        100,\n        Duration::from_secs(2),\n    );\n\n    Ok(dispatcher.start(shutdown_rx))\n}\n```",

    "numLines": 509,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 509
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_15.rs\n//!\n//! An event\u2013driven badge\u2013awarding micro-service for the\n//! \u201cEduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub\u201d platform.\n//!\n//! This module demonstrates how the core application publishes\n//! domain events while an out-of-process, autonomous component\n//! (here: `BadgeAwardService`) consumes them, performs business\n//! logic, persists the result and publishes its own follow-up\n//! events.  \n//!\n//! The service is completely self-contained: it hosts an in-memory\n//! dispatcher for local development, yet persists events and state\n//! in Postgres so it can be moved into a distributed setting\n//! later on (e.g. with NATS/Kafka\u2010backed `Dispatcher`).\n//!\n//! Architectural patterns shown here:\n//!   \u2022 Repository Pattern      \u2013 decoupling persistence\n//!   \u2022 Service Layer           \u2013 `BadgeAwardService`\n//!   \u2022 Event Sourcing          \u2013 immutable `EventStore`\n//!   \u2022 Async Rust w/ Tokio     \u2013 non-blocking I/O\n//!   \u2022 Error Handling w/ thiserror + anyhow\n//!\n//! NOTE: In a real-world project this module would be split\n//! into several files/crates.  Everything is co-located here only\n//! to satisfy the single-file constraint of the exercise.\n\nuse std::{\n    collections::HashSet,\n    sync::Arc,\n    time::Duration,\n};\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tokio::{\n    select,\n    sync::{broadcast, mpsc, RwLock},\n    task::JoinHandle,\n};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Domain events which can be emitted by EduPulse services.\n///\n/// IMPORTANT: New variants **must** implement `Serialize`/`Deserialize`\n/// so they can be persisted in the event store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"payload\")]\npub enum DomainEvent {\n    QuizSubmitted(QuizSubmitted),\n    BadgeAwarded(BadgeAwarded),\n    // \u2026 more variants omitted for brevity\n}\n\nimpl DomainEvent {\n    /// Helper returning event name for logging / metrics.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(_) => \"QuizSubmitted\",\n            DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(_) => \"BadgeAwarded\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Metadata common to all envelopes\u2014separates immutable event data\n/// from peripheral transport concerns such as IDs and timestamps.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct EventEnvelope {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub event: DomainEvent,\n}\n\nimpl EventEnvelope {\n    pub fn new(event: DomainEvent) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n            event,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Event data triggered when a learner submits a quiz.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QuizSubmitted {\n    pub quiz_id: Uuid,\n    pub learner_id: Uuid,\n    pub score: f32,\n}\n\n/// Event data emitted once the learner qualifies for a badge.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BadgeAwarded {\n    pub badge_id: Uuid,\n    pub learner_id: Uuid,\n    pub awarded_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub reason: String,\n}\n\n/// Central event dispatcher.  \n/// Internally uses a Tokio broadcast channel so any number of\n/// in-process consumers can receive events concurrently.\n///\n/// In production you might replace the implementation with NATS,\n/// Kafka, RabbitMQ\u2026 etc., leaving the public API intact.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Dispatcher {\n    sender: broadcast::Sender<EventEnvelope>,\n}\n\nimpl Dispatcher {\n    /// Create a new dispatcher with a configurable ring buffer size.\n    pub fn new(channel_size: usize) -> Self {\n        let (sender, _rx) = broadcast::channel(channel_size);\n        Self { sender }\n    }\n\n    /// Publish an envelope to all subscribers.\n    pub async fn publish(&self, envelope: EventEnvelope) {\n        // broadcast::Sender::send is synchronous but inexpensive.\n        // Failure only occurs when there are no active receivers;\n        // still, we ignore that because the event is persisted in\n        // the store before reaching this point.\n        let _ = self.sender.send(envelope);\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to a continuous stream of events.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<EventEnvelope> {\n        self.sender.subscribe()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Persisted event stream; provides idempotent append + read access.\n/// Uses Postgres JSONB for flexible schema evolution.\n///\n/// Table DDL (run via migrations):\n/// ```sql\n/// CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS event_store (\n///     id           UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n///     occurred_at  TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,\n///     event_name   TEXT NOT NULL,\n///     payload      JSONB NOT NULL\n/// );\n/// ```\npub struct EventStore {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl EventStore {\n    pub async fn new(db_url: &str, max_connections: u32) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {\n        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n            .max_connections(max_connections)\n            .connect(db_url)\n            .await?;\n        Ok(Self { pool })\n    }\n\n    /// Append envelope to store.\n    pub async fn append(&self, env: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO event_store (id, occurred_at, event_name, payload)\n            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n            \"#,\n            env.id,\n            env.occurred_at,\n            env.event.name(),\n            serde_json::to_value(&env.event)?\n        )\n        .execute(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Read events by name\u2014useful for rebuilding read models.\n    pub async fn load_by_event(\n        &self,\n        event_name: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, sqlx::Error> {\n        let recs = sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT id, occurred_at, payload\n            FROM event_store\n            WHERE event_name = $1\n            ORDER BY occurred_at ASC\n            \"#,\n            event_name\n        )\n        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        recs.into_iter()\n            .map(|r| {\n                let event = match event_name {\n                    \"QuizSubmitted\" => DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n                        serde_json::from_value(r.payload)?,\n                    ),\n                    \"BadgeAwarded\" => DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(\n                        serde_json::from_value(r.payload)?,\n                    ),\n                    _ => unreachable!(\"Unknown event stored in DB\"),\n                };\n                Ok(EventEnvelope {\n                    id: r.id,\n                    occurred_at: r.occurred_at,\n                    event,\n                })\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error type covering all repository/service error cases.\n#[derive(Error, Debug)]\npub enum ServiceError {\n    #[error(\"Database error: {0}\")]\n    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"Event serialisation error: {0}\")]\n    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"Internal channel closed\")]\n    ChannelClosed,\n}\n\n/// Repository abstraction for badge persistence.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait BadgeRepository: Send + Sync {\n    async fn learner_already_has_badge(\n        &self,\n        learner_id: Uuid,\n        badge_id: Uuid,\n    ) -> Result<bool, ServiceError>;\n\n    async fn assign_badge(\n        &self,\n        learner_id: Uuid,\n        badge: &BadgeAwarded,\n    ) -> Result<(), ServiceError>;\n}\n\n/// Postgres-based implementation of the `BadgeRepository`.\n///\n/// Table DDL:\n/// ```sql\n/// CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learner_badges (\n///     learner_id UUID NOT NULL,\n///     badge_id   UUID NOT NULL,\n///     awarded_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,\n///     reason     TEXT NOT NULL,\n///     PRIMARY KEY (learner_id, badge_id)\n/// );\n/// ```\npub struct PgBadgeRepository {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PgBadgeRepository {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl BadgeRepository for PgBadgeRepository {\n    async fn learner_already_has_badge(\n        &self,\n        learner_id: Uuid,\n        badge_id: Uuid,\n    ) -> Result<bool, ServiceError> {\n        let res = sqlx::query_scalar!(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT EXISTS (\n                SELECT 1\n                FROM learner_badges\n                WHERE learner_id = $1 AND badge_id = $2\n            ) AS \"exists!\"\n            \"#,\n            learner_id,\n            badge_id\n        )\n        .fetch_one(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        Ok(res.unwrap_or(false))\n    }\n\n    async fn assign_badge(\n        &self,\n        learner_id: Uuid,\n        badge: &BadgeAwarded,\n    ) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO learner_badges (learner_id, badge_id, awarded_at, reason)\n            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n            \"#,\n            learner_id,\n            badge.badge_id,\n            badge.awarded_at,\n            badge.reason\n        )\n        .execute(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Business logic deciding whether a quiz submission qualifies\n/// for a badge.  Runs fully asynchronously, keeping its own\n/// subscription to the dispatcher.\n///\n/// Scoring rules used here are intentionally simple:  \n/// \u2013 Score \u2265 90 %      \u21d2 \u201cQuiz Master\u201d badge (UUID hard-coded)  \n/// \u2013 Score exactly 100 \u21d2 special \u201cPerfect Score\u201d badge\npub struct BadgeAwardService<R: BadgeRepository + 'static> {\n    repo: Arc<R>,\n    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n    event_store: Arc<EventStore>,\n    subscribed_badges: HashSet<Uuid>,\n}\n\nimpl<R: BadgeRepository + 'static> BadgeAwardService<R> {\n    pub fn new(\n        repo: Arc<R>,\n        dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n        event_store: Arc<EventStore>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        // Hard-coded UUIDs of known badges.\n        let subscribed_badges = vec![\n            // \u201cQuiz Master\u201d\n            Uuid::parse_str(\"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\").unwrap(),\n            // \u201cPerfect Score\u201d\n            Uuid::parse_str(\"22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222\").unwrap(),\n        ]\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect();\n\n        Self {\n            repo,\n            dispatcher,\n            event_store,\n            subscribed_badges,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn the service on the Tokio runtime; returns its `JoinHandle`\n    /// so callers can `.await` graceful shutdown.\n    pub fn spawn(self: Arc<Self>) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n        let mut rx = self.dispatcher.subscribe();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            loop {\n                select! {\n                    biased;\n\n                    // React to new events\n                    msg = rx.recv() => match msg {\n                        Ok(env) => {\n                            if let Err(e) = self.process_event(env).await {\n                                tracing::error!(\"BadgeAwardService failed: {e}\");\n                            }\n                        },\n                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => {\n                            tracing::warn!(\"Dispatcher closed, BadgeAwardService exiting.\");\n                            break;\n                        },\n                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(skipped)) => {\n                            tracing::warn!(\"BadgeAwardService lagged, skipped {skipped} messages\");\n                        },\n                    },\n\n                    // Allow cooperative cancellation.\n                    _ = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(60)) => {},\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn process_event(&self, env: EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n        match env.event {\n            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(sub) => self.maybe_award_badge(sub).await?,\n            _ => { /* ignore */ }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn maybe_award_badge(&self, sub: QuizSubmitted) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n        // Determine qualifying badge.\n        let (badge_id, reason) = if (sub.score - 100.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON {\n            (\n                Uuid::parse_str(\"22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222\").unwrap(),\n                \"Perfect quiz score!\".to_owned(),\n            )\n        } else if sub.score >= 90.0 {\n            (\n                Uuid::parse_str(\"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\").unwrap(),\n                \"Scored 90% or above on a quiz.\".to_owned(),\n            )\n        } else {\n            return Ok(()); // Not eligible\n        };\n\n        // Filter unknown badge IDs (forward-compat).\n        if !self.subscribed_badges.contains(&badge_id) {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Idempotency check: learner may resubmit quiz multiple times.\n        if self\n            .repo\n            .learner_already_has_badge(sub.learner_id, badge_id)\n            .await?\n        {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Compose and persist badge award.\n        let badge = BadgeAwarded {\n            badge_id,\n            learner_id: sub.learner_id,\n            awarded_at: Utc::now(),\n            reason,\n        };\n\n        self.repo.assign_badge(sub.learner_id, &badge).await?;\n\n        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(badge.clone()));\n        self.event_store.append(&envelope).await?;\n        self.dispatcher.publish(envelope).await;\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            learner=%sub.learner_id,\n            badge=%badge.badge_id,\n            \"Badge awarded!\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//  Bootstrap / Example\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// Start an in-process example\u2014useful when the module is compiled\n/// as `cargo run --example badge_service`.\n#[cfg(feature = \"example\")]\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    // Use dotenv if available.\n    dotenvy::dotenv().ok();\n    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n\n    let db_url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n        .expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set (e.g. postgres://user:pw@localhost/web_social)\");\n\n    // \u2500\u2500 Infrastructure \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    let event_store = Arc::new(EventStore::new(&db_url, 5).await?);\n    let dispatcher = Dispatcher::new(1024);\n\n    let badge_repo = Arc::new(PgBadgeRepository::new(event_store.pool.clone()));\n\n    // \u2500\u2500 Start service \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    let service = Arc::new(BadgeAwardService::new(\n        badge_repo,\n        dispatcher.clone(),\n        event_store.clone(),\n    ));\n    let handle = service.clone().spawn();\n\n    // \u2500\u2500 Emit demo event \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    let demo_event = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n        QuizSubmitted {\n            quiz_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            learner_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            score: 96.5,\n        },\n    ));\n    event_store.append(&demo_event).await?;\n    dispatcher.publish(demo_event).await;\n\n    // Wait for service to process event.\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Cancel task gracefully (development only).\n    handle.abort();\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//  Tests\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use sqlx::Executor;\n\n    // Spawns a temporary in-memory Postgres (requires `postgres` feature\n    // of `sqlx-test` or a running database in CI).\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn badge_award_flow_happy_path() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        // Skip test when DATABASE_URL not provided; this allows `cargo test`\n        // without database.\n        let db_url = match std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\") {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(_) => return Ok(()),\n        };\n\n        // Prepare schema (idempotent).\n        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n            .max_connections(1)\n            .connect(&db_url)\n            .await?;\n        pool.execute(\n            r#\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS event_store (\n                id UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n                occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n                event_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                payload JSONB NOT NULL\n            );\n\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learner_badges (\n                learner_id UUID NOT NULL,\n                badge_id UUID NOT NULL,\n                awarded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n                reason TEXT NOT NULL,\n                PRIMARY KEY (learner_id, badge_id)\n            );\n            \"#,\n        )\n        .await?;\n\n        let event_store = Arc::new(EventStore { pool: pool.clone() });\n        let dispatcher = Dispatcher::new(16);\n        let badge_repo = Arc::new(PgBadgeRepository::new(pool.clone()));\n\n        let service = Arc::new(BadgeAwardService::new(\n            badge_repo.clone(),\n            dispatcher.clone(),\n            event_store.clone(),\n        ));\n        let _handle = service.clone().spawn();\n\n        let learner_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n        let quiz_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n\n        // Emit qualifying quiz submission.\n        let env = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n            QuizSubmitted {\n                quiz_id,\n                learner_id,\n                score: 97.0,\n            },\n        ));\n        event_store.append(&env).await?;\n        dispatcher.publish(env.clone()).await;\n\n        // Wait for async processing.\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(600)).await;\n\n        // Verify badge persisted.\n        let badges = sqlx::query!(\n            \"SELECT badge_id FROM learner_badges WHERE learner_id = $1\",\n            learner_id\n        )\n        .fetch_all(pool.as_ref())\n        .await?;\n\n        assert_eq!(badges.len(), 1);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_15.txt",
    "numLines": 580,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 580
  },
  "type": "text"
}

24. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_36.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_36.rs\n//!\n//! Learning-Pulse domain module.\n//!\n//! This module handles the life-cycle of a `LearningPulseResponse`:\n//!   1.  Persist the response in Postgres via `sqlx`.\n//!   2.  Emit a domain event onto the message broker (RabbitMQ) so that\n//!      downstream services (plagiarism check, rubric scoring, notifications,\n//!      adaptive recommendation, search indexing, etc.) can react\n//!      asynchronously.\n//!\n//! The code is production-ready, uses Tokio for async, `tracing` for\n//! structured logging and `thiserror` for rich error handling.  \n//! All public APIs are expressed as traits to facilitate dependency\n//! injection and unit testing.\n//\n//  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n//  \u2502                                               \u2502\n//  \u2502  HTTP Layer (Actix-web / Axum)                \u2502\n//  \u2502           \u2502                                   \u2502\n//  \u2502           \u25bc                                   \u2502\n//  \u2502  --------LearningPulseService --------------- \u2502\n//  \u2502    \u2502 persist()             \u2502 publish()        \u2502\n//  \u2502    \u25bc                       \u25bc                  \u2502\n//  \u2502 PostgresRepository   RabbitMqEventPublisher   \u2502\n//  \u2502                                               \u2502\n//  \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tokio::{spawn, task::JoinHandle};\nuse tracing::{error, info};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Domain Models \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n\n/// Unique identifier type aliases just to provide extra compile-time safety.\npub type PulseId = Uuid;\npub type ResponseId = Uuid;\npub type UserId = Uuid;\n\n/// A student reply to a Learning Pulse.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LearningPulseResponse {\n    pub id: ResponseId,\n    pub pulse_id: PulseId,\n    pub author_id: UserId,\n    pub content: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Helper DTO used by service callers (presentation layer) to submit a reply.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\npub struct SubmitResponseInput {\n    pub pulse_id: PulseId,\n    pub content: String,\n}\n\n/// Additional request context extracted by auth/session middleware.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RequestContext {\n    pub user_id: UserId,\n    /// Trace ID injected by instrumentation middleware so logs can be\n    /// correlated end-to-end.\n    pub trace_id: Uuid,\n}\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Repository (Persistence) Layer \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n\n/// Storage abstraction for `LearningPulseResponse`.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait ResponseRepository: Send + Sync {\n    async fn insert(\n        &self,\n        response: &LearningPulseResponse,\n    ) -> Result<(), RepositoryError>;\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum RepositoryError {\n    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n}\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Event / Messaging Layer \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n\n/// An event emitted whenever a student submits a response.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ResponseSubmittedEvent {\n    pub response_id: ResponseId,\n    pub pulse_id: PulseId,\n    pub author_id: UserId,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Generic publisher interface.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish<T: Serialize + Send + Sync>(\n        &self,\n        routing_key: &str,\n        payload: &T,\n    ) -> Result<(), PublisherError>;\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum PublisherError {\n    #[error(\"broker error: {0}\")]\n    Broker(#[from] lapin::Error),\n    #[error(\"serialization error: {0}\")]\n    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n}\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Service Layer \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n\n/// Business-logic errors surfaced to controllers/UI layer.\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum ServiceError {\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Repository(#[from] RepositoryError),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Publisher(#[from] PublisherError),\n}\n\n/// Service orchestrating repository + broker actions atomically.\npub struct LearningPulseService<R, P>\nwhere\n    R: ResponseRepository + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    repo: Arc<R>,\n    publisher: Arc<P>,\n}\n\nimpl<R, P> LearningPulseService<R, P>\nwhere\n    R: ResponseRepository + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    pub fn new(repo: Arc<R>, publisher: Arc<P>) -> Self {\n        Self { repo, publisher }\n    }\n\n    /// Persist response and asynchronously publish event.\n    ///\n    /// The response is committed first; event publication runs in the\n    /// background\u2014if it fails, we log the failure but keep the write durable\n    /// (outbox pattern could be used to guarantee eventual publish).\n    pub async fn submit_response(\n        &self,\n        input: SubmitResponseInput,\n        ctx: RequestContext,\n    ) -> Result<LearningPulseResponse, ServiceError> {\n        // Construct domain entity\n        let response = LearningPulseResponse {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            pulse_id: input.pulse_id,\n            author_id: ctx.user_id,\n            content: input.content,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n        };\n\n        // 1) Persist to the database\n        self.repo.insert(&response).await?;\n\n        // 2) Fire-and-forget event publication\n        let publisher = self.publisher.clone();\n        let event = ResponseSubmittedEvent {\n            response_id: response.id,\n            pulse_id: response.pulse_id,\n            author_id: response.author_id,\n            occurred_at: response.created_at,\n        };\n        let trace_id = ctx.trace_id;\n\n        spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = publisher\n                .publish(\"learning_pulse.response_submitted\", &event)\n                .await\n            {\n                error!(\n                    %trace_id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to publish ResponseSubmittedEvent\"\n                );\n            } else {\n                info!(%trace_id, \"ResponseSubmittedEvent published\");\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(response)\n    }\n}\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Concrete Infrastructure Implementations \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool};\n\n/// Production Postgres implementation.\n///\n/// The struct is clone-able because `PgPool` is internally reference counted.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct PostgresResponseRepository {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresResponseRepository {\n    pub async fn connect(database_url: &str) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {\n        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n            .max_connections(8)\n            .connect(database_url)\n            .await?;\n\n        // Run migrations (idempotent).  In production, migrations are executed\n        // separately by CI/CD pipelines; we keep this for dev/integ test\n        // convenience.\n        sqlx::migrate!().run(&pool).await?;\n\n        Ok(Self { pool })\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ResponseRepository for PostgresResponseRepository {\n    async fn insert(\n        &self,\n        response: &LearningPulseResponse,\n    ) -> Result<(), RepositoryError> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO learning_pulse_responses\n                    (id, pulse_id, author_id, content, created_at)\n            VALUES  ($1, $2,       $3,        $4,      $5)\n            \"#,\n            response.id,\n            response.pulse_id,\n            response.author_id,\n            response.content,\n            response.created_at,\n        )\n        .execute(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nuse lapin::{\n    options::BasicPublishOptions,\n    publisher_confirm::Confirmation,\n    types::FieldTable,\n    BasicProperties, Channel, Connection, ConnectionProperties,\n};\n\n/// RabbitMQ implementation using `lapin`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RabbitMqEventPublisher {\n    channel: Channel,\n}\n\nimpl RabbitMqEventPublisher {\n    pub async fn connect(uri: &str) -> Result<Self, lapin::Error> {\n        let conn = Connection::connect(uri, ConnectionProperties::default()).await?;\n        let channel = conn.create_channel().await?;\n        Ok(Self { channel })\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for RabbitMqEventPublisher {\n    async fn publish<T: Serialize + Send + Sync>(\n        &self,\n        routing_key: &str,\n        payload: &T,\n    ) -> Result<(), PublisherError> {\n        let data = serde_json::to_vec(payload)?;\n\n        let confirm: Confirmation = self\n            .channel\n            .basic_publish(\n                \"edupulse_domain\",                // exchange\n                routing_key,\n                BasicPublishOptions::default(),\n                &data,\n                BasicProperties::default()\n                    .with_content_type(\"application/json\".into())\n                    .with_delivery_mode(2), // persistent\n            )\n            .await?\n            .await?; // wait for ack\n\n        if confirm.is_nack() {\n            Err(lapin::Error::InvalidAck.into())\n        } else {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n//\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Unit Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n//\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use std::collections::VecDeque;\n    use tokio::sync::Mutex;\n\n    /// In-memory test double for repository layer.\n    struct InMemoryRepo {\n        storage: Mutex<VecDeque<LearningPulseResponse>>,\n    }\n\n    impl InMemoryRepo {\n        fn new() -> Self {\n            Self {\n                storage: Mutex::new(VecDeque::new()),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl ResponseRepository for InMemoryRepo {\n        async fn insert(\n            &self,\n            response: &LearningPulseResponse,\n        ) -> Result<(), RepositoryError> {\n            self.storage.lock().await.push_back(response.clone());\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test double for the publisher layer.\n    struct StubPublisher;\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl EventPublisher for StubPublisher {\n        async fn publish<T: Serialize + Send + Sync>(\n            &self,\n            _routing_key: &str,\n            _payload: &T,\n        ) -> Result<(), PublisherError> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn submit_response_persists_and_publishes() {\n        let svc = LearningPulseService::new(\n            Arc::new(InMemoryRepo::new()),\n            Arc::new(StubPublisher),\n        );\n\n        let input = SubmitResponseInput {\n            pulse_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            content: \"E = mc^2\".into(),\n        };\n\n        let ctx = RequestContext {\n            user_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            trace_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n        };\n\n        let resp = svc.submit_response(input, ctx).await.unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(resp.content, \"E = mc^2\");\n    }\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_36.txt",
    "numLines": 383,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 383
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```markdown\n# EduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub\n\n![CI](https://github.com/edupulse/live/workflows/CI/badge.svg)\n![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/edupulse/live)\n![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-1.74%2B-orange)\n\nEduPulse Live is an expert-level, event-driven social network for educators and learners that blends course discussions, real-time collaboration, and academic resource sharing.  \nEvery domain concept\u2014lesson publication, quiz submission, peer feedback, badge awards, or payment for premium micro-courses\u2014**emits domain events** that flow through a message broker, keeping the core HTTP API lean and highly responsive.\n\n> TL;DR  \n> `cargo run --package web_social` will spin up the API on <https://localhost:8080>, publish domain events to **RabbitMQ**, persist state in **PostgreSQL**, index resources in **MeiliSearch**, and serve a blazing-fast interactive SPA via **Trunk + Wasm**.\n\n---\n\n## \u2728 Key Capabilities\n\n| Category            | Highlights                                                                              |\n|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Real-time Learning  | \u201cLearning pulses\u201d, live Q&A threads, WebSocket rooms, in-browser code runners           |\n| Event-Driven Core   | Every aggregate action produces a strongly-typed CloudEvent                             |\n| Search & Discovery  | Incremental MeiliSearch indexing via asynchronous workers                               |\n| Achievements        | Badge / XP engine powered by event-sourced projections                                  |\n| Compliance          | GDPR \u201cright to be forgotten\u201d implemented as compensating erase events                   |\n| Secure by Default   | TLS-terminated, OAuth2 (Google EDU, Microsoft), rotating Redis sessions, rate limiting  |\n\n---\n\n## \ud83c\udfd7\ufe0f  Tech Stack\n\n* **Rust 1.74+**   \u2013 Actix Web, Tokio, SeaORM  \n* **RabbitMQ 3.12** \u2013 AMQP 0-9-1 event bus  \n* **PostgreSQL 15** \u2013 primary data store, CQRS projections  \n* **MeiliSearch 1.4** \u2013 full-text search  \n* **Redis 7**        \u2013 cache + session store  \n* **React 18 + WASM** \u2013 front-end (see `/frontend`)  \n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\ude80 Quick Start\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/edupulse/live.git\ncd live\n\n# 1. Configure environment variables\ncp .env.example .env\nvi .env           # adjust DB urls, secrets, etc.\n\n# 2. Database & broker\ndocker compose up -d postgres rabbitmq redis meilisearch\n\n# 3. Apply migrations\ncargo install sea-orm-cli\nsea migrate up\n\n# 4. Run the whole stack (API + workers)\ncargo make dev         # dev profile with hot-reloading (`cargo-make` is a dev-dependency)\n\n# 5. Hit the health-check\ncurl -k https://localhost:8080/healthz\n```\n\n`make dev` orchestrates:\n\n* `web_social` (Actix HTTP API)\n* `social_worker` (background AMQP consumer)\n* `trunk serve` (front-end local dev)\n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\uddc2\ufe0f Repository Layout\n\n```\n.\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 crates\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 domain          # Domain aggregates, value objects, events\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 web_social      # REST/WS API crate (Actix)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 social_worker   # Async workers (search, mail, achievements\u2026)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 shared          # Cross-cutting (error types, tracing, config)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 frontend            # React + WASM widgets\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 deploy              # Helm charts, Terraform, GitHub Actions\n```\n\n---\n\n## \ud83e\udde9 Architecture Primer\n\nWe follow a pragmatic **Service-Layer + Repository Pattern** with clean separation of concerns.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\nA[HTTP / WS] -->|DTO| B(API Controller)\nB -->|call| C(Service Layer)\nC -->|persist| D{Repository}\nC -->|publish| E[Domain Event]\nE -->|AMQP| F[Worker]\nF -->|side-effect| G[MeiliSearch / Email / Analytics]\n```\n\n### 1. Domain Event Definition\n\n```rust\n// crates/domain/src/event/pulse.rs\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// CloudEvent-compliant wrapper for all domain events\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CloudEvent<T>\nwhere\n    T: Serialize,\n{\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub source: String,\n    pub specversion: String,\n    pub r#type: String,\n    pub datacontenttype: String,\n    pub time: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub data: T,\n}\n\n/// Specific payload for a LearningPulseCreated event\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LearningPulseCreated {\n    pub pulse_id: Uuid,\n    pub author_id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Service Layer Publishes Events\n\n```rust\n// crates/web_social/src/service/pulse_service.rs\nuse domain::event::{CloudEvent, LearningPulseCreated};\nuse shared::{\n    amqp::Publisher,\n    db::RepoPool,\n    errors::ServiceError,\n};\n\npub struct PulseService {\n    repo: RepoPool,\n    publisher: Publisher,\n}\n\nimpl PulseService {\n    pub async fn create_pulse(&self, cmd: CreatePulse) -> Result<PulseDto, ServiceError> {\n        let pulse = self.repo.pulse().insert(&cmd).await?;\n        let event = CloudEvent::from_payload(\n            \"com.edupulse.pulse.created\",\n            &LearningPulseCreated {\n                pulse_id: pulse.id,\n                author_id: pulse.author_id,\n                title: pulse.title.clone(),\n                expires_at: pulse.expires_at,\n            },\n        );\n        self.publisher.publish(event).await?;\n        Ok(PulseDto::from(pulse))\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Worker Listens & Acts\n\n```rust\n// crates/social_worker/src/handlers/search_index.rs\nuse domain::event::LearningPulseCreated;\nuse shared::{amqp::Consumer, search::SearchIndexer};\n\npub async fn start_search_listener(consumer: Consumer, indexer: SearchIndexer) {\n    consumer\n        .listen::<LearningPulseCreated>(\"com.edupulse.pulse.created\", move |evt| {\n            let idx = indexer.clone();\n            async move {\n                idx.index_pulse(evt.data).await?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        })\n        .await\n        .expect(\"Search listener crashed\");\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\udd12 Security Notes\n\n1. All external traffic is forced through **TLS 1.3** (`rustls`, HSTS, OCSP stapling).  \n2. Cookies: `__Secure-sid`, `SameSite=Lax`, 15 min idle, 8 hour absolute, rotated every login.  \n3. Input validation enforced at DTO boundaries (`validator` crate).  \n4. **Brute-force guard** via Redis-backed sliding window rate limiter.  \n5. Secrets managed through `age`-encrypted SOPS files + GitHub OIDC \u2192 AWS Secrets Manager.\n\n---\n\n## \ud83e\uddea Testing Matrix\n\n| Layer            | Tooling            | Command                           |\n|------------------|--------------------|-----------------------------------|\n| Unit (lib)       | `cargo test`       | `cargo test --workspace`          |\n| Integration API  | `rstest` + `sqlx`  | `cargo test -p web_social it_`    |\n| End-to-End       | `playwright`       | `npm run test:e2e` (frontend)     |\n| Load / chaos     | `k6` + `tc`        | `make perf`                       |\n\nCI runs on GitHub Actions in parallel Kubernetes jobs with 90%+ branch coverage threshold (gated).\n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\ude4c Contributing\n\n1. Fork \u2192 feature branch (conventional commits please: `feat(api): add reactions`)\n2. `cargo fmt && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`\n3. Open PR; two approvals & green CI required to merge.\n\nSee [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) for full guidelines.\n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\udcdc License\n\nEduPulse Live is licensed under the **Apache 2.0** License \u2013 see [`LICENSE`](LICENSE).\n\nHappy learning \ud83c\udf93\ud83d\ude80\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/README.md",
    "numLines": 229,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 229
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_1.rs\n//! Authentication & Session Management module for **EduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub**\n//!\n//! This module demonstrates the Repository pattern, a thin Service layer,\n//! domain-event emission, and secure session management (JWT + Redis).\n//! It is self-contained and can be dropped into the larger workspace.\n//!\n//! Dependencies expected in `Cargo.toml` (not shown here):\n//! --------------------------------------------------------------------\n//! [dependencies]\n//! sqlx           = { version = \"0.7\", features = [\"postgres\", \"runtime-tokio-rustls\", \"uuid\"] }\n//! async-trait    = \"0.1\"\n//! serde          = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n//! serde_json     = \"1.0\"\n//! uuid           = { version = \"1\", features = [\"v4\"] }\n//! argon2         = \"0.5\"\n//! jsonwebtoken   = \"8\"\n//! chrono         = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\n//! thiserror      = \"1.0\"\n//! tracing        = \"0.1\"\n//! deadpool-redis = { version = \"0.12\", features = [\"rt_tokio_1\"] }\n//! anyhow         = \"1.0\"\n//!\n//! Feature flags: `database_integration`, `session_management`, `user_authentication`\n//! --------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nuse std::env;\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgRow, PgPool, Row};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tracing::{error, info};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse argon2::{self, password_hash::SaltString, Argon2, PasswordHash, PasswordHasher, PasswordVerifier};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{\n    decode, encode, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, TokenData, Validation,\n};\n\n/// Custom result type for the auth domain\npub type Result<T, E = AuthError> = std::result::Result<T, E>;\n\n/// Central error enumeration covering common failure scenarios.\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum AuthError {\n    #[error(\"hashing error\")]\n    Hashing(#[from] argon2::password_hash::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"database error\")]\n    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"invalid credentials\")]\n    InvalidCredentials,\n\n    #[error(\"jwt error\")]\n    Jwt(#[from] jsonwebtoken::errors::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"event bus error: {0}\")]\n    EventBus(#[from] anyhow::Error),\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Domain model\n/// =============================================================================================\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct User {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub email: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing)]\n    pub password_hash: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct NewUser {\n    pub email: String,\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct Session {\n    pub token: String,\n    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// JWT claims stored in the access token\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct Claims {\n    sub: String,\n    exp: usize,\n    iat: usize,\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Repository layer\n/// =============================================================================================\n#[async_trait]\npub trait UserRepository: Send + Sync {\n    async fn insert(&self, user: &User) -> Result<()>;\n    async fn find_by_email(&self, email: &str) -> Result<Option<User>>;\n    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>>;\n}\n\npub struct PostgresUserRepository {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresUserRepository {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl UserRepository for PostgresUserRepository {\n    async fn insert(&self, user: &User) -> Result<()> {\n        sqlx::query(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, created_at)\n            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n            \"#,\n        )\n        .bind(user.id)\n        .bind(&user.email)\n        .bind(&user.password_hash)\n        .bind(user.created_at)\n        .execute(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn find_by_email(&self, email: &str) -> Result<Option<User>> {\n        let maybe_row = sqlx::query(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at\n            FROM users\n            WHERE email = $1\n            \"#,\n        )\n        .bind(email)\n        .map(map_row_to_user)\n        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        Ok(maybe_row)\n    }\n\n    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>> {\n        let maybe_row = sqlx::query(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at\n            FROM users\n            WHERE id = $1\n            \"#,\n        )\n        .bind(id)\n        .map(map_row_to_user)\n        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        Ok(maybe_row)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Helper for mapping a row into a [`User`].\nfn map_row_to_user(row: PgRow) -> User {\n    User {\n        id: row.get(\"id\"),\n        email: row.get(\"email\"),\n        password_hash: row.get(\"password_hash\"),\n        created_at: row.get(\"created_at\"),\n    }\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Event bus abstraction\n/// =============================================================================================\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub enum DomainEvent {\n    UserRegistered { user_id: Uuid, email: String },\n    UserLoggedIn { user_id: Uuid },\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish(&self, evt: &DomainEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()>;\n}\n\n/// A naive, logging-only event bus implementation. Replace with Kafka/NATS/RabbitMQ as needed.\npub struct TracingEventBus;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventBus for TracingEventBus {\n    async fn publish(&self, evt: &DomainEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        info!(?evt, \"domain event published\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Session cache (Redis)\n/// =============================================================================================\nuse deadpool_redis::{cmd, redis::AsyncCommands, Config, Connection, Pool};\n\npub struct SessionStore {\n    pool: Pool,\n}\n\nimpl SessionStore {\n    pub fn new(redis_url: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Self> {\n        let mut cfg = Config::from_url(redis_url);\n        cfg.pool.timeout = Some(std::time::Duration::from_secs(3));\n\n        let pool = cfg.create_pool(Some(deadpool_redis::Runtime::Tokio1))?;\n        Ok(Self { pool })\n    }\n\n    async fn put(&self, token: &str, user_id: Uuid, ttl: Duration) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n        cmd(\"SET\")\n            .arg(token)\n            .arg(user_id.to_string())\n            .arg(\"EX\")\n            .arg(ttl.num_seconds())\n            .query_async(&mut conn)\n            .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    async fn get_user_id(&self, token: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Uuid>> {\n        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n        let resp: Option<String> = conn.get(token).await?;\n        Ok(resp.and_then(|s| Uuid::parse_str(&s).ok()))\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, token: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n        conn.del(token).await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Service layer\n/// =============================================================================================\npub struct AuthService<R: UserRepository, B: EventBus> {\n    repo: R,\n    event_bus: B,\n    session_store: SessionStore,\n    jwt_secret: String,\n    session_ttl: Duration,\n    argon2: Argon2<'static>,\n}\n\nimpl<R: UserRepository, B: EventBus> AuthService<R, B> {\n    /// Create an [`AuthService`] with sensible defaults.  \n    /// `jwt_secret` **must** be a strong, randomly-generated 256-bit key (base64 encoded).\n    pub fn new(repo: R, event_bus: B, session_store: SessionStore, jwt_secret: String) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            repo,\n            event_bus,\n            session_store,\n            jwt_secret,\n            session_ttl: Duration::hours(12),\n            argon2: Argon2::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Registers a new user, returning a newly minted [`Session`] on success.\n    pub async fn register_user(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session> {\n        if self.repo.find_by_email(&new_user.email).await?.is_some() {\n            return Err(AuthError::InvalidCredentials); // simplified: email already taken\n        }\n\n        let salt = SaltString::generate(&mut rand::thread_rng());\n        let password_hash = self\n            .argon2\n            .hash_password(new_user.password.as_bytes(), &salt)?\n            .to_string();\n\n        let user = User {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            email: new_user.email,\n            password_hash,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n        };\n\n        self.repo.insert(&user).await?;\n        self.event_bus\n            .publish(&DomainEvent::UserRegistered {\n                user_id: user.id,\n                email: user.email.clone(),\n            })\n            .await?;\n\n        let session = self.create_session(user.id).await?;\n        Ok(session)\n    }\n\n    /// Authenticates a user and returns a valid session token.\n    pub async fn login_user(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session> {\n        let user = self\n            .repo\n            .find_by_email(email)\n            .await?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n\n        // Verify password\n        let parsed_hash = PasswordHash::new(&user.password_hash)?;\n        self.argon2\n            .verify_password(password.as_bytes(), &parsed_hash)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n\n        let session = self.create_session(user.id).await?;\n\n        self.event_bus\n            .publish(&DomainEvent::UserLoggedIn { user_id: user.id })\n            .await?;\n\n        Ok(session)\n    }\n\n    /// Invalidates an existing session.\n    pub async fn logout_user(&self, token: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.session_store\n            .delete(token)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Helper: create a JWT token + Redis cache entry.\n    async fn create_session(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<Session> {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let exp = (now + self.session_ttl).timestamp() as usize;\n\n        let claims = Claims {\n            sub: user_id.to_string(),\n            exp,\n            iat: now.timestamp() as usize,\n        };\n\n        let header = Header::default();\n        let token = encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(self.jwt_secret.as_bytes()),\n        )?;\n\n        // Cache in Redis for server-side revocation\n        self.session_store\n            .put(&token, user_id, self.session_ttl)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?;\n\n        Ok(Session {\n            token,\n            expires_at: now + self.session_ttl,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Validate a token and return the user id embedded in it.\n    pub async fn validate_token(&self, token: &str) -> Result<Uuid> {\n        // 1. Verify signature / expiration\n        let TokenData { claims, .. } = decode::<Claims>(\n            token,\n            &DecodingKey::from_secret(self.jwt_secret.as_bytes()),\n            &Validation::default(),\n        )?;\n\n        // 2. Check server-side revocation list (Redis)\n        if self\n            .session_store\n            .get_user_id(token)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?\n            .is_none()\n        {\n            return Err(AuthError::InvalidCredentials);\n        }\n\n        let uuid = Uuid::parse_str(&claims.sub).map_err(|_| AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n        Ok(uuid)\n    }\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// REST handler glue (simplified for brevity). In a real project, this would live in actix-web or\n/// axum route modules. Here we show just enough to demonstrate SVC integration.\n/// =============================================================================================\n#[cfg(feature = \"rest_api\")]\npub mod rest {\n    use super::*;\n    use axum::{\n        extract::{Extension, Json},\n        http::StatusCode,\n        response::IntoResponse,\n        routing::post,\n        Router,\n    };\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n\n    #[derive(Clone)]\n    pub struct AppState<A> {\n        pub auth_service: Arc<A>,\n    }\n\n    pub fn router<A: 'static + Send + Sync>(\n        state: AppState<A>,\n    ) -> Router\n    where\n        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n    {\n        Router::new()\n            .route(\"/api/v1/auth/register\", post(register::<A>))\n            .route(\"/api/v1/auth/login\", post(login::<A>))\n            .layer(Extension(state))\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    pub trait AuthServiceTrait: Send + Sync {\n        async fn register(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session>;\n        async fn login(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session>;\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl<R, B> AuthServiceTrait for super::AuthService<R, B>\n    where\n        R: UserRepository + 'static,\n        B: EventBus + 'static,\n    {\n        async fn register(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session> {\n            self.register_user(new_user).await\n        }\n\n        async fn login(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session> {\n            self.login_user(email, password).await\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// ----------------------------------------\n    /// Route handlers\n    /// ----------------------------------------\n    async fn register<A>(\n        Json(payload): Json<NewUser>,\n        Extension(state): Extension<AppState<A>>,\n    ) -> impl IntoResponse\n    where\n        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n    {\n        match state.auth_service.register(payload).await {\n            Ok(session) => (StatusCode::CREATED, Json(session)).into_response(),\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"registration error: {e}\");\n                (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, Json(e.to_string())).into_response()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[derive(Deserialize)]\n    struct LoginPayload {\n        email: String,\n        password: String,\n    }\n\n    async fn login<A>(\n        Json(payload): Json<LoginPayload>,\n        Extension(state): Extension<AppState<A>>,\n    ) -> impl IntoResponse\n    where\n        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n    {\n        match state\n            .auth_service\n            .login(&payload.email, &payload.password)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(session) => (StatusCode::OK, Json(session)).into_response(),\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"login error: {e}\");\n                (StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, Json(e.to_string())).into_response()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// =============================================================================================\n/// Convenience: Build everything from environment variables\n/// =============================================================================================\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub async fn build_auth_service() -> anyhow::Result<\n    AuthService<PostgresUserRepository, TracingEventBus>,\n> {\n    let database_url =\n        env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\").expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set (postgres connection string)\");\n    let redis_url = env::var(\"REDIS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"redis://127.0.0.1/\".into());\n    let jwt_secret =\n        env::var(\"JWT_SECRET\").expect(\"JWT_SECRET must be set (base64 encoded 256-bit random key)\");\n\n    let pool = PgPool::connect(&database_url).await?;\n\n    let repo = PostgresUserRepository::new(pool);\n    let bus = TracingEventBus;\n    let session_store = SessionStore::new(&redis_url)?;\n\n    Ok(AuthService::new(repo, bus, session_store, jwt_secret))\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_1.txt",
    "numLines": 512,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 512
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Callers can also pass a pre-initialised `PgPool`\n/app/project/src/module_22.txt:103:/// caller code can use `?` without additional noise.\n/app/project/src/module_22.txt:297:        // Fire-and-forget the event; let caller decide whether to await or spawn.\n/app/project/src/module_83.txt:275:        // after spawn), so the caller knows if publication failed.\n/app/project/src/module_28.txt:145:    /// caller to manually stop a subscriber at runtime.\n/app/project/src/module_33.txt:258:    /// **not** rolled back (best-effort), but the caller is informed through\n/app/project/src/module_3.txt:122:/// caller to swap implementations (e.g. for tests or for sharding).\n/app/project/src/module_65.txt:342:/// Keeps the public API clean while avoiding `Any` imports in caller code.\n/app/project/src/module_85.txt:267:///  \u2022 Broker failure    => event *already* persisted, caller should retry\n/app/project/src/module_85.txt:270:/// For brevity we just propagate the error back to the caller.\n/app/project/src/config.txt:17://! driven_; callers only need to care about the resulting strongly\n/app/project/src/module_15.txt:353:    /// so callers can `.await` graceful shutdown.\n/app/project/src/module_11.txt:101:/// Must be invoked within the caller's SQL transaction.\n/app/project/src/module_11.txt:174:    /// Caller decides the sleep interval between attempts.\n/app/project/src/module_44.txt:246:        // Fire-and-forget caching (we still propagate error to caller).\n/app/project/src/module_69.txt:59:/// The concrete payload is stored in a `serde_json::Value`, therefore callers\n/app/project/src/module_69.txt:87:    pub principal_id: Option<uuid::Uuid>,\n/app/project/src/module_69.txt:319:    // Store sender on dispatcher so caller can gracefully stop it (simplified)\n/app/project/src/module_36.txt:59:/// Helper DTO used by service callers (presentation layer) to submit a reply.\n/app/project/src/module_9.txt:255:    /// This method is *synchronous* with respect to the caller\u2019s DB\n/app/project/src/module_9.txt:329:                // Log and backoff; caller decides global error handling.",
