39. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/docs/api.md"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```markdown\n# EduPulse Live \u2013 Core HTTP API\nThis document serves as a *living* reference for the Rust implementation of EduPulse Live\u2019s\npublic HTTP API.  \nAll examples are fully\u2013functional and can be compiled **as-is** by pasting the contents of the\nmain code block into `src/main.rs` of a fresh `cargo new --bin edupulse_core` project.\n\n> \u2139\ufe0f **Edition**: 2021  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Async runtime**: `tokio`  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Web framework**: `actix-web`  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Database**: PostgreSQL (`sqlx`)  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Message broker**: Redis Streams  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Search**: OpenSearch / Elasticsearch via `reqwest`  \n> \u2139\ufe0f **Auth**: JWT (RFC 7519) + rotating Redis session store  \n\n---\n\n## 1. End-to-End Example\nThe following single file demonstrates a _production-grade_, slice-through implementation\nshowing how EduPulse Live stitches together:\n\n* Service-layer + Repository pattern\n* Domain event publishing\n* JWT-based authentication middleware\n* Redis-backed session management\n* Search indexing (fire-and-forget)\n* TLS configuration\n\n```rust\n//! src/main.rs\n//! Run with: cargo run --features \"native-tls\"\n//!\n//! ATTENTION:  For brevity, error handling is simplified to `anyhow::Error`.\n//!             In production, design rich error types (crate `thiserror`).\n\nuse std::{net::TcpListener, sync::Arc, time::Duration};\n\nuse actix_session::{config::PersistentSession, storage::RedisSessionStore, SessionMiddleware};\nuse actix_web::{\n    cookie::Key,\n    dev::Server,\n    get, http::header, middleware::Logger, post,\n    web::{self, Data},\n    App, HttpResponse, HttpServer, Responder,\n};\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{decode, encode, Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation};\nuse redis::{aio::ConnectionManager, AsyncCommands};\nuse reqwest::Client as HttpClient;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool};\nuse tokio::{signal, task};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n// ---------- Domain Model ----------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone)]\npub struct LearningPulse {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub description: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n// ---------- DTOs ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CreatePulseRequest {\n    pub title: String,\n    pub description: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct PulseResponse {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub description: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n// ---------- Authentication --------------------------------------------------\n\nconst JWT_EXPIRY_SECS: u64 = 60 * 60 * 2; // 2 hours\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct Claims {\n    sub: String, // user id\n    exp: usize,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct JwtKeys {\n    enc: EncodingKey,\n    dec: DecodingKey,\n}\n\nimpl JwtKeys {\n    pub fn new(secret: &[u8]) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enc: EncodingKey::from_secret(secret),\n            dec: DecodingKey::from_secret(secret),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub fn generate_jwt(user_id: Uuid, keys: &JwtKeys) -> Result<String> {\n    let claims = Claims {\n        sub: user_id.to_string(),\n        exp: (Utc::now().timestamp() as u64 + JWT_EXPIRY_SECS) as usize,\n    };\n    encode(&Header::default(), &claims, &keys.enc).context(\"sign jwt\")\n}\n\npub fn validate_jwt(token: &str, keys: &JwtKeys) -> Result<Uuid> {\n    let data = decode::<Claims>(\n        token,\n        &keys.dec,\n        &Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n    )\n    .context(\"decode jwt\")?;\n    Ok(Uuid::parse_str(&data.claims.sub)?)\n}\n\n// ---------- Domain Events ---------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum DomainEvent {\n    PulseCreated { pulse_id: Uuid, teacher_id: Uuid },\n    // \u2026 more events (QuizSubmitted, BadgeGranted, etc.)\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) -> Result<()>;\n}\n\npub struct RedisEventPublisher {\n    conn: ConnectionManager,\n}\n\nimpl RedisEventPublisher {\n    pub async fn new(redis_uri: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let client = redis::Client::open(redis_uri)?;\n        let conn = ConnectionManager::new(client).await?;\n        Ok(Self { conn })\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for RedisEventPublisher {\n    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n        let payload = serde_json::to_string(&event)?;\n        let _: () = self\n            .conn\n            .clone()\n            .xadd(\"edupulse_events\", \"*\", &[(\"event\", &payload)])\n            .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------- Repository Layer ------------------------------------------------\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait PulseRepository: Send + Sync {\n    async fn create(&self, pulse: &LearningPulse) -> Result<()>;\n    async fn list_by_teacher(&self, teacher: Uuid) -> Result<Vec<LearningPulse>>;\n}\n\npub struct PgPulseRepository {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PgPulseRepository {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl PulseRepository for PgPulseRepository {\n    async fn create(&self, pulse: &LearningPulse) -> Result<()> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO learning_pulses (id, teacher_id, title, description, created_at)\n            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n            \"#,\n            pulse.id,\n            pulse.teacher_id,\n            pulse.title,\n            pulse.description,\n            pulse.created_at\n        )\n        .execute(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn list_by_teacher(&self, teacher: Uuid) -> Result<Vec<LearningPulse>> {\n        let records = sqlx::query_as!(\n            LearningPulse,\n            r#\"\n            SELECT id, teacher_id, title, description, created_at\n            FROM learning_pulses\n            WHERE teacher_id = $1\n            ORDER BY created_at DESC\n            \"#,\n            teacher\n        )\n        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(records)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------- Service Layer ---------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct PulseService {\n    repo: Arc<dyn PulseRepository>,\n    events: Arc<dyn EventPublisher>,\n    search: HttpClient,\n}\n\nimpl PulseService {\n    pub fn new(\n        repo: Arc<dyn PulseRepository>,\n        events: Arc<dyn EventPublisher>,\n        search: HttpClient,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            repo,\n            events,\n            search,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub async fn create_pulse(\n        &self,\n        teacher_id: Uuid,\n        req: CreatePulseRequest,\n    ) -> Result<LearningPulse> {\n        let pulse = LearningPulse {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            teacher_id,\n            title: req.title,\n            description: req.description,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n        };\n\n        self.repo.create(&pulse).await?;\n\n        // Fire-and-forget search indexing\n        let search_clone = self.search.clone();\n        let pulse_clone = pulse.clone();\n        task::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = search_clone\n                .post(\"http://localhost:9200/edupulse_pulses/_doc\")\n                .json(&pulse_clone)\n                .send()\n                .await;\n        });\n\n        // Publish domain event\n        self.events\n            .publish(DomainEvent::PulseCreated {\n                pulse_id: pulse.id,\n                teacher_id: pulse.teacher_id,\n            })\n            .await?;\n\n        Ok(pulse)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------- HTTP Handlers ---------------------------------------------------\n\n#[post(\"/v1/pulses\")]\nasync fn create_pulse(\n    service: Data<PulseService>,\n    jwt_keys: Data<JwtKeys>,\n    session: actix_session::Session,\n    req: web::Json<CreatePulseRequest>,\n    auth_header: Option<header::HeaderValue>,\n) -> Result<impl Responder, actix_web::Error> {\n    let token = auth_header\n        .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|h| h.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \"))\n        .ok_or_else(|| actix_web::error::ErrorUnauthorized(\"Missing token\"))?;\n\n    let user_id = validate_jwt(token, &jwt_keys)\n        .map_err(|_| actix_web::error::ErrorUnauthorized(\"Invalid token\"))?;\n\n    // Persist refreshed session (sliding expiration)\n    session.renew();\n\n    let pulse = service\n        .create_pulse(user_id, req.into_inner())\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            log::error!(\"create_pulse: {:?}\", e);\n            actix_web::error::ErrorInternalServerError(\"failed to create pulse\")\n        })?;\n\n    Ok(HttpResponse::Created().json(PulseResponse {\n        id: pulse.id,\n        title: pulse.title,\n        description: pulse.description,\n        created_at: pulse.created_at,\n    }))\n}\n\n#[get(\"/v1/pulses\")]\nasync fn list_pulses(\n    service: Data<PulseService>,\n    jwt_keys: Data<JwtKeys>,\n    auth_header: Option<header::HeaderValue>,\n) -> Result<impl Responder, actix_web::Error> {\n    let token = auth_header\n        .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|h| h.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \"))\n        .ok_or_else(|| actix_web::error::ErrorUnauthorized(\"Missing token\"))?;\n\n    let user_id = validate_jwt(token, &jwt_keys)\n        .map_err(|_| actix_web::error::ErrorUnauthorized(\"Invalid token\"))?;\n\n    let pulses = service\n        .repo\n        .list_by_teacher(user_id)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            log::error!(\"list_pulses: {:?}\", e);\n            actix_web::error::ErrorInternalServerError(\"failed to list pulses\")\n        })?;\n\n    Ok(HttpResponse::Ok().json(\n        pulses\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|p| PulseResponse {\n                id: p.id,\n                title: p.title,\n                description: p.description,\n                created_at: p.created_at,\n            })\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>(),\n    ))\n}\n\n// ---------- Bootstrap (SSL + Middleware) ------------------------------------\n\npub async fn build_server(\n    db_url: &str,\n    redis_url: &str,\n    bind_addr: &str,\n    tls_key: &str,\n    tls_cert: &str,\n) -> Result<Server> {\n    // Logging\n    env_logger::init_from_env(env_logger::Env::default().default_filter_or(\"info\"));\n\n    // Postgres pool\n    let db_pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n        .max_connections(10)\n        .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n        .connect(db_url)\n        .await?;\n\n    // Repositories & event publisher\n    let repo = Arc::new(PgPulseRepository::new(db_pool)) as Arc<dyn PulseRepository>;\n    let events = Arc::new(RedisEventPublisher::new(redis_url).await?) as Arc<dyn EventPublisher>;\n\n    // Search\n    let search_http = HttpClient::builder()\n        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n        .build()?;\n\n    // Service\n    let service = PulseService::new(repo, events, search_http);\n\n    // JWT keys\n    let secret = std::env::var(\"EP_JWT_SECRET\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"super-secret\".repeat(3));\n    let jwt_keys = JwtKeys::new(secret.as_bytes());\n\n    // Session key (32 bytes)\n    let session_key = Key::from(secret.as_bytes());\n\n    // Redis session store\n    let session_store = RedisSessionStore::new(redis_url).await?;\n\n    let listener = TcpListener::bind(bind_addr)?;\n    let server = HttpServer::new(move || {\n        App::new()\n            .app_data(Data::new(service.clone()))\n            .app_data(Data::new(jwt_keys.clone()))\n            .wrap(Logger::default())\n            .wrap(\n                SessionMiddleware::builder(session_store.clone(), session_key.clone())\n                    .session_lifecycle(PersistentSession::default().session_ttl(\n                        Duration::from_secs(JWT_EXPIRY_SECS),\n                    ))\n                    .build(),\n            )\n            .service(create_pulse)\n            .service(list_pulses)\n    })\n    .listen_openssl(\n        listener,\n        openssl::ssl::SslAcceptor::mozilla_modern(openssl::ssl::SslMethod::tls())?\n            .set_private_key_file(tls_key, openssl::ssl::SslFiletype::PEM)?\n            .set_certificate_chain_file(tls_cert)?,\n    )?\n    .run();\n\n    Ok(server)\n}\n\n// ---------- Main ------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> Result<()> {\n    let db_url = std::env::var(\"EP_DATABASE_URL\").expect(\"PG URL\");\n    let redis_url = std::env::var(\"EP_REDIS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"redis://127.0.0.1/\".into());\n    let bind_addr = std::env::var(\"EP_BIND_ADDR\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"0.0.0.0:8443\".into());\n\n    let tls_key = std::env::var(\"EP_TLS_KEY\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"certs/key.pem\".into());\n    let tls_cert = std::env::var(\"EP_TLS_CERT\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"certs/cert.pem\".into());\n\n    let server = build_server(&db_url, &redis_url, &bind_addr, &tls_key, &tls_cert).await?;\n\n    // Graceful shutdown\n    tokio::select! {\n        res = server => res?,\n        _ = signal::ctrl_c() => {\n            println!(\"Shutdown signal received\");\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\nAdd the following dependencies to `Cargo.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[dependencies]\nactix-web          = { version = \"4.5\", features = [\"tls\", \"openssl\"] }\nactix-session      = \"0.9\"\nanyhow             = \"1.0\"\nasync-trait        = \"0.1\"\nchrono             = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\nenv_logger         = \"0.11\"\njsonwebtoken       = \"9\"\nlog                = \"0.4\"\nopenssl            = { version = \"0.10\", features = [\"vendored\"] }\nredis              = { version = \"0.23\", features = [\"tokio-comp\"] }\nreqwest            = { version = \"0.11\", features = [\"json\", \"rustls-tls\"], default-features = false }\nserde              = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"derive\"] }\nsqlx               = { version = \"0.7\", features = [\"postgres\", \"runtime-tokio\", \"chrono\", \"uuid\", \"tls-native-tls\"] }\ntokio              = { version = \"1.37\", features = [\"macros\", \"rt-multi-thread\", \"signal\"] }\nuuid               = { version = \"1.8\", features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\n```\n\n> **Migration**  \n> Ensure the `learning_pulses` table exists:\n> ```sql\n> CREATE TABLE learning_pulses (\n>   id          UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n>   teacher_id  UUID NOT NULL,\n>   title       TEXT NOT NULL,\n>   description TEXT NOT NULL,\n>   created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL\n> );\n> ```\n\n---\n\n## 2. API Quick Reference\n\n| Method | Path          | Description                         | Auth | Notes               |\n|-------:|--------------:|-------------------------------------|------|---------------------|\n| POST   | `/v1/pulses`  | Create a new Learning Pulse         | JWT  | Body: `title`, `description` |\n| GET    | `/v1/pulses`  | List Learning Pulses by current user| JWT  | \u2014                   |\n\nRequest bodies are `application/json`; responses conform to the structures\nillustrated in the example above.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Event Contract (`DomainEvent`)\n\n```jsonc\n// Redis Stream entry\n{\n  \"event\": \"{                                      // JSON string\n    \\\"PulseCreated\\\": {\n      \\\"pulse_id\\\": \\\"bbd1-4a51-9d54-\u2026\\\",\n      \\\"teacher_id\\\": \\\"9f3d-a7c2-\u2026\\\"\n    }\n  }\"\n}\n```\n\n**Down-stream services** (search indexer, mailer, analytics, etc.) subscribe\nto the `edupulse_events` stream and handle their respective responsibilities,\nkeeping the HTTP API layer thin and responsive.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Security Checklist\n\u2714 100 % HTTPS with modern TLS (OpenSSL \u201cMozilla modern\u201d profile)  \n\u2714 Short-lived JWTs; sessions stored in Redis and automatically rotated  \n\u2714 Column-level encryption is recommended for PII (out-of-scope here)  \n\u2714 All secrets supplied via environment variables (12-factor compliant)  \n\n---\n\nHappy hacking! \ud83c\udf93\u2728\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/docs/api.md",
    "numLines": 520,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 520
  },
  "type": "text"
}

43. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_52.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_52.rs\n//!\n//! Event-bus abstraction and RabbitMQ publisher for the EduPulse Live\n//! platform.  All domain services depend on this module to asynchronously\n//! propagate events such as `LearningPulseSubmitted`, `QuizGraded`, or\n//! `BadgeAwarded` to downstream consumers (search indexing, notifications,\n//! analytics, \u2026).  The implementation adheres to the Repository pattern and\n//! hides the AMQP details behind a clean service interface.\n//!\n//! # Highlights\n//! \u2022 Async/await end-to-end (Tokio runtime).  \n//! \u2022 Connection pooling and automatic reconnection.  \n//! \u2022 Structured logging with `tracing`.  \n//! \u2022 Comprehensive error handling with `anyhow`.  \n//! \u2022 Zero-copy JSON serialization via `serde`.  \n//! \u2022 Feature-gated so the core API stays lightweight if the message bus is\n//!   disabled (`cargo build --no-default-features`).\n\n#![forbid(unsafe_code)]\n#![warn(\n    clippy::all,\n    clippy::pedantic,\n    clippy::cargo,\n    missing_docs,\n    rust_2018_idioms\n)]\n\nuse std::{env, time::Duration};\n\nuse anyhow::{anyhow, Context, Result};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse lapin::{\n    options::{BasicPublishOptions, ExchangeDeclareOptions},\n    publisher_confirm::Confirmation,\n    Connection, ConnectionProperties, ExchangeKind,\n};\nuse once_cell::sync::OnceCell;\nuse serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tokio::{\n    select,\n    sync::{mpsc, Mutex},\n    task::JoinHandle,\n};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Maximum number of events that can queue up before back-pressure is applied.\nconst CHANNEL_BUFFER_SIZE: usize = 10_000;\n/// Re-connection back-off in case of an AMQP outage.\nconst RECONNECT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_secs(3);\n\n/// Describes every event emitted inside the bounded context.\n///\n/// Implementors must be serialisable to JSON so they can be transmitted over\n/// the wire.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait DomainEvent: Send + Sync + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + 'static {\n    /// Logical name of the event (e.g. `\"LearningPulseSubmitted\"`).\n    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str;\n\n    /// Convert a concrete event into an envelope ready for transport.\n    fn into_envelope(self) -> Envelope\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Envelope::from_event(self)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Standard metadata wrapper for domain events.\n///\n/// The wrapper ensures every message is self-contained, traceable, and can be\n/// routed by any consumer regardless of the concrete payload.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Envelope {\n    /// UUIDv4 \u2013 event identifier.\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    /// ISO-8601 UTC time stamp.\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Event _kind_ (routing key), e.g. `\"LearningPulseSubmitted\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// Opaque JSON payload.\n    pub data: Value,\n}\n\nimpl Envelope {\n    /// Create an envelope from a strongly-typed event.\n    pub fn from_event<E>(event: E) -> Self\n    where\n        E: DomainEvent,\n    {\n        let data = serde_json::to_value(&event).expect(\"DomainEvent must be serializable\");\n        Self {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n            event_type: event.event_type().to_string(),\n            data,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Abstract publisher for domain events.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n    /// Publish a single [`Envelope`].\n    async fn publish(&self, envelope: Envelope) -> Result<()>;\n\n    /// Convenience helper for publishing a concrete domain event.\n    async fn publish_event<E>(&self, event: E) -> Result<()>\n    where\n        E: DomainEvent,\n    {\n        self.publish(event.into_envelope()).await\n    }\n}\n\n/// AMQP configuration loaded from environment variables.\n///\n/// All fields are optional so sensible defaults kick in automatically in\n/// development environments.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct AmqpConfig {\n    /// AMQP connection URI (e.g. `amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672/%2f`).\n    pub uri: Option<String>,\n    /// Exchange name to publish events to.\n    pub exchange: Option<String>,\n    /// Timeout for individual publish operations.\n    #[serde(default = \"AmqpConfig::default_publish_timeout_secs\")]\n    pub publish_timeout_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl AmqpConfig {\n    fn default_publish_timeout_secs() -> u64 {\n        5\n    }\n\n    /// Load configuration from the process environment (fails fast in CI).\n    pub fn from_env() -> Result<Self> {\n        let cfg = envy::prefixed(\"EDUPULSE_AMQP_\").from_env::<AmqpConfig>()?;\n        Ok(cfg)\n    }\n\n    /// Provide nice fallbacks.\n    fn uri(&self) -> &str {\n        self.uri\n            .as_deref()\n            .unwrap_or(\"amqp://guest:guest@127.0.0.1:5672/%2f\")\n    }\n\n    fn exchange(&self) -> &str {\n        self.exchange.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"edupulse.events\")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Concrete EventBus implementation based on RabbitMQ / AMQP 0-9-1.\n///\n/// The bus owns a worker thread that consumes events from an mpsc channel and\n/// handles the (re-)connection logic transparently.\npub struct RabbitMqBus {\n    /// Sender side of the channel; cloned for each producer.\n    tx: mpsc::Sender<Envelope>,\n    /// JoinHandle for the background task (so we can await graceful shutdown).\n    _bg_handle: JoinHandle<()>,\n}\n\nimpl RabbitMqBus {\n    /// Lazily initialised, globally shared instance.\n    pub fn global() -> Result<Arc<Self>> {\n        static INSTANCE: OnceCell<Arc<RabbitMqBus>> = OnceCell::new();\n        INSTANCE\n            .get_or_try_init(|| {\n                let cfg = AmqpConfig::from_env()\n                    .context(\"Failed to read EDUPULSE_AMQP_* environment variables\")?;\n                Self::new(cfg)\n            })\n            .cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new connection pool and spawn the background dispatcher loop.\n    pub fn new(cfg: AmqpConfig) -> Result<Arc<Self>> {\n        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(CHANNEL_BUFFER_SIZE);\n        let cfg_clone = cfg.clone();\n\n        let bg_handle = tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = Self::dispatcher_loop(cfg_clone, rx).await {\n                error!(\"RabbitMqBus shut down unexpectedly: {e:#}\");\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(Arc::new(Self { tx, _bg_handle: bg_handle }))\n    }\n\n    /// Main worker loop: connect / reconnect, drain the buffer, publish events.\n    async fn dispatcher_loop(cfg: AmqpConfig, mut rx: mpsc::Receiver<Envelope>) -> Result<()> {\n        loop {\n            match Self::connect(&cfg).await {\n                Ok((conn, channel)) => {\n                    info!(\"RabbitMQ connected ({}).\", cfg.uri());\n\n                    // Drain queue until connection drops or channel is closed.\n                    while let Some(envelope) = rx.recv().await {\n                        // Serialize once per message.\n                        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(&envelope)?;\n                        let confirm = channel\n                            .basic_publish(\n                                cfg.exchange(),\n                                &envelope.event_type,\n                                BasicPublishOptions::default(),\n                                payload,\n                                lapin::BasicProperties::default(),\n                            )\n                            .await?\n                            .await; // Await publish confirmation.\n\n                        match confirm {\n                            Ok(Confirmation::NotRequested) | Ok(Confirmation::Ack(_)) => {\n                                debug!(\"Event published: {}\", envelope.id);\n                            }\n                            Ok(Confirmation::Nack(_)) | Err(_) => {\n                                error!(\"Message nack'd by broker, requeueing...\");\n                                // Push back into channel for retry.\n                                rx.send(envelope).await.ok();\n                                break; // Break to reconnect.\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(err) => {\n                    error!(\"RabbitMQ connection error: {err:?}\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            info!(\n                \"RabbitMQ retrying in {}s \u2026\",\n                RECONNECT_BACKOFF.as_secs()\n            );\n            tokio::time::sleep(RECONNECT_BACKOFF).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Establish a fresh AMQP connection and declare the required exchange.\n    async fn connect(\n        cfg: &AmqpConfig,\n    ) -> Result<(Connection, lapin::Channel)> {\n        let conn = Connection::connect(cfg.uri(), ConnectionProperties::default()).await?;\n        let channel = conn.create_channel().await?;\n\n        channel\n            .exchange_declare(\n                cfg.exchange(),\n                ExchangeKind::Topic,\n                ExchangeDeclareOptions {\n                    durable: true,\n                    auto_delete: false,\n                    ..ExchangeDeclareOptions::default()\n                },\n                Default::default(),\n            )\n            .await?;\n\n        Ok((conn, channel))\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventBus for RabbitMqBus {\n    #[instrument(skip_all, err)]\n    async fn publish(&self, envelope: Envelope) -> Result<()> {\n        self.tx\n            .send(envelope)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| anyhow!(\"EventBus channel closed: {e}\"))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Example domain event for demonstration purposes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LearningPulseSubmitted {\n    /// Unique identifier of the pulse.\n    pub pulse_id: Uuid,\n    /// Authoring student.\n    pub author_id: Uuid,\n    /// Course context for routing.\n    pub course_id: Uuid,\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl DomainEvent for LearningPulseSubmitted {\n    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n        \"LearningPulseSubmitted\"\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convenience function used by controller / service layers.\n///\n/// Publishes an event via the global event bus.  \n/// The indirection avoids tight coupling between application code and the\n/// specific message broker.\n#[instrument(level = \"info\", skip(event))]\npub async fn emit<E>(event: E) -> Result<()>\nwhere\n    E: DomainEvent,\n{\n    RabbitMqBus::global()?\n        .publish_event(event)\n        .await\n}\n\n/// Local test that spins up an in-memory broker (assumes `docker compose up`).\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use std::time::Duration;\n\n    /// Integration test \u2013 should succeed if a RabbitMQ instance is reachable.\n    #[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\", worker_threads = 2)]\n    async fn smoke_publish_event() -> Result<()> {\n        // Skip on CI if no broker is available.\n        if env::var(\"CI\").is_ok() {\n            eprintln!(\"Skipping RabbitMQ test on CI\");\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        tracing_subscriber::fmt::try_init().ok();\n\n        let event = LearningPulseSubmitted {\n            pulse_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            author_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            course_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n        };\n\n        emit(event).await?;\n\n        // Allow the background task to flush.\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_52.txt",
    "numLines": 342,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 342
  },
  "type": "text"
}

47. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/tests/test_main.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! Integration\u2010style tests for the \u201cEduPulse Live\u201d social learning hub.\n//!\n//! The real application is a large, event-driven distributed system.  In these\n//! tests we spin up **in-memory stand-ins** for some of the platform\u2019s core\n//! services (event bus, authentication/session management, analytics) and make\n//! sure that the public behaviour\u2014the contract\u2014matches expectations.\n//!\n//! The code in this file is fully self-contained so that it can compile and run\n//! without the rest of the production crate being present.  In the real\n//! code-base each component would live in its own module/crate and expose a\n//! clear interface.\n\n// Std-lib imports ------------------------------------------------------------\nuse std::{\n    collections::{HashMap, HashSet},\n    fmt,\n    sync::{\n        atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering},\n        mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender},\n        Arc, Mutex,\n    },\n    thread,\n    time::{Duration, SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH},\n};\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Domain events\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// All significant state changes inside EduPulse Live emit a DomainEvent.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nenum DomainEvent {\n    LessonPublished {\n        lesson_id: u64,\n        title: String,\n    },\n    PulseReplied {\n        user_id: u64,\n        pulse_id: u64,\n        reply: String,\n    },\n    UserLoggedIn {\n        user_id: u64,\n        session_token: String,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Basic error type for the EventBus.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum BusError {\n    PublishFailed,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for BusError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            BusError::PublishFailed => write!(f, \"unable to publish event\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// In-memory EventBus (fan-out)\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// Very small, in-process, fan-out event bus used exclusively for testing.\n///\n/// Every call to `subscribe()` registers a new channel (Sender) that will\n/// receive a **copy** of every published DomainEvent.  Publication errors are\n/// silently swallowed by individual subscribers so that one slow consumer\n/// never blocks the rest.\n#[derive(Clone, Default)]\nstruct EventBus {\n    subscribers: Arc<Mutex<Vec<Sender<DomainEvent>>>>,\n}\n\nimpl EventBus {\n    /// Create a fresh, empty bus.\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Publish an event to all currently active subscribers.\n    fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) -> Result<(), BusError> {\n        let subs = self\n            .subscribers\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"poisoned subscriber list in EventBus\");\n\n        for tx in subs.iter() {\n            // Ignore per-subscriber errors; fire-and-forget semantics.\n            let _ = tx.send(event.clone());\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new subscriber and get the receiving end of its channel.\n    fn subscribe(&self) -> Receiver<DomainEvent> {\n        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();\n\n        self.subscribers\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"poisoned subscriber list in EventBus\")\n            .push(tx);\n\n        rx\n    }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Authentication & session handling\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// Errors produced by `AuthService`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum AuthError {\n    UnknownUser,\n    TokenCollision,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for AuthError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            AuthError::UnknownUser => write!(f, \"user not found\"),\n            AuthError::TokenCollision => write!(f, \"session-token collision\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Thread-safe, in-memory authentication service used in tests.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct AuthService {\n    // In production this would be a database call via a repository.\n    registered_users: HashSet<u64>,\n\n    // session_token -> user_id\n    sessions: Mutex<HashMap<String, u64>>,\n}\n\nimpl AuthService {\n    fn new(registered_users: &[u64]) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            registered_users: registered_users.iter().copied().collect(),\n            sessions: Mutex::new(HashMap::new()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Log the given user in and produce a fresh session token.\n    ///\n    /// A DomainEvent is emitted on success.\n    fn login(\n        &self,\n        user_id: u64,\n        bus: &EventBus,\n    ) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        if !self.registered_users.contains(&user_id) {\n            return Err(AuthError::UnknownUser);\n        }\n\n        let token = Self::generate_token(user_id);\n\n        let mut guard = self.sessions.lock().expect(\"poisoned session map\");\n        if guard.contains_key(&token) {\n            // Unlikely, but should never overwrite another session.\n            return Err(AuthError::TokenCollision);\n        }\n        guard.insert(token.clone(), user_id);\n        drop(guard); // Explicitly drop lock before we publish an event.\n\n        bus.publish(DomainEvent::UserLoggedIn {\n            user_id,\n            session_token: token.clone(),\n        })\n        .expect(\"event bus must live for the entire test run\");\n\n        Ok(token)\n    }\n\n    /// Very naive, deterministic \u201crandom\u201d token generator for test purposes.\n    fn generate_token(user_id: u64) -> String {\n        let epoch_millis = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .expect(\"system time before 1970?\")\n            .as_millis();\n\n        format!(\"{user_id:x}-{epoch_millis:x}\")\n    }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Analytics service (consumer)\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// Simple analytics worker.  In production this would run in its own process\n/// and push data into a columnar store; in tests we only count events.\nstruct AnalyticsService {\n    processed_pulse_replies: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n}\n\nimpl AnalyticsService {\n    fn new() -> (Self, Arc<AtomicBool>) {\n        let flag = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n        let svc = Self {\n            processed_pulse_replies: flag.clone(),\n        };\n        (svc, flag)\n    }\n\n    /// Start an **owned** worker thread that listens for events until the\n    /// supplied `shutdown_rx` channel is closed.\n    fn start(self, bus_rx: Receiver<DomainEvent>, shutdown_rx: Receiver<()>) {\n        thread::spawn(move || {\n            loop {\n                // prefer shutdown over event\u2014non-blocking check\n                if let Ok(_) = shutdown_rx.try_recv() {\n                    break;\n                }\n                match bus_rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100)) {\n                    Ok(event) => self.handle_event(event),\n                    Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => continue,\n                    Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => break,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    fn handle_event(&self, ev: DomainEvent) {\n        match ev {\n            DomainEvent::PulseReplied { .. } => {\n                self.processed_pulse_replies\n                    .store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n            }\n            _ => {} // ignore all other events\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n// Tests\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/// Make sure that a PulseReplied event is observed by the analytics worker.\n#[test]\nfn pulse_reply_is_forwarded_to_analytics_service() {\n    // Arrange\n    let bus = EventBus::new();\n\n    // Channel to allow clean shutdown when the test finishes.\n    let (shutdown_tx, shutdown_rx) = mpsc::channel();\n\n    // Subscriber and worker bootstrap\n    let bus_rx = bus.subscribe();\n    let (analytics_service, processed_flag) = AnalyticsService::new();\n    analytics_service.start(bus_rx, shutdown_rx);\n\n    // Act\n    bus.publish(DomainEvent::PulseReplied {\n        user_id: 42,\n        pulse_id: 7,\n        reply: \"Here is my work\u2026\".into(),\n    })\n    .expect(\"failed to publish event\");\n\n    // Give the worker up to 2 seconds real time.\n    for _ in 0..20 {\n        if processed_flag.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n            break;\n        }\n        thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));\n    }\n\n    // Clean shutdown\n    let _ = shutdown_tx.send(());\n\n    // Assert\n    assert!(\n        processed_flag.load(Ordering::SeqCst),\n        \"analytics worker never saw PulseReplied event\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Verify that a successful login creates a session and emits the right event.\n#[test]\nfn successful_login_creates_session_and_emits_event() {\n    // Arrange\n    let bus = EventBus::new();\n    let rx = bus.subscribe();\n\n    let auth = AuthService::new(&[1, 2, 3]); // user 1 is registered\n\n    // Act\n    let token = auth\n        .login(1, &bus)\n        .expect(\"login should succeed for registered user\");\n\n    // Assert token shape (very coarse sanity check)\n    assert!(\n        token.starts_with(\"1-\"),\n        \"unexpected token format: {token:?}\"\n    );\n\n    // The next event on the bus must be our login.\n    let event =\n        rx.recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(1)).expect(\"no event received\");\n\n    match event {\n        DomainEvent::UserLoggedIn {\n            user_id,\n            session_token,\n        } => {\n            assert_eq!(user_id, 1);\n            assert_eq!(session_token, token);\n        }\n        other => panic!(\"unexpected event: {other:?}\"),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Attempting to log in with an unknown user should be rejected.\n#[test]\nfn unknown_user_cannot_log_in() {\n    let bus = EventBus::new();\n    let auth = AuthService::new(&[99]); // only user 99 exists\n\n    let res = auth.login(1234, &bus);\n\n    assert!(\n        matches!(res, Err(AuthError::UnknownUser)),\n        \"expected UnknownUser, got {res:?}\"\n    );\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/test_main.txt",
    "numLines": 332,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 332
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! tests/test_utils.rs\n//! -------------------------------------------------------------\n//! Integration-style tests for the `web_social` utility helpers.\n//!\n//! NOTE: In a real repository these helpers would live in\n//! `web_social::utils` and the following import would be used:\n//\n//!     use web_social::utils::*;\n//\n//! For the purpose of making this file self-contained and\n//! compilable in isolation, a greatly simplified copy of the\n//! utilities is embedded directly below under `mod utils`.\n//!\n//! The tests exercise the most important behaviour around:\n//!   \u2022 Secure token generation\n//!   \u2022 Slugification of user-facing titles\n//!   \u2022 Lightweight JSON event-schema validation\n//!\n//! The test-cases intentionally cover happy-paths, edge-cases, and\n//! concurrency scenarios to simulate real-world production load.\n//! -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Dummy implementation (stand-in for the real library code)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\nmod utils {\n    use std::collections::HashSet;\n    use std::str::FromStr;\n\n    use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    use thiserror::Error;\n    use uuid::Uuid;\n\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    // Secure Token Generation\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    #[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    pub enum TokenError {\n        #[error(\"requested token length must be > 0\")]\n        InvalidLength,\n    }\n\n    /// Generates a cryptographically secure, URL-safe token with\n    /// the exact length requested. Internally we create a\n    /// `UuidV4` and extend it with additional random bytes if\n    /// necessary.\n    pub fn generate_secure_token(target_len: usize) -> Result<String, TokenError> {\n        if target_len == 0 {\n            return Err(TokenError::InvalidLength);\n        }\n\n        let mut token = base64::encode(Uuid::new_v4().as_bytes())\n            .replace(['+', '/', '='], \"\"); // URL safe\n\n        // Extend until we reach the requested length\n        while token.len() < target_len {\n            token.push_str(\n                &base64::encode(Uuid::new_v4().as_bytes())\n                    .replace(['+', '/', '='], \"\"),\n            );\n        }\n        token.truncate(target_len);\n        Ok(token)\n    }\n\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    // Slugification\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    /// Returns a URL-safe slug for titles, user names, etc.\n    ///\n    /// Rules:\n    ///   \u2022 ASCII-alphanumeric kept as is (lower-cased)\n    ///   \u2022 Unicode letters are transliterated to ASCII if possible\n    ///   \u2022 Any sequence of non-alphanumeric chars becomes a single '-'\n    ///   \u2022 Leading / trailing '-' are trimmed\n    pub fn slugify(input: &str) -> String {\n        let mut slug = String::with_capacity(input.len());\n        let mut prev_dash = false;\n\n        for ch in input.chars() {\n            let lc = ch.to_ascii_lowercase();\n            if lc.is_ascii_alphanumeric() {\n                slug.push(lc);\n                prev_dash = false;\n            } else {\n                if !prev_dash {\n                    slug.push('-');\n                    prev_dash = true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        slug.trim_matches('-').to_string()\n    }\n\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    // Event Schema Validation\n    // ---------------------------------------------------------\n    #[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n    pub struct Event<'a> {\n        pub id: Uuid,\n        #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n        pub event_type: &'a str,\n        pub version: u16,\n        #[serde(with = \"chrono::serde::ts_milliseconds\")]\n        pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    }\n\n    #[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    pub enum EventValidationError {\n        #[error(\"payload is not valid JSON: {0}\")]\n        JsonError(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n        #[error(\"field 'version' must be greater than 0\")]\n        InvalidVersion,\n        #[error(\"'id' field is not a valid UUID\")]\n        InvalidId,\n        #[error(\"event type must be non-empty\")]\n        EmptyEventType,\n        #[error(\"duplicate event id\")]\n        DuplicateId,\n    }\n\n    /// Parses a JSON payload into an `Event` and performs\n    /// additional domain-specific validation such as:\n    ///   \u2022 `version` must be > 0\n    ///   \u2022 `event_type` must not be empty\n    ///   \u2022 UUID must be valid\n    ///   \u2022 Id must be unique within the provided `seen_ids` set\n    pub fn verify_event_schema<'a>(\n        payload: &'a str,\n        seen_ids: &mut HashSet<Uuid>,\n    ) -> Result<Event<'a>, EventValidationError> {\n        let evt: Event<'a> = serde_json::from_str(payload)?;\n        if evt.version == 0 {\n            return Err(EventValidationError::InvalidVersion);\n        }\n        if evt.event_type.trim().is_empty() {\n            return Err(EventValidationError::EmptyEventType);\n        }\n        // UUID validity is already enforced by serde + Uuid but\n        // we add an explicit check for completeness.\n        Uuid::from_str(evt.id.as_hyphenated().to_string().as_str())\n            .map_err(|_| EventValidationError::InvalidId)?;\n\n        if !seen_ids.insert(evt.id) {\n            return Err(EventValidationError::DuplicateId);\n        }\n\n        Ok(evt)\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Actual test-cases\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\n\nuse utils::*;\n\n#[test]\nfn test_secure_token_length_and_uniqueness() {\n    const TOKEN_LEN: usize = 32;\n    const RUNS: usize = 1_000;\n\n    let mut unique = std::collections::HashSet::with_capacity(RUNS);\n    for _ in 0..RUNS {\n        let token = generate_secure_token(TOKEN_LEN).expect(\"token generation failed\");\n        assert_eq!(\n            token.len(),\n            TOKEN_LEN,\n            \"generated token does not match requested length\"\n        );\n        // Make sure token is URL-safe\n        assert!(\n            token.chars()\n                .all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_'),\n            \"token contains non-URL-safe characters\"\n        );\n        unique.insert(token);\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        unique.len(),\n        RUNS,\n        \"expected all generated tokens to be unique\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_slugify_various_inputs() {\n    let cases = [\n        (\"Hello World!\", \"hello-world\"),\n        (\"Rust\u2013\u2013The-Book\", \"rust-the-book\"),\n        (\"___Multiple   separators___\", \"multiple-separators\"),\n        (\"Trailing & Leading *Punctuation*\", \"trailing-leading-punctuation\"),\n        (\"\", \"\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, expected) in cases {\n        assert_eq!(\n            slugify(input),\n            expected,\n            \"slug mismatch for input '{input}'\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_event_schema_valid_payload() {\n    let json = format!(\n        r#\"{{\n            \"id\": \"{uuid}\",\n            \"type\": \"lesson.published\",\n            \"version\": 3,\n            \"created_at\": {timestamp}\n        }}\"#,\n        uuid = uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n        timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis()\n    );\n\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    let evt = verify_event_schema(&json, &mut seen).expect(\"should be valid\");\n    assert_eq!(evt.version, 3);\n    assert_eq!(evt.event_type, \"lesson.published\");\n    assert!(seen.contains(&evt.id), \"event id should be registered as seen\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_event_schema_invalid_cases() {\n    // Missing event_type\n    let json_missing_field = format!(\n        r#\"{{\n            \"id\": \"{uuid}\",\n            \"version\": 1,\n            \"created_at\": {timestamp}\n        }}\"#,\n        uuid = uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n        timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis()\n    );\n\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    let err = verify_event_schema(&json_missing_field, &mut seen).unwrap_err();\n    matches!(err, utils::EventValidationError::JsonError(_));\n\n    // Duplicate ID\n    let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4();\n    let json1 = format!(\n        r#\"{{\n            \"id\": \"{id}\",\n            \"type\": \"quiz.graded\",\n            \"version\": 1,\n            \"created_at\": {ts1}\n        }}\"#,\n        id = id,\n        ts1 = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis()\n    );\n    let json2 = format!(\n        r#\"{{\n            \"id\": \"{id}\",\n            \"type\": \"quiz.graded\",\n            \"version\": 2,\n            \"created_at\": {ts2}\n        }}\"#,\n        id = id,\n        ts2 = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis()\n    );\n\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    assert!(verify_event_schema(&json1, &mut seen).is_ok());\n    let err = verify_event_schema(&json2, &mut seen).unwrap_err();\n    matches!(err, utils::EventValidationError::DuplicateId);\n\n    // Version = 0\n    let json_bad_version = format!(\n        r#\"{{\n            \"id\": \"{uuid}\",\n            \"type\": \"asset.uploaded\",\n            \"version\": 0,\n            \"created_at\": {timestamp}\n        }}\"#,\n        uuid = uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n        timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis()\n    );\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    let err = verify_event_schema(&json_bad_version, &mut seen).unwrap_err();\n    matches!(err, utils::EventValidationError::InvalidVersion);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_concurrent_token_generation() {\n    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n    use std::thread;\n\n    const THREADS: usize = 8;\n    const TOKENS_PER_THREAD: usize = 500;\n    const TOKEN_LEN: usize = 48;\n\n    let uniq = Arc::new(Mutex::new(std::collections::HashSet::new()));\n    let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(THREADS);\n\n    for _ in 0..THREADS {\n        let uniq = Arc::clone(&uniq);\n        handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n            for _ in 0..TOKENS_PER_THREAD {\n                let token = generate_secure_token(TOKEN_LEN).unwrap();\n                let mut guard = uniq.lock().unwrap();\n                assert!(\n                    guard.insert(token),\n                    \"duplicate token generated across threads\"\n                );\n            }\n        }));\n    }\n\n    for h in handles {\n        h.join().expect(\"thread failed\");\n    }\n\n    let guard = uniq.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(\n        guard.len(),\n        THREADS * TOKENS_PER_THREAD,\n        \"unexpected number of unique tokens\"\n    );\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/test_utils.txt",
    "numLines": 328,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 328
  },
  "type": "text"
}

52. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_4.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_4.rs\n//!\n//! Centralised domain-event infrastructure for EduPulse Live.\n//!\n//! This module exposes:\n//!   * DomainEvent & DomainEventKind \u2013 strongly-typed, versioned event\n//!     definitions shared across bounded contexts.\n//!   * EventPublisher \u2013 service-layer abstraction allowing the core API to\n//!     remain ignorant of transport details (NATS, Kafka, etc.).\n//!   * NatsEventPublisher \u2013 production implementation that publishes JSON\n//!     envelopes to a NATS JetStream subject hierarchy.\n//!   * EventStore \u2013 ACID append-only persistence used for audit trail / GDPR\n//!     erase workflow.\n//!   * PostgresEventStore \u2013 repository implementation via `sqlx`.\n//!   * LearningPulseService \u2013 sample use-case that persists a new learning\n//!     pulse and emits the corresponding event within a single transaction.\n//!\n//! The code follows Repository + Service-Layer patterns and is intended to be\n//! wired up by the dependency-injection crate of your choice (e.g. `shuttle`,\n//! `axum::extract::State`, or `tower` layers).\n//!\n//! # Crate features required\n//! Make sure `Cargo.toml` enables the following dependencies:\n//!\n//! ```toml\n//! [dependencies]\n//! async-nats = { version = \"0.34\", default-features = false, features = [\"jetstream\"] }\n//! async-trait = \"0.1\"\n//! anyhow = \"1\"\n//! chrono = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\n//! serde = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n//! serde_json = \"1.0\"\n//! sqlx = { version = \"0.7\", default-features = false, features = [\"runtime-tokio-rustls\", \"postgres\", \"chrono\", \"uuid\", \"json\"] }\n//! tokio = { version = \"1\", features = [\"macros\", \"rt-multi-thread\"] }\n//! tracing = \"0.1\"\n//! uuid = { version = \"1\", features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Warning\n//! Production environments should tune JetStream options (e.g., stream\n//! retention), connection retries, and TLS settings. This sample is simplified\n//! for readability.\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse async_nats::jetstream::{self, stream::Stream};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgQueryResult, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Domain Event Definitions\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Enum representing all event payloads produced by the **web_social** bounded\n/// context. Each variant must remain strictly additive (append-only) to avoid\n/// breaking consumers performing pattern matching.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"data\")]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum DomainEventKind {\n    LessonPublished {\n        lesson_id: Uuid,\n        teacher_id: Uuid,\n    },\n    QuizSubmitted {\n        quiz_id: Uuid,\n        student_id: Uuid,\n        score_percentage: f32,\n    },\n    BadgeAwarded {\n        badge_id: Uuid,\n        student_id: Uuid,\n    },\n    PaymentProcessed {\n        payment_id: Uuid,\n        order_total_cents: i64,\n        payer_id: Uuid,\n    },\n    LearningPulseCreated {\n        pulse_id: Uuid,\n        teacher_id: Uuid,\n    },\n    LearningPulseResponded {\n        pulse_id: Uuid,\n        student_id: Uuid,\n        response_id: Uuid,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Envelope that wraps every event with rich metadata required for correlation\n/// and causation analysis.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DomainEvent {\n    /// Unique ID for this event. Uses UUID v4 for randomness and horizontal\n    /// partitioning friendliness.\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    /// ID that allows grouping of events that belong to the same workflow.\n    pub correlation_id: Option<Uuid>,\n    /// ID of the event that directly triggered this one, if any.\n    pub causation_id: Option<Uuid>,\n    /// Actor responsible (user, system) \u2013 useful for audit trail.\n    pub actor_id: Option<Uuid>,\n    /// Time of creation in UTC with millisecond precision.\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Semantic version of the payload schema.\n    pub schema_version: u16,\n    /// Actual business payload.\n    pub payload: DomainEventKind,\n}\n\nimpl DomainEvent {\n    /// Creates a new envelope, stamping with `Utc::now()`.\n    pub fn new(actor_id: Option<Uuid>, payload: DomainEventKind) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            correlation_id: None,\n            causation_id: None,\n            actor_id,\n            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n            schema_version: 1,\n            payload,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the recommended NATS subject where this event should be\n    /// published, following the `edupulse.{bounded_context}.{event_name}` style\n    /// to facilitate fine-grained subscriptions.\n    pub fn subject(&self) -> String {\n        let event_name = match &self.payload {\n            DomainEventKind::LessonPublished { .. } => \"lesson_published\",\n            DomainEventKind::QuizSubmitted { .. } => \"quiz_submitted\",\n            DomainEventKind::BadgeAwarded { .. } => \"badge_awarded\",\n            DomainEventKind::PaymentProcessed { .. } => \"payment_processed\",\n            DomainEventKind::LearningPulseCreated { .. } => \"learning_pulse_created\",\n            DomainEventKind::LearningPulseResponded { .. } => \"learning_pulse_responded\",\n        };\n        format!(\"edupulse.web_social.{}\", event_name)\n    }\n}\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// EventPublisher Abstraction\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Transport-agnostic publisher for domain events. Implementations must be\n/// idempotent and **MUST NOT** panic; failures are returned as `Err`.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish(&self, event: &DomainEvent) -> Result<()>;\n}\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NATS JetStream Implementation\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Production-grade NATS JetStream publisher, supporting at-least-once delivery.\n/// Uses JSON serialisation for maximum interoperability with polyglot services.\npub struct NatsEventPublisher {\n    client: async_nats::Client,\n    jetstream: jetstream::Context,\n    stream: Stream,\n}\n\nimpl NatsEventPublisher {\n    /// Creates a new publisher, auto-provisioning the `edupulse` stream if it\n    /// does not exist. The stream is configured for message-size budgets\n    /// typical to domain events (< 64 KiB).\n    pub async fn new(nats_url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let client = async_nats::connect(nats_url).await?;\n        let jetstream = jetstream::new(client.clone());\n\n        let stream = match jetstream.get_stream(\"EDUPULSE\").await {\n            Ok(stream) => stream,\n            Err(_) => {\n                info!(\"creating JetStream stream `EDUPULSE`\");\n                jetstream\n                    .create_stream(jetstream::stream::Config {\n                        name: \"EDUPULSE\".to_owned(),\n                        subjects: vec![\"edupulse.>\".to_owned()],\n                        max_age: chrono::Duration::days(7).to_std().unwrap(),\n                        storage: jetstream::stream::StorageType::File,\n                        ..Default::default()\n                    })\n                    .await?\n            }\n        };\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            client,\n            jetstream,\n            stream,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for NatsEventPublisher {\n    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(event_id = %event.id, subject = %event.subject()))]\n    async fn publish(&self, event: &DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n        let bytes = serde_json::to_vec(event)?;\n        self.client\n            .publish(event.subject(), bytes.into())\n            .await\n            .context(\"failed to publish event to NATS\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Event Store Repository\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Append-only store for domain events, enabling event sourcing and audit trail\n/// reconstruction. Each implementation should ensure **exactly-once** writes.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventStore: Send + Sync {\n    async fn append(&self, tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>, event: &DomainEvent)\n        -> Result<()>;\n    async fn list_by_aggregate(\n        &self,\n        aggregate_id: Uuid,\n        limit: i64,\n        offset: i64,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<DomainEvent>>;\n}\n\n/// Postgres implementation utilising `jsonb` column for payload + metadata.\npub struct PostgresEventStore {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresEventStore {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventStore for PostgresEventStore {\n    async fn append(\n        &self,\n        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n        event: &DomainEvent,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let payload_json = serde_json::to_value(event)?;\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO domain_events (\n                id,\n                occurred_at,\n                aggregate_id,\n                correlation_id,\n                causation_id,\n                actor_id,\n                schema_version,\n                payload\n            )\n            VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5,$6,$7,$8)\n            \"#,\n            event.id,\n            event.occurred_at,\n            extract_aggregate_id(event), // helper below\n            event.correlation_id,\n            event.causation_id,\n            event.actor_id,\n            event.schema_version as i32,\n            payload_json\n        )\n        .execute(&mut *tx)\n        .await\n        .context(\"failed to append domain event\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn list_by_aggregate(\n        &self,\n        aggregate_id: Uuid,\n        limit: i64,\n        offset: i64,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<DomainEvent>> {\n        let rows = sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            SELECT payload\n            FROM domain_events\n            WHERE aggregate_id = $1\n            ORDER BY occurred_at\n            LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3\n            \"#,\n            aggregate_id,\n            limit,\n            offset\n        )\n        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n        .await?;\n\n        rows.into_iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let value: serde_json::Value = row.payload;\n                let ev: DomainEvent = serde_json::from_value(value)\n                    .context(\"failed to deserialize stored event\")?;\n                Ok(ev)\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extracts a stable aggregate identifier from the given event. For some events\n/// the aggregate is obvious (e.g., `lesson_id`), for others a different domain\n/// decision may be needed. For demo purposes we pick the first UUID.\nfn extract_aggregate_id(event: &DomainEvent) -> Uuid {\n    match &event.payload {\n        DomainEventKind::LessonPublished { lesson_id, .. } => *lesson_id,\n        DomainEventKind::QuizSubmitted { quiz_id, .. } => *quiz_id,\n        DomainEventKind::BadgeAwarded { badge_id, .. } => *badge_id,\n        DomainEventKind::PaymentProcessed { payment_id, .. } => *payment_id,\n        DomainEventKind::LearningPulseCreated { pulse_id, .. } => *pulse_id,\n        DomainEventKind::LearningPulseResponded { pulse_id, .. } => *pulse_id,\n    }\n}\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// LearningPulse Service (Use-Case Example)\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// DTO used by controllers when a teacher creates a new learning pulse.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct NewLearningPulse {\n    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub due_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n}\n\n/// Lightweight representation of a persisted learning pulse.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct LearningPulse {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub due_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Service encapsulating the invariants and business rules related to Learning\n/// Pulses. Other bounded contexts interact via REST or gRPC, never directly\n/// with repositories.\npub struct LearningPulseService {\n    pool: PgPool,\n    event_store: Arc<dyn EventStore>,\n    publisher: Arc<dyn EventPublisher>,\n}\n\nimpl LearningPulseService {\n    pub fn new(\n        pool: PgPool,\n        event_store: Arc<dyn EventStore>,\n        publisher: Arc<dyn EventPublisher>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            pool,\n            event_store,\n            publisher,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Creates a new learning pulse in a single ACID transaction, appends and\n    /// publishes the corresponding domain event only **after** the DB write\n    /// succeeds (`outbox` pattern).\n    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n    pub async fn create_pulse(&self, dto: NewLearningPulse) -> Result<LearningPulse> {\n        // Begin transaction.\n        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n        let pulse_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n\n        // Insert row.\n        let q: PgQueryResult = sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO learning_pulses (id, teacher_id, title, description, due_date)\n            VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5)\n            \"#,\n            pulse_id,\n            dto.teacher_id,\n            dto.title,\n            dto.description,\n            dto.due_date\n        )\n        .execute(&mut *tx)\n        .await\n        .context(\"failed to insert learning pulse\")?;\n\n        debug!(rows_affected = q.rows_affected(), \"inserted learning pulse\");\n\n        // Build domain event.\n        let event = DomainEvent::new(\n            Some(dto.teacher_id),\n            DomainEventKind::LearningPulseCreated {\n                pulse_id,\n                teacher_id: dto.teacher_id,\n            },\n        );\n\n        // Persist event to store.\n        self.event_store.append(&mut tx, &event).await?;\n\n        // Commit.\n        tx.commit().await?;\n\n        // Publish *after* commit to ensure read-your-own writes semantics.\n        if let Err(e) = self.publisher.publish(&event).await {\n            // Publishing failures should be retried via a dead-letter queue in\n            // real deployments. We log the error and continue \u2013 state is\n            // durable because the event is stored.\n            error!(error = %e, \"failed to publish learning_pulse_created\");\n        }\n\n        Ok(LearningPulse {\n            id: pulse_id,\n            teacher_id: dto.teacher_id,\n            title: dto.title,\n            description: dto.description,\n            due_date: dto.due_date,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// SQL bootstrap helpers (optional)\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Executes the  required DDL statements if they do not yet exist. In a real\n/// environment, run migrations separately (e.g., with `sqlx migrate`), but this\n/// is handy for integration tests / demos.\npub async fn ensure_schema(pool: &PgPool) -> Result<()> {\n    info!(\"ensuring Postgres schema for domain events & learning pulses\");\n\n    sqlx::query(\n        r#\"\n        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learning_pulses (\n            id UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n            teacher_id UUID NOT NULL,\n            title TEXT NOT NULL,\n            description TEXT,\n            due_date TIMESTAMPTZ,\n            created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()\n        );\n        \"#,\n    )\n    .execute(pool)\n    .await?;\n\n    sqlx::query(\n        r#\"\n        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_events (\n            id UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n            occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n            aggregate_id UUID NOT NULL,\n            correlation_id UUID,\n            causation_id UUID,\n            actor_id UUID,\n            schema_version SMALLINT NOT NULL,\n            payload JSONB NOT NULL\n        );\n        \"#,\n    )\n    .execute(pool)\n    .await?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_4.txt",
    "numLines": 477,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 477
  },
  "type": "text"
}
