"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_48.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Event-Driven Infrastructure for EduPulse Live\n     5\u2192//! ============================================\n     6\u2192//! This module encapsulates the **Outbox \u2192 Broker** integration used across the\n     7\u2192//! platform.  Each bounded context persists `DomainEvent`s to its local\n     8\u2192//! PostgreSQL **outbox** table as part of the same transaction that mutates\n     9\u2192//! aggregates.  A dedicated background task (see [`EventDispatcher`]) then\n    10\u2192//! reliably publishes those events to the message broker (NATS) and marks them\n    11\u2192//! as dispatched, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery while preserving\n    12\u2192//! transactional integrity.\n    13\u2192//!\n    14\u2192//! The implementation follows DDD, Repository, and Service Layer patterns and\n    15\u2192//! demonstrates:\n    16\u2192//!   \u2022 Database integration with `sqlx`\n    17\u2192//!   \u2022 Async, streaming message publication with `nats`\n    18\u2192//!   \u2022 Robust error handling and observability (`thiserror`, `tracing`)\n    19\u2192//!   \u2022 Support for GDPR erase requests through an explicit `UserEraseRequested`\n    20\u2192//!     event visible to downstream services.\n    21\u2192//!\n    22\u2192//! # Public API\n    23\u2192//! * [`DomainEvent`]: Trait implemented by every event emitted by the domain.\n    24\u2192//! * [`EventEnvelope`]: Serializable wrapper containing metadata & payload.\n    25\u2192//! * [`EventOutboxRepository`]: Abstract persistence port.\n    26\u2192//! * [`PgOutboxRepository`]: Postgres-backed implementation.\n    27\u2192//! * [`NatsPublisher`]: Message broker adapter.\n    28\u2192//! * [`EventDispatcher`]: Orchestrates fetch \u2192 publish \u2192 acknowledge loop.\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192use std::{\n    31\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    32\u2192    time::{Duration, SystemTime},\n    33\u2192};\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    36\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    37\u2192use futures::{stream, StreamExt};\n    38\u2192use nats::asynk::{self, Connection};\n    39\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    40\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, QueryBuilder};\n    41\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    42\u2192use tokio::{select, task::JoinHandle, time::sleep};\n    43\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    44\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    47\u2192/*                          Domain Event Abstractions                        */\n    48\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192/// Marker trait implemented by *all* domain events.\n    51\u2192///\n    52\u2192/// Implementations **must** be serializable (`Serialize + DeserializeOwned`) and\n    53\u2192/// versioned via the `EVENT_VERSION` constant to facilitate schema evolution.\n    54\u2192#[async_trait]\n    55\u2192pub trait DomainEvent:\n    56\u2192    Send + Sync + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + 'static + std::fmt::Debug\n    57\u2192{\n    58\u2192    /// Logical type discriminator, e.g. `\"lesson.published.v1\"`.\n    59\u2192    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str;\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    /// Semantic version of the event's JSON schema.\n    62\u2192    const EVENT_VERSION: u8 = 1;\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    /// Globally unique identifier used for idempotency by consumers.\n    65\u2192    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /// Aggregate root identifier the event originated from.\n    68\u2192    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    /// Timestamp (UTC) at which the event occurred.\n    71\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192/// Example event emitted when a lesson is published.\n    75\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    76\u2192pub struct LessonPublished {\n    77\u2192    pub event_id: Uuid,\n    78\u2192    pub lesson_id: Uuid,\n    79\u2192    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    80\u2192    pub title: String,\n    81\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192#[async_trait]\n    85\u2192impl DomainEvent for LessonPublished {\n    86\u2192    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str = \"lesson.published.v1\";\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n    89\u2192        self.event_id\n    90\u2192    }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n    93\u2192        self.lesson_id\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n    97\u2192        self.occurred_at\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192}\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192/// Event raised when a user requests GDPR account erasure.\n   102\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   103\u2192pub struct UserEraseRequested {\n   104\u2192    pub event_id: Uuid,\n   105\u2192    pub user_id: Uuid,\n   106\u2192    pub requested_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192#[async_trait]\n   110\u2192impl DomainEvent for UserEraseRequested {\n   111\u2192    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str = \"user.erase_requested.v1\";\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n   114\u2192        self.event_id\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n   118\u2192        self.user_id\n   119\u2192    }\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   122\u2192        self.requested_at\n   123\u2192    }\n   124\u2192}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   127\u2192/*                         Event Envelope & Serialization                    */\n   128\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192/// Wrapper persisted to the outbox and exchanged via NATS.\n   131\u2192///\n   132\u2192/// We store payload as raw JSON (`payload_json`) to avoid polymorphism issues\n   133\u2192/// in the DB layer while still enabling typed deserialization by consumers.\n   134\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   135\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n   136\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n   137\u2192    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n   138\u2192    pub event_type: String,\n   139\u2192    pub event_version: u8,\n   140\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    /// Raw JSON representation of the event.\n   143\u2192    pub payload_json: serde_json::Value,\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192impl EventEnvelope {\n   147\u2192    /// Create a new envelope from a concrete [`DomainEvent`] implementation.\n   148\u2192    pub fn from_event<E: DomainEvent>(event: &E) -> serde_json::Result<Self> {\n   149\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   150\u2192            id: event.event_id(),\n   151\u2192            aggregate_id: event.aggregate_id(),\n   152\u2192            event_type: E::EVENT_TYPE.to_string(),\n   153\u2192            event_version: E::EVENT_VERSION,\n   154\u2192            occurred_at: event.occurred_at(),\n   155\u2192            payload_json: serde_json::to_value(event)?,\n   156\u2192        })\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192}\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   161\u2192/*                          Repository: Outbox Storage                       */\n   162\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   165\u2192pub enum OutboxError {\n   166\u2192    #[error(\"Database error: {0}\")]\n   167\u2192    Db(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   168\u2192    #[error(\"Serde error: {0}\")]\n   169\u2192    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   170\u2192}\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192#[async_trait]\n   173\u2192pub trait EventOutboxRepository: Send + Sync {\n   174\u2192    /// Persist a new domain event within an existing DB transaction.\n   175\u2192    async fn add_event<E: DomainEvent + Send + Sync>(\n   176\u2192        &self,\n   177\u2192        tx: &mut sqlx::Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   178\u2192        event: &E,\n   179\u2192    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError>;\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    /// Fetch a *batch* of events that have not yet been dispatched.\n   182\u2192    async fn fetch_pending(\n   183\u2192        &self,\n   184\u2192        limit: i64,\n   185\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, OutboxError>;\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192    /// Mark the given event IDs as dispatched.\n   188\u2192    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, ids: &[Uuid]) -> Result<(), OutboxError>;\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192/// PostgreSQL implementation of [`EventOutboxRepository`].\n   192\u2192pub struct PgOutboxRepository {\n   193\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   194\u2192}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192impl PgOutboxRepository {\n   197\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   198\u2192        Self { pool }\n   199\u2192    }\n   200\u2192}\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192#[async_trait]\n   203\u2192impl EventOutboxRepository for PgOutboxRepository {\n   204\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, tx, event))]\n   205\u2192    async fn add_event<E: DomainEvent + Send + Sync>(\n   206\u2192        &self,\n   207\u2192        tx: &mut sqlx::Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   208\u2192        event: &E,\n   209\u2192    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError> {\n   210\u2192        let env = EventEnvelope::from_event(event)?;\n   211\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   212\u2192            r#\"\n   213\u2192            INSERT INTO outbox_events (\n   214\u2192                id,\n   215\u2192                aggregate_id,\n   216\u2192                event_type,\n   217\u2192                event_version,\n   218\u2192                occurred_at,\n   219\u2192                payload_json\n   220\u2192            )\n   221\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n   222\u2192            \"#,\n   223\u2192            env.id,\n   224\u2192            env.aggregate_id,\n   225\u2192            env.event_type,\n   226\u2192            env.event_version as i16,\n   227\u2192            env.occurred_at,\n   228\u2192            env.payload_json,\n   229\u2192        )\n   230\u2192        .execute(&mut **tx)\n   231\u2192        .await?;\n   232\u2192        Ok(())\n   233\u2192    }\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   236\u2192    async fn fetch_pending(\n   237\u2192        &self,\n   238\u2192        limit: i64,\n   239\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, OutboxError> {\n   240\u2192        let raw = sqlx::query!(\n   241\u2192            r#\"\n   242\u2192            SELECT id,\n   243\u2192                   aggregate_id,\n   244\u2192                   event_type,\n   245\u2192                   event_version,\n   246\u2192                   occurred_at,\n   247\u2192                   payload_json\n   248\u2192            FROM outbox_events\n   249\u2192            WHERE dispatched_at IS NULL\n   250\u2192            ORDER BY occurred_at\n   251\u2192            LIMIT $1\n   252\u2192            \"#,\n   253\u2192            limit\n   254\u2192        )\n   255\u2192        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n   256\u2192        .await?;\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192        let mut events = Vec::with_capacity(raw.len());\n   259\u2192        for row in raw {\n   260\u2192            events.push(EventEnvelope {\n   261\u2192                id: row.id,\n   262\u2192                aggregate_id: row.aggregate_id,\n   263\u2192                event_type: row.event_type,\n   264\u2192                event_version: row.event_version as u8,\n   265\u2192                occurred_at: row.occurred_at,\n   266\u2192                payload_json: row.payload_json,\n   267\u2192            });\n   268\u2192        }\n   269\u2192        Ok(events)\n   270\u2192    }\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, ids))]\n   273\u2192    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, ids: &[Uuid]) -> Result<(), OutboxError> {\n   274\u2192        if ids.is_empty() {\n   275\u2192            return Ok(());\n   276\u2192        }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192        let mut qb: QueryBuilder<Postgres> = QueryBuilder::new(\n   279\u2192            \"UPDATE outbox_events SET dispatched_at = NOW() WHERE id IN (\",\n   280\u2192        );\n   281\u2192        let mut separated = qb.separated(\", \");\n   282\u2192        for id in ids {\n   283\u2192            separated.push_bind(id);\n   284\u2192        }\n   285\u2192        separated.push_unseparated(\")\");\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192        qb.build().execute(&self.pool).await?;\n   288\u2192        Ok(())\n   289\u2192    }\n   290\u2192}\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   293\u2192/*                         Message Broker Integration                        */\n   294\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   297\u2192pub enum PublishError {\n   298\u2192    #[error(\"NATS error: {0}\")]\n   299\u2192    Nats(#[from] nats::Error),\n   300\u2192    #[error(\"Serialization error: {0}\")]\n   301\u2192    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   302\u2192}\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192#[async_trait]\n   305\u2192pub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n   306\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), PublishError>;\n   307\u2192}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192pub struct NatsPublisher {\n   310\u2192    conn: Connection,\n   311\u2192    subject_prefix: String,\n   312\u2192}\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192impl NatsPublisher {\n   315\u2192    pub async fn connect(\n   316\u2192        nats_url: &str,\n   317\u2192        jwt: Option<String>,\n   318\u2192        prefix: impl Into<String>,\n   319\u2192    ) -> Result<Self, PublishError> {\n   320\u2192        let conn = if let Some(token) = jwt {\n   321\u2192            asynk::Options::with_jwt(token, |nonce| async move {\n   322\u2192                // TODO: Provide signature callback for NATS auth if required.\n   323\u2192                Ok(vec![])\n   324\u2192            })\n   325\u2192            .connect(nats_url)\n   326\u2192            .await?\n   327\u2192        } else {\n   328\u2192            asynk::connect(nats_url).await?\n   329\u2192        };\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   332\u2192            conn,\n   333\u2192            subject_prefix: prefix.into(),\n   334\u2192        })\n   335\u2192    }\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192    fn subject_for(&self, event_type: &str) -> String {\n   338\u2192        format!(\"{}.{}\", self.subject_prefix, event_type)\n   339\u2192    }\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192#[async_trait]\n   343\u2192impl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n   344\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, event))]\n   345\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n   346\u2192        let subject = self.subject_for(&event.event_type);\n   347\u2192        let data = serde_json::to_vec(event)?;\n   348\u2192        self.conn.publish(subject, data).await?;\n   349\u2192        Ok(())\n   350\u2192    }\n   351\u2192}\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   354\u2192/*                             Event Dispatcher                              */\n   355\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   358\u2192pub enum DispatchError {\n   359\u2192    #[error(transparent)]\n   360\u2192    Outbox(#[from] OutboxError),\n   361\u2192    #[error(transparent)]\n   362\u2192    Publish(#[from] PublishError),\n   363\u2192}\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192/// Repeatedly polls the outbox and hands events to the broker.\n   366\u2192///\n   367\u2192/// Spawn via [`tokio::spawn`]; it terminates when the passed `shutdown`\n   368\u2192/// [`tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver`] is signalled.\n   369\u2192pub struct EventDispatcher<R: EventOutboxRepository, P: EventPublisher> {\n   370\u2192    repo: Arc<R>,\n   371\u2192    publisher: Arc<P>,\n   372\u2192    batch_size: i64,\n   373\u2192    poll_interval: Duration,\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192impl<R, P> EventDispatcher<R, P>\n   377\u2192where\n   378\u2192    R: EventOutboxRepository + 'static,\n   379\u2192    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n   380\u2192{\n   381\u2192    pub fn new(\n   382\u2192        repo: Arc<R>,\n   383\u2192        publisher: Arc<P>,\n   384\u2192        batch_size: i64,\n   385\u2192        poll_interval: Duration,\n   386\u2192    ) -> Self {\n   387\u2192        Self {\n   388\u2192            repo,\n   389\u2192            publisher,\n   390\u2192            batch_size,\n   391\u2192            poll_interval,\n   392\u2192        }\n   393\u2192    }\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, shutdown_rx), name = \"event_dispatch_loop\")]\n   396\u2192    pub fn start(\n   397\u2192        self,\n   398\u2192        mut shutdown_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<()>,\n   399\u2192    ) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n   400\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   401\u2192            loop {\n   402\u2192                select! {\n   403\u2192                    _ = shutdown_rx.recv() => {\n   404\u2192                        info!(\"EventDispatcher received shutdown signal\");\n   405\u2192                        break;\n   406\u2192                    }\n   407\u2192                    _ = self.tick() => {}\n   408\u2192                }\n   409\u2192            }\n   410\u2192        })\n   411\u2192    }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192    /// Single poll \u2192 publish \u2192 ack iteration.\n   414\u2192    async fn tick(&self) {\n   415\u2192        match self\n   416\u2192            .repo\n   417\u2192            .fetch_pending(self.batch_size)\n   418\u2192            .await\n   419\u2192        {\n   420\u2192            Ok(events) if events.is_empty() => {\n   421\u2192                sleep(self.poll_interval).await;\n   422\u2192            }\n   423\u2192            Ok(events) => {\n   424\u2192                let ids: Vec<Uuid> = events.iter().map(|e| e.id).collect();\n   425\u2192                debug!(count = events.len(), \"Publishing events\");\n   426\u2192\n   427\u2192                // Publish concurrently but limit concurrency to avoid backpressure.\n   428\u2192                let results = stream::iter(events)\n   429\u2192                    .map(|e| async {\n   430\u2192                        if let Err(err) = self.publisher.publish(&e).await {\n   431\u2192                            error!(error = %err, \"Failed to publish event\");\n   432\u2192                            Err(err)\n   433\u2192                        } else {\n   434\u2192                            Ok(())\n   435\u2192                        }\n   436\u2192                    })\n   437\u2192                    .buffer_unordered(8)\n   438\u2192                    .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n   439\u2192                    .await;\n   440\u2192\n   441\u2192                if results.iter().all(Result::is_ok) {\n   442\u2192                    if let Err(err) = self.repo.mark_dispatched(&ids).await {\n   443\u2192                        error!(%err, \"Failed to mark events as dispatched\");\n   444\u2192                    }\n   445\u2192                } else {\n   446\u2192                    warn!(\"Some events failed to publish; will retry later\");\n   447\u2192                }\n   448\u2192            }\n   449\u2192            Err(err) => {\n   450\u2192                error!(%err, \"Failed to fetch pending events\");\n   451\u2192                sleep(self.poll_interval).await;\n   452\u2192            }\n   453\u2192        }\n   454\u2192    }\n   455\u2192}\n   456\u2192\n   457\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   458\u2192/*                         Module Initialization Helper                      */\n   459\u2192/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192/// Build all dependencies and start dispatcher.\n   462\u2192///\n   463\u2192/// Example usage (in `main.rs`):\n   464\u2192///\n   465\u2192/// ```rust,no_run\n   466\u2192/// #[tokio::main]\n   467\u2192/// async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   468\u2192///     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n   469\u2192///\n   470\u2192///     let (_shutdown_tx, shutdown_rx) = tokio::sync::broadcast::channel(1);\n   471\u2192///     let handle = module_48::init_and_spawn(\"postgres://...\", \"nats://localhost\", None, shutdown_rx).await?;\n   472\u2192///\n   473\u2192///     // ...\n   474\u2192///\n   475\u2192///     // On ctrl-c\n   476\u2192///     _shutdown_tx.send(())?;\n   477\u2192///     handle.await?;\n   478\u2192///     Ok(())\n   479\u2192/// }\n   480\u2192/// ```\n   481\u2192pub async fn init_and_spawn(\n   482\u2192    pg_dsn: &str,\n   483\u2192    nats_url: &str,\n   484\u2192    nats_jwt: Option<String>,\n   485\u2192    shutdown_rx: tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<()>,\n   486\u2192) -> Result<JoinHandle<()>, anyhow::Error> {\n   487\u2192    let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   488\u2192        .max_connections(8)\n   489\u2192        .connect(pg_dsn)\n   490\u2192        .await?;\n   491\u2192\n   492\u2192    let repo = Arc::new(PgOutboxRepository::new(pool));\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192    let publisher = Arc::new(\n   495\u2192        NatsPublisher::connect(nats_url, nats_jwt, \"edupulse\")\n   496\u2192            .await\n   497\u2192            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?,\n   498\u2192    );\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192    let dispatcher = EventDispatcher::new(\n   501\u2192        repo,\n   502\u2192        publisher,\n   503\u2192        100,\n   504\u2192        Duration::from_secs(2),\n   505\u2192    );\n   506\u2192\n   507\u2192    Ok(dispatcher.start(shutdown_rx))\n   508\u2192}\n   509\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_15.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! An event\u2013driven badge\u2013awarding micro-service for the\n     5\u2192//! \u201cEduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub\u201d platform.\n     6\u2192//!\n     7\u2192//! This module demonstrates how the core application publishes\n     8\u2192//! domain events while an out-of-process, autonomous component\n     9\u2192//! (here: `BadgeAwardService`) consumes them, performs business\n    10\u2192//! logic, persists the result and publishes its own follow-up\n    11\u2192//! events.  \n    12\u2192//!\n    13\u2192//! The service is completely self-contained: it hosts an in-memory\n    14\u2192//! dispatcher for local development, yet persists events and state\n    15\u2192//! in Postgres so it can be moved into a distributed setting\n    16\u2192//! later on (e.g. with NATS/Kafka\u2010backed `Dispatcher`).\n    17\u2192//!\n    18\u2192//! Architectural patterns shown here:\n    19\u2192//!   \u2022 Repository Pattern      \u2013 decoupling persistence\n    20\u2192//!   \u2022 Service Layer           \u2013 `BadgeAwardService`\n    21\u2192//!   \u2022 Event Sourcing          \u2013 immutable `EventStore`\n    22\u2192//!   \u2022 Async Rust w/ Tokio     \u2013 non-blocking I/O\n    23\u2192//!   \u2022 Error Handling w/ thiserror + anyhow\n    24\u2192//!\n    25\u2192//! NOTE: In a real-world project this module would be split\n    26\u2192//! into several files/crates.  Everything is co-located here only\n    27\u2192//! to satisfy the single-file constraint of the exercise.\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192use std::{\n    30\u2192    collections::HashSet,\n    31\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    32\u2192    time::Duration,\n    33\u2192};\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    36\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    37\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    38\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool};\n    39\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    40\u2192use tokio::{\n    41\u2192    select,\n    42\u2192    sync::{broadcast, mpsc, RwLock},\n    43\u2192    task::JoinHandle,\n    44\u2192};\n    45\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192/// Domain events which can be emitted by EduPulse services.\n    48\u2192///\n    49\u2192/// IMPORTANT: New variants **must** implement `Serialize`/`Deserialize`\n    50\u2192/// so they can be persisted in the event store.\n    51\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    52\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"payload\")]\n    53\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    54\u2192    QuizSubmitted(QuizSubmitted),\n    55\u2192    BadgeAwarded(BadgeAwarded),\n    56\u2192    // \u2026 more variants omitted for brevity\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192impl DomainEvent {\n    60\u2192    /// Helper returning event name for logging / metrics.\n    61\u2192    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n    62\u2192        match self {\n    63\u2192            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(_) => \"QuizSubmitted\",\n    64\u2192            DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(_) => \"BadgeAwarded\",\n    65\u2192        }\n    66\u2192    }\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192/// Metadata common to all envelopes\u2014separates immutable event data\n    70\u2192/// from peripheral transport concerns such as IDs and timestamps.\n    71\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    72\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n    73\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    74\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    75\u2192    pub event: DomainEvent,\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192impl EventEnvelope {\n    79\u2192    pub fn new(event: DomainEvent) -> Self {\n    80\u2192        Self {\n    81\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    82\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n    83\u2192            event,\n    84\u2192        }\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192/// Event data triggered when a learner submits a quiz.\n    89\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    90\u2192pub struct QuizSubmitted {\n    91\u2192    pub quiz_id: Uuid,\n    92\u2192    pub learner_id: Uuid,\n    93\u2192    pub score: f32,\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192/// Event data emitted once the learner qualifies for a badge.\n    97\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    98\u2192pub struct BadgeAwarded {\n    99\u2192    pub badge_id: Uuid,\n   100\u2192    pub learner_id: Uuid,\n   101\u2192    pub awarded_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   102\u2192    pub reason: String,\n   103\u2192}\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192/// Central event dispatcher.  \n   106\u2192/// Internally uses a Tokio broadcast channel so any number of\n   107\u2192/// in-process consumers can receive events concurrently.\n   108\u2192///\n   109\u2192/// In production you might replace the implementation with NATS,\n   110\u2192/// Kafka, RabbitMQ\u2026 etc., leaving the public API intact.\n   111\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   112\u2192pub struct Dispatcher {\n   113\u2192    sender: broadcast::Sender<EventEnvelope>,\n   114\u2192}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192impl Dispatcher {\n   117\u2192    /// Create a new dispatcher with a configurable ring buffer size.\n   118\u2192    pub fn new(channel_size: usize) -> Self {\n   119\u2192        let (sender, _rx) = broadcast::channel(channel_size);\n   120\u2192        Self { sender }\n   121\u2192    }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    /// Publish an envelope to all subscribers.\n   124\u2192    pub async fn publish(&self, envelope: EventEnvelope) {\n   125\u2192        // broadcast::Sender::send is synchronous but inexpensive.\n   126\u2192        // Failure only occurs when there are no active receivers;\n   127\u2192        // still, we ignore that because the event is persisted in\n   128\u2192        // the store before reaching this point.\n   129\u2192        let _ = self.sender.send(envelope);\n   130\u2192    }\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    /// Subscribe to a continuous stream of events.\n   133\u2192    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<EventEnvelope> {\n   134\u2192        self.sender.subscribe()\n   135\u2192    }\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192/// Persisted event stream; provides idempotent append + read access.\n   139\u2192/// Uses Postgres JSONB for flexible schema evolution.\n   140\u2192///\n   141\u2192/// Table DDL (run via migrations):\n   142\u2192/// ```sql\n   143\u2192/// CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS event_store (\n   144\u2192///     id           UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n   145\u2192///     occurred_at  TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,\n   146\u2192///     event_name   TEXT NOT NULL,\n   147\u2192///     payload      JSONB NOT NULL\n   148\u2192/// );\n   149\u2192/// ```\n   150\u2192pub struct EventStore {\n   151\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192impl EventStore {\n   155\u2192    pub async fn new(db_url: &str, max_connections: u32) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {\n   156\u2192        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   157\u2192            .max_connections(max_connections)\n   158\u2192            .connect(db_url)\n   159\u2192            .await?;\n   160\u2192        Ok(Self { pool })\n   161\u2192    }\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    /// Append envelope to store.\n   164\u2192    pub async fn append(&self, env: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n   165\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   166\u2192            r#\"\n   167\u2192            INSERT INTO event_store (id, occurred_at, event_name, payload)\n   168\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n   169\u2192            \"#,\n   170\u2192            env.id,\n   171\u2192            env.occurred_at,\n   172\u2192            env.event.name(),\n   173\u2192            serde_json::to_value(&env.event)?\n   174\u2192        )\n   175\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   176\u2192        .await?;\n   177\u2192        Ok(())\n   178\u2192    }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    /// Read events by name\u2014useful for rebuilding read models.\n   181\u2192    pub async fn load_by_event(\n   182\u2192        &self,\n   183\u2192        event_name: &str,\n   184\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<EventEnvelope>, sqlx::Error> {\n   185\u2192        let recs = sqlx::query!(\n   186\u2192            r#\"\n   187\u2192            SELECT id, occurred_at, payload\n   188\u2192            FROM event_store\n   189\u2192            WHERE event_name = $1\n   190\u2192            ORDER BY occurred_at ASC\n   191\u2192            \"#,\n   192\u2192            event_name\n   193\u2192        )\n   194\u2192        .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n   195\u2192        .await?;\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192        recs.into_iter()\n   198\u2192            .map(|r| {\n   199\u2192                let event = match event_name {\n   200\u2192                    \"QuizSubmitted\" => DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n   201\u2192                        serde_json::from_value(r.payload)?,\n   202\u2192                    ),\n   203\u2192                    \"BadgeAwarded\" => DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(\n   204\u2192                        serde_json::from_value(r.payload)?,\n   205\u2192                    ),\n   206\u2192                    _ => unreachable!(\"Unknown event stored in DB\"),\n   207\u2192                };\n   208\u2192                Ok(EventEnvelope {\n   209\u2192                    id: r.id,\n   210\u2192                    occurred_at: r.occurred_at,\n   211\u2192                    event,\n   212\u2192                })\n   213\u2192            })\n   214\u2192            .collect()\n   215\u2192    }\n   216\u2192}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192/// Error type covering all repository/service error cases.\n   219\u2192#[derive(Error, Debug)]\n   220\u2192pub enum ServiceError {\n   221\u2192    #[error(\"Database error: {0}\")]\n   222\u2192    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192    #[error(\"Event serialisation error: {0}\")]\n   225\u2192    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192    #[error(\"Internal channel closed\")]\n   228\u2192    ChannelClosed,\n   229\u2192}\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192/// Repository abstraction for badge persistence.\n   232\u2192#[async_trait]\n   233\u2192pub trait BadgeRepository: Send + Sync {\n   234\u2192    async fn learner_already_has_badge(\n   235\u2192        &self,\n   236\u2192        learner_id: Uuid,\n   237\u2192        badge_id: Uuid,\n   238\u2192    ) -> Result<bool, ServiceError>;\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192    async fn assign_badge(\n   241\u2192        &self,\n   242\u2192        learner_id: Uuid,\n   243\u2192        badge: &BadgeAwarded,\n   244\u2192    ) -> Result<(), ServiceError>;\n   245\u2192}\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192/// Postgres-based implementation of the `BadgeRepository`.\n   248\u2192///\n   249\u2192/// Table DDL:\n   250\u2192/// ```sql\n   251\u2192/// CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learner_badges (\n   252\u2192///     learner_id UUID NOT NULL,\n   253\u2192///     badge_id   UUID NOT NULL,\n   254\u2192///     awarded_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,\n   255\u2192///     reason     TEXT NOT NULL,\n   256\u2192///     PRIMARY KEY (learner_id, badge_id)\n   257\u2192/// );\n   258\u2192/// ```\n   259\u2192pub struct PgBadgeRepository {\n   260\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   261\u2192}\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192impl PgBadgeRepository {\n   264\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   265\u2192        Self { pool }\n   266\u2192    }\n   267\u2192}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192#[async_trait]\n   270\u2192impl BadgeRepository for PgBadgeRepository {\n   271\u2192    async fn learner_already_has_badge(\n   272\u2192        &self,\n   273\u2192        learner_id: Uuid,\n   274\u2192        badge_id: Uuid,\n   275\u2192    ) -> Result<bool, ServiceError> {\n   276\u2192        let res = sqlx::query_scalar!(\n   277\u2192            r#\"\n   278\u2192            SELECT EXISTS (\n   279\u2192                SELECT 1\n   280\u2192                FROM learner_badges\n   281\u2192                WHERE learner_id = $1 AND badge_id = $2\n   282\u2192            ) AS \"exists!\"\n   283\u2192            \"#,\n   284\u2192            learner_id,\n   285\u2192            badge_id\n   286\u2192        )\n   287\u2192        .fetch_one(&self.pool)\n   288\u2192        .await?;\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192        Ok(res.unwrap_or(false))\n   291\u2192    }\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192    async fn assign_badge(\n   294\u2192        &self,\n   295\u2192        learner_id: Uuid,\n   296\u2192        badge: &BadgeAwarded,\n   297\u2192    ) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n   298\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   299\u2192            r#\"\n   300\u2192            INSERT INTO learner_badges (learner_id, badge_id, awarded_at, reason)\n   301\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n   302\u2192            \"#,\n   303\u2192            learner_id,\n   304\u2192            badge.badge_id,\n   305\u2192            badge.awarded_at,\n   306\u2192            badge.reason\n   307\u2192        )\n   308\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   309\u2192        .await?;\n   310\u2192        Ok(())\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192}\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192/// Business logic deciding whether a quiz submission qualifies\n   315\u2192/// for a badge.  Runs fully asynchronously, keeping its own\n   316\u2192/// subscription to the dispatcher.\n   317\u2192///\n   318\u2192/// Scoring rules used here are intentionally simple:  \n   319\u2192/// \u2013 Score \u2265 90 %      \u21d2 \u201cQuiz Master\u201d badge (UUID hard-coded)  \n   320\u2192/// \u2013 Score exactly 100 \u21d2 special \u201cPerfect Score\u201d badge\n   321\u2192pub struct BadgeAwardService<R: BadgeRepository + 'static> {\n   322\u2192    repo: Arc<R>,\n   323\u2192    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n   324\u2192    event_store: Arc<EventStore>,\n   325\u2192    subscribed_badges: HashSet<Uuid>,\n   326\u2192}\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192impl<R: BadgeRepository + 'static> BadgeAwardService<R> {\n   329\u2192    pub fn new(\n   330\u2192        repo: Arc<R>,\n   331\u2192        dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n   332\u2192        event_store: Arc<EventStore>,\n   333\u2192    ) -> Self {\n   334\u2192        // Hard-coded UUIDs of known badges.\n   335\u2192        let subscribed_badges = vec![\n   336\u2192            // \u201cQuiz Master\u201d\n   337\u2192            Uuid::parse_str(\"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\").unwrap(),\n   338\u2192            // \u201cPerfect Score\u201d\n   339\u2192            Uuid::parse_str(\"22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222\").unwrap(),\n   340\u2192        ]\n   341\u2192        .into_iter()\n   342\u2192        .collect();\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192        Self {\n   345\u2192            repo,\n   346\u2192            dispatcher,\n   347\u2192            event_store,\n   348\u2192            subscribed_badges,\n   349\u2192        }\n   350\u2192    }\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192    /// Spawn the service on the Tokio runtime; returns its `JoinHandle`\n   353\u2192    /// so callers can `.await` graceful shutdown.\n   354\u2192    pub fn spawn(self: Arc<Self>) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n   355\u2192        let mut rx = self.dispatcher.subscribe();\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   358\u2192            loop {\n   359\u2192                select! {\n   360\u2192                    biased;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192                    // React to new events\n   363\u2192                    msg = rx.recv() => match msg {\n   364\u2192                        Ok(env) => {\n   365\u2192                            if let Err(e) = self.process_event(env).await {\n   366\u2192                                tracing::error!(\"BadgeAwardService failed: {e}\");\n   367\u2192                            }\n   368\u2192                        },\n   369\u2192                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => {\n   370\u2192                            tracing::warn!(\"Dispatcher closed, BadgeAwardService exiting.\");\n   371\u2192                            break;\n   372\u2192                        },\n   373\u2192                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(skipped)) => {\n   374\u2192                            tracing::warn!(\"BadgeAwardService lagged, skipped {skipped} messages\");\n   375\u2192                        },\n   376\u2192                    },\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192                    // Allow cooperative cancellation.\n   379\u2192                    _ = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(60)) => {},\n   380\u2192                }\n   381\u2192            }\n   382\u2192        })\n   383\u2192    }\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192    async fn process_event(&self, env: EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n   386\u2192        match env.event {\n   387\u2192            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(sub) => self.maybe_award_badge(sub).await?,\n   388\u2192            _ => { /* ignore */ }\n   389\u2192        }\n   390\u2192        Ok(())\n   391\u2192    }\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192    async fn maybe_award_badge(&self, sub: QuizSubmitted) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n   394\u2192        // Determine qualifying badge.\n   395\u2192        let (badge_id, reason) = if (sub.score - 100.0).abs() < f32::EPSILON {\n   396\u2192            (\n   397\u2192                Uuid::parse_str(\"22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222\").unwrap(),\n   398\u2192                \"Perfect quiz score!\".to_owned(),\n   399\u2192            )\n   400\u2192        } else if sub.score >= 90.0 {\n   401\u2192            (\n   402\u2192                Uuid::parse_str(\"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\").unwrap(),\n   403\u2192                \"Scored 90% or above on a quiz.\".to_owned(),\n   404\u2192            )\n   405\u2192        } else {\n   406\u2192            return Ok(()); // Not eligible\n   407\u2192        };\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192        // Filter unknown badge IDs (forward-compat).\n   410\u2192        if !self.subscribed_badges.contains(&badge_id) {\n   411\u2192            return Ok(());\n   412\u2192        }\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192        // Idempotency check: learner may resubmit quiz multiple times.\n   415\u2192        if self\n   416\u2192            .repo\n   417\u2192            .learner_already_has_badge(sub.learner_id, badge_id)\n   418\u2192            .await?\n   419\u2192        {\n   420\u2192            return Ok(());\n   421\u2192        }\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192        // Compose and persist badge award.\n   424\u2192        let badge = BadgeAwarded {\n   425\u2192            badge_id,\n   426\u2192            learner_id: sub.learner_id,\n   427\u2192            awarded_at: Utc::now(),\n   428\u2192            reason,\n   429\u2192        };\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192        self.repo.assign_badge(sub.learner_id, &badge).await?;\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded(badge.clone()));\n   434\u2192        self.event_store.append(&envelope).await?;\n   435\u2192        self.dispatcher.publish(envelope).await;\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192        tracing::info!(\n   438\u2192            learner=%sub.learner_id,\n   439\u2192            badge=%badge.badge_id,\n   440\u2192            \"Badge awarded!\"\n   441\u2192        );\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192        Ok(())\n   444\u2192    }\n   445\u2192}\n   446\u2192\n   447\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   448\u2192//  Bootstrap / Example\n   449\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192/// Start an in-process example\u2014useful when the module is compiled\n   452\u2192/// as `cargo run --example badge_service`.\n   453\u2192#[cfg(feature = \"example\")]\n   454\u2192#[tokio::main]\n   455\u2192async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   456\u2192    // Use dotenv if available.\n   457\u2192    dotenvy::dotenv().ok();\n   458\u2192    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192    let db_url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n   461\u2192        .expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set (e.g. postgres://user:pw@localhost/web_social)\");\n   462\u2192\n   463\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Infrastructure \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   464\u2192    let event_store = Arc::new(EventStore::new(&db_url, 5).await?);\n   465\u2192    let dispatcher = Dispatcher::new(1024);\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192    let badge_repo = Arc::new(PgBadgeRepository::new(event_store.pool.clone()));\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Start service \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   470\u2192    let service = Arc::new(BadgeAwardService::new(\n   471\u2192        badge_repo,\n   472\u2192        dispatcher.clone(),\n   473\u2192        event_store.clone(),\n   474\u2192    ));\n   475\u2192    let handle = service.clone().spawn();\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192    // \u2500\u2500 Emit demo event \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   478\u2192    let demo_event = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n   479\u2192        QuizSubmitted {\n   480\u2192            quiz_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   481\u2192            learner_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   482\u2192            score: 96.5,\n   483\u2192        },\n   484\u2192    ));\n   485\u2192    event_store.append(&demo_event).await?;\n   486\u2192    dispatcher.publish(demo_event).await;\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192    // Wait for service to process event.\n   489\u2192    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192    // Cancel task gracefully (development only).\n   492\u2192    handle.abort();\n   493\u2192    Ok(())\n   494\u2192}\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   497\u2192//  Tests\n   498\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   499\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   500\u2192mod tests {\n   501\u2192    use super::*;\n   502\u2192    use sqlx::Executor;\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192    // Spawns a temporary in-memory Postgres (requires `postgres` feature\n   505\u2192    // of `sqlx-test` or a running database in CI).\n   506\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   507\u2192    async fn badge_award_flow_happy_path() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   508\u2192        // Skip test when DATABASE_URL not provided; this allows `cargo test`\n   509\u2192        // without database.\n   510\u2192        let db_url = match std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\") {\n   511\u2192            Ok(v) => v,\n   512\u2192            Err(_) => return Ok(()),\n   513\u2192        };\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192        // Prepare schema (idempotent).\n   516\u2192        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   517\u2192            .max_connections(1)\n   518\u2192            .connect(&db_url)\n   519\u2192            .await?;\n   520\u2192        pool.execute(\n   521\u2192            r#\"\n   522\u2192            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS event_store (\n   523\u2192                id UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n   524\u2192                occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n   525\u2192                event_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n   526\u2192                payload JSONB NOT NULL\n   527\u2192            );\n   528\u2192\n   529\u2192            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learner_badges (\n   530\u2192                learner_id UUID NOT NULL,\n   531\u2192                badge_id UUID NOT NULL,\n   532\u2192                awarded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n   533\u2192                reason TEXT NOT NULL,\n   534\u2192                PRIMARY KEY (learner_id, badge_id)\n   535\u2192            );\n   536\u2192            \"#,\n   537\u2192        )\n   538\u2192        .await?;\n   539\u2192\n   540\u2192        let event_store = Arc::new(EventStore { pool: pool.clone() });\n   541\u2192        let dispatcher = Dispatcher::new(16);\n   542\u2192        let badge_repo = Arc::new(PgBadgeRepository::new(pool.clone()));\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192        let service = Arc::new(BadgeAwardService::new(\n   545\u2192            badge_repo.clone(),\n   546\u2192            dispatcher.clone(),\n   547\u2192            event_store.clone(),\n   548\u2192        ));\n   549\u2192        let _handle = service.clone().spawn();\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192        let learner_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   552\u2192        let quiz_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   553\u2192\n   554\u2192        // Emit qualifying quiz submission.\n   555\u2192        let env = EventEnvelope::new(DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(\n   556\u2192            QuizSubmitted {\n   557\u2192                quiz_id,\n   558\u2192                learner_id,\n   559\u2192                score: 97.0,\n   560\u2192            },\n   561\u2192        ));\n   562\u2192        event_store.append(&env).await?;\n   563\u2192        dispatcher.publish(env.clone()).await;\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192        // Wait for async processing.\n   566\u2192        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(600)).await;\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192        // Verify badge persisted.\n   569\u2192        let badges = sqlx::query!(\n   570\u2192            \"SELECT badge_id FROM learner_badges WHERE learner_id = $1\",\n   571\u2192            learner_id\n   572\u2192        )\n   573\u2192        .fetch_all(pool.as_ref())\n   574\u2192        .await?;\n   575\u2192\n   576\u2192        assert_eq!(badges.len(), 1);\n   577\u2192        Ok(())\n   578\u2192    }\n   579\u2192}\n   580\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
