"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192// src/module_84.txt\n     3\u2192//\n     4\u2192// Eventing module for \u201cEduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub\u201d.\n     5\u2192// This file defines strongly-typed domain events, wraps them in a CloudEvent\n     6\u2192// compliant envelope, and ships them to a message broker (NATS in this case).\n     7\u2192//\n     8\u2192// The code is production-grade, async-safe, and can be reused by any service\n     9\u2192// in the ecosystem that needs to emit or consume events generated by the core\n    10\u2192// API layer.\n    11\u2192//\n    12\u2192// Dependencies (Cargo.toml):\n    13\u2192// --------------------------\n    14\u2192// [dependencies]\n    15\u2192// async-nats   = \"0.30\"\n    16\u2192// async-trait  = \"0.1\"\n    17\u2192// chrono       = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\n    18\u2192// serde        = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n    19\u2192// serde_json   = \"1.0\"\n    20\u2192// thiserror    = \"1.0\"\n    21\u2192// tracing      = \"0.1\"\n    22\u2192// uuid         = { version = \"1\", features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    25\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    26\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    27\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    28\u2192use tracing::{error, instrument, warn};\n    29\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    32\u2192// Domain-level events\n    33\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/// LearningPulseCreated is fired the moment a teacher posts a new \u201clearning pulse\u201d.\n    36\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    37\u2192pub struct LearningPulseCreated {\n    38\u2192    pub pulse_id: Uuid,\n    39\u2192    pub teacher_id: Uuid,\n    40\u2192    pub course_id: Uuid,\n    41\u2192    pub title: String,\n    42\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/// StudentReplySubmitted is emitted when a student uploads a reply to a pulse.\n    46\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    47\u2192pub struct StudentReplySubmitted {\n    48\u2192    pub reply_id: Uuid,\n    49\u2192    pub student_id: Uuid,\n    50\u2192    pub pulse_id: Uuid,\n    51\u2192    pub submitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192/// QuizSubmitted is triggered once a student has finalised a quiz attempt.\n    55\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    56\u2192pub struct QuizSubmitted {\n    57\u2192    pub quiz_id: Uuid,\n    58\u2192    pub student_id: Uuid,\n    59\u2192    pub attempt_id: Uuid,\n    60\u2192    pub score: Option<f32>,\n    61\u2192    pub submitted_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192/// Enum wrapper that allows serialising / deserialising heterogeneous events.\n    65\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    66\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"attributes\")]\n    67\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    68\u2192    #[serde(rename = \"learning_pulse.created\")]\n    69\u2192    LearningPulseCreated(LearningPulseCreated),\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    #[serde(rename = \"student_reply.submitted\")]\n    72\u2192    StudentReplySubmitted(StudentReplySubmitted),\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192    #[serde(rename = \"quiz.submitted\")]\n    75\u2192    QuizSubmitted(QuizSubmitted),\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    79\u2192// CloudEvent envelope\n    80\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192/// Minimal CloudEvent 1.0 implementation tailored to our needs.\n    83\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    84\u2192pub struct CloudEvent {\n    85\u2192    /// Unique event id.\n    86\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192    /// Identifies the organisation or service emitting the event, e.g. \"auth-service\".\n    89\u2192    pub source: String,\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    /// Must be `\"1.0\"` according to the spec.\n    92\u2192    pub specversion: &'static str,\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192    /// Event type, e.g. `\"learning_pulse.created\"`.\n    95\u2192    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    96\u2192    pub event_type: String,\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    /// MIME type of `data` (always `application/json` here).\n    99\u2192    pub datacontenttype: &'static str,\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192    /// RFC 3339 timestamp.\n   102\u2192    pub time: DateTime<Utc>,\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    /// Optional subject\u2014left for downstream services that need multi-tenant routing.\n   105\u2192    pub subject: Option<String>,\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    /// Actual domain event payload.\n   108\u2192    pub data: DomainEvent,\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192impl CloudEvent {\n   112\u2192    /// Wraps a `DomainEvent` in a CloudEvent envelope.\n   113\u2192    pub fn new<S: Into<String>>(source: S, event: DomainEvent) -> Self {\n   114\u2192        let event_type = match &event {\n   115\u2192            DomainEvent::LearningPulseCreated(_) => \"learning_pulse.created\",\n   116\u2192            DomainEvent::StudentReplySubmitted(_) => \"student_reply.submitted\",\n   117\u2192            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(_) => \"quiz.submitted\",\n   118\u2192        }\n   119\u2192        .to_owned();\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192        Self {\n   122\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   123\u2192            source: source.into(),\n   124\u2192            specversion: \"1.0\",\n   125\u2192            event_type,\n   126\u2192            datacontenttype: \"application/json\",\n   127\u2192            time: Utc::now(),\n   128\u2192            subject: None,\n   129\u2192            data: event,\n   130\u2192        }\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   135\u2192// Message-bus abstraction\n   136\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192/// Generic, async event-bus interface.\n   139\u2192///\n   140\u2192/// By using `async_trait` we keep the call-sites ergonomic while retaining the\n   141\u2192/// option to plug in any broker\u2014NATS, Kafka, Redis Streams, or Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY.\n   142\u2192#[async_trait]\n   143\u2192pub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n   144\u2192    /// Publishes a CloudEvent to the underlying transport.\n   145\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), EventBusError>;\n   146\u2192}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192/// Runtime errors that may occur while communicating with the event bus.\n   149\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   150\u2192pub enum EventBusError {\n   151\u2192    #[error(\"JSON serialisation failed: {0}\")]\n   152\u2192    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    #[error(\"NATS I/O error: {0}\")]\n   155\u2192    Nats(#[from] async_nats::Error),\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    #[error(\"transport layer closed unexpectedly\")]\n   158\u2192    Closed,\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    #[error(\"unknown error: {0}\")]\n   161\u2192    Other(#[from] Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync>),\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   165\u2192// NATS implementation\n   166\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192/// Concrete `EventBus` backed by a NATS subject hierarchy.\n   169\u2192///\n   170\u2192/// Events are published under `<prefix>.<event_type>`; e.g.\n   171\u2192/// `edupulse.learning_pulse.created`.\n   172\u2192pub struct NatsEventBus {\n   173\u2192    client: async_nats::Client,\n   174\u2192    subject_prefix: String,\n   175\u2192}\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192impl NatsEventBus {\n   178\u2192    /// Connects to a NATS server and returns an `EventBus` implementation.\n   179\u2192    pub async fn connect<S: Into<String>>(\n   180\u2192        nats_url: &str,\n   181\u2192        subject_prefix: S,\n   182\u2192    ) -> Result<Self, async_nats::Error> {\n   183\u2192        let client = async_nats::connect(nats_url).await?;\n   184\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   185\u2192            client,\n   186\u2192            subject_prefix: subject_prefix.into(),\n   187\u2192        })\n   188\u2192    }\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192    #[inline]\n   191\u2192    fn to_subject(&self, event_type: &str) -> String {\n   192\u2192        format!(\"{}.{}\", self.subject_prefix, event_type.replace('/', \".\"))\n   193\u2192    }\n   194\u2192}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192#[async_trait]\n   197\u2192impl EventBus for NatsEventBus {\n   198\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, event), fields(event_type = %event.event_type))]\n   199\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   200\u2192        let subject = self.to_subject(&event.event_type);\n   201\u2192        let bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&event)?;\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192        // The underlying client uses at-most-once semantics. For financial\n   204\u2192        // operations we would switch to JetStream with persistent storage.\n   205\u2192        self.client.publish(subject, bytes.into()).await?;\n   206\u2192        Ok(())\n   207\u2192    }\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   211\u2192// High-level publisher fa\u00e7ade\n   212\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192/// Convenience wrapper that exposes explicit functions for each domain event.\n   215\u2192///\n   216\u2192/// This keeps controller code highly readable:\n   217\u2192/// `publisher.student_reply_submitted(payload).await?;`\n   218\u2192pub struct EventPublisher<B: EventBus> {\n   219\u2192    bus: B,\n   220\u2192    source: String,\n   221\u2192}\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192impl<B: EventBus> EventPublisher<B> {\n   224\u2192    pub fn new<S: Into<String>>(bus: B, source: S) -> Self {\n   225\u2192        Self {\n   226\u2192            bus,\n   227\u2192            source: source.into(),\n   228\u2192        }\n   229\u2192    }\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   232\u2192    pub async fn learning_pulse_created(\n   233\u2192        &self,\n   234\u2192        payload: LearningPulseCreated,\n   235\u2192    ) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   236\u2192        let ev =\n   237\u2192            CloudEvent::new(self.source.clone(), DomainEvent::LearningPulseCreated(payload));\n   238\u2192        self.bus.publish(ev).await\n   239\u2192    }\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   242\u2192    pub async fn student_reply_submitted(\n   243\u2192        &self,\n   244\u2192        payload: StudentReplySubmitted,\n   245\u2192    ) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   246\u2192        let ev =\n   247\u2192            CloudEvent::new(self.source.clone(), DomainEvent::StudentReplySubmitted(payload));\n   248\u2192        self.bus.publish(ev).await\n   249\u2192    }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   252\u2192    pub async fn quiz_submitted(&self, payload: QuizSubmitted) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   253\u2192        let ev = CloudEvent::new(self.source.clone(), DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted(payload));\n   254\u2192        self.bus.publish(ev).await\n   255\u2192    }\n   256\u2192}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   259\u2192// Unit tests\n   260\u2192// -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   263\u2192mod tests {\n   264\u2192    use super::*;\n   265\u2192    use futures::executor::block_on;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    /// Simple in-memory event-bus used to assert that we can publish events\n   268\u2192    /// without hitting a real NATS instance. It captures the last message so\n   269\u2192    /// that assertions can be made on the envelope contents.\n   270\u2192    struct DummyBus {\n   271\u2192        last_event: std::sync::Mutex<Option<CloudEvent>>,\n   272\u2192    }\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192    impl DummyBus {\n   275\u2192        fn new() -> Self {\n   276\u2192            Self {\n   277\u2192                last_event: std::sync::Mutex::new(None),\n   278\u2192            }\n   279\u2192        }\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   283\u2192    impl EventBus for DummyBus {\n   284\u2192        async fn publish(&self, event: CloudEvent) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   285\u2192            *self\n   286\u2192                .last_event\n   287\u2192                .lock()\n   288\u2192                .expect(\"mutex poisoned\") = Some(event);\n   289\u2192            Ok(())\n   290\u2192        }\n   291\u2192    }\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192    #[test]\n   294\u2192    fn publisher_emits_correct_event_type() {\n   295\u2192        let bus = DummyBus::new();\n   296\u2192        let publisher = EventPublisher::new(bus, \"unit-test\");\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192        let pulse_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   299\u2192        let teacher_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   300\u2192        let course_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192        let payload = LearningPulseCreated {\n   303\u2192            pulse_id,\n   304\u2192            teacher_id,\n   305\u2192            course_id,\n   306\u2192            title: \"The Unit Circle Challenge\".into(),\n   307\u2192            created_at: Utc::now(),\n   308\u2192        };\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192        block_on(async {\n   311\u2192            publisher\n   312\u2192                .learning_pulse_created(payload.clone())\n   313\u2192                .await\n   314\u2192                .expect(\"publish failed\");\n   315\u2192        });\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192        let evt = publisher\n   318\u2192            .bus\n   319\u2192            .last_event\n   320\u2192            .lock()\n   321\u2192            .unwrap()\n   322\u2192            .clone()\n   323\u2192            .expect(\"no event captured\");\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192        match evt.data {\n   326\u2192            DomainEvent::LearningPulseCreated(inner) => {\n   327\u2192                assert_eq!(inner.pulse_id, pulse_id);\n   328\u2192                assert_eq!(evt.event_type, \"learning_pulse.created\");\n   329\u2192            }\n   330\u2192            _ => panic!(\"unexpected event\"),\n   331\u2192        }\n   332\u2192    }\n   333\u2192}\n   334\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
81. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_77.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_77.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Outbox & Domain-Event infrastructure for EduPulse Live.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! This module demonstrates a pragmatic implementation of the\n     7\u2192//!  \u2022 domain\u2010event pattern,\n     8\u2192//!  \u2022 transactional outbox,\n     9\u2192//!  \u2022 asynchronous relay into a broker (e.g. NATS, Rabbit, Kafka),\n    10\u2192//! while keeping the core API synchronous and highly responsive.\n    11\u2192//\n    12\u2192//! The implementation follows EduPulse\u2019s architecture guidelines: Service\n    13\u2192//! layer + Repository pattern, backed by a PostgreSQL database via `sqlx`.\n    14\u2192//!\n    15\u2192//! NOTE: In a real workspace this file would live under `src/infra/\u2026`, but the\n    16\u2192//!       challenge requires the path `src/module_77.txt`.  The extension is left\n    17\u2192//!       unchanged for demonstration purposes, yet `cargo` happily compiles it\n    18\u2192//!       because Rust only considers the `mod` system, not the filename suffix.\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192use std::time::Duration;\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    23\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    24\u2192use futures::{stream::FuturesUnordered, StreamExt};\n    25\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    26\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgRow, query, query_as, PgPool, Postgres, Row, Transaction};\n    27\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    28\u2192use tokio::{\n    29\u2192    select,\n    30\u2192    sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender},\n    31\u2192    task::JoinHandle,\n    32\u2192    time,\n    33\u2192};\n    34\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    35\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192const MAX_RETRY_COUNT: i16 = 5;\n    38\u2192const BATCH_SIZE: i64 = 100;\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192/// High-level domain events used throughout EduPulse.\n    41\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    42\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"data\")]\n    43\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    44\u2192    /// A new learning pulse was published by an instructor.\n    45\u2192    PulseCreated {\n    46\u2192        pulse_id: Uuid,\n    47\u2192        author_id: Uuid,\n    48\u2192        title: String,\n    49\u2192        published_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    50\u2192    },\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    /// A learner submitted an answer to a pulse.\n    53\u2192    PulseAnswered {\n    54\u2192        pulse_id: Uuid,\n    55\u2192        answer_id: Uuid,\n    56\u2192        student_id: Uuid,\n    57\u2192        answered_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    58\u2192    },\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192    /// A badge has been awarded.\n    61\u2192    BadgeAwarded {\n    62\u2192        badge_id: Uuid,\n    63\u2192        user_id: Uuid,\n    64\u2192        reason: String,\n    65\u2192        awarded_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    66\u2192    },\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192/// Envelope that wraps any [`DomainEvent`] together with outbox metadata.\n    70\u2192///\n    71\u2192/// This allows fault-tolerant, ordered replay while keeping the event payload\n    72\u2192/// opaque to the infrastructure layer.\n    73\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    74\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n    75\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    76\u2192    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    77\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    78\u2192    pub event: DomainEvent,\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192/// Database representation of an outbox message.\n    82\u2192///\n    83\u2192/// The table is expected to look like:\n    84\u2192///\n    85\u2192/// ```sql\n    86\u2192/// CREATE TABLE outbox (\n    87\u2192///     id              uuid      PRIMARY KEY,\n    88\u2192///     aggregate_id    uuid      NOT NULL,\n    89\u2192///     event_type      text      NOT NULL,\n    90\u2192///     payload         jsonb     NOT NULL,\n    91\u2192///     occurred_at     timestamptz NOT NULL,\n    92\u2192///     retries         smallint     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    93\u2192///     locked_until    timestamptz,\n    94\u2192///     delivered_at    timestamptz\n    95\u2192/// );\n    96\u2192/// CREATE INDEX idx_outbox_retries ON outbox (retries);\n    97\u2192/// CREATE INDEX idx_outbox_pending ON outbox (delivered_at) WHERE delivered_at IS NULL;\n    98\u2192/// ```\n    99\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   100\u2192struct OutboxRow {\n   101\u2192    id: Uuid,\n   102\u2192    aggregate_id: Uuid,\n   103\u2192    event_type: String,\n   104\u2192    payload: serde_json::Value,\n   105\u2192    occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   106\u2192    retries: i16,\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192impl OutboxRow {\n   110\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip_all)]\n   111\u2192    fn from_envelope(envelope: EventEnvelope) -> sqlx::Result<Self> {\n   112\u2192        let event_type = match &envelope.event {\n   113\u2192            DomainEvent::PulseCreated { .. } => \"PulseCreated\",\n   114\u2192            DomainEvent::PulseAnswered { .. } => \"PulseAnswered\",\n   115\u2192            DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded { .. } => \"BadgeAwarded\",\n   116\u2192        }\n   117\u2192        .to_string();\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   120\u2192            id: envelope.id,\n   121\u2192            aggregate_id: envelope.aggregate_id,\n   122\u2192            event_type,\n   123\u2192            payload: serde_json::to_value(&envelope.event)?,\n   124\u2192            occurred_at: envelope.occurred_at,\n   125\u2192            retries: 0,\n   126\u2192        })\n   127\u2192    }\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192/// Errors originating in the outbox layer.\n   131\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   132\u2192pub enum OutboxError {\n   133\u2192    #[error(\"Persistence failure: {0}\")]\n   134\u2192    Persistence(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    #[error(\"Serialization failure: {0}\")]\n   137\u2192    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192    #[error(\"Channel closed\")]\n   140\u2192    ChannelClosed,\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192/// Abstraction over anything that can store events in the outbox.\n   144\u2192#[async_trait]\n   145\u2192pub trait OutboxRepository {\n   146\u2192    async fn save(\n   147\u2192        &self,\n   148\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   149\u2192        envelope: EventEnvelope,\n   150\u2192    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError>;\n   151\u2192}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192/// PostgreSQL implementation of the outbox repository.\n   154\u2192pub struct PgOutboxRepository {\n   155\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   156\u2192}\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192impl PgOutboxRepository {\n   159\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   160\u2192        Self { pool }\n   161\u2192    }\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192#[async_trait]\n   165\u2192impl OutboxRepository for PgOutboxRepository {\n   166\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip_all, fields(event_id=%envelope.id))]\n   167\u2192    async fn save(\n   168\u2192        &self,\n   169\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   170\u2192        envelope: EventEnvelope,\n   171\u2192    ) -> Result<(), OutboxError> {\n   172\u2192        let row = OutboxRow::from_envelope(envelope)?;\n   173\u2192        query!(\n   174\u2192            r#\"\n   175\u2192            INSERT INTO outbox (id, aggregate_id, event_type, payload, occurred_at)\n   176\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n   177\u2192            \"#,\n   178\u2192            row.id,\n   179\u2192            row.aggregate_id,\n   180\u2192            row.event_type,\n   181\u2192            row.payload,\n   182\u2192            row.occurred_at,\n   183\u2192        )\n   184\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   185\u2192        .await?;\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192        Ok(())\n   188\u2192    }\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192/// Contract for anything that can publish events from the outbox to an external\n   192\u2192/// broker (NATS, RabbitMQ, Kafka, \u2026).\n   193\u2192#[async_trait]\n   194\u2192pub trait EventPublisher {\n   195\u2192    async fn publish(&self, envelope: EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>;\n   196\u2192}\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   199\u2192/// Background task that continuously pulls pending messages, publishes them,\n   200\u2192/// and marks them as delivered (or retries with exponential back-off).\n   201\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   202\u2192pub struct OutboxRelay<P> {\n   203\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   204\u2192    publisher: P,\n   205\u2192    interval: Duration,\n   206\u2192    concurrency: usize,\n   207\u2192}\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192impl<P> OutboxRelay<P>\n   210\u2192where\n   211\u2192    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync + 'static,\n   212\u2192{\n   213\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool, publisher: P) -> Self {\n   214\u2192        Self {\n   215\u2192            pool,\n   216\u2192            publisher,\n   217\u2192            interval: Duration::from_secs(5),\n   218\u2192            concurrency: 8,\n   219\u2192        }\n   220\u2192    }\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192    pub fn with_interval(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self {\n   223\u2192        self.interval = duration;\n   224\u2192        self\n   225\u2192    }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192    pub fn with_concurrency(mut self, n: usize) -> Self {\n   228\u2192        self.concurrency = n;\n   229\u2192        self\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    /// Spawns the relay as an independent Tokio task and returns the join handle.\n   233\u2192    pub fn spawn(self) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n   234\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   235\u2192            self.run().await;\n   236\u2192        })\n   237\u2192    }\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    #[instrument(name = \"outbox_relay\", skip(self))]\n   240\u2192    pub async fn run(self) {\n   241\u2192        info!(\"OutboxRelay started; interval = {:?}\", self.interval);\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel::<EventEnvelope>(self.concurrency * 2);\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192        // Spawn worker tasks\n   246\u2192        let mut workers: FuturesUnordered<_> = (0..self.concurrency)\n   247\u2192            .map(|idx| {\n   248\u2192                let rx = rx.clone();\n   249\u2192                let publisher = &self.publisher;\n   250\u2192                tokio::spawn(worker_task(idx, rx, publisher))\n   251\u2192            })\n   252\u2192            .collect();\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192        drop(rx); // allow graceful exit when all senders are dropped\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192        let mut ticker = time::interval(self.interval);\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192        loop {\n   259\u2192            select! {\n   260\u2192                _ = ticker.tick() => {\n   261\u2192                    match self.fetch_pending(&tx).await {\n   262\u2192                        Ok(count) => {\n   263\u2192                            debug!(%count, \"Fetched pending events\");\n   264\u2192                        }\n   265\u2192                        Err(e) => {\n   266\u2192                            error!(error = ?e, \"Failed to fetch pending events\");\n   267\u2192                        }\n   268\u2192                    }\n   269\u2192                }\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192                // Drain finished workers to avoid memory leak of JoinHandles.\n   272\u2192                Some(res) = workers.next() => {\n   273\u2192                    if let Err(e) = res {\n   274\u2192                        error!(error = ?e, \"Worker crashed\");\n   275\u2192                    }\n   276\u2192                }\n   277\u2192            }\n   278\u2192        }\n   279\u2192    }\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192    /// Fetches pending events and sends them to the worker channel.\n   282\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(batch_size = BATCH_SIZE))]\n   283\u2192    async fn fetch_pending(&self, tx_chan: &Sender<EventEnvelope>) -> Result<usize, OutboxError> {\n   284\u2192        let mut conn = self.pool.acquire().await?;\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192        // Lock rows to prevent double delivery from multiple nodes.\n   287\u2192        let rows: Vec<PgRow> = query(\n   288\u2192            r#\"\n   289\u2192            UPDATE outbox\n   290\u2192            SET locked_until = NOW() + INTERVAL '1 minute'\n   291\u2192            WHERE id IN (\n   292\u2192                SELECT id\n   293\u2192                FROM outbox\n   294\u2192                WHERE delivered_at IS NULL\n   295\u2192                  AND (locked_until IS NULL OR locked_until < NOW())\n   296\u2192                  AND retries < $1\n   297\u2192                ORDER BY occurred_at\n   298\u2192                LIMIT $2\n   299\u2192                FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED\n   300\u2192            )\n   301\u2192            RETURNING id, aggregate_id, event_type, payload, occurred_at, retries\n   302\u2192            \"#,\n   303\u2192        )\n   304\u2192        .bind(MAX_RETRY_COUNT)\n   305\u2192        .bind(BATCH_SIZE)\n   306\u2192        .fetch_all(&mut *conn)\n   307\u2192        .await?;\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192        let count = rows.len();\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192        for row in rows {\n   312\u2192            let envelope = EventEnvelope {\n   313\u2192                id: row.get(\"id\"),\n   314\u2192                aggregate_id: row.get(\"aggregate_id\"),\n   315\u2192                occurred_at: row.get(\"occurred_at\"),\n   316\u2192                event: serde_json::from_value::<DomainEvent>(row.get(\"payload\"))?,\n   317\u2192            };\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192            tx_chan.send(envelope).await.map_err(|_| OutboxError::ChannelClosed)?;\n   320\u2192        }\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192        Ok(count)\n   323\u2192    }\n   324\u2192}\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192/// Background worker that receives envelopes and publishes them through the\n   327\u2192/// provided `EventPublisher`.\n   328\u2192#[instrument(skip(rx, publisher))]\n   329\u2192async fn worker_task<P>(idx: usize, mut rx: Receiver<EventEnvelope>, publisher: &P)\n   330\u2192where\n   331\u2192    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync + 'static,\n   332\u2192{\n   333\u2192    while let Some(envelope) = rx.recv().await {\n   334\u2192        match publisher.publish(envelope.clone()).await {\n   335\u2192            Ok(()) => {\n   336\u2192                debug!(worker = idx, event_id = %envelope.id, \"Published event\");\n   337\u2192                // Mark as delivered\n   338\u2192                // We perform a fire-and-forget update; eventual consistency is enough here.\n   339\u2192                if let Err(e) = mark_delivered(envelope.id).await {\n   340\u2192                    warn!(error=?e, \"Failed to mark outbox row as delivered\");\n   341\u2192                }\n   342\u2192            }\n   343\u2192            Err(e) => {\n   344\u2192                error!(worker = idx, event_id = %envelope.id, error = ?e, \"Failed to publish event\");\n   345\u2192                if let Err(db_err) = increment_retries(envelope.id).await {\n   346\u2192                    error!(error = ?db_err, \"Failed to increment retry counter\");\n   347\u2192                }\n   348\u2192            }\n   349\u2192        }\n   350\u2192    }\n   351\u2192}\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192/// Updates `delivered_at` timestamp.\n   354\u2192async fn mark_delivered(event_id: Uuid) -> sqlx::Result<()> {\n   355\u2192    // Acquire from a global PgPool. In a real project we\u2019d inject it, but for\n   356\u2192    // this isolated file we rely on the `DATABASE_URL` environment variable and\n   357\u2192    // lazy connection init.\n   358\u2192    static POOL: once_cell::sync::Lazy<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::Lazy::new(|| {\n   359\u2192        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().expect(\"tokio rt\");\n   360\u2192        rt.block_on(async {\n   361\u2192            let url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n   362\u2192                .expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set for outbox relay\");\n   363\u2192            PgPool::connect(&url).await.expect(\"failed to connect\")\n   364\u2192        })\n   365\u2192    });\n   366\u2192\n   367\u2192    query!(\n   368\u2192        \"UPDATE outbox SET delivered_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1\",\n   369\u2192        event_id\n   370\u2192    )\n   371\u2192    .execute(&*POOL)\n   372\u2192    .await?;\n   373\u2192    Ok(())\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192/// Increments retry counter for failed deliveries.\n   377\u2192async fn increment_retries(event_id: Uuid) -> sqlx::Result<()> {\n   378\u2192    static POOL: once_cell::sync::Lazy<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::Lazy::new(|| {\n   379\u2192        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().expect(\"tokio rt\");\n   380\u2192        rt.block_on(async {\n   381\u2192            let url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n   382\u2192                .expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set for outbox relay\");\n   383\u2192            PgPool::connect(&url).await.expect(\"failed to connect\")\n   384\u2192        })\n   385\u2192    });\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192    query!(\n   388\u2192        \"UPDATE outbox SET retries = retries + 1 WHERE id = $1\",\n   389\u2192        event_id\n   390\u2192    )\n   391\u2192    .execute(&*POOL)\n   392\u2192    .await?;\n   393\u2192    Ok(())\n   394\u2192}\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   397\u2192/// Mock publisher for unit-testing \u2013 prints events to the console.\n   398\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   399\u2192pub struct StdoutPublisher;\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192#[async_trait]\n   402\u2192impl EventPublisher for StdoutPublisher {\n   403\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"info\", skip(self))]\n   404\u2192    async fn publish(&self, envelope: EventEnvelope) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {\n   405\u2192        println!(\"\ud83d\udd14 Publishing event: {envelope:#?}\");\n   406\u2192        Ok(())\n   407\u2192    }\n   408\u2192}\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   411\u2192/// Unit tests\n   412\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   413\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   414\u2192mod tests {\n   415\u2192    use super::*;\n   416\u2192    use chrono::TimeZone;\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   419\u2192    async fn serde_roundtrip() {\n   420\u2192        let original = EventEnvelope {\n   421\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   422\u2192            aggregate_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   423\u2192            occurred_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n   424\u2192            event: DomainEvent::PulseCreated {\n   425\u2192                pulse_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   426\u2192                author_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   427\u2192                title: \"Intro to Topology\".to_string(),\n   428\u2192                published_at: Utc::now(),\n   429\u2192            },\n   430\u2192        };\n   431\u2192\n   432\u2192        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&original).expect(\"serialize\");\n   433\u2192        let deserialized: EventEnvelope = serde_json::from_str(&json).expect(\"deserialize\");\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192        assert_eq!(original.id, deserialized.id);\n   436\u2192        match deserialized.event {\n   437\u2192            DomainEvent::PulseCreated { title, .. } => assert_eq!(title, \"Intro to Topology\"),\n   438\u2192            _ => unreachable!(),\n   439\u2192        }\n   440\u2192    }\n   441\u2192}\n   442\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
82. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_75.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_75.rs\n     3\u2192//! Event bus, store, and sample handlers for EduPulse Live  \ud83d\ude80\n     4\u2192//!\n     5\u2192//! This module provides a minimal yet production-grade, in-process event-bus\n     6\u2192//! accompanied by a Postgres-backed event store.  The bus is used by multiple\n     7\u2192//! back-end micro-services (search indexing, achievements, notifications, \u2026)\n     8\u2192//! that react on published domain events such as `LessonPublished` or\n     9\u2192//! `QuizSubmitted`.\n    10\u2192//!\n    11\u2192//! The implementation focuses on correctness, observability, and graceful\n    12\u2192//! shutdown while staying fully async (Tokio runtime).\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192use std::{\n    15\u2192    collections::HashMap,\n    16\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    17\u2192    time::{Duration, Instant},\n    18\u2192};\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192use anyhow::{Context, Result};\n    21\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    22\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    23\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    24\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    25\u2192use tokio::{\n    26\u2192    select,\n    27\u2192    sync::{broadcast, mpsc, oneshot, RwLock},\n    28\u2192    task::JoinHandle,\n    29\u2192};\n    30\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    31\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    34\u2192/// Domain events\n    35\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    38\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n    39\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    40\u2192    LessonPublished {\n    41\u2192        lesson_id: Uuid,\n    42\u2192        author_id: Uuid,\n    43\u2192        title: String,\n    44\u2192    },\n    45\u2192    QuizSubmitted {\n    46\u2192        quiz_id: Uuid,\n    47\u2192        student_id: Uuid,\n    48\u2192        score: u16,\n    49\u2192    },\n    50\u2192    PeerFeedbackGiven {\n    51\u2192        feedback_id: Uuid,\n    52\u2192        from_user_id: Uuid,\n    53\u2192        to_user_id: Uuid,\n    54\u2192        rating: u8,\n    55\u2192    },\n    56\u2192    BadgeAwarded {\n    57\u2192        badge_id: Uuid,\n    58\u2192        to_user_id: Uuid,\n    59\u2192        reason: String,\n    60\u2192    },\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192/// Envelope adds metadata required by infrastructure concerns such as ordering,\n    64\u2192/// idempotence, auditing, etc.\n    65\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    66\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n    67\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    68\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    69\u2192    pub payload: DomainEvent,\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192impl EventEnvelope {\n    73\u2192    pub fn new(event: DomainEvent) -> Self {\n    74\u2192        Self {\n    75\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    76\u2192            created_at: Utc::now(),\n    77\u2192            payload: event,\n    78\u2192        }\n    79\u2192    }\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    83\u2192/// Event store (Postgres) \u2013 append-only\n    84\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n    87\u2192pub struct PgEventStore {\n    88\u2192    pool: sqlx::Pool<sqlx::Postgres>,\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192impl PgEventStore {\n    92\u2192    pub async fn new(database_url: &str, max_connections: u32) -> Result<Self> {\n    93\u2192        let pool = sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions::new()\n    94\u2192            .max_connections(max_connections)\n    95\u2192            .connect(database_url)\n    96\u2192            .await\n    97\u2192            .context(\"Failed to establish Postgres connection pool for PgEventStore\")?;\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192        // Ensure the table exists. In production, use migrations.\n   100\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   101\u2192            r#\"\n   102\u2192            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_events (\n   103\u2192                id          UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n   104\u2192                created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n   105\u2192                event_type  TEXT        NOT NULL,\n   106\u2192                payload     JSONB       NOT NULL\n   107\u2192            )\n   108\u2192        \"#,\n   109\u2192        )\n   110\u2192        .execute(&pool)\n   111\u2192        .await\n   112\u2192        .context(\"Failed to ensure domain_events table\")?;\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192        Ok(Self { pool })\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192    /// Append an event in a durable, transactional way.\n   118\u2192    pub async fn append(&self, evt: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   119\u2192        let event_type = match &evt.payload {\n   120\u2192            DomainEvent::LessonPublished { .. } => \"lesson_published\",\n   121\u2192            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted { .. } => \"quiz_submitted\",\n   122\u2192            DomainEvent::PeerFeedbackGiven { .. } => \"peer_feedback_given\",\n   123\u2192            DomainEvent::BadgeAwarded { .. } => \"badge_awarded\",\n   124\u2192        };\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   127\u2192            r#\"\n   128\u2192            INSERT INTO domain_events (id, created_at, event_type, payload)\n   129\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n   130\u2192        \"#,\n   131\u2192        )\n   132\u2192        .bind(evt.id)\n   133\u2192        .bind(evt.created_at)\n   134\u2192        .bind(event_type)\n   135\u2192        .bind(sqlx::types::Json(evt))\n   136\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   137\u2192        .await\n   138\u2192        .context(\"Failed to persist domain event\")?;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192        Ok(())\n   141\u2192    }\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   145\u2192/// Event bus \u2013 fan-out (Tokio broadcast + mpsc)\n   146\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192#[async_trait]\n   149\u2192pub trait EventHandler: Send + Sync + 'static {\n   150\u2192    /// Handle an incoming event.\n   151\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()>;\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192/// How handlers communicate their lifecycle termination back to the bus.\n   155\u2192struct HandlerControl {\n   156\u2192    /// Ask the handler to gracefully shutdown.\n   157\u2192    stop_tx: oneshot::Sender<()>,\n   158\u2192    /// `JoinHandle` that finishes when the handler exits.\n   159\u2192    join: JoinHandle<()>,\n   160\u2192}\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192/// An in-memory event bus that allows dynamic registration of handlers. Each\n   163\u2192/// handler runs on its own task and receives events through a dedicated\n   164\u2192/// `mpsc::Receiver`.\n   165\u2192pub struct EventBus {\n   166\u2192    sender: broadcast::Sender<EventEnvelope>,\n   167\u2192    handlers: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, HandlerControl>>>,\n   168\u2192    store: PgEventStore,\n   169\u2192}\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192impl EventBus {\n   172\u2192    pub fn new(store: PgEventStore, channel_capacity: usize) -> Self {\n   173\u2192        let (sender, _) = broadcast::channel(channel_capacity);\n   174\u2192        Self {\n   175\u2192            sender,\n   176\u2192            handlers: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n   177\u2192            store,\n   178\u2192        }\n   179\u2192    }\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192    /// Publish an event: persist it first, then fan-out to handlers. If a\n   182\u2192    /// handler is lagging behind, the broadcast channel drops the event; we log\n   183\u2192    /// a warning so operations can react (in practice, use Kafka / NATS).\n   184\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   185\u2192    pub async fn publish(&self, evt: DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n   186\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(evt);\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192        self.store\n   189\u2192            .append(&envelope)\n   190\u2192            .await\n   191\u2192            .context(\"Persisting event\")?;\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192        match self.sender.send(envelope.clone()) {\n   194\u2192            Ok(cnt) => {\n   195\u2192                debug!(\"Event published to {cnt} handlers\");\n   196\u2192            }\n   197\u2192            Err(err) => {\n   198\u2192                warn!(?err, \"No active handlers \u2013 event not delivered\");\n   199\u2192            }\n   200\u2192        }\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192        Ok(())\n   203\u2192    }\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192    /// Register a new handler, returning an error when a duplicate key is\n   206\u2192    /// supplied. The handler\u2019s `key` serves as idempotent registration id.\n   207\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, handler))]\n   208\u2192    pub async fn register_handler<H>(&self, key: &str, handler: H) -> Result<()>\n   209\u2192    where\n   210\u2192        H: EventHandler,\n   211\u2192    {\n   212\u2192        let mut guard = self.handlers.write().await;\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192        if guard.contains_key(key) {\n   215\u2192            return Err(EventBusError::DuplicateHandler(key.to_owned()).into());\n   216\u2192        }\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192        let mut rx = self.sender.subscribe();\n   219\u2192        let (stop_tx, stop_rx) = oneshot::channel::<()>();\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192        let join: JoinHandle<()> = tokio::spawn(async move {\n   222\u2192            info!(\"Event handler `{key}` started\");\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192            // Buffer to avoid blocking broadcast when handler takes a while\n   225\u2192            let (evt_tx, mut evt_rx) = mpsc::channel::<EventEnvelope>(1024);\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192            // Fan-out from broadcast subscription to the internal buffer\n   228\u2192            let forwarder = tokio::spawn(async move {\n   229\u2192                loop {\n   230\u2192                    match rx.recv().await {\n   231\u2192                        Ok(evt) => {\n   232\u2192                            if evt_tx.send(evt).await.is_err() {\n   233\u2192                                break;\n   234\u2192                            }\n   235\u2192                        }\n   236\u2192                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(skipped)) => {\n   237\u2192                            warn!(\"Handler `{key}` skipped {skipped} messages\");\n   238\u2192                        }\n   239\u2192                        Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => break,\n   240\u2192                    }\n   241\u2192                }\n   242\u2192            });\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192            // Main handling loop\n   245\u2192            select! {\n   246\u2192                _ = async {\n   247\u2192                    while let Some(evt) = evt_rx.recv().await {\n   248\u2192                        if let Err(err) = handler.handle(evt).await {\n   249\u2192                            error!(?err, \"Handler `{key}` failed\");\n   250\u2192                        }\n   251\u2192                    }\n   252\u2192                } => {},\n   253\u2192                _ = stop_rx => {\n   254\u2192                    debug!(\"Handler `{key}` received shutdown signal\");\n   255\u2192                }\n   256\u2192            }\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192            forwarder.abort();\n   259\u2192            info!(\"Event handler `{key}` stopped\");\n   260\u2192        });\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192        guard.insert(\n   263\u2192            key.to_owned(),\n   264\u2192            HandlerControl {\n   265\u2192                stop_tx,\n   266\u2192                join,\n   267\u2192            },\n   268\u2192        );\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192        Ok(())\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    /// Ask all handlers to stop and wait for them to finish (within timeout).\n   274\u2192    pub async fn shutdown(&self, timeout: Duration) {\n   275\u2192        let mut guard = self.handlers.write().await;\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192        for (key, ctl) in guard.drain() {\n   278\u2192            if ctl.stop_tx.send(()).is_err() {\n   279\u2192                warn!(\"Handler `{key}` already terminated\");\n   280\u2192            }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192            let key_clone = key.clone();\n   283\u2192            match tokio::time::timeout(timeout, ctl.join).await {\n   284\u2192                Ok(_) => info!(\"Handler `{key_clone}` gracefully terminated\"),\n   285\u2192                Err(_) => warn!(\"Handler `{key_clone}` did not terminate in time\"),\n   286\u2192            }\n   287\u2192        }\n   288\u2192    }\n   289\u2192}\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   292\u2192pub enum EventBusError {\n   293\u2192    #[error(\"Handler with key `{0}` is already registered\")]\n   294\u2192    DuplicateHandler(String),\n   295\u2192}\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   298\u2192/// Sample handlers\n   299\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192/// Simulate search index updates (e.g., Elasticsearch/OpenSearch)\n   302\u2192pub struct SearchIndexer;\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192#[async_trait]\n   305\u2192impl EventHandler for SearchIndexer {\n   306\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   307\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   308\u2192        match &event.payload {\n   309\u2192            DomainEvent::LessonPublished { lesson_id, title, .. } => {\n   310\u2192                info!(%lesson_id, %title, \"Indexing lesson\");\n   311\u2192                // simulate latency\n   312\u2192                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(75)).await;\n   313\u2192            }\n   314\u2192            _ => {}\n   315\u2192        }\n   316\u2192        Ok(())\n   317\u2192    }\n   318\u2192}\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192/// Simulate achievement calculation service\n   321\u2192pub struct AchievementService;\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192#[async_trait]\n   324\u2192impl EventHandler for AchievementService {\n   325\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   326\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   327\u2192        match &event.payload {\n   328\u2192            DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted {\n   329\u2192                student_id,\n   330\u2192                score,\n   331\u2192                quiz_id,\n   332\u2192            } => {\n   333\u2192                info!(%student_id, %quiz_id, %score, \"Calculating achievements\");\n   334\u2192                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n   335\u2192            }\n   336\u2192            _ => {}\n   337\u2192        }\n   338\u2192        Ok(())\n   339\u2192    }\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192/// Simulate email / push notifications\n   343\u2192pub struct NotificationService;\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192#[async_trait]\n   346\u2192impl EventHandler for NotificationService {\n   347\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   348\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   349\u2192        info!(\"Sending notifications for event {:?}\", event.payload);\n   350\u2192        Ok(())\n   351\u2192    }\n   352\u2192}\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   355\u2192/// Demonstration entry point (can be removed in production binaries)\n   356\u2192/// --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192#[tokio::main]\n   359\u2192async fn main() -> Result<()> {\n   360\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   361\u2192    // Initialise observability & infrastructure\n   362\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   363\u2192    dotenvy::dotenv().ok();\n   364\u2192    tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n   365\u2192    let db_url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n   366\u2192        .unwrap_or_else(|_| \"postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost/edupulse\".into());\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192    let store = PgEventStore::new(&db_url, 5).await?;\n   369\u2192    let bus = Arc::new(EventBus::new(store, 512));\n   370\u2192\n   371\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   372\u2192    // Register handlers\n   373\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   374\u2192    bus.register_handler(\"search_indexer\", SearchIndexer).await?;\n   375\u2192    bus.register_handler(\"achievement_service\", AchievementService)\n   376\u2192        .await?;\n   377\u2192    bus.register_handler(\"notification_service\", NotificationService)\n   378\u2192        .await?;\n   379\u2192\n   380\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   381\u2192    // Simulate business flow\n   382\u2192    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   383\u2192    let t0 = Instant::now();\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192    bus.publish(DomainEvent::LessonPublished {\n   386\u2192        lesson_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   387\u2192        author_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   388\u2192        title: \"Traits in Rust \ud83e\udd80\".into(),\n   389\u2192    })\n   390\u2192    .await?;\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192    bus.publish(DomainEvent::QuizSubmitted {\n   393\u2192        quiz_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   394\u2192        student_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   395\u2192        score: 87,\n   396\u2192    })\n   397\u2192    .await?;\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192    // Give handlers time to process before shutdown\n   400\u2192    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n   401\u2192    bus.shutdown(Duration::from_secs(3)).await;\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192    info!(\"Demo finished in {:?}\", t0.elapsed());\n   404\u2192    Ok(())\n   405\u2192}\n   406\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Input

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_76.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Generic, production-grade event bus for EduPulse Live.\n     5\u2192//! ======================================================\n     6\u2192//! This module wires domain events to asynchronous handlers while persisting\n     7\u2192//! them into an append-only event store.  Designed to live inside the core API\n     8\u2192//! crate, it exposes a minimal fa\u00e7ade so that higher-level modules\n     9\u2192//! (lessons/quizzes/auth/etc.) can remain agnostic of the underlying message\n    10\u2192//! transport.\n    11\u2192//!\n    12\u2192//! Highlights\n    13\u2192//! ----------\n    14\u2192//! \u2022 Tokio-powered, back-pressure-aware broadcast channel  \n    15\u2192//! \u2022 Pluggable `EventStore` (in-memory + SQLx/Postgres ref. impl)  \n    16\u2192//! \u2022 Dynamic subscription registry using `async_trait` + `DashMap`  \n    17\u2192//! \u2022 JSON-serialised events for wire compatibility (RabbitMQ/NATS/etc.)  \n    18\u2192//! \u2022 Tracing-instrumented for observability\n    19\u2192//!\n    20\u2192//! Usage\n    21\u2192//! -----\n    22\u2192//! ```rust,no_run\n    23\u2192//! let event_bus = Arc::new(EventBus::new(InMemoryEventStore::default()));\n    24\u2192//! event_bus.start_worker_pool(4);                    // spawn background workers\n    25\u2192//! event_bus.register_handler(SearchIndexer::new()?); // Tantivy search handler\n    26\u2192//! event_bus.emit(DomainEvent::LessonPublished {...}).await?;\n    27\u2192//! ```\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192use std::{\n    30\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    31\u2192    time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH},\n    32\u2192};\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192use anyhow::{Context, Result};\n    35\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    36\u2192use dashmap::DashMap;\n    37\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    38\u2192use tokio::{\n    39\u2192    select,\n    40\u2192    spawn,\n    41\u2192    sync::{broadcast, mpsc},\n    42\u2192    task::JoinHandle,\n    43\u2192};\n    44\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    45\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192/// Default broadcast channel capacity (per Tokio docs, power of two for perf)\n    48\u2192const CHANNEL_CAPACITY: usize = 1 << 8;\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192/// All domain-level events that flow through the platform.\n    51\u2192///\n    52\u2192/// When this enum grows large, consider splitting variants into separate enums\n    53\u2192/// and boxing them in an `enum`/`trait object` for compile-time boundary\n    54\u2192/// management.\n    55\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    56\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"data\")]\n    57\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    58\u2192    LessonPublished {\n    59\u2192        lesson_id: Uuid,\n    60\u2192        author_id: Uuid,\n    61\u2192        title: String,\n    62\u2192        published_at: UnixTimestamp,\n    63\u2192    },\n    64\u2192    QuizSubmitted {\n    65\u2192        quiz_id: Uuid,\n    66\u2192        student_id: Uuid,\n    67\u2192        score: u8,\n    68\u2192        submitted_at: UnixTimestamp,\n    69\u2192    },\n    70\u2192    // --- truncated: add more events as needed ---\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/// Simple millisecond-precision unix timestamp wrapper.\n    74\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    75\u2192pub struct UnixTimestamp(u64);\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192impl Default for UnixTimestamp {\n    78\u2192    fn default() -> Self {\n    79\u2192        Self(\n    80\u2192            SystemTime::now()\n    81\u2192                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n    82\u2192                .expect(\"time went backwards\")\n    83\u2192                .as_millis() as u64,\n    84\u2192        )\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192/// Persistence fa\u00e7ade for storing domain events.\n    89\u2192///\n    90\u2192/// The event store is append-only and can be replayed for audits, projections\n    91\u2192/// or rebuilding read models.\n    92\u2192#[async_trait]\n    93\u2192pub trait EventStore: Send + Sync + 'static {\n    94\u2192    async fn append(&self, event: &DomainEvent) -> Result<()>;\n    95\u2192    async fn stream_all<E>(&self) -> Result<Vec<E>>\n    96\u2192    where\n    97\u2192        E: DeserializeOwned + Send + 'static;\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192/// In-memory reference implementation; suitable for tests / dev playground.\n   101\u2192#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n   102\u2192pub struct InMemoryEventStore {\n   103\u2192    events: tokio::sync::RwLock<Vec<Vec<u8>>>,\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192#[async_trait]\n   107\u2192impl EventStore for InMemoryEventStore {\n   108\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, event))]\n   109\u2192    async fn append(&self, event: &DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n   110\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(event).context(\"serialising event\")?;\n   111\u2192        self.events.write().await.push(payload);\n   112\u2192        Ok(())\n   113\u2192    }\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192    async fn stream_all<E>(&self) -> Result<Vec<E>>\n   116\u2192    where\n   117\u2192        E: DeserializeOwned + Send + 'static,\n   118\u2192    {\n   119\u2192        let raw = self.events.read().await.clone();\n   120\u2192        let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(raw.len());\n   121\u2192        for bytes in raw {\n   122\u2192            let evt = serde_json::from_slice::<E>(&bytes).context(\"deserialising\")?;\n   123\u2192            out.push(evt);\n   124\u2192        }\n   125\u2192        Ok(out)\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192/// Event handler contract.\n   130\u2192///\n   131\u2192/// Note: we purposefully consume by value (`self`) to allow stateful handlers to\n   132\u2192/// move into the async task when registered.\n   133\u2192#[async_trait]\n   134\u2192pub trait EventHandler: Send + Sync + 'static {\n   135\u2192    /// A human-readable name for observability.\n   136\u2192    fn name(&self) -> &'static str;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    /// Business logic invoked for each event.\n   139\u2192    async fn handle(&self, evt: DomainEvent) -> Result<()>;\n   140\u2192}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192/// Thread-safe, async event bus.\n   143\u2192#[derive(Debug)]\n   144\u2192pub struct EventBus<S: EventStore> {\n   145\u2192    tx: broadcast::Sender<DomainEvent>,\n   146\u2192    store: Arc<S>,\n   147\u2192    handlers: DashMap<&'static str, Box<dyn EventHandler>>,\n   148\u2192    /// Workers receive events from the broadcast channel and sequentially\n   149\u2192    /// deliver them to handlers (de-duplicated per handler).\n   150\u2192    worker_txs: Vec<mpsc::Sender<DomainEvent>>,\n   151\u2192}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192impl<S: EventStore> EventBus<S> {\n   154\u2192    pub fn new(store: S) -> Self {\n   155\u2192        let (tx, _rx) = broadcast::channel(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);\n   156\u2192        Self {\n   157\u2192            tx,\n   158\u2192            store: Arc::new(store),\n   159\u2192            handlers: DashMap::new(),\n   160\u2192            worker_txs: Vec::new(),\n   161\u2192        }\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    /// Emit a single event.\n   165\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   166\u2192    pub async fn emit(&self, evt: DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n   167\u2192        self.store.append(&evt).await?;\n   168\u2192        self.tx\n   169\u2192            .send(evt)\n   170\u2192            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"broadcast failure: {e}\"))?;\n   171\u2192        Ok(())\n   172\u2192    }\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    /// Register a handler; idempotent per handler name.\n   175\u2192    pub fn register_handler<H>(&self, handler: H)\n   176\u2192    where\n   177\u2192        H: EventHandler,\n   178\u2192    {\n   179\u2192        if self.handlers.insert(handler.name(), Box::new(handler)).is_some() {\n   180\u2192            warn!(\"handler {} replaced at runtime\", handler.name());\n   181\u2192        } else {\n   182\u2192            info!(\"handler {} registered\", handler.name());\n   183\u2192        }\n   184\u2192    }\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192    /// Spawn a worker pool that fans-out events to handlers.\n   187\u2192    ///\n   188\u2192    /// Each worker owns an mpsc channel (to avoid contention) and reads from\n   189\u2192    /// the *same* broadcast channel.  This guarantees at-least-once delivery to\n   190\u2192    /// each handler.\n   191\u2192    pub fn start_worker_pool(self: &Arc<Self>, workers: usize) -> Vec<JoinHandle<()>> {\n   192\u2192        let mut handles = Vec::with_capacity(workers);\n   193\u2192        for id in 0..workers {\n   194\u2192            let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel::<DomainEvent>(CHANNEL_CAPACITY);\n   195\u2192            self.worker_txs.push(tx);\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192            let bus = Arc::clone(self);\n   198\u2192            let mut broadcast_rx = bus.tx.subscribe();\n   199\u2192            handles.push(spawn(async move {\n   200\u2192                info!(worker = id, \"event worker started\");\n   201\u2192                loop {\n   202\u2192                    select! {\n   203\u2192                        Ok(evt) = broadcast_rx.recv() => {\n   204\u2192                            // fan-in to single queue\n   205\u2192                            if let Err(e) = rx.try_send(evt) {\n   206\u2192                                debug!(%e, worker = id, \"worker queue full; dropping\");\n   207\u2192                            }\n   208\u2192                        },\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192                        Some(evt) = rx.recv() => {\n   211\u2192                            for item in bus.handlers.iter() {\n   212\u2192                                let handler = item.value();\n   213\u2192                                let evt_clone = evt.clone();\n   214\u2192                                let h_name = handler.name();\n   215\u2192                                let res = handler.handle(evt_clone).await;\n   216\u2192                                if let Err(e) = res {\n   217\u2192                                    error!(%e, handler = h_name, \"event handler failed\");\n   218\u2192                                }\n   219\u2192                            }\n   220\u2192                        }\n   221\u2192                    }\n   222\u2192                }\n   223\u2192            }));\n   224\u2192        }\n   225\u2192        handles\n   226\u2192    }\n   227\u2192}\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   230\u2192/* ----------------------- Example Production Handler ----------------------- */\n   231\u2192/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192/// Tantivy-powered search indexer.\n   234\u2192///\n   235\u2192/// In a real deployment this would:\n   236\u2192/// 1. Open a persistent `Index` directory;\n   237\u2192/// 2. Map domain events to documents;\n   238\u2192/// 3. Commit in batches for throughput.\n   239\u2192pub struct SearchIndexer {\n   240\u2192    // index: tantivy::Index,\n   241\u2192}\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192impl SearchIndexer {\n   244\u2192    pub fn new() -> Result<Self> {\n   245\u2192        // let index = tantivy::Index::open_or_create(...)?;\n   246\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   247\u2192            // index,\n   248\u2192        })\n   249\u2192    }\n   250\u2192}\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192#[async_trait]\n   253\u2192impl EventHandler for SearchIndexer {\n   254\u2192    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n   255\u2192        \"search_indexer\"\n   256\u2192    }\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   259\u2192    async fn handle(&self, evt: DomainEvent) -> Result<()> {\n   260\u2192        match evt {\n   261\u2192            DomainEvent::LessonPublished {\n   262\u2192                lesson_id,\n   263\u2192                title,\n   264\u2192                author_id,\n   265\u2192                ..\n   266\u2192            } => {\n   267\u2192                debug!(\n   268\u2192                    ?lesson_id,\n   269\u2192                    %title,\n   270\u2192                    ?author_id,\n   271\u2192                    \"indexing lesson into search engine\"\n   272\u2192                );\n   273\u2192                // let mut writer = self.index.writer(50_000_000)?;\n   274\u2192                // writer.add_document(...);\n   275\u2192                // writer.commit()?;\n   276\u2192            }\n   277\u2192            _ => { /* ignore other events */ }\n   278\u2192        }\n   279\u2192        Ok(())\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192}\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   284\u2192/* ------------------------------- Unit Tests ------------------------------- */\n   285\u2192/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   288\u2192mod tests {\n   289\u2192    use super::*;\n   290\u2192    use std::time::Duration;\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192    #[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\", worker_threads = 2)]\n   293\u2192    async fn event_roundtrip() -> Result<()> {\n   294\u2192        tracing_subscriber::fmt::try_init().ok();\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192        let bus = Arc::new(EventBus::new(InMemoryEventStore::default()));\n   297\u2192        bus.register_handler(SearchIndexer::new()?);\n   298\u2192        bus.start_worker_pool(2);\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        let evt = DomainEvent::LessonPublished {\n   301\u2192            lesson_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   302\u2192            author_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   303\u2192            title: \"Rust for Edu-preneurs\".into(),\n   304\u2192            published_at: UnixTimestamp::default(),\n   305\u2192        };\n   306\u2192        bus.emit(evt.clone()).await?;\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192        // allow some processing time\n   309\u2192        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(250)).await;\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192        // ensure event persisted\n   312\u2192        let events: Vec<DomainEvent> = bus.store.stream_all().await?;\n   313\u2192        assert_eq!(events.len(), 1);\n   314\u2192        assert!(matches!(events[0], DomainEvent::LessonPublished { .. }));\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192        Ok(())\n   317\u2192    }\n   318\u2192}\n   319\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
