"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_80.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Event-bus fa\u00e7ade for EduPulse Live.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! This module encapsulates the AMQP message\u2013broker integration used by every\n     7\u2192//! bounded context (search indexing, notifications, achievement calculation,\n     8\u2192//! \u2026).  It exposes a thin, type-safe layer around lapin so domain services can\n     9\u2192//! publish/consume events without depending on AMQP details.  All events are\n    10\u2192//! JSON\u2010serialised envelopes carrying rich metadata for traceability and GDPR-\n    11\u2192//! compliant erasure.\n    12\u2192//\n    13\u2192//  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Crate Features \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    14\u2192//  \u2022 async/await powered by Tokio\n    15\u2192//  \u2022 AMQP implementation built on top of lapin\n    16\u2192//  \u2022 audit trail persisted with sqlx & Postgres\n    17\u2192//  \u2022 exponential back-off reconnect\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192//  NOTE: The public API deliberately hides lapin types to ease future\n    20\u2192//        migrations (e.g. to NATS or Kafka).\n    21\u2192//\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192use std::{\n    24\u2192    fmt::Debug,\n    25\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    26\u2192    time::{Duration, SystemTime},\n    27\u2192};\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    30\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    31\u2192use lapin::{\n    32\u2192    options::{BasicConsumeOptions, BasicPublishOptions, QueueDeclareOptions},\n    33\u2192    types::FieldTable,\n    34\u2192    BasicProperties, Channel, Connection, ConnectionProperties,\n    35\u2192};\n    36\u2192use rand::{thread_rng, Rng};\n    37\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    38\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool};\n    39\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    40\u2192use tokio::{select, sync::Notify, task, time};\n    41\u2192use tokio_retry::{strategy::ExponentialBackoff, Retry};\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192/// Global exchange used by all micro-services.\n    44\u2192const EXCHANGE_NAME: &str = \"edupulse.events\";\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/// AMQP dead-letter exchange for failed messages.\n    47\u2192const DLX_EXCHANGE_NAME: &str = \"edupulse.dlx\";\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192/// Domain error returned by this module.\n    50\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    51\u2192pub enum EventBusError {\n    52\u2192    #[error(\"AMQP error: {0}\")]\n    53\u2192    Amqp(#[from] lapin::Error),\n    54\u2192    #[error(\"connection closed unexpectedly\")]\n    55\u2192    ConnectionClosed,\n    56\u2192    #[error(\"serialization error: {0}\")]\n    57\u2192    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    58\u2192    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n    59\u2192    Db(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    60\u2192    #[error(\"task join error: {0}\")]\n    61\u2192    Join(#[from] tokio::task::JoinError),\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192/// Marker trait implemented by all domain events.\n    65\u2192///\n    66\u2192/// The `event_type` method returns a canonical snake-case event name that\n    67\u2192/// becomes the AMQP routing key (e.g. `lesson_published`, `pulse_replied`).\n    68\u2192/// Implementations should be `Clone`, `Serialize`, and `DeserializeOwned`.\n    69\u2192#[async_trait]\n    70\u2192pub trait DomainEvent:\n    71\u2192    Debug + Clone + Send + Sync + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + 'static\n    72\u2192{\n    73\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str;\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192/// Envelope adds cross-cutting metadata around a [`DomainEvent`].\n    77\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    78\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope<E: DomainEvent> {\n    79\u2192    pub id: uuid::Uuid,\n    80\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    81\u2192    pub correlation_id: Option<uuid::Uuid>,\n    82\u2192    #[serde(bound = \"E: DomainEvent\")]\n    83\u2192    pub payload: E,\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192impl<E: DomainEvent> EventEnvelope<E> {\n    87\u2192    pub fn new(payload: E) -> Self {\n    88\u2192        Self {\n    89\u2192            id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n    90\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n    91\u2192            correlation_id: None,\n    92\u2192            payload,\n    93\u2192        }\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    /// Returns the routing key for AMQP based on the event type.\n    97\u2192    pub fn routing_key() -> &'static str {\n    98\u2192        E::event_type()\n    99\u2192    }\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192/// Public fa\u00e7ade for publishing and subscribing to events.\n   103\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   104\u2192pub struct EventBus {\n   105\u2192    inner: Arc<EventBusInner>,\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192struct EventBusInner {\n   109\u2192    channel: Channel,\n   110\u2192    notify_disconnect: Arc<Notify>,\n   111\u2192    audit_pool: PgPool,\n   112\u2192}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192impl EventBus {\n   115\u2192    /// Connects to the broker and initialises required exchanges/queues.\n   116\u2192    pub async fn connect(amqp_url: &str, postgres_url: &str) -> Result<Self, EventBusError> {\n   117\u2192        let conn = Connection::connect(\n   118\u2192            amqp_url,\n   119\u2192            ConnectionProperties::default().with_default_executor(8),\n   120\u2192        )\n   121\u2192        .await?;\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192        let channel = conn.create_channel().await?;\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192        // Declare global exchange (topic) + DLX.\n   126\u2192        channel\n   127\u2192            .exchange_declare(\n   128\u2192                EXCHANGE_NAME,\n   129\u2192                lapin::ExchangeKind::Topic,\n   130\u2192                lapin::options::ExchangeDeclareOptions {\n   131\u2192                    durable: true,\n   132\u2192                    ..Default::default()\n   133\u2192                },\n   134\u2192                FieldTable::default(),\n   135\u2192            )\n   136\u2192            .await?;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192        channel\n   139\u2192            .exchange_declare(\n   140\u2192                DLX_EXCHANGE_NAME,\n   141\u2192                lapin::ExchangeKind::Fanout,\n   142\u2192                lapin::options::ExchangeDeclareOptions {\n   143\u2192                    durable: true,\n   144\u2192                    ..Default::default()\n   145\u2192                },\n   146\u2192                FieldTable::default(),\n   147\u2192            )\n   148\u2192            .await?;\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192        let audit_pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   151\u2192            .max_connections(5)\n   152\u2192            .connect(postgres_url)\n   153\u2192            .await?;\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192        let inner = Arc::new(EventBusInner {\n   156\u2192            channel,\n   157\u2192            notify_disconnect: Arc::new(Notify::new()),\n   158\u2192            audit_pool,\n   159\u2192        });\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192        // Spawn watchdog for connection loss.\n   162\u2192        Self::spawn_watchdog(inner.clone());\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192        Ok(Self { inner })\n   165\u2192    }\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    /// Publish a domain event inside an envelope.\n   168\u2192    ///\n   169\u2192    /// \u2013 Persist to audit table (fire-and-forget).\n   170\u2192    /// \u2013 Publish persistent message to RabbitMQ.\n   171\u2192    pub async fn publish<E>(&self, event: EventEnvelope<E>) -> Result<(), EventBusError>\n   172\u2192    where\n   173\u2192        E: DomainEvent,\n   174\u2192    {\n   175\u2192        let json = serde_json::to_vec(&event)?;\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192        // Insert into audit log without blocking caller on I/O latency.\n   178\u2192        let pool = self.inner.audit_pool.clone();\n   179\u2192        let audit_clone = event.clone();\n   180\u2192        task::spawn(async move {\n   181\u2192            if let Err(e) = sqlx::query!(\n   182\u2192                r#\"\n   183\u2192                INSERT INTO audit_events (id, event_type, occurred_at, payload)\n   184\u2192                VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n   185\u2192                \"#,\n   186\u2192                audit_clone.id,\n   187\u2192                E::event_type(),\n   188\u2192                audit_clone.occurred_at,\n   189\u2192                serde_json::to_value(audit_clone.payload).ok()\n   190\u2192            )\n   191\u2192            .execute(&pool)\n   192\u2192            .await\n   193\u2192            {\n   194\u2192                tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to persist audit event\");\n   195\u2192            }\n   196\u2192        });\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192        self.inner\n   199\u2192            .channel\n   200\u2192            .basic_publish(\n   201\u2192                EXCHANGE_NAME,\n   202\u2192                E::event_type(),\n   203\u2192                BasicPublishOptions {\n   204\u2192                    mandatory: true,\n   205\u2192                    ..Default::default()\n   206\u2192                },\n   207\u2192                &json,\n   208\u2192                BasicProperties::default().with_content_type(\"application/json\".into()),\n   209\u2192            )\n   210\u2192            .await?\n   211\u2192            .await?; // wait for confirmation\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192        Ok(())\n   214\u2192    }\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192    /// Consume events of a specific type.\n   217\u2192    ///\n   218\u2192    /// Handlers run on separate tasks. A manual `ack` will only be sent if\n   219\u2192    /// handler returns `Ok(())`; otherwise the message is rejected and sent\n   220\u2192    /// to DLX for later inspection.\n   221\u2192    pub async fn subscribe<E, H>(&self, queue_name: &str, handler: H) -> Result<(), EventBusError>\n   222\u2192    where\n   223\u2192        E: DomainEvent,\n   224\u2192        H: EventHandler<E>,\n   225\u2192    {\n   226\u2192        let channel = self.inner.channel.clone();\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192        channel\n   229\u2192            .queue_declare(\n   230\u2192                queue_name,\n   231\u2192                QueueDeclareOptions {\n   232\u2192                    durable: true,\n   233\u2192                    ..Default::default()\n   234\u2192                },\n   235\u2192                FieldTable::from([\n   236\u2192                    (\"x-dead-letter-exchange\".into(), DLX_EXCHANGE_NAME.into()),\n   237\u2192                ]),\n   238\u2192            )\n   239\u2192            .await?;\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192        channel\n   242\u2192            .queue_bind(\n   243\u2192                queue_name,\n   244\u2192                EXCHANGE_NAME,\n   245\u2192                E::event_type(),\n   246\u2192                lapin::options::QueueBindOptions::default(),\n   247\u2192                FieldTable::default(),\n   248\u2192            )\n   249\u2192            .await?;\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192        let consumer = channel\n   252\u2192            .basic_consume(\n   253\u2192                queue_name,\n   254\u2192                \"\",\n   255\u2192                BasicConsumeOptions {\n   256\u2192                    manual_ack: true,\n   257\u2192                    ..Default::default()\n   258\u2192                },\n   259\u2192                FieldTable::default(),\n   260\u2192            )\n   261\u2192            .await?;\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192        let handler_arc = Arc::new(handler);\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192        // Spawn a detached task that processes the stream.\n   266\u2192        task::spawn(async move {\n   267\u2192            for delivery in consumer {\n   268\u2192                match delivery {\n   269\u2192                    Ok(delivery) => {\n   270\u2192                        let envelope = match serde_json::from_slice::<EventEnvelope<E>>(\n   271\u2192                            &delivery.data,\n   272\u2192                        ) {\n   273\u2192                            Ok(env) => env,\n   274\u2192                            Err(err) => {\n   275\u2192                                tracing::error!(error = %err, \"failed to deserialize event\");\n   276\u2192                                // Reject so it hits DLX.\n   277\u2192                                if let Err(e) = delivery\n   278\u2192                                    .nack(lapin::options::BasicNackOptions::default())\n   279\u2192                                    .await\n   280\u2192                                {\n   281\u2192                                    tracing::error!(error = %e, \"nack failed\");\n   282\u2192                                }\n   283\u2192                                continue;\n   284\u2192                            }\n   285\u2192                        };\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192                        let ctx = EventContext {\n   288\u2192                            redelivered: delivery.redelivered,\n   289\u2192                        };\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192                        match handler_arc.handle(envelope, ctx).await {\n   292\u2192                            Ok(_) => {\n   293\u2192                                if let Err(e) = delivery\n   294\u2192                                    .ack(lapin::options::BasicAckOptions::default())\n   295\u2192                                    .await\n   296\u2192                                {\n   297\u2192                                    tracing::error!(error = %e, \"ack failed\");\n   298\u2192                                }\n   299\u2192                            }\n   300\u2192                            Err(e) => {\n   301\u2192                                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"handler error, rejecting message\");\n   302\u2192                                if let Err(e) = delivery\n   303\u2192                                    .nack(lapin::options::BasicNackOptions {\n   304\u2192                                        requeue: false,\n   305\u2192                                        ..Default::default()\n   306\u2192                                    })\n   307\u2192                                    .await\n   308\u2192                                {\n   309\u2192                                    tracing::error!(error = %e, \"nack failed\");\n   310\u2192                                }\n   311\u2192                            }\n   312\u2192                        }\n   313\u2192                    }\n   314\u2192                    Err(err) => {\n   315\u2192                        tracing::error!(error = %err, \"rabbitmq consumer error\");\n   316\u2192                        break;\n   317\u2192                    }\n   318\u2192                }\n   319\u2192            }\n   320\u2192        });\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192        Ok(())\n   323\u2192    }\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192    /// Background watchdog: listens for disconnections and tries to reconnect\n   326\u2192    /// with an exponential back-off.  In a real-world setup, we would signal\n   327\u2192    /// higher-level components on permanent failure.\n   328\u2192    fn spawn_watchdog(inner: Arc<EventBusInner>) {\n   329\u2192        task::spawn(async move {\n   330\u2192            // Wait until channel is closed (lapin emits this internally).\n   331\u2192            let close_reason = inner.channel.on_error().await;\n   332\u2192            tracing::warn!(?close_reason, \"AMQP channel closed, watchdog engaged\");\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192            inner.notify_disconnect.notify_waiters();\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192            // Retry loop for reconnection attempts.\n   337\u2192            let backoff = ExponentialBackoff::from_millis(250).take(8);\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192            if let Err(e) = Retry::spawn(backoff, || async {\n   340\u2192                let conn = Connection::connect(\n   341\u2192                    // Re-use original URI from env\n   342\u2192                    &std::env::var(\"AMQP_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"amqp://guest:guest@127.0.0.1:5672/%2f\".into()),\n   343\u2192                    ConnectionProperties::default(),\n   344\u2192                )\n   345\u2192                .await?;\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192                let ch = conn.create_channel().await?;\n   348\u2192                *Arc::get_mut(&mut inner.clone()).unwrap() = EventBusInner {\n   349\u2192                    channel: ch,\n   350\u2192                    notify_disconnect: inner.notify_disconnect.clone(),\n   351\u2192                    audit_pool: inner.audit_pool.clone(),\n   352\u2192                };\n   353\u2192                Ok::<_, lapin::Error>(())\n   354\u2192            })\n   355\u2192            .await\n   356\u2192            {\n   357\u2192                tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to reconnect to AMQP \u2013 manual intervention required\");\n   358\u2192            }\n   359\u2192        });\n   360\u2192    }\n   361\u2192}\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192/// Metadata passed to event handlers.\n   364\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   365\u2192pub struct EventContext {\n   366\u2192    /// Was the message redelivered by RabbitMQ?\n   367\u2192    pub redelivered: bool,\n   368\u2192}\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192/// Handler trait for a single event type.\n   371\u2192#[async_trait]\n   372\u2192pub trait EventHandler<E: DomainEvent>: Send + Sync {\n   373\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope<E>, ctx: EventContext) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error>;\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Example Domain Events \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   379\u2192pub struct LessonPublished {\n   380\u2192    pub lesson_id: uuid::Uuid,\n   381\u2192    pub author_id: uuid::Uuid,\n   382\u2192    pub title: String,\n   383\u2192}\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192#[async_trait]\n   386\u2192impl DomainEvent for LessonPublished {\n   387\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str {\n   388\u2192        \"lesson_published\"\n   389\u2192    }\n   390\u2192}\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   393\u2192pub struct PulseReplied {\n   394\u2192    pub pulse_id: uuid::Uuid,\n   395\u2192    pub student_id: uuid::Uuid,\n   396\u2192    pub attachments: Vec<String>,\n   397\u2192}\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192#[async_trait]\n   400\u2192impl DomainEvent for PulseReplied {\n   401\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str {\n   402\u2192        \"pulse_replied\"\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192}\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192// \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 Unit Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   409\u2192mod tests {\n   410\u2192    use super::*;\n   411\u2192    use std::env;\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192    struct NoopHandler;\n   414\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   415\u2192    impl EventHandler<LessonPublished> for NoopHandler {\n   416\u2192        async fn handle(\n   417\u2192            &self,\n   418\u2192            _event: EventEnvelope<LessonPublished>,\n   419\u2192            _ctx: EventContext,\n   420\u2192        ) -> Result<(), anyhow::Error> {\n   421\u2192            Ok(())\n   422\u2192        }\n   423\u2192    }\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   426\u2192    async fn envelope_roundtrip() {\n   427\u2192        let event = LessonPublished {\n   428\u2192            lesson_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n   429\u2192            author_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n   430\u2192            title: \"Rust Traits 101\".into(),\n   431\u2192        };\n   432\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(event.clone());\n   433\u2192        let json = serde_json::to_vec(&envelope).unwrap();\n   434\u2192        let deser: EventEnvelope<LessonPublished> = serde_json::from_slice(&json).unwrap();\n   435\u2192        assert_eq!(deser.payload.lesson_id, event.lesson_id);\n   436\u2192    }\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192    // The following test requires a local RabbitMQ & Postgres and is therefore\n   439\u2192    // ignored by default.  Run with:\n   440\u2192    //     AMQP_URL=amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672/%2f \\\n   441\u2192    //     DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:password@localhost/edupulse \\\n   442\u2192    //     cargo test -- --ignored\n   443\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   444\u2192    #[ignore]\n   445\u2192    async fn integration_publish_then_consume() {\n   446\u2192        dotenv::dotenv().ok();\n   447\u2192        let amqp = env::var(\"AMQP_URL\").expect(\"AMQP_URL not set\");\n   448\u2192        let db = env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\").expect(\"DATABASE_URL not set\");\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192        let bus = EventBus::connect(&amqp, &db).await.unwrap();\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192        let lesson = LessonPublished {\n   453\u2192            lesson_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n   454\u2192            author_id: uuid::Uuid::new_v4(),\n   455\u2192            title: \"Integration Test\".into(),\n   456\u2192        };\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(lesson);\n   459\u2192        bus.publish(envelope.clone()).await.unwrap();\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192        bus.subscribe(\"test_queue\", NoopHandler).await.unwrap();\n   462\u2192\n   463\u2192        // Give the async consumer some time.\n   464\u2192        time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n   465\u2192    }\n   466\u2192}\n   467\u2192```\n   468\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
87. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_68.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_68.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Event-driven plumbing for the \u201cLearning Pulse\u201d workflow.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! The code below demonstrates how a single bounded context (\u201clearning pulses\u201d)\n     7\u2192//! reacts to a `SubmissionCreated` domain event and orchestrates **two**\n     8\u2192//! asynchronous side-effects:\n     9\u2192//!   1. A plagiarism check through an external HTTP service.\n    10\u2192//!   2. A rubric-based automatic scoring job.\n    11\u2192//!\n    12\u2192//! Once each task finishes the appropriate *follow-up* event is published so\n    13\u2192//! that other micro-services (notifications, analytics, etc.) can react\n    14\u2192//! independently.\n    15\u2192//!\n    16\u2192//! # Compile features\n    17\u2192//!\n    18\u2192//! - `amqp` \u2013 use a real RabbitMQ event-bus (enabled in CI / prod).\n    19\u2192//! - *none* \u2013 fall back to an in-memory event-bus for local development & tests.\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192use std::collections::HashMap;\n    22\u2192use std::sync::Arc;\n    23\u2192use std::time::Duration;\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    26\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    27\u2192use reqwest::StatusCode;\n    28\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    29\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    30\u2192use tokio::sync::{mpsc, RwLock};\n    31\u2192use tokio::task::JoinHandle;\n    32\u2192use tokio::time::timeout;\n    33\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/// Correlation identifier propagated across bounded contexts.\n    36\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    37\u2192pub struct CorrelationId(String);\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192impl CorrelationId {\n    40\u2192    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    41\u2192        Self(uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string())\n    42\u2192    }\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/// A marker trait implemented by *every* domain event.\n    46\u2192///\n    47\u2192/// The obvious choice would be enum-dispatch, however a trait object allows new\n    48\u2192/// events to be added without modifying a central enum \u2013 a desirable property\n    49\u2192/// in a highly modular micro-service code-base.\n    50\u2192#[async_trait]\n    51\u2192pub trait DomainEvent: Send + Sync {\n    52\u2192    /// Type discriminator (`\"submission.created\"`, `\"plagiarism.checked\"`, \u2026).\n    53\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str;\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    /// Monotonic temporal marker.\n    56\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    /// Correlation id used for distributed tracing.\n    59\u2192    fn correlation_id(&self) -> &CorrelationId;\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    /// Serialize event instance to JSON for transport on the wire.\n    62\u2192    fn to_json(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, EventError> {\n    63\u2192        serde_json::to_vec(self).map_err(EventError::serialization)\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192/// Business-level error type for this module.\n    68\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    69\u2192pub enum EventError {\n    70\u2192    #[error(\"serialization error: {0}\")]\n    71\u2192    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    #[error(\"http error: {0}\")]\n    74\u2192    Http(#[from] reqwest::Error),\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    #[error(\"event bus error: {0}\")]\n    77\u2192    EventBus(String),\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192    #[error(\"timeout waiting for plagiarism service\")]\n    80\u2192    TimeOut,\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192    #[error(\"unexpected response from plagiarism service: {0}\")]\n    83\u2192    InvalidPlagiarismResponse(String),\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192impl EventError {\n    87\u2192    fn serialization(err: serde_json::Error) -> Self {\n    88\u2192        Self::Serialization(err)\n    89\u2192    }\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192/// Concrete domain event emitted when a student uploads a submission.\n    93\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    94\u2192pub struct SubmissionCreated {\n    95\u2192    pub submission_id: String,\n    96\u2192    pub author_id: String,\n    97\u2192    pub pulse_id: String,\n    98\u2192    pub content: String,\n    99\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   100\u2192    pub correlation_id: CorrelationId,\n   101\u2192}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192#[async_trait]\n   104\u2192impl DomainEvent for SubmissionCreated {\n   105\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n   106\u2192        \"submission.created\"\n   107\u2192    }\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   110\u2192        self.occurred_at\n   111\u2192    }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192    fn correlation_id(&self) -> &CorrelationId {\n   114\u2192        &self.correlation_id\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192/// Event emitted after plagiarism verification finishes.\n   119\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   120\u2192pub struct PlagiarismChecked {\n   121\u2192    pub submission_id: String,\n   122\u2192    pub similarity_score: f32,\n   123\u2192    pub is_suspect: bool,\n   124\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   125\u2192    pub correlation_id: CorrelationId,\n   126\u2192}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192#[async_trait]\n   129\u2192impl DomainEvent for PlagiarismChecked {\n   130\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n   131\u2192        \"plagiarism.checked\"\n   132\u2192    }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   135\u2192        self.occurred_at\n   136\u2192    }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    fn correlation_id(&self) -> &CorrelationId {\n   139\u2192        &self.correlation_id\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192/// Event emitted after the automatic score is calculated.\n   144\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   145\u2192pub struct AutoScoreCompleted {\n   146\u2192    pub submission_id: String,\n   147\u2192    pub score: u8,\n   148\u2192    pub rubric_version: String,\n   149\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   150\u2192    pub correlation_id: CorrelationId,\n   151\u2192}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192#[async_trait]\n   154\u2192impl DomainEvent for AutoScoreCompleted {\n   155\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n   156\u2192        \"autoscore.completed\"\n   157\u2192    }\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   160\u2192        self.occurred_at\n   161\u2192    }\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    fn correlation_id(&self) -> &CorrelationId {\n   164\u2192        &self.correlation_id\n   165\u2192    }\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192/// Event bus abstraction.\n   169\u2192#[async_trait]\n   170\u2192pub trait EventBus {\n   171\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &(dyn DomainEvent + 'static)) -> Result<(), EventError>;\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192    /// Subscribe with a channel of boxed events.   \n   174\u2192    ///\n   175\u2192    /// In real life we would use strongly typed channels; for brevity we\n   176\u2192    /// down-cast at the consuming site.\n   177\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self) -> mpsc::Receiver<Box<dyn DomainEvent>>;\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192/// In-memory implementation (dev / tests).\n   181\u2192pub struct InMemoryEventBus {\n   182\u2192    tx: mpsc::Sender<Box<dyn DomainEvent>>,\n   183\u2192    rx: RwLock<mpsc::Receiver<Box<dyn DomainEvent>>>,\n   184\u2192}\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192impl InMemoryEventBus {\n   187\u2192    pub fn new(buffer: usize) -> Self {\n   188\u2192        let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel(buffer);\n   189\u2192        Self {\n   190\u2192            tx,\n   191\u2192            rx: RwLock::new(rx),\n   192\u2192        }\n   193\u2192    }\n   194\u2192}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192#[async_trait]\n   197\u2192impl EventBus for InMemoryEventBus {\n   198\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &(dyn DomainEvent + 'static)) -> Result<(), EventError> {\n   199\u2192        let mut cloned = Vec::new();\n   200\u2192        // Re-serialize to guarantee we send a **copy** (preventing accidental\n   201\u2192        // mutation from other tasks).\n   202\u2192        let bytes = event.to_json()?;\n   203\u2192        let evt_type = event.event_type();\n   204\u2192        cloned.extend_from_slice(&bytes);\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192        let deserialized: Box<dyn DomainEvent> = match evt_type {\n   207\u2192            \"submission.created\" => {\n   208\u2192                let ev: SubmissionCreated = serde_json::from_slice(&cloned)?;\n   209\u2192                Box::new(ev)\n   210\u2192            }\n   211\u2192            \"plagiarism.checked\" => {\n   212\u2192                let ev: PlagiarismChecked = serde_json::from_slice(&cloned)?;\n   213\u2192                Box::new(ev)\n   214\u2192            }\n   215\u2192            \"autoscore.completed\" => {\n   216\u2192                let ev: AutoScoreCompleted = serde_json::from_slice(&cloned)?;\n   217\u2192                Box::new(ev)\n   218\u2192            }\n   219\u2192            _ => {\n   220\u2192                warn!(\"unrecognized event type on in-memory bus: {evt_type}\");\n   221\u2192                return Ok(());\n   222\u2192            }\n   223\u2192        };\n   224\u2192        self.tx\n   225\u2192            .send(deserialized)\n   226\u2192            .await\n   227\u2192            .map_err(|e| EventError::EventBus(e.to_string()))\n   228\u2192    }\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self) -> mpsc::Receiver<Box<dyn DomainEvent>> {\n   231\u2192        self.rx.write().await.recv().await;\n   232\u2192        // clone underlying channel\n   233\u2192        let mut rx = self.rx.write().await;\n   234\u2192        rx.clone()\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192}\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192/// Application service orchestrating plagiarism & auto-scoring.\n   239\u2192pub struct LearningPulseService {\n   240\u2192    event_bus: Arc<dyn EventBus + Send + Sync>,\n   241\u2192    http: reqwest::Client,\n   242\u2192    plagiarism_endpoint: String,\n   243\u2192    rubric_store: Arc<RubricRepository>,\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192impl LearningPulseService {\n   247\u2192    pub fn new(\n   248\u2192        event_bus: Arc<dyn EventBus + Send + Sync>,\n   249\u2192        plagiarism_endpoint: impl Into<String>,\n   250\u2192        rubric_store: Arc<RubricRepository>,\n   251\u2192    ) -> Self {\n   252\u2192        Self {\n   253\u2192            event_bus,\n   254\u2192            http: reqwest::Client::new(),\n   255\u2192            plagiarism_endpoint: plagiarism_endpoint.into(),\n   256\u2192            rubric_store,\n   257\u2192        }\n   258\u2192    }\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192    /// Run the long-running background task.\n   261\u2192    pub fn spawn(self: Arc<Self>) -> JoinHandle<()> {\n   262\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   263\u2192            let mut rx = self.event_bus.subscribe().await;\n   264\u2192            info!(\"learning-pulse service ready to process events\");\n   265\u2192            while let Some(evt) = rx.recv().await {\n   266\u2192                if let Some(submission_evt) = evt.downcast_ref::<SubmissionCreated>() {\n   267\u2192                    let svc = self.clone();\n   268\u2192                    tokio::spawn(async move {\n   269\u2192                        if let Err(err) = svc.process_submission(submission_evt.clone()).await {\n   270\u2192                            error!(?err, \"failed to process submission\");\n   271\u2192                        }\n   272\u2192                    });\n   273\u2192                }\n   274\u2192            }\n   275\u2192        })\n   276\u2192    }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(submission_id = %submission.submission_id))]\n   279\u2192    async fn process_submission(&self, submission: SubmissionCreated) -> Result<(), EventError> {\n   280\u2192        let correlation_id = submission.correlation_id.clone();\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192        // 1) Spawn plagiarism request\n   283\u2192        let plagiarism_future = self\n   284\u2192            .request_plagiarism_check(&submission)\n   285\u2192            .await\n   286\u2192            .map(|res| PlagiarismChecked {\n   287\u2192                submission_id: submission.submission_id.clone(),\n   288\u2192                similarity_score: res.score,\n   289\u2192                is_suspect: res.is_suspect,\n   290\u2192                occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   291\u2192                correlation_id: correlation_id.clone(),\n   292\u2192            });\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192        match plagiarism_future {\n   295\u2192            Ok(evt) => {\n   296\u2192                self.event_bus.publish(&evt).await?;\n   297\u2192            }\n   298\u2192            Err(e) => error!(?e, \"plagiarism check failed\"),\n   299\u2192        }\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192        // 2) Spawn scoring\n   302\u2192        let score_evt = self\n   303\u2192            .auto_score(&submission)\n   304\u2192            .await\n   305\u2192            .map(|score| AutoScoreCompleted {\n   306\u2192                submission_id: submission.submission_id.clone(),\n   307\u2192                score,\n   308\u2192                rubric_version: self.rubric_store.latest_version(),\n   309\u2192                occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   310\u2192                correlation_id,\n   311\u2192            })?;\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192        self.event_bus.publish(&score_evt).await?;\n   314\u2192        Ok(())\n   315\u2192    }\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192    /// Calls the external plagiarism API (with timeout protection).\n   318\u2192    async fn request_plagiarism_check(\n   319\u2192        &self,\n   320\u2192        submission: &SubmissionCreated,\n   321\u2192    ) -> Result<PlagiarismResponse, EventError> {\n   322\u2192        #[derive(Serialize)]\n   323\u2192        struct Payload<'a> {\n   324\u2192            submission_id: &'a str,\n   325\u2192            content: &'a str,\n   326\u2192            student_id: &'a str,\n   327\u2192        }\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192        let body = Payload {\n   330\u2192            submission_id: &submission.submission_id,\n   331\u2192            content: &submission.content,\n   332\u2192            student_id: &submission.author_id,\n   333\u2192        };\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192        let request = self\n   336\u2192            .http\n   337\u2192            .post(&self.plagiarism_endpoint)\n   338\u2192            .json(&body)\n   339\u2192            .build()?;\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192        let response = timeout(Duration::from_secs(8), self.http.execute(request)).await;\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192        let response = match response {\n   344\u2192            Ok(res) => res?,\n   345\u2192            Err(_) => return Err(EventError::TimeOut),\n   346\u2192        };\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192        if response.status() != StatusCode::OK {\n   349\u2192            return Err(EventError::InvalidPlagiarismResponse(format!(\n   350\u2192                \"status {}\",\n   351\u2192                response.status()\n   352\u2192            )));\n   353\u2192        }\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192        let result: PlagiarismApiPayload = response.json().await?;\n   356\u2192        Ok(result.into())\n   357\u2192    }\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192    /// Very naive auto-scoring based on a rubric repository.\n   360\u2192    async fn auto_score(&self, submission: &SubmissionCreated) -> Result<u8, EventError> {\n   361\u2192        let rubric = self.rubric_store.fetch_current().await;\n   362\u2192        let words = submission.content.split_whitespace().count() as u32;\n   363\u2192        let mut score = 0;\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192        if words >= rubric.min_word_count {\n   366\u2192            score += 60;\n   367\u2192        }\n   368\u2192        if submission.content.contains(\"because\") {\n   369\u2192            score += 15;\n   370\u2192        }\n   371\u2192        if submission.content.contains(\"therefore\") {\n   372\u2192            score += 15;\n   373\u2192        }\n   374\u2192        if submission.content.ends_with('.') {\n   375\u2192            score += 10;\n   376\u2192        }\n   377\u2192        Ok(score.min(100) as u8)\n   378\u2192    }\n   379\u2192}\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192/// DTO returned from the remote plagiarism service.\n   382\u2192#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n   383\u2192struct PlagiarismApiPayload {\n   384\u2192    score: f32,\n   385\u2192    is_suspect: bool,\n   386\u2192}\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192#[derive(Debug)]\n   389\u2192struct PlagiarismResponse {\n   390\u2192    score: f32,\n   391\u2192    is_suspect: bool,\n   392\u2192}\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192impl From<PlagiarismApiPayload> for PlagiarismResponse {\n   395\u2192    fn from(val: PlagiarismApiPayload) -> Self {\n   396\u2192        Self {\n   397\u2192            score: val.score,\n   398\u2192            is_suspect: val.is_suspect,\n   399\u2192        }\n   400\u2192    }\n   401\u2192}\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192/// Simplistic rubric repository.\n   404\u2192/// In production this would talk to Postgres or Config Service.\n   405\u2192#[derive(Default)]\n   406\u2192pub struct RubricRepository {\n   407\u2192    current: RwLock<Rubric>,\n   408\u2192}\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192/// Represents a single rubric configuration.\n   411\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   412\u2192pub struct Rubric {\n   413\u2192    version: String,\n   414\u2192    min_word_count: u32,\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192impl RubricRepository {\n   418\u2192    pub async fn fetch_current(&self) -> Rubric {\n   419\u2192        self.current.read().await.clone()\n   420\u2192    }\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192    pub fn latest_version(&self) -> String {\n   423\u2192        self.current\n   424\u2192            .blocking_read()\n   425\u2192            .version\n   426\u2192            .clone()\n   427\u2192    }\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192    #[allow(dead_code)]\n   430\u2192    pub async fn update(&self, rubric: Rubric) {\n   431\u2192        *self.current.write().await = rubric;\n   432\u2192    }\n   433\u2192}\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   436\u2192// Example bootstrap \u2013 would live in `main.rs` in the actual micro-service.\n   437\u2192// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   438\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   439\u2192mod tests {\n   440\u2192    use super::*;\n   441\u2192    use tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter;\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   444\u2192    async fn submission_flow_emits_follow_up_events() {\n   445\u2192        // Initialize test logger\n   446\u2192        let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()\n   447\u2192            .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env())\n   448\u2192            .try_init();\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192        let bus: Arc<dyn EventBus + Send + Sync> = Arc::new(InMemoryEventBus::new(16));\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192        let rubric_store = Arc::new(RubricRepository::default());\n   453\u2192        rubric_store\n   454\u2192            .update(Rubric {\n   455\u2192                version: \"v1\".into(),\n   456\u2192                min_word_count: 5,\n   457\u2192            })\n   458\u2192            .await;\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192        let service = Arc::new(LearningPulseService::new(\n   461\u2192            bus.clone(),\n   462\u2192            \"http://localhost:1234/plagiarism\", // mocked in this test\n   463\u2192            rubric_store,\n   464\u2192        ));\n   465\u2192\n   466\u2192        service.spawn();\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192        // Fire off a submission\n   469\u2192        let evt = SubmissionCreated {\n   470\u2192            submission_id: \"sub-123\".into(),\n   471\u2192            author_id: \"user-456\".into(),\n   472\u2192            pulse_id: \"pulse-789\".into(),\n   473\u2192            content: \"I think therefore I am. Because logic.\".into(),\n   474\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   475\u2192            correlation_id: CorrelationId::new(),\n   476\u2192        };\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192        bus.publish(&evt).await.unwrap();\n   479\u2192\n   480\u2192        // Wait briefly to allow background tasks to finish\n   481\u2192        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192        // Assert we have some messages in the bus.  \n   484\u2192        // (In a real test we would use a mock / spy implementation.)\n   485\u2192        let mut rx = bus.subscribe().await;\n   486\u2192        let mut seen: HashMap<&'static str, usize> = HashMap::new();\n   487\u2192        let start = Utc::now();\n   488\u2192        while Utc::now().signed_duration_since(start).num_milliseconds() < 500 {\n   489\u2192            if let Ok(Some(msg)) = timeout(Duration::from_millis(50), rx.recv()).await {\n   490\u2192                *seen.entry(msg.event_type()).or_default() += 1;\n   491\u2192            }\n   492\u2192        }\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192        assert!(seen.get(\"plagiarism.checked\").cloned().unwrap_or(0) >= 1);\n   495\u2192        assert!(seen.get(\"autoscore.completed\").cloned().unwrap_or(0) >= 1);\n   496\u2192    }\n   497\u2192}\n   498\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_69.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Reactive domain-events infrastructure for EduPulse Live.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! This module exposes the abstractions and a reference implementation for an\n     7\u2192//! event bus that backs the entire **EduPulse Live** ecosystem.  All bounded\n     8\u2192//! contexts in the application rely on these traits to publish / subscribe to\n     9\u2192//! business events such as:\n    10\u2192//!     * `LearningPulsePublished`\n    11\u2192//!     * `LearningPulseResponseSubmitted`\n    12\u2192//!     * `BadgeAwarded`\n    13\u2192//!\n    14\u2192//! Down-stream consumers (search-indexer, notification worker, analytics, \u2026)\n    15\u2192//! connect to the same bus transparently\u2014regardless of whether the underlying\n    16\u2192//! transport is an embedded channel for tests, a Kafka cluster, or an AMQP\n    17\u2192//! broker in production.\n    18\u2192//!\n    19\u2192//! # Crate features\n    20\u2192//! * `test-helpers` \u2013 Exposes synchronous helpers for black-box tests.\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192use std::{\n    23\u2192    collections::HashMap,\n    24\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    25\u2192};\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192use anyhow::{Context, Result};\n    28\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    29\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    30\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    31\u2192use tokio::{\n    32\u2192    select,\n    33\u2192    sync::{broadcast, mpsc, oneshot, RwLock},\n    34\u2192};\n    35\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, warn};\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192/// Unique identifier for each event.\n    38\u2192///\n    39\u2192/// We use `uuid::Uuid` under the hood; however the type is kept opaque\n    40\u2192/// to prevent ad-hoc string concatenations.\n    41\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    42\u2192#[serde(transparent)]\n    43\u2192pub struct EventId(uuid::Uuid);\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192impl Default for EventId {\n    46\u2192    fn default() -> Self {\n    47\u2192        Self(uuid::Uuid::new_v4())\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192}\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192impl std::fmt::Display for EventId {\n    52\u2192    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n    53\u2192        <uuid::Uuid as std::fmt::Display>::fmt(&self.0, f)\n    54\u2192    }\n    55\u2192}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192/// Common envelope that wraps *all* domain events.\n    58\u2192///\n    59\u2192/// The concrete payload is stored in a `serde_json::Value`, therefore callers\n    60\u2192/// can treat the bus as opaque and cast back to the concrete type via\n    61\u2192/// `serde_json::from_value`.\n    62\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    63\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n    64\u2192    pub id: EventId,\n    65\u2192    pub kind: EventKind,\n    66\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    67\u2192    pub metadata: EventMetadata,\n    68\u2192    pub payload: serde_json::Value,\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192/// High-level classification of an event.\n    72\u2192/// Can be used for selective subscriptions.\n    73\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    74\u2192#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n    75\u2192pub enum EventKind {\n    76\u2192    LearningPulsePublished,\n    77\u2192    LearningPulseResponseSubmitted,\n    78\u2192    BadgeAwarded,\n    79\u2192    PaymentProcessed,\n    80\u2192    // \u2026 add new kinds here\n    81\u2192}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192/// Additional data that is orthogonal to the domain payload.\n    84\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n    85\u2192pub struct EventMetadata {\n    86\u2192    /// Initiating user, if any (for audit trail).\n    87\u2192    pub principal_id: Option<uuid::Uuid>,\n    88\u2192    /// Tenant / school / organisation in multi-tenant setups.\n    89\u2192    pub tenant: Option<String>,\n    90\u2192    /// Correlation id for tracing distributed workflows.\n    91\u2192    pub correlation_id: Option<String>,\n    92\u2192}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192/// Asynchronous event bus.\n    95\u2192///\n    96\u2192/// Publishing an event returns immediately\u2014consumers will *eventually* handle it.\n    97\u2192#[async_trait]\n    98\u2192pub trait EventBus: Send + Sync + 'static {\n    99\u2192    /// Publish a single event onto the bus.\n   100\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()>;\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192    /// Subscribe to *all* events.\n   103\u2192    ///\n   104\u2192    /// Implementations should deliver events in **at-least-once** fashion.\n   105\u2192    /// Event ordering is preserved *per partition/key* only.\n   106\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self) -> Result<EventStream>;\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192/// Concrete stream type returned by `subscribe`.\n   110\u2192pub type EventStream = mpsc::Receiver<EventEnvelope>;\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/// A local in-memory event bus backed by `tokio::sync` primitives.\n   113\u2192///\n   114\u2192/// This is intended for unit/integration tests and _development_ servers only.\n   115\u2192/// It should never be used in production because:\n   116\u2192///  1. Events are lost on process shutdown.\n   117\u2192///  2. Back-pressure is bounded by memory and the channel size.\n   118\u2192///\n   119\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   120\u2192pub struct InMemoryBus {\n   121\u2192    sender: broadcast::Sender<EventEnvelope>,\n   122\u2192}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192impl InMemoryBus {\n   125\u2192    pub fn new(buffer: usize) -> Self {\n   126\u2192        let (sender, _) = broadcast::channel(buffer);\n   127\u2192        Self { sender }\n   128\u2192    }\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192#[async_trait]\n   132\u2192impl EventBus for InMemoryBus {\n   133\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, event))]\n   134\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   135\u2192        self.sender\n   136\u2192            .send(event)\n   137\u2192            .map(|_| ())\n   138\u2192            .context(\"in-memory bus lagged\")\n   139\u2192    }\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self) -> Result<EventStream> {\n   142\u2192        let mut rx = self.sender.subscribe();\n   143\u2192        // Fan-out broadcast receiver into an mpsc channel\n   144\u2192        let (tx, stream) = mpsc::channel::<EventEnvelope>(1024);\n   145\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   146\u2192            loop {\n   147\u2192                match rx.recv().await {\n   148\u2192                    Ok(evt) => {\n   149\u2192                        if tx.send(evt).await.is_err() {\n   150\u2192                            // Consumer dropped -> exit task\n   151\u2192                            break;\n   152\u2192                        }\n   153\u2192                    }\n   154\u2192                    Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(skipped)) => {\n   155\u2192                        warn!(skipped, \"consumer lagged behind in-memory bus\");\n   156\u2192                    }\n   157\u2192                    Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => break,\n   158\u2192                }\n   159\u2192            }\n   160\u2192        });\n   161\u2192        Ok(stream)\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192/// Persists events for durability and audit trail.\n   166\u2192///\n   167\u2192/// In production we use Postgres via `sqlx`.  The repository is used by the\n   168\u2192/// HTTP API + background workers to reconstruct an aggregate\u2019s event stream.\n   169\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   170\u2192pub struct PgEventStore {\n   171\u2192    pool: sqlx::PgPool,\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192impl PgEventStore {\n   175\u2192    pub fn new(pool: sqlx::PgPool) -> Self {\n   176\u2192        Self { pool }\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    /// Append a new envelope.\n   180\u2192    pub async fn append(&self, event: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   181\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_value(event)\n   182\u2192            .context(\"failed serialising event envelope\")?;\n   183\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   184\u2192            r#\"\n   185\u2192            INSERT INTO event_store(id, kind, occurred_at, metadata, payload)\n   186\u2192            VALUES($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n   187\u2192            \"#,\n   188\u2192            event.id.0,\n   189\u2192            event.kind as EventKind, // enum mapping\n   190\u2192            event.occurred_at,\n   191\u2192            serde_json::to_value(&event.metadata)?,\n   192\u2192            payload\n   193\u2192        )\n   194\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   195\u2192        .await\n   196\u2192        .context(\"cannot persist event\")\n   197\u2192        .map(|_| ())\n   198\u2192    }\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192/// Event handler abstraction.\n   202\u2192///\n   203\u2192/// Implementations are registered in a service provider and wired up by the\n   204\u2192/// [`Dispatcher`].\n   205\u2192#[async_trait]\n   206\u2192pub trait EventHandler: Send + Sync {\n   207\u2192    /// Which event kind does this handler deal with?\n   208\u2192    fn interested_in(&self) -> EventKind;\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192    /// Handle the incoming event.\n   211\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()>;\n   212\u2192}\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192/// Multiplexes events to specific [`EventHandler`]s.\n   215\u2192///\n   216\u2192/// The dispatcher owns the subscription stream and forwards each incoming\n   217\u2192/// envelope to **all** handlers that declared interest in that kind.\n   218\u2192/// Errors are logged, yet _never_ propagated\u2014otherwise one faulty consumer\n   219\u2192/// would block the whole pipeline.\n   220\u2192#[derive(Debug)]\n   221\u2192pub struct Dispatcher<B: EventBus> {\n   222\u2192    bus: Arc<B>,\n   223\u2192    handlers: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<EventKind, Vec<Arc<dyn EventHandler>>>>>,\n   224\u2192}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192impl<B: EventBus> Dispatcher<B> {\n   227\u2192    pub fn new(bus: Arc<B>) -> Self {\n   228\u2192        Self {\n   229\u2192            bus,\n   230\u2192            handlers: Arc::default(),\n   231\u2192        }\n   232\u2192    }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192    /// Register a new event handler.\n   235\u2192    pub async fn register(&self, handler: Arc<dyn EventHandler>) {\n   236\u2192        let kind = handler.interested_in();\n   237\u2192        let mut map = self.handlers.write().await;\n   238\u2192        map.entry(kind).or_default().push(handler);\n   239\u2192    }\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    /// Start forwarding events to handlers until `shutdown` is signalled.\n   242\u2192    pub async fn run(self: Arc<Self>, mut shutdown: oneshot::Receiver<()>) -> Result<()> {\n   243\u2192        let mut stream = self.bus.subscribe().await?;\n   244\u2192        loop {\n   245\u2192            select! {\n   246\u2192                Some(event) = stream.recv() => {\n   247\u2192                    let handlers = {\n   248\u2192                        let map = self.handlers.read().await;\n   249\u2192                        map.get(&event.kind).cloned()\n   250\u2192                    };\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192                    if let Some(handlers) = handlers {\n   253\u2192                        for handler in handlers {\n   254\u2192                            // Spawn each handler in its own task.\n   255\u2192                            let event_clone = event.clone();\n   256\u2192                            tokio::spawn(async move {\n   257\u2192                                if let Err(e) = handler.handle(event_clone).await {\n   258\u2192                                    error!(error = ?e, \"event handler failed\");\n   259\u2192                                }\n   260\u2192                            });\n   261\u2192                        }\n   262\u2192                    }\n   263\u2192                },\n   264\u2192                _ = &mut shutdown => {\n   265\u2192                    info!(\"dispatcher shutdown\");\n   266\u2192                    break;\n   267\u2192                }\n   268\u2192            }\n   269\u2192        }\n   270\u2192        Ok(())\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192}\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192/* ---- Example Handler ----------------------------------------------- */\n   275\u2192\n   276\u2192/// Trigger plagiarism check whenever a student submits a response.\n   277\u2192///\n   278\u2192/// In real production we'd integrate with an external micro-service through\n   279\u2192/// gRPC / HTTP.  For demonstration we just log the intent.\n   280\u2192pub struct PlagiarismCheckHandler;\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192#[async_trait]\n   283\u2192impl EventHandler for PlagiarismCheckHandler {\n   284\u2192    fn interested_in(&self) -> EventKind {\n   285\u2192        EventKind::LearningPulseResponseSubmitted\n   286\u2192    }\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192    #[instrument(name=\"plagiarism_check\", skip(self))]\n   289\u2192    async fn handle(&self, event: EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   290\u2192        debug!(\n   291\u2192            event_id = %event.id,\n   292\u2192            \"initiating plagiarism check (mock)\"\n   293\u2192        );\n   294\u2192        // TODO: make network call here\n   295\u2192        Ok(())\n   296\u2192    }\n   297\u2192}\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192/* ---- Helper to wire everything together for binary / tests --------- */\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192/// Build a fully-wired in-memory dispatcher that can be used inside integration\n   302\u2192/// tests without touching the database.\n   303\u2192pub async fn testing_dispatcher() -> Arc<Dispatcher<InMemoryBus>> {\n   304\u2192    let bus = Arc::new(InMemoryBus::new(512));\n   305\u2192    let dispatcher = Arc::new(Dispatcher::new(bus.clone()));\n   306\u2192    dispatcher\n   307\u2192        .register(Arc::new(PlagiarismCheckHandler))\n   308\u2192        .await;\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192    // Kick off dispatcher loop in background\n   311\u2192    let (tx, rx) = oneshot::channel();\n   312\u2192    let cloned = dispatcher.clone();\n   313\u2192    tokio::spawn(async move {\n   314\u2192        if let Err(e) = cloned.run(rx).await {\n   315\u2192            error!(error = ?e, \"dispatcher terminated with error\");\n   316\u2192        }\n   317\u2192    });\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192    // Store sender on dispatcher so caller can gracefully stop it (simplified)\n   320\u2192    tokio::spawn(async move {\n   321\u2192        // Shut down after 60 seconds in tests by default\n   322\u2192        tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(60)).await;\n   323\u2192        let _ = tx.send(());\n   324\u2192    });\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192    dispatcher\n   327\u2192}\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192/* ---- Integration test ---------------------------------------------- */\n   330\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   331\u2192mod tests {\n   332\u2192    use super::*;\n   333\u2192    use serde_json::json;\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   336\u2192    async fn handler_is_triggered() -> Result<()> {\n   337\u2192        let dispatcher = testing_dispatcher().await;\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope {\n   340\u2192            id: EventId::default(),\n   341\u2192            kind: EventKind::LearningPulseResponseSubmitted,\n   342\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   343\u2192            metadata: EventMetadata::default(),\n   344\u2192            payload: json!({\n   345\u2192                \"response_id\": \"12345\",\n   346\u2192                \"student_id\": \"abcd-ef12\",\n   347\u2192                \"answer\": \"Rust code snippet\"\n   348\u2192            }),\n   349\u2192        };\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192        dispatcher.bus.publish(envelope).await?;\n   352\u2192        // Sleep a bit to allow async handler to run\n   353\u2192        tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192        // Nothing to assert\u2014if handler panics test will fail anyway\n   356\u2192        Ok(())\n   357\u2192    }\n   358\u2192}\n   359\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_7.txt"
}
Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_7.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Event-bus abstraction used across the EduPulse Live back-end.  All domain\n     5\u2192//! models raise strongly-typed events that are wrapped in an `EventEnvelope`\n     6\u2192//! and forwarded to a message broker (e.g. NATS, Kafka) so that downstream\n     7\u2192//! services can react asynchronously (scoring, analytics, notifications, \u2026).\n     8\u2192//!\n     9\u2192//! Compile-time feature flags\n    10\u2192//! \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    11\u2192//! \u2502 default      = [\"memory\"]\n    12\u2192//! \u2502 \"nats\"       = enable NATS-backed publisher/subscriber\n    13\u2192//! \u2502 \"memory\"     = enable in-memory publisher/subscriber (tests & dev)\n    14\u2192//! \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    15\u2192//! Note: add the corresponding optional dependencies to `Cargo.toml`\n    16\u2192//!\n    17\u2192//!     async-nats = { version = \"0.32\", optional = true, features = [\"tls\"] }\n    18\u2192//!     tokio      = { version = \"1.36\", features = [\"full\"] }\n    19\u2192//!     serde      = { version = \"1.0\",  features = [\"derive\"] }\n    20\u2192//!     async-trait= \"0.1\"\n    21\u2192//!     uuid       = { version = \"1.7\",  features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\n    22\u2192//!     chrono     = { version = \"0.4\",  features = [\"serde\"] }\n    23\u2192//!     tracing    = \"0.1\"\n    24\u2192//!     thiserror  = \"1.0\"\n    25\u2192//!\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192use std::fmt::Debug;\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    30\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    31\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    32\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    33\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, warn};\n    34\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/// Common failure modes that can occur when publishing or handling events.\n    37\u2192#[derive(Error, Debug)]\n    38\u2192pub enum EventBusError {\n    39\u2192    #[error(\"serialization error: {0}\")]\n    40\u2192    Serialization(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    #[cfg(feature = \"nats\")]\n    43\u2192    #[error(\"NATS error: {0}\")]\n    44\u2192    Nats(#[from] async_nats::Error),\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    #[error(\"channel send error: {0}\")]\n    47\u2192    ChannelSend(String),\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    #[error(\"handler error: {0}\")]\n    50\u2192    Handler(String),\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192/// A domain event encapsulates something that occurred within a bounded context.\n    54\u2192///\n    55\u2192/// It must be cheap to copy (hence `Clone`) and serializable (`Serialize`, `DeserializeOwned`).\n    56\u2192pub trait DomainEvent: Debug + Clone + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + Send + Sync + 'static {\n    57\u2192    /// Globally unique event name, e.g. `\"LearningPulseCreated\"`.\n    58\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str;\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192/// Envelope that adds cross-cutting metadata (id, timestamp) to an event.\n    62\u2192///\n    63\u2192/// This is the actual payload sent across the wire.\n    64\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    65\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope<E: DomainEvent> {\n    66\u2192    pub id:            Uuid,\n    67\u2192    pub occurred_at:   DateTime<Utc>,\n    68\u2192    pub aggregate_id:  Uuid,\n    69\u2192    pub payload:       E,\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192impl<E: DomainEvent> EventEnvelope<E> {\n    73\u2192    pub fn new(aggregate_id: Uuid, payload: E) -> Self {\n    74\u2192        Self {\n    75\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    76\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n    77\u2192            aggregate_id,\n    78\u2192            payload,\n    79\u2192        }\n    80\u2192    }\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192    /// Returns the subject / topic name that should be used when publishing the event.\n    83\u2192    pub fn subject(&self) -> String {\n    84\u2192        format!(\"edu.{}\", E::event_type())\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192/// Publish-only half of the event bus.\n    89\u2192///\n    90\u2192/// Services emitting events depend only on `EventPublisher`.  They do not care\n    91\u2192/// about how the events are shipped (NATS, Kafka, in-memory).\n    92\u2192#[async_trait]\n    93\u2192pub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    94\u2192    async fn publish<E: DomainEvent>(&self, envelope: EventEnvelope<E>) -> Result<(), EventBusError>;\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192/// Subscribe-side abstraction that allows registering strongly-typed handlers.\n    98\u2192///\n    99\u2192/// A handler can be any async function that takes the deserialized envelope.\n   100\u2192#[async_trait]\n   101\u2192pub trait EventSubscriber: Send + Sync {\n   102\u2192    async fn subscribe<E, F>(&self, handler: F) -> Result<(), EventBusError>\n   103\u2192    where\n   104\u2192        E: DomainEvent,\n   105\u2192        F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> futures::future::BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n   106\u2192            + Send\n   107\u2192            + Sync\n   108\u2192            + 'static;\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   112\u2192/// In-memory reference implementation (dev / tests)\n   113\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   114\u2192#[cfg(feature = \"memory\")]\n   115\u2192pub mod memory {\n   116\u2192    use super::*;\n   117\u2192    use futures::{future::BoxFuture, FutureExt};\n   118\u2192    use std::collections::HashMap;\n   119\u2192    use tokio::sync::broadcast;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    type DynSender = broadcast::Sender<Vec<u8>>;\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    /// Simple bus built on `tokio::broadcast`. Great for unit tests.\n   124\u2192    #[derive(Clone, Default)]\n   125\u2192    pub struct InMemoryBus {\n   126\u2192        channels: std::sync::Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<HashMap<&'static str, DynSender>>>,\n   127\u2192    }\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192    impl InMemoryBus {\n   130\u2192        async fn get_sender(&self, topic: &'static str) -> DynSender {\n   131\u2192            let mut map = self.channels.lock().await;\n   132\u2192            map.entry(topic)\n   133\u2192                .or_insert_with(|| broadcast::channel::<Vec<u8>>(1024).0)\n   134\u2192                .clone()\n   135\u2192        }\n   136\u2192    }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   139\u2192    impl EventPublisher for InMemoryBus {\n   140\u2192        async fn publish<E: DomainEvent>(\n   141\u2192            &self,\n   142\u2192            envelope: EventEnvelope<E>,\n   143\u2192        ) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   144\u2192            let bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&envelope)?;\n   145\u2192            let sender = self.get_sender(E::event_type()).await;\n   146\u2192            sender\n   147\u2192                .send(bytes)\n   148\u2192                .map_err(|e| EventBusError::ChannelSend(e.to_string()))?;\n   149\u2192            debug!(\n   150\u2192                \"InMemoryBus published event {} ({})\",\n   151\u2192                E::event_type(),\n   152\u2192                envelope.id\n   153\u2192            );\n   154\u2192            Ok(())\n   155\u2192        }\n   156\u2192    }\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   159\u2192    impl EventSubscriber for InMemoryBus {\n   160\u2192        async fn subscribe<E, F>(&self, handler: F) -> Result<(), EventBusError>\n   161\u2192        where\n   162\u2192            E: DomainEvent,\n   163\u2192            F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n   164\u2192                + Send\n   165\u2192                + Sync\n   166\u2192                + 'static,\n   167\u2192        {\n   168\u2192            let receiver = self.get_sender(E::event_type()).await.subscribe();\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192            tokio::spawn(async move {\n   171\u2192                let mut rx = receiver;\n   172\u2192                while let Ok(bytes) = rx.recv().await {\n   173\u2192                    match serde_json::from_slice::<EventEnvelope<E>>(&bytes) {\n   174\u2192                        Ok(env) => {\n   175\u2192                            if let Err(err) = handler(env).await {\n   176\u2192                                error!(\"event handler failed: {}\", err);\n   177\u2192                            }\n   178\u2192                        }\n   179\u2192                        Err(err) => error!(\"failed to deserialize event: {}\", err),\n   180\u2192                    }\n   181\u2192                }\n   182\u2192            });\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192            Ok(())\n   185\u2192        }\n   186\u2192    }\n   187\u2192}\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   190\u2192/// NATS implementation (production)\n   191\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   192\u2192#[cfg(feature = \"nats\")]\n   193\u2192pub mod nats {\n   194\u2192    use super::*;\n   195\u2192    use async_nats::{ConnectOptions, Connection, Subscriber};\n   196\u2192    use futures::{future::BoxFuture, StreamExt};\n   197\u2192    use std::sync::Arc;\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    /// NATS-based pub/sub using JSON payloads.\n   200\u2192    #[derive(Clone)]\n   201\u2192    pub struct NatsBus {\n   202\u2192        connection: Arc<Connection>,\n   203\u2192        subject_prefix: String,\n   204\u2192    }\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192    impl NatsBus {\n   207\u2192        /// Establish a TLS-enabled NATS connection.  Supply a comma-separated list\n   208\u2192        /// of servers (e.g. `\"nats://localhost:4222,nats://backup:4222\"`).\n   209\u2192        pub async fn new(servers: &str, subject_prefix: impl Into<String>) -> Result<Self, EventBusError> {\n   210\u2192            let conn = ConnectOptions::new()\n   211\u2192                .require_tls(true)\n   212\u2192                .connect(servers)\n   213\u2192                .await?;\n   214\u2192            Ok(Self {\n   215\u2192                connection: Arc::new(conn),\n   216\u2192                subject_prefix: subject_prefix.into(),\n   217\u2192            })\n   218\u2192        }\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192        fn compose_subject<E: DomainEvent>(&self) -> String {\n   221\u2192            format!(\"{}.{}\", self.subject_prefix, E::event_type())\n   222\u2192        }\n   223\u2192    }\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   226\u2192    impl EventPublisher for NatsBus {\n   227\u2192        async fn publish<E: DomainEvent>(\n   228\u2192            &self,\n   229\u2192            envelope: EventEnvelope<E>,\n   230\u2192        ) -> Result<(), EventBusError> {\n   231\u2192            let subject = self.compose_subject::<E>();\n   232\u2192            let data = serde_json::to_vec(&envelope)?;\n   233\u2192            self.connection.publish(subject.clone(), data.into()).await?;\n   234\u2192            info!(\n   235\u2192                \"NatsBus published event {} on '{}'\",\n   236\u2192                envelope.id, subject\n   237\u2192            );\n   238\u2192            Ok(())\n   239\u2192        }\n   240\u2192    }\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   243\u2192    impl EventSubscriber for NatsBus {\n   244\u2192        async fn subscribe<E, F>(&self, handler: F) -> Result<(), EventBusError>\n   245\u2192        where\n   246\u2192            E: DomainEvent,\n   247\u2192            F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n   248\u2192                + Send\n   249\u2192                + Sync\n   250\u2192                + 'static,\n   251\u2192        {\n   252\u2192            let subject = self.compose_subject::<E>();\n   253\u2192            let sub: Subscriber = self.connection.subscribe(subject.clone()).await?;\n   254\u2192            info!(\"NatsBus subscribed to '{}'\", subject);\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192            tokio::spawn(async move {\n   257\u2192                let mut stream = sub;\n   258\u2192                while let Some(msg) = stream.next().await {\n   259\u2192                    if let Ok(env) = serde_json::from_slice::<EventEnvelope<E>>(&msg.payload) {\n   260\u2192                        if let Err(err) = handler(env).await {\n   261\u2192                            error!(\"handler error: {}\", err);\n   262\u2192                        }\n   263\u2192                    } else {\n   264\u2192                        warn!(\"discarding invalid event payload on '{}'\", subject);\n   265\u2192                    }\n   266\u2192                }\n   267\u2192            });\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192            Ok(())\n   270\u2192        }\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192}\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   275\u2192/// Example domain events\n   276\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192/// Emitted when an instructor creates a new learning pulse (short challenge).\n   279\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   280\u2192pub struct LearningPulseCreated {\n   281\u2192    pub pulse_id:      Uuid,\n   282\u2192    pub instructor_id: Uuid,\n   283\u2192    pub title:         String,\n   284\u2192}\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192impl DomainEvent for LearningPulseCreated {\n   287\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str {\n   288\u2192        \"LearningPulseCreated\"\n   289\u2192    }\n   290\u2192}\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192/// Emitted when a student submits a response to a learning pulse.\n   293\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   294\u2192pub struct SubmissionReceived {\n   295\u2192    pub submission_id: Uuid,\n   296\u2192    pub pulse_id:      Uuid,\n   297\u2192    pub student_id:    Uuid,\n   298\u2192    pub attachment_url: Option<String>,\n   299\u2192}\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192impl DomainEvent for SubmissionReceived {\n   302\u2192    fn event_type() -> &'static str {\n   303\u2192        \"SubmissionReceived\"\n   304\u2192    }\n   305\u2192}\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   308\u2192/// Unit tests exercising the in-memory bus\n   309\u2192////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////\n   310\u2192#[cfg(all(test, feature = \"memory\"))]\n   311\u2192mod tests {\n   312\u2192    use super::*;\n   313\u2192    use futures::FutureExt;\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   316\u2192    async fn in_memory_bus_roundtrip() {\n   317\u2192        let bus = memory::InMemoryBus::default();\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192        // Subscribe to `LearningPulseCreated`\n   320\u2192        bus.subscribe::<LearningPulseCreated, _>(|env| {\n   321\u2192            async move {\n   322\u2192                assert_eq!(env.payload.title, \"Week 3 Quiz\");\n   323\u2192                Ok(())\n   324\u2192            }\n   325\u2192            .boxed()\n   326\u2192        })\n   327\u2192        .await\n   328\u2192        .unwrap();\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192        let payload = LearningPulseCreated {\n   331\u2192            pulse_id:      Uuid::new_v4(),\n   332\u2192            instructor_id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   333\u2192            title:         \"Week 3 Quiz\".to_string(),\n   334\u2192        };\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192        bus.publish(EventEnvelope::new(payload.pulse_id, payload))\n   337\u2192            .await\n   338\u2192            .unwrap();\n   339\u2192    }\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
