"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_55.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! GDPR-compliant user\u2013data erasure workflow.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! The core API stays lean by persisting the intent to erase a user\u2019s data\n     7\u2192//! and emitting an `DomainEvent::UserErasureApproved { user_id }` event that\n     8\u2192//! asynchronous workers subscribe to (search index removal, cache eviction,\n     9\u2192//! backups scrubbing, etc.).\n    10\u2192//!\n    11\u2192//! 1.  A user creates a deletion ticket (double-opt in).\n    12\u2192//! 2.  The ticket is confirmed (e.g. via e-mail or admin console).\n    13\u2192//! 3.  All downstream consumers receive a single event and perform their part\n    14\u2192//!     of the hard-delete in a time-boxed manner.\n    15\u2192//!\n    16\u2192//! This file focuses on steps 1 & 2 plus event dispatch.\n    17\u2192//!\n    18\u2192//! # Compile Features\n    19\u2192//!\n    20\u2192//! ```toml\n    21\u2192//! [dependencies]\n    22\u2192//! sqlx          = { version = \"0.7\", features = [\"postgres\", \"runtime-tokio\", \"uuid\"] }\n    23\u2192//! uuid          = { version = \"1\" }\n    24\u2192//! chrono        = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\n    25\u2192//! serde         = { version = \"1\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n    26\u2192//! async-trait   = \"0.1\"\n    27\u2192//! thiserror     = \"1\"\n    28\u2192//! tracing       = \"0.1\"\n    29\u2192//! ```\n    30\u2192//!\n    31\u2192//! NOTE: Keep the SQL migrations in `migrations/` in sync with the SQL below:\n    32\u2192//!\n    33\u2192//! ```sql\n    34\u2192//! CREATE TYPE erase_request_status AS ENUM ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected');\n    35\u2192//! CREATE TABLE erase_requests (\n    36\u2192//!     id          UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n    37\u2192//!     user_id     UUID NOT NULL,\n    38\u2192//!     created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    39\u2192//!     processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\n    40\u2192//!     status      erase_request_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    41\u2192//! );\n    42\u2192//! ```\n    43\u2192//!\n    44\u2192//! Real brokers like NATS/Kafka/RabbitMQ would live behind `EventBus`.\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192use std::sync::Arc;\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    49\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    50\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    51\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgRow, PgPool, Row};\n    52\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    53\u2192use tracing::{error, info, instrument};\n    54\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192/// Domain-level errors surfaced by the service.\n    57\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    58\u2192pub enum ErasureError {\n    59\u2192    /// Error originated from the database layer.\n    60\u2192    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n    61\u2192    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    /// A request with the given id does not exist.\n    64\u2192    #[error(\"erase request not found: {0}\")]\n    65\u2192    NotFound(Uuid),\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /// Invalid state transition attempted, e.g. approving an already approved request.\n    68\u2192    #[error(\"invalid state transition for request {0}\")]\n    69\u2192    InvalidState(Uuid),\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192    /// Any other error.\n    72\u2192    #[error(\"internal erasure service error\")]\n    73\u2192    Internal,\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192/// Convenience alias for results returned by the module.\n    77\u2192pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ErasureError>;\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192/// The legal state of a GDPR erase request.\n    80\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, sqlx::Type, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    81\u2192#[sqlx(type_name = \"erase_request_status\", rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\n    82\u2192pub enum EraseStatus {\n    83\u2192    Pending,\n    84\u2192    Approved,\n    85\u2192    Rejected,\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192/// Projection of the `erase_requests` table.\n    89\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    90\u2192pub struct EraseRequest {\n    91\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    92\u2192    pub user_id: Uuid,\n    93\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    94\u2192    pub processed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    95\u2192    pub status: EraseStatus,\n    96\u2192}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192impl<'r> sqlx::FromRow<'r, PgRow> for EraseRequest {\n    99\u2192    fn from_row(row: &PgRow) -> std::result::Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {\n   100\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   101\u2192            id: row.try_get(\"id\")?,\n   102\u2192            user_id: row.try_get(\"user_id\")?,\n   103\u2192            created_at: row.try_get(\"created_at\")?,\n   104\u2192            processed_at: row.try_get(\"processed_at\")?,\n   105\u2192            status: row.try_get(\"status\")?,\n   106\u2192        })\n   107\u2192    }\n   108\u2192}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192/// Events published onto the broker.\n   111\u2192///\n   112\u2192/// Downstream micro-services can listen for those and perform any expensive,\n   113\u2192/// potentially long-running tasks (e.g. search index removal, email revocation,\n   114\u2192/// push-notification unsubscriptions, backups purge, etc.).\n   115\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n   116\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n   117\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n   118\u2192    /// Signals that a user\u2019s data must be removed from all storages.\n   119\u2192    UserErasureApproved { user_id: Uuid },\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192/// Abstraction over an async event bus.\n   123\u2192#[async_trait]\n   124\u2192pub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n   125\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent);\n   126\u2192}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192/// No-op bus useful in tests or for local single-process deployments.\n   129\u2192pub struct LogEventBus;\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192#[async_trait]\n   132\u2192impl EventBus for LogEventBus {\n   133\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) {\n   134\u2192        info!(?event, \"publishing event (stdout)\");\n   135\u2192    }\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192/// Service responsible for GDPR erase requests.\n   139\u2192///\n   140\u2192/// Implements the repository & service layer for better testability.\n   141\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   142\u2192pub struct ErasureService<E: EventBus> {\n   143\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   144\u2192    bus: Arc<E>,\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192impl<E: EventBus> ErasureService<E> {\n   148\u2192    /// Construct a new service given a DB connection pool and an event bus.\n   149\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool, bus: Arc<E>) -> Self {\n   150\u2192        Self { pool, bus }\n   151\u2192    }\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    /// A learner requests full removal of their personal data.\n   154\u2192    ///\n   155\u2192    /// Returns the freshly persisted `EraseRequest`.\n   156\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   157\u2192    pub async fn submit_request(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<EraseRequest> {\n   158\u2192        let request_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   159\u2192        let rec = sqlx::query_as::<_, EraseRequest>(\n   160\u2192            r#\"\n   161\u2192            INSERT INTO erase_requests (id, user_id)\n   162\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2)\n   163\u2192            RETURNING *\n   164\u2192            \"#,\n   165\u2192        )\n   166\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   167\u2192        .bind(user_id)\n   168\u2192        .fetch_one(&self.pool)\n   169\u2192        .await?;\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192        Ok(rec)\n   172\u2192    }\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    /// Approve a pending request. Downstream hard-delete kicks in via event.\n   175\u2192    ///\n   176\u2192    /// Emits `DomainEvent::UserErasureApproved { user_id }`.\n   177\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   178\u2192    pub async fn approve_request(&self, request_id: Uuid) -> Result<EraseRequest> {\n   179\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192        // Validate state & transition.\n   182\u2192        let current: Option<EraseRequest> = sqlx::query_as(\n   183\u2192            r#\"\n   184\u2192            SELECT * FROM erase_requests\n   185\u2192            WHERE id = $1 FOR UPDATE\n   186\u2192            \"#,\n   187\u2192        )\n   188\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   189\u2192        .fetch_optional(&mut *tx)\n   190\u2192        .await?;\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192        let mut current = match current {\n   193\u2192            Some(rec) => rec,\n   194\u2192            None => return Err(ErasureError::NotFound(request_id)),\n   195\u2192        };\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192        if current.status != EraseStatus::Pending {\n   198\u2192            return Err(ErasureError::InvalidState(request_id));\n   199\u2192        }\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192        current.status = EraseStatus::Approved;\n   202\u2192        current.processed_at = Some(Utc::now());\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192        // Persist changes.\n   205\u2192        let updated: EraseRequest = sqlx::query_as(\n   206\u2192            r#\"\n   207\u2192            UPDATE erase_requests\n   208\u2192            SET status = 'approved', processed_at = NOW()\n   209\u2192            WHERE id = $1\n   210\u2192            RETURNING *\n   211\u2192            \"#,\n   212\u2192        )\n   213\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   214\u2192        .fetch_one(&mut *tx)\n   215\u2192        .await?;\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192        // Fire-and-forget event. We do not propagate bus errors upwards to keep\n   220\u2192        // transactional guarantees on DB independent of broker availability.\n   221\u2192        let user_id = updated.user_id;\n   222\u2192        let event = DomainEvent::UserErasureApproved { user_id };\n   223\u2192        self.bus.publish(event).await;\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192        Ok(updated)\n   226\u2192    }\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    /// Reject a request (e.g. user revokes the deletion or legal reasons).\n   229\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self))]\n   230\u2192    pub async fn reject_request(&self, request_id: Uuid) -> Result<EraseRequest> {\n   231\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192        let current: Option<EraseRequest> = sqlx::query_as(\n   234\u2192            r#\"\n   235\u2192            SELECT * FROM erase_requests\n   236\u2192            WHERE id = $1 FOR UPDATE\n   237\u2192            \"#,\n   238\u2192        )\n   239\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   240\u2192        .fetch_optional(&mut *tx)\n   241\u2192        .await?;\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192        let mut current = match current {\n   244\u2192            Some(rec) => rec,\n   245\u2192            None => return Err(ErasureError::NotFound(request_id)),\n   246\u2192        };\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192        if current.status != EraseStatus::Pending {\n   249\u2192            return Err(ErasureError::InvalidState(request_id));\n   250\u2192        }\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192        current.status = EraseStatus::Rejected;\n   253\u2192        current.processed_at = Some(Utc::now());\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192        let updated: EraseRequest = sqlx::query_as(\n   256\u2192            r#\"\n   257\u2192            UPDATE erase_requests\n   258\u2192            SET status = 'rejected', processed_at = NOW()\n   259\u2192            WHERE id = $1\n   260\u2192            RETURNING *\n   261\u2192            \"#,\n   262\u2192        )\n   263\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   264\u2192        .fetch_one(&mut *tx)\n   265\u2192        .await?;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192        Ok(updated)\n   270\u2192    }\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192    /// Retrieve a single request or `None` if it does not exist.\n   273\u2192    pub async fn get_request(&self, request_id: Uuid) -> std::result::Result<Option<EraseRequest>, sqlx::Error> {\n   274\u2192        let rec = sqlx::query_as::<_, EraseRequest>(\n   275\u2192            \"SELECT * FROM erase_requests WHERE id = $1\",\n   276\u2192        )\n   277\u2192        .bind(request_id)\n   278\u2192        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n   279\u2192        .await?;\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192        Ok(rec)\n   282\u2192    }\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192    /// List requests, optionally filtered by `status`.\n   285\u2192    pub async fn list_requests(\n   286\u2192        &self,\n   287\u2192        status: Option<EraseStatus>,\n   288\u2192        limit: i64,\n   289\u2192        offset: i64,\n   290\u2192    ) -> std::result::Result<Vec<EraseRequest>, sqlx::Error> {\n   291\u2192        let query = match status {\n   292\u2192            Some(_) => \"\n   293\u2192                SELECT * FROM erase_requests\n   294\u2192                WHERE status = $1\n   295\u2192                ORDER BY created_at DESC\n   296\u2192                LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3\",\n   297\u2192            None => \"\n   298\u2192                SELECT * FROM erase_requests\n   299\u2192                ORDER BY created_at DESC\n   300\u2192                LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2\",\n   301\u2192        };\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192        let rows = if let Some(status) = status {\n   304\u2192            sqlx::query_as::<_, EraseRequest>(query)\n   305\u2192                .bind(status)\n   306\u2192                .bind(limit)\n   307\u2192                .bind(offset)\n   308\u2192                .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n   309\u2192                .await?\n   310\u2192        } else {\n   311\u2192            sqlx::query_as::<_, EraseRequest>(query)\n   312\u2192                .bind(limit)\n   313\u2192                .bind(offset)\n   314\u2192                .fetch_all(&self.pool)\n   315\u2192                .await?\n   316\u2192        };\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192        Ok(rows)\n   319\u2192    }\n   320\u2192}\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192/// Example of wiring the service into the outer application layer.\n   323\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   324\u2192mod tests {\n   325\u2192    use super::*;\n   326\u2192    use sqlx::{Connection, Executor, PgConnection};\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192    struct TestBus;\n   329\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   330\u2192    impl EventBus for TestBus {\n   331\u2192        async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) {\n   332\u2192            info!(\"test bus published: {:?}\", event);\n   333\u2192        }\n   334\u2192    }\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    async fn setup_db() -> PgPool {\n   337\u2192        // Spin up a temporary in-memory Postgres or use a test container.\n   338\u2192        let url = std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n   339\u2192            .unwrap_or_else(|_| \"postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost/edupulse_test\".into());\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192        let mut conn = PgConnection::connect(&url).await.unwrap();\n   342\u2192        // Clean table between tests.\n   343\u2192        conn.execute(\"TRUNCATE TABLE erase_requests\").await.unwrap();\n   344\u2192        drop(conn);\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192        PgPool::connect(&url).await.unwrap()\n   347\u2192    }\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   350\u2192    async fn roundtrip_submit_and_approve() {\n   351\u2192        let pool = setup_db().await;\n   352\u2192        let bus = Arc::new(TestBus);\n   353\u2192        let service = ErasureService::new(pool, bus);\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192        let user_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   356\u2192        let req = service.submit_request(user_id).await.unwrap();\n   357\u2192        assert_eq!(req.status, EraseStatus::Pending);\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192        let approved = service.approve_request(req.id).await.unwrap();\n   360\u2192        assert_eq!(approved.status, EraseStatus::Approved);\n   361\u2192        assert!(approved.processed_at.is_some());\n   362\u2192    }\n   363\u2192}\n   364\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be Persist `LearningPulse` aggregates in the relational store via `sqlx` (repository pattern).\n     8\u2192//! 2. Publish canonical domain events to the event bus (NATS) after successful commits.\n     9\u2192//! 3. Expose an application service API that the HTTP layer can invoke.\n    10\u2192//!\n    11\u2192//! NOTE:  \n    12\u2192//! \u2022 The module is self-contained so it can be copy-pasted into the workspace until proper\n    13\u2192//!   crate boundaries are laid out.  \n    14\u2192//! \u2022 PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    32\u2192pub struct PulseId(Uuid);\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    35\u2192pub struct UserId(Uuid);\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192impl PulseId {\n    38\u2192    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    39\u2192        Self(Uuid::new_v4())\n    40\u2192    }\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192impl UserId {\n    43\u2192    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    44\u2192        Self(Uuid::new_v4())\n    45\u2192    }\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192/// Aggregate root\n    49\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n    50\u2192pub struct LearningPulse {\n    51\u2192    pub id: PulseId,\n    52\u2192    pub author_id: UserId,\n    53\u2192    pub title: String,\n    54\u2192    pub body: String,\n    55\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    56\u2192    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192impl LearningPulse {\n    60\u2192    pub fn new(author_id: UserId, title: impl Into<String>, body: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n    61\u2192        let now = Utc::now();\n    62\u2192        Self {\n    63\u2192            id: PulseId::new(),\n    64\u2192            author_id,\n    65\u2192            title: title.into(),\n    66\u2192            body: body.into(),\n    67\u2192            created_at: now,\n    68\u2192            updated_at: now,\n    69\u2192        }\n    70\u2192    }\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/// Canonical domain events ----------------------------------------------------\n    74\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    75\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n    76\u2192pub enum PulseEvent {\n    77\u2192    PulseCreated(PulseCreated),\n    78\u2192    PulseUpdated(PulseUpdated),\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    82\u2192pub struct PulseCreated {\n    83\u2192    pub pulse_id: PulseId,\n    84\u2192    pub author_id: UserId,\n    85\u2192    pub title: String,\n    86\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    90\u2192pub struct PulseUpdated {\n    91\u2192    pub pulse_id: PulseId,\n    92\u2192    pub title: String,\n    93\u2192    pub body: String,\n    94\u2192    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192/// Repository abstraction -----------------------------------------------------\n    98\u2192#[async_trait]\n    99\u2192pub trait PulseRepository: Send + Sync + 'static {\n   100\u2192    async fn insert(\n   101\u2192        &self,\n   102\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   103\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   104\u2192    ) -> Result<(), RepoError>;\n   105\u2192    async fn update(\n   106\u2192        &self,\n   107\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   108\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   109\u2192    ) -> Result<(), RepoError>;\n   110\u2192    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: PulseId) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>, RepoError>;\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   114\u2192pub enum RepoError {\n   115\u2192    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n   116\u2192    Sqlx(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   117\u2192}\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192/// Postgres implementation (sqlx) --------------------------------------------\n   120\u2192pub struct PostgresPulseRepository {\n   121\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   122\u2192}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192impl PostgresPulseRepository {\n   125\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   126\u2192        Self { pool }\n   127\u2192    }\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192#[async_trait]\n   131\u2192impl PulseRepository for PostgresPulseRepository {\n   132\u2192    async fn insert(\n   133\u2192        &self,\n   134\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   135\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   136\u2192    ) -> Result<(), RepoError> {\n   137\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   138\u2192            r#\"\n   139\u2192            INSERT INTO learning_pulse(id, author_id, title, body, created_at, updated_at)\n   140\u2192            VALUES($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n   141\u2192            \"#,\n   142\u2192            pulse.id.0,\n   143\u2192            pulse.author_id.0,\n   144\u2192            pulse.title,\n   145\u2192            pulse.body,\n   146\u2192            pulse.created_at,\n   147\u2192            pulse.updated_at\n   148\u2192        )\n   149\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   150\u2192        .await?;\n   151\u2192        Ok(())\n   152\u2192    }\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    async fn update(\n   155\u2192        &self,\n   156\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   157\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   158\u2192    ) -> Result<(), RepoError> {\n   159\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   160\u2192            r#\"\n   161\u2192            UPDATE learning_pulse\n   162\u2192            SET title = $2, body = $3, updated_at = $4\n   163\u2192            WHERE id = $1\n   164\u2192            \"#,\n   165\u2192            pulse.id.0,\n   166\u2192            pulse.title,\n   167\u2192            pulse.body,\n   168\u2192            pulse.updated_at\n   169\u2192        )\n   170\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   171\u2192        .await?;\n   172\u2192        Ok(())\n   173\u2192    }\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: PulseId) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>, RepoError> {\n   176\u2192        let rec = sqlx::query!(\n   177\u2192            r#\"\n   178\u2192            SELECT id, author_id, title, body, created_at, updated_at\n   179\u2192            FROM learning_pulse\n   180\u2192            WHERE id = $1\n   181\u2192            \"#,\n   182\u2192            id.0\n   183\u2192        )\n   184\u2192        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n   185\u2192        .await?;\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192        Ok(rec.map(|row| LearningPulse {\n   188\u2192            id: PulseId(row.id),\n   189\u2192            author_id: UserId(row.author_id),\n   190\u2192            title: row.title,\n   191\u2192            body: row.body,\n   192\u2192            created_at: row.created_at,\n   193\u2192            updated_at: row.updated_at,\n   194\u2192        }))\n   195\u2192    }\n   196\u2192}\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192/// Event publisher abstraction ------------------------------------------------\n   199\u2192#[async_trait]\n   200\u2192pub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync + 'static {\n   201\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublishError>;\n   202\u2192}\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   205\u2192pub enum PublishError {\n   206\u2192    #[error(\"serialization error: {0}\")]\n   207\u2192    Serialize(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n   208\u2192    #[error(\"nats I/O error: {0}\")]\n   209\u2192    Nats(#[from] async_nats::Error),\n   210\u2192}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192/// JetStream-backed publisher (NATS) -----------------------------------------\n   213\u2192pub struct NatsPublisher {\n   214\u2192    subject: String,\n   215\u2192    jetstream: jetstream::Context,\n   216\u2192    acknowledger: mpsc::Sender<PublishAck>, // track acks in background task\n   217\u2192}\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192impl NatsPublisher {\n   220\u2192    pub async fn new(\n   221\u2192        nats_url: &str,\n   222\u2192        subject: impl Into<String>,\n   223\u2192    ) -> Result<Self, async_nats::Error> {\n   224\u2192        let client = async_nats::connect(nats_url).await?;\n   225\u2192        let jetstream = jetstream::new(client);\n   226\u2192        let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel::<PublishAck>(32);\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192        // Spawn a background task that logs JetStream publish ACKs.\n   229\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   230\u2192            while let Some(ack) = rx.recv().await {\n   231\u2192                match ack.await {\n   232\u2192                    Ok(meta) => debug!(\"Event persisted to stream: {meta:?}\"),\n   233\u2192                    Err(err) => error!(\"Failed to persist event: {err}\"),\n   234\u2192                }\n   235\u2192            }\n   236\u2192        });\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   239\u2192            subject: subject.into(),\n   240\u2192            jetstream,\n   241\u2192            acknowledger: tx,\n   242\u2192        })\n   243\u2192    }\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192#[async_trait]\n   247\u2192impl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n   248\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: &PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n   249\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(event)?;\n   250\u2192        let ack = self\n   251\u2192            .jetstream\n   252\u2192            .publish(self.subject.to_owned(), payload.into())\n   253\u2192            .await?;\n   254\u2192        // Fire-and-forget tracking of the ack so we don\u2019t block callers.\n   255\u2192        let _ = self.acknowledger.send(ack).await;\n   256\u2192        Ok(())\n   257\u2192    }\n   258\u2192}\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192/// Service layer --------------------------------------------------------------\n   261\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   262\u2192pub struct LearningPulseService<R: PulseRepository, P: EventPublisher> {\n   263\u2192    repo: R,\n   264\u2192    publisher: P,\n   265\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   266\u2192}\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   269\u2192pub enum ServiceError {\n   270\u2192    #[error(transparent)]\n   271\u2192    Repo(#[from] RepoError),\n   272\u2192    #[error(transparent)]\n   273\u2192    Db(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   274\u2192    #[error(transparent)]\n   275\u2192    Publish(#[from] PublishError),\n   276\u2192    #[error(\"pulse not found\")]\n   277\u2192    NotFound,\n   278\u2192}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192impl<R: PulseRepository, P: EventPublisher> LearningPulseService<R, P> {\n   281\u2192    pub fn new(repo: R, publisher: P, pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   282\u2192        Self {\n   283\u2192            repo,\n   284\u2192            publisher,\n   285\u2192            pool,\n   286\u2192        }\n   287\u2192    }\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192    /// Create a new learning pulse and publish a `pulse_created` event.\n   290\u2192    pub async fn create_pulse(\n   291\u2192        &self,\n   292\u2192        author_id: UserId,\n   293\u2192        title: impl Into<String>,\n   294\u2192        body: impl Into<String>,\n   295\u2192    ) -> Result<PulseId, ServiceError> {\n   296\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   297\u2192        let pulse = LearningPulse::new(author_id, title, body);\n   298\u2192        self.repo.insert(&mut tx, &pulse).await?;\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        let event = PulseEvent::PulseCreated(PulseCreated {\n   301\u2192            pulse_id: pulse.id,\n   302\u2192            author_id: pulse.author_id,\n   303\u2192            title: pulse.title.clone(),\n   304\u2192            created_at: pulse.created_at,\n   305\u2192        });\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   308\u2192        self.publisher.publish(&event).await?;\n   309\u2192        info!(\"Created new pulse {} by {:?}\", pulse.id.0, author_id.0);\n   310\u2192        Ok(pulse.id)\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    /// Update existing pulse; returns error if pulse does not exist.\n   314\u2192    pub async fn update_pulse(\n   315\u2192        &self,\n   316\u2192        pulse_id: PulseId,\n   317\u2192        title: impl Into<String>,\n   318\u2192        body: impl Into<String>,\n   319\u2192    ) -> Result<(), ServiceError> {\n   320\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   321\u2192        let Some(mut pulse) = self.repo.find_by_id(pulse_id).await? else {\n   322\u2192            return Err(ServiceError::NotFound);\n   323\u2192        };\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192        pulse.title = title.into();\n   326\u2192        pulse.body = body.into();\n   327\u2192        pulse.updated_at = Utc::now();\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192        self.repo.update(&mut tx, &pulse).await?;\n   330\u2192        let event = PulseEvent::PulseUpdated(PulseUpdated {\n   331\u2192            pulse_id,\n   332\u2192            title: pulse.title.clone(),\n   333\u2192            body: pulse.body.clone(),\n   334\u2192            updated_at: pulse.updated_at,\n   335\u2192        });\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   338\u2192        self.publisher.publish(&event).await?;\n   339\u2192        info!(\"Updated pulse {}\", pulse_id.0);\n   340\u2192        Ok(())\n   341\u2192    }\n   342\u2192}\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192/// Database schema migrations -------------------------------------------------\n   345\u2192///\n   346\u2192/// WARNING: In production, migrations must be applied using `sqlx migrate`.\n   347\u2192/// The following helper ensures that the required table exists when running\n   348\u2192/// tests or the local binary. It will **not** run in production clusters.\n   349\u2192pub async fn ensure_schema(pool: &PgPool) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n   350\u2192    let mut builder: QueryBuilder<Postgres> = QueryBuilder::new(\n   351\u2192        r#\"\n   352\u2192        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learning_pulse(\n   353\u2192            id UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n   354\u2192            author_id UUID NOT NULL,\n   355\u2192            title TEXT NOT NULL,\n   356\u2192            body TEXT NOT NULL,\n   357\u2192            created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\n   358\u2192            updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL\n   359\u2192        );\n   360\u2192        \"#,\n   361\u2192    );\n   362\u2192    builder.build().execute(pool).await?;\n   363\u2192    Ok(())\n   364\u2192}\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192/// ------------------------------ Tests ---------------------------------------\n   367\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   368\u2192mod tests {\n   369\u2192    use super::*;\n   370\u2192    use std::time::Duration;\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192    struct InMemoryPublisher {\n   373\u2192        tx: mpsc::Sender<PulseEvent>,\n   374\u2192    }\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n   377\u2192    impl EventPublisher for InMemoryPublisher {\n   378\u2192        async fn publish(&self, event: &PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n   379\u2192            self.tx\n   380\u2192                .send(event.clone())\n   381\u2192                .await\n   382\u2192                .map_err(|_| async_nats::Error::from(std::io::ErrorKind::BrokenPipe))?;\n   383\u2192            Ok(())\n   384\u2192        }\n   385\u2192    }\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192    #[sqlx::test(migrations = \"../../migrations\")]\n   388\u2192    async fn pulse_crud(pool: PgPool) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   389\u2192        ensure_schema(&pool).await?;\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192        let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(8);\n   392\u2192        let repo = PostgresPulseRepository::new(pool.clone());\n   393\u2192        let service = LearningPulseService::new(\n   394\u2192            repo,\n   395\u2192            InMemoryPublisher { tx },\n   396\u2192            pool.clone(),\n   397\u2192        );\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192        let author = UserId::new();\n   400\u2192        let pulse_id = service\n   401\u2192            .create_pulse(author, \"Test Title\", \"Initial Body\")\n   402\u2192            .await?;\n   403\u2192\n   404\u2192        // Wait for the async publish to propagate in the test.\n   405\u2192        tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), rx.recv())\n   406\u2192            .await?\n   407\u2192            .expect(\"event should be published\");\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192        service\n   410\u2192            .update_pulse(pulse_id, \"New Title\", \"Updated Body\")\n   411\u2192            .await?;\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192        Ok(())\n   414\u2192    }\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
94. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_62.txt"

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_62.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Learning-pulse domain service, repository implementation and event publisher\n     5\u2192//! for the EduPulse Live platform.\n     6\u2192//!\n     7\u2192//! This module demonstrates how the platform persists domain aggregates\n     8\u2192//! transactionally while emitting integration events to an external broker\n     9\u2192//! (NATS in this example) using the Outbox pattern.\n    10\u2192//!\n    11\u2192//! The code is *production-grade*: it is fully asynchronous, uses connection\n    12\u2192//! pooling, structured logging, typed errors, unit-tested boundaries, and is\n    13\u2192//! ready for dependency injection through traits.\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192use std::sync::Arc;\n    16\u2192use std::time::Duration;\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192use async_nats::{self, jetstream};\n    19\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    20\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    21\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    22\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgQueryResult, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\n    23\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    24\u2192use tokio::time::timeout;\n    25\u2192use tracing::{error, info, instrument, warn};\n    26\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192/* ============================================================================\n    29\u2192   Domain\n    30\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192/// Aggregate root representing a short activity or challenge created\n    33\u2192/// by an educator.\n    34\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    35\u2192pub struct LearningPulse {\n    36\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    37\u2192    pub author_id: Uuid,\n    38\u2192    pub title: String,\n    39\u2192    pub description: String,\n    40\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    41\u2192    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    42\u2192    pub archived: bool,\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192impl LearningPulse {\n    46\u2192    pub fn new(author_id: Uuid, title: impl Into<String>, description: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n    47\u2192        Self {\n    48\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    49\u2192            author_id,\n    50\u2192            title: title.into(),\n    51\u2192            description: description.into(),\n    52\u2192            created_at: Utc::now(),\n    53\u2192            due_at: None,\n    54\u2192            archived: false,\n    55\u2192        }\n    56\u2192    }\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192/// Represents a learner submission / response to a `LearningPulse`.\n    60\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    61\u2192pub struct PulseResponse {\n    62\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    63\u2192    pub pulse_id: Uuid,\n    64\u2192    pub responder_id: Uuid,\n    65\u2192    pub content: String,\n    66\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192impl PulseResponse {\n    70\u2192    pub fn new(pulse_id: Uuid, responder_id: Uuid, content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n    71\u2192        Self {\n    72\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    73\u2192            pulse_id,\n    74\u2192            responder_id,\n    75\u2192            content: content.into(),\n    76\u2192            created_at: Utc::now(),\n    77\u2192        }\n    78\u2192    }\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192/// Integration events emitted by the learning-pulse bounded context.\n    82\u2192///\n    83\u2192/// They are serialised and pushed to the outbox table as *part* of the same\n    84\u2192/// database transaction that handles the write on the aggregate, preventing\n    85\u2192/// the dreaded \u201clost updates\u201d or \u201czombie messages\u201d scenario.\n    86\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    87\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"payload\")]\n    88\u2192pub enum PulseEvent {\n    89\u2192    PulseCreated {\n    90\u2192        pulse_id: Uuid,\n    91\u2192        author_id: Uuid,\n    92\u2192        occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    93\u2192    },\n    94\u2192    PulseResponded {\n    95\u2192        pulse_id: Uuid,\n    96\u2192        response_id: Uuid,\n    97\u2192        responder_id: Uuid,\n    98\u2192        occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    99\u2192    },\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192/* ============================================================================\n   103\u2192   Errors\n   104\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   107\u2192pub enum ServiceError {\n   108\u2192    #[error(\"Validation error: {0}\")]\n   109\u2192    Validation(String),\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192    #[error(\"Repository error\")]\n   112\u2192    Repository(#[from] RepositoryError),\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    #[error(\"Publisher error\")]\n   115\u2192    Publisher(#[from] PublisherError),\n   116\u2192}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   119\u2192pub enum RepositoryError {\n   120\u2192    #[error(\"Database error: {0}\")]\n   121\u2192    Sqlx(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    #[error(\"Pulse not found\")]\n   124\u2192    NotFound,\n   125\u2192}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n   128\u2192pub enum PublisherError {\n   129\u2192    #[error(\"NATS error: {0}\")]\n   130\u2192    Nats(#[from] async_nats::Error),\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    #[error(\"Timeout after {0:?}\")]\n   133\u2192    Timeout(Duration),\n   134\u2192}\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192/* ============================================================================\n   137\u2192   Repository\n   138\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192/// Abstraction over persistence of pulses and responses.\n   141\u2192///\n   142\u2192/// Allows the service layer to remain decoupled from any concrete storage\n   143\u2192/// technology (Postgres, MySQL, DynamoDB, etc.).\n   144\u2192#[async_trait]\n   145\u2192pub trait PulseRepository: Send + Sync {\n   146\u2192    async fn insert_pulse(\n   147\u2192        &self,\n   148\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   149\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   150\u2192    ) -> Result<PgQueryResult, RepositoryError>;\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192    async fn insert_response(\n   153\u2192        &self,\n   154\u2192        response: &PulseResponse,\n   155\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   156\u2192    ) -> Result<PgQueryResult, RepositoryError>;\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192/// Postgres implementation using `sqlx`.\n   160\u2192pub struct PostgresPulseRepository {\n   161\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192impl PostgresPulseRepository {\n   165\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   166\u2192        Self { pool }\n   167\u2192    }\n   168\u2192}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192#[async_trait]\n   171\u2192impl PulseRepository for PostgresPulseRepository {\n   172\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(pulse_id = %pulse.id))]\n   173\u2192    async fn insert_pulse(\n   174\u2192        &self,\n   175\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   176\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   177\u2192    ) -> Result<PgQueryResult, RepositoryError> {\n   178\u2192        let res = sqlx::query!(\n   179\u2192            r#\"\n   180\u2192            INSERT INTO learning_pulse \n   181\u2192                (id, author_id, title, description, created_at, due_at, archived)\n   182\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)\n   183\u2192            \"#,\n   184\u2192            pulse.id,\n   185\u2192            pulse.author_id,\n   186\u2192            pulse.title,\n   187\u2192            pulse.description,\n   188\u2192            pulse.created_at,\n   189\u2192            pulse.due_at,\n   190\u2192            pulse.archived,\n   191\u2192        )\n   192\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   193\u2192        .await?;\n   194\u2192        Ok(res)\n   195\u2192    }\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(response_id = %response.id, pulse_id = %response.pulse_id))]\n   198\u2192    async fn insert_response(\n   199\u2192        &self,\n   200\u2192        response: &PulseResponse,\n   201\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   202\u2192    ) -> Result<PgQueryResult, RepositoryError> {\n   203\u2192        let res = sqlx::query!(\n   204\u2192            r#\"\n   205\u2192            INSERT INTO pulse_response\n   206\u2192                (id, pulse_id, responder_id, content, created_at)\n   207\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n   208\u2192            \"#,\n   209\u2192            response.id,\n   210\u2192            response.pulse_id,\n   211\u2192            response.responder_id,\n   212\u2192            response.content,\n   213\u2192            response.created_at,\n   214\u2192        )\n   215\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   216\u2192        .await?;\n   217\u2192        Ok(res)\n   218\u2192    }\n   219\u2192}\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192/* ============================================================================\n   222\u2192   Event Publisher\n   223\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192/// Publishes integration events to the rest of the platform.\n   226\u2192///\n   227\u2192/// The trait is implemented behind a thin wrapper so it can be mocked\n   228\u2192/// in unit tests.\n   229\u2192#[async_trait]\n   230\u2192pub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n   231\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublisherError>;\n   232\u2192}\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192/// NATS implementation (JetStream) with *at-least-once* guarantees.\n   235\u2192pub struct NatsPublisher {\n   236\u2192    jetstream: jetstream::Context,\n   237\u2192    subject: String,\n   238\u2192    timeout: Duration,\n   239\u2192}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192impl NatsPublisher {\n   242\u2192    pub async fn new(nats_url: &str, subject: impl Into<String>) -> Result<Self, PublisherError> {\n   243\u2192        let client = async_nats::connect(nats_url).await?;\n   244\u2192        let jetstream = jetstream::new(client);\n   245\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   246\u2192            jetstream,\n   247\u2192            subject: subject.into(),\n   248\u2192            timeout: Duration::from_secs(3),\n   249\u2192        })\n   250\u2192    }\n   251\u2192}\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192#[async_trait]\n   254\u2192impl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n   255\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, fields(event = ?event))]\n   256\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublisherError> {\n   257\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(&event).expect(\"serialization never fails\");\n   258\u2192        let publish_fut = self\n   259\u2192            .jetstream\n   260\u2192            .publish(&self.subject, payload.into())\n   261\u2192            .await\n   262\u2192            .map(|_| ());\n   263\u2192        timeout(self.timeout, publish_fut)\n   264\u2192            .await\n   265\u2192            .map_err(|_| PublisherError::Timeout(self.timeout))??;\n   266\u2192        Ok(())\n   267\u2192    }\n   268\u2192}\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192/* ============================================================================\n   271\u2192   Service Layer\n   272\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192pub struct LearningPulseService<R: PulseRepository, P: EventPublisher> {\n   275\u2192    repo: Arc<R>,\n   276\u2192    publisher: Arc<P>,\n   277\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   278\u2192}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192impl<R: PulseRepository, P: EventPublisher> LearningPulseService<R, P> {\n   281\u2192    pub fn new(repo: Arc<R>, publisher: Arc<P>, pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   282\u2192        Self {\n   283\u2192            repo,\n   284\u2192            publisher,\n   285\u2192            pool,\n   286\u2192        }\n   287\u2192    }\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192    /// Creates a new learning pulse atomically and emits a `PulseCreated` event.\n   290\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, err)]\n   291\u2192    pub async fn create_pulse(\n   292\u2192        &self,\n   293\u2192        author_id: Uuid,\n   294\u2192        title: String,\n   295\u2192        description: String,\n   296\u2192    ) -> Result<LearningPulse, ServiceError> {\n   297\u2192        if title.trim().is_empty() {\n   298\u2192            return Err(ServiceError::Validation(\"Title cannot be empty\".into()));\n   299\u2192        }\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192        let pulse = LearningPulse::new(author_id, title, description);\n   302\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192        self.repo.insert_pulse(&pulse, &mut tx).await?;\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192        // Outbox pattern \u2014 store the event in DB to be picked up by a\n   307\u2192        // relay. For demo, we publish directly **within** the txn boundary\n   308\u2192        // (not recommended in prod, but fine for showcase).\n   309\u2192        let event = PulseEvent::PulseCreated {\n   310\u2192            pulse_id: pulse.id,\n   311\u2192            author_id: pulse.author_id,\n   312\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   313\u2192        };\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192        self.publisher.publish(event).await?;\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192        Ok(pulse)\n   320\u2192    }\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192    /// Adds a learner response and emits a `PulseResponded` event.\n   323\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, err)]\n   324\u2192    pub async fn respond_to_pulse(\n   325\u2192        &self,\n   326\u2192        pulse_id: Uuid,\n   327\u2192        responder_id: Uuid,\n   328\u2192        content: String,\n   329\u2192    ) -> Result<PulseResponse, ServiceError> {\n   330\u2192        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n   331\u2192            return Err(ServiceError::Validation(\"Content cannot be empty\".into()));\n   332\u2192        }\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192        let response = PulseResponse::new(pulse_id, responder_id, content);\n   335\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192        self.repo.insert_response(&response, &mut tx).await?;\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192        let event = PulseEvent::PulseResponded {\n   340\u2192            pulse_id,\n   341\u2192            response_id: response.id,\n   342\u2192            responder_id,\n   343\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   344\u2192        };\n   345\u2192        self.publisher.publish(event).await?;\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192        Ok(response)\n   350\u2192    }\n   351\u2192}\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192/* ============================================================================\n   354\u2192   Tests\n   355\u2192   ========================================================================= */\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   358\u2192mod tests {\n   359\u2192    use super::*;\n   360\u2192    use sqlx::{migrate::Migrator, PgPoolOptions};\n   361\u2192    use std::env;\n   362\u2192    use std::path::Path;\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192    static MIGRATOR: Migrator = sqlx::migrate!(); // assumes migrations are in /migrations\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192    struct DummyPublisher;\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   369\u2192    impl EventPublisher for DummyPublisher {\n   370\u2192        async fn publish(&self, _event: PulseEvent) -> Result<(), PublisherError> {\n   371\u2192            Ok(())\n   372\u2192        }\n   373\u2192    }\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   376\u2192    async fn integration_create_and_respond() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   377\u2192        // Set DATABASE_URL env var for test DB (e.g. in CI service)\n   378\u2192        let db_url =\n   379\u2192            env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\").expect(\"DATABASE_URL env var must be set for tests\");\n   380\u2192        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   381\u2192            .max_connections(5)\n   382\u2192            .connect(&db_url)\n   383\u2192            .await?;\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192        // Ensure schema is up-to-date (idempotent)\n   386\u2192        MIGRATOR.run(&pool).await?;\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192        let repo = Arc::new(PostgresPulseRepository::new(pool.clone()));\n   389\u2192        let publisher = Arc::new(DummyPublisher {});\n   390\u2192        let service = LearningPulseService::new(repo, publisher, pool.clone());\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192        let author_id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   393\u2192        let pulse = service\n   394\u2192            .create_pulse(author_id, \"Fibonacci Kata\".into(), \"Solve it in Rust\".into())\n   395\u2192            .await?;\n   396\u2192        assert_eq!(pulse.title, \"Fibonacci Kata\");\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192        let response = service\n   399\u2192            .respond_to_pulse(pulse.id, Uuid::new_v4(), \"Here is my solution\".into())\n   400\u2192            .await?;\n   401\u2192        assert_eq!(response.pulse_id, pulse.id);\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192        Ok(())\n   404\u2192    }\n   405\u2192}\n   406\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_45.txt"

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_45.rs\n     3\u2192//! -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n     4\u2192//! GDPR\u2013Ready Event\u2013Sourced Erasure Service\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! This module lives inside EduPulse Live\u2019s core and is responsible for:\n     7\u2192//!   1. Persisting domain events in a Postgres event-store.\n     8\u2192//!   2. Publishing the events to the message broker for downstream processing.\n     9\u2192//!   3. Handling GDPR-related commands (`EraseUser`) by producing *soft-delete*\n    10\u2192//!      events that cascading services understand (search index pruning,\n    11\u2192//!      cache eviction, file storage tomb-stones, etc.).\n    12\u2192//!\n    13\u2192//! The implementation follows the Repository + Service pattern so that the\n    14\u2192//! application layer can depend on abstractions, making it easy to embed in an\n    15\u2192//! Actix-Web REST handler or a gRPC fa\u00e7ade.\n    16\u2192//!\n    17\u2192//! Note: the code is written for illustrative purposes, focusing on correctness\n    18\u2192//! and idiomatic Rust rather than compile-time completeness in every edge case.\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    23\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    24\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    25\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\n    26\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    27\u2192use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};\n    28\u2192use tracing::{error, info, instrument};\n    29\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    32\u2192// Domain API\n    33\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192/// High-level commands that can be issued to the GDPR service.\n    36\u2192#[derive(Debug)]\n    37\u2192pub enum GdprCommand {\n    38\u2192    /// Permanently & irreversibly remove PII for the given user account.\n    39\u2192    EraseUser {\n    40\u2192        requesting_admin_id: Uuid,\n    41\u2192        target_user_id: Uuid,\n    42\u2192        reason: String,\n    43\u2192    },\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/// Top-level domain events.\n    47\u2192///\n    48\u2192/// All events SHOULD be append-only; compensating actions must be expressed as\n    49\u2192/// new events, never by mutating existing records.\n    50\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    51\u2192#[serde(tag = \"type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n    52\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n    53\u2192    /// An irreversible erasure was requested and queued.\n    54\u2192    UserErasureRequested {\n    55\u2192        target_user_id: Uuid,\n    56\u2192        requesting_admin_id: Uuid,\n    57\u2192        reason: String,\n    58\u2192    },\n    59\u2192    /// All PII for a user is now anonymised.\n    60\u2192    UserErased {\n    61\u2192        target_user_id: Uuid,\n    62\u2192        erased_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    63\u2192    },\n    64\u2192    // \u2026 other domain events live elsewhere\n    65\u2192}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192/// Metadata wrapper around a DomainEvent, useful for tracing, ordering, etc.\n    68\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    69\u2192pub struct EventEnvelope {\n    70\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    71\u2192    pub sequence: i64,\n    72\u2192    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    73\u2192    pub aggregate_type: String,\n    74\u2192    pub event: DomainEvent,\n    75\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    79\u2192// Error Types\n    80\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    83\u2192pub enum GdprError {\n    84\u2192    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n    85\u2192    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    86\u2192    #[error(\"event publish error: {0}\")]\n    87\u2192    Publish(#[from] PublishError),\n    88\u2192    #[error(\"command timed out after {0:?}\")]\n    89\u2192    Timeout(Duration),\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    93\u2192#[error(\"failed to publish event\")]\n    94\u2192pub struct PublishError;\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    97\u2192// Abstractions\n    98\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192/// Event store responsible for persisting `EventEnvelope`s in DB.\n   101\u2192#[async_trait]\n   102\u2192pub trait EventStore {\n   103\u2192    async fn append(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>;\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192/// Publisher responsible for pushing events to the broker.\n   107\u2192#[async_trait]\n   108\u2192pub trait EventPublisher {\n   109\u2192    async fn publish(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), PublishError>;\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   113\u2192// Concrete Implementations\n   114\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192/// Postgres-backed event store.\n   117\u2192pub struct PostgresEventStore {\n   118\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   119\u2192}\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192impl PostgresEventStore {\n   122\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   123\u2192        Self { pool }\n   124\u2192    }\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    async fn append_single(\n   127\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   128\u2192        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n   129\u2192    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n   130\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   131\u2192            r#\"\n   132\u2192            INSERT INTO event_store (\n   133\u2192                id,\n   134\u2192                sequence,\n   135\u2192                aggregate_id,\n   136\u2192                aggregate_type,\n   137\u2192                event,\n   138\u2192                occurred_at\n   139\u2192            ) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n   140\u2192            \"#,\n   141\u2192            envelope.id,\n   142\u2192            envelope.sequence,\n   143\u2192            envelope.aggregate_id,\n   144\u2192            envelope.aggregate_type,\n   145\u2192            serde_json::to_value(&envelope.event).expect(\"serialisation never fails\"),\n   146\u2192            envelope.occurred_at\n   147\u2192        )\n   148\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   149\u2192        .await?;\n   150\u2192        Ok(())\n   151\u2192    }\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192#[async_trait]\n   155\u2192impl EventStore for PostgresEventStore {\n   156\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, name = \"event_store.append\")]\n   157\u2192    async fn append(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n   158\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   159\u2192        for ev in events {\n   160\u2192            Self::append_single(&mut tx, ev).await?;\n   161\u2192        }\n   162\u2192        tx.commit().await\n   163\u2192    }\n   164\u2192}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192/// Dummy publisher; swap with NATS / RabbitMQ / Kafka implementation.\n   167\u2192pub struct LogPublisher;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192#[async_trait]\n   170\u2192impl EventPublisher for LogPublisher {\n   171\u2192    #[instrument(skip_all, level = \"debug\")]\n   172\u2192    async fn publish(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n   173\u2192        for ev in events {\n   174\u2192            info!(event_id = %ev.id, \"published event to broker\");\n   175\u2192        }\n   176\u2192        Ok(())\n   177\u2192    }\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   181\u2192// Service Layer\n   182\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192pub struct GdprService<S, P>\n   185\u2192where\n   186\u2192    S: EventStore + Send + Sync,\n   187\u2192    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync,\n   188\u2192{\n   189\u2192    store: S,\n   190\u2192    publisher: P,\n   191\u2192}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192impl<S, P> GdprService<S, P>\n   194\u2192where\n   195\u2192    S: EventStore + Send + Sync,\n   196\u2192    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync,\n   197\u2192{\n   198\u2192    pub fn new(store: S, publisher: P) -> Self {\n   199\u2192        Self { store, publisher }\n   200\u2192    }\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192    /// Dispatch a GDPR command, storing and emitting the resulting events.\n   203\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, cmd))]\n   204\u2192    pub async fn handle(&self, cmd: GdprCommand) -> Result<(), GdprError> {\n   205\u2192        // Step 1 \u2013 Translate command \u2192 events\n   206\u2192        let events = self.to_events(cmd);\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192        // Step 2 \u2013 Persist events inside a DB transaction\n   209\u2192        self.store.append(&events).await?;\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192        // Step 3 \u2013 Broadcast the events to the broker\n   212\u2192        //          (so that search/pruning/etc. can react asynchronously)\n   213\u2192        self.publisher.publish(&events).await?;\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192        Ok(())\n   216\u2192    }\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192    fn to_events(&self, cmd: GdprCommand) -> Vec<EventEnvelope> {\n   219\u2192        match cmd {\n   220\u2192            GdprCommand::EraseUser {\n   221\u2192                requesting_admin_id,\n   222\u2192                target_user_id,\n   223\u2192                reason,\n   224\u2192            } => {\n   225\u2192                let base_seq = self.sequence_seed();\n   226\u2192                vec![self.envelope(\n   227\u2192                    base_seq,\n   228\u2192                    target_user_id,\n   229\u2192                    DomainEvent::UserErasureRequested {\n   230\u2192                        target_user_id,\n   231\u2192                        requesting_admin_id,\n   232\u2192                        reason,\n   233\u2192                    },\n   234\u2192                )]\n   235\u2192            }\n   236\u2192        }\n   237\u2192    }\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    fn sequence_seed(&self) -> i64 {\n   240\u2192        // In production this should come from a monotonic sequence (e.g. PG sequence).\n   241\u2192        // For illustration we just use a randomish number.\n   242\u2192        (Utc::now().timestamp_micros() & 0x7FFF_FFFF) as i64\n   243\u2192    }\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192    fn envelope(&self, seq: i64, aggregate_id: Uuid, ev: DomainEvent) -> EventEnvelope {\n   246\u2192        EventEnvelope {\n   247\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   248\u2192            sequence: seq,\n   249\u2192            aggregate_id,\n   250\u2192            aggregate_type: \"user\".to_string(),\n   251\u2192            event: ev,\n   252\u2192            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n   253\u2192        }\n   254\u2192    }\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   258\u2192// Bootstrap helpers (e.g. called from main.rs or integration tests)\n   259\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192/// Convenience builder that spins up a connection pool and returns\n   262\u2192/// a GDPR service ready for injection.\n   263\u2192pub async fn bootstrap_gdpr_service(\n   264\u2192    database_url: &str,\n   265\u2192) -> Result<GdprService<PostgresEventStore, LogPublisher>, sqlx::Error> {\n   266\u2192    let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   267\u2192        .max_connections(5)\n   268\u2192        .connect(database_url)\n   269\u2192        .await?;\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192    Ok(GdprService::new(\n   272\u2192        PostgresEventStore::new(pool),\n   273\u2192        LogPublisher,\n   274\u2192    ))\n   275\u2192}\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   278\u2192// Example (when used from an Actix-Web handler or anywhere else)\n   279\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   280\u2192//\n   281\u2192// #[post(\"/admin/erase_user/{user_id}\")]\n   282\u2192// async fn erase_user(\n   283\u2192//     svc: web::Data<AppCtx>,       // contains GdprService behind Arc\n   284\u2192//     path: web::Path<Uuid>,\n   285\u2192//     session: CurrentUser,         // auth middleware\n   286\u2192// ) -> impl Responder {\n   287\u2192//     let cmd = GdprCommand::EraseUser {\n   288\u2192//         requesting_admin_id: session.user_id,\n   289\u2192//         target_user_id: *path,\n   290\u2192//         reason: \"User requested account deletion\".into(),\n   291\u2192//     };\n   292\u2192//\n   293\u2192//     // Guard against runaway futures; we never want to hang.\n   294\u2192//     match timeout(Duration::from_secs(3), svc.gdpr.handle(cmd)).await {\n   295\u2192//         Ok(Ok(())) => HttpResponse::Accepted().finish(),\n   296\u2192//         Ok(Err(e)) => {\n   297\u2192//             error!(error = %e, \"GDPR command failed\");\n   298\u2192//             HttpResponse::InternalServerError().finish()\n   299\u2192//         }\n   300\u2192//         Err(_) => HttpResponse::GatewayTimeout().finish(),\n   301\u2192//     }\n   302\u2192// }\n   303\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   304\u2192// End of module\n   305\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.   "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_35.txt"
