Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! module_35 \u2013 Event-sourced learning-pulse subsystem\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! This module demonstrates how EduPulse Live persists and publishes\n     5\u2192//! domain events for the \u201clearning-pulse\u201d feature using an out-box pattern.\n     6\u2192//!\n     7\u2192//! Production-quality concerns addressed in this file\n     8\u2192//! --------------------------------------------------\n     9\u2192//! \u2022 Service-layer abstraction (`LearningPulseService`)  \n    10\u2192//! \u2022 Repository pattern backed by `sqlx` (`PgLearningPulseRepo`)  \n    11\u2192//! \u2022 Event store + message publisher decoupling  \n    12\u2192//! \u2022 Asynchronous, cancel-safe I/O (`tokio`, `sqlx::transaction`)  \n    13\u2192//! \u2022 Structured error handling with context propagation (`anyhow`, `thiserror`)  \n    14\u2192//! \u2022 Serde-powered event serialization (JSONB column + NATS transport)  \n    15\u2192//! \u2022 TLS-first NATS client configuration  \n    16\u2192//!\n    17\u2192//! Compile with:  \n    18\u2192//! `cargo build --features runtime-tokio-rustls,sqlx-postgres,nats`\n    19\u2192//!\n    20\u2192//! Database schema (PostgreSQL)\n    21\u2192//! ----------------------------\n    22\u2192//! ```sql\n    23\u2192//! CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learning_pulses (\n    24\u2192//!     id              UUID            PRIMARY KEY,\n    25\u2192//!     title           TEXT            NOT NULL,\n    26\u2192//!     author_id       UUID            NOT NULL,\n    27\u2192//!     created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()\n    28\u2192//! );\n    29\u2192 //!\n    30\u2192//! CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_event_outbox (\n    31\u2192//!     id              BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n    32\u2192//!     aggregate_id    UUID            NOT NULL,\n    33\u2192//!     event_type      TEXT            NOT NULL,\n    34\u2192//!     payload         JSONB           NOT NULL,\n    35\u2192//!     occurred_at     TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    36\u2192//!     dispatched      BOOLEAN         NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE\n    37\u2192//! );\n    38\u2192//! ```\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192use std::sync::Arc;\n    42\u2192use std::time::Duration;\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192use anyhow::{Context, Result};\n    45\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    46\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    47\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    48\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\n    49\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    50\u2192use tokio::time;\n    51\u2192use tracing::{error, info};\n    52\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    55\u2192// Common types & traits\n    56\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192/// A domain event emitted by an aggregate root.\n    59\u2192///\n    60\u2192/// Every event has:\n    61\u2192/// \u2022 a stable, unique id (for idempotency)  \n    62\u2192/// \u2022 the id of the aggregate it belongs to  \n    63\u2192/// \u2022 an ISO-timestamp of when it occurred within the domain  \n    64\u2192/// \u2022 a semantic `event_type` string used for routing on the message bus  \n    65\u2192/// \u2022 a `version` field that allows evolving schema without breaking consumers\n    66\u2192#[typetag::serde(tag = \"event_type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n    67\u2192pub trait DomainEvent: Send + Sync {\n    68\u2192    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    69\u2192    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    70\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n    71\u2192    fn version(&self) -> u8;\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192/// Generic envelope stored in the outbox and delivered to the broker.\n    75\u2192#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    76\u2192struct EventEnvelope {\n    77\u2192    pub event_id: Uuid,\n    78\u2192    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    79\u2192    pub event_type: String,\n    80\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    81\u2192    pub version: u8,\n    82\u2192    pub payload: serde_json::Value,\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192impl EventEnvelope {\n    86\u2192    fn new<E: DomainEvent + Serialize + ?Sized>(event: &E) -> Result<Self> {\n    87\u2192        Ok(Self {\n    88\u2192            event_id: event.event_id(),\n    89\u2192            aggregate_id: event.aggregate_id(),\n    90\u2192            event_type: std::any::type_name::<E>().to_string(), // e.g. \"learning_pulse::Created\"\n    91\u2192            occurred_at: event.occurred_at(),\n    92\u2192            version: event.version(),\n    93\u2192            payload: serde_json::to_value(event)?,\n    94\u2192        })\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    99\u2192// Learning-pulse domain\n   100\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192/// Aggregate root for a learning pulse.\n   103\u2192///\n   104\u2192/// Only minimal data is stored here. Business rules live in `impl`.\n   105\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n   106\u2192pub struct LearningPulse {\n   107\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n   108\u2192    pub title: String,\n   109\u2192    pub author_id: Uuid,\n   110\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192impl LearningPulse {\n   114\u2192    /// Factory method ensuring invariants\n   115\u2192    pub fn new(title: impl Into<String>, author_id: Uuid) -> Result<(Self, LearningPulseCreated)> {\n   116\u2192        let title = title.into();\n   117\u2192        if title.trim().is_empty() {\n   118\u2192            return Err(DomainError::Validation(\n   119\u2192                \"Title must not be empty\".into(),\n   120\u2192            )\n   121\u2192            .into());\n   122\u2192        }\n   123\u2192        let id = Uuid::new_v4();\n   124\u2192        let created_at = Utc::now();\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192        let pulse = Self {\n   127\u2192            id,\n   128\u2192            title: title.clone(),\n   129\u2192            author_id,\n   130\u2192            created_at,\n   131\u2192        };\n   132\u2192        let evt = LearningPulseCreated {\n   133\u2192            id,\n   134\u2192            title,\n   135\u2192            author_id,\n   136\u2192            occurred_at: created_at,\n   137\u2192            version: 1,\n   138\u2192        };\n   139\u2192        Ok((pulse, evt))\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   144\u2192// Events\n   145\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   148\u2192pub struct LearningPulseCreated {\n   149\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n   150\u2192    pub title: String,\n   151\u2192    pub author_id: Uuid,\n   152\u2192    #[serde(with = \"chrono::serde::ts_seconds\")]\n   153\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   154\u2192    pub version: u8,\n   155\u2192}\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192#[typetag::serde]\n   158\u2192impl DomainEvent for LearningPulseCreated {\n   159\u2192    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n   160\u2192        self.id\n   161\u2192    }\n   162\u2192    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n   163\u2192        self.id\n   164\u2192    }\n   165\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   166\u2192        self.occurred_at\n   167\u2192    }\n   168\u2192    fn version(&self) -> u8 {\n   169\u2192        self.version\n   170\u2192    }\n   171\u2192}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   174\u2192// Repositories\n   175\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192#[async_trait]\n   178\u2192pub trait LearningPulseRepository: Send + Sync {\n   179\u2192    async fn insert(&self, pulse: &LearningPulse, tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>) -> Result<()>;\n   180\u2192    async fn by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>>;\n   181\u2192}\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192/// PostgreSQL implementation using `sqlx`\n   184\u2192pub struct PgLearningPulseRepo;\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192#[async_trait]\n   187\u2192impl LearningPulseRepository for PgLearningPulseRepo {\n   188\u2192    async fn insert(\n   189\u2192        &self,\n   190\u2192        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n   191\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   192\u2192    ) -> Result<()> {\n   193\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   194\u2192            r#\"INSERT INTO learning_pulses (id, title, author_id, created_at)\n   195\u2192               VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\"#,\n   196\u2192            pulse.id,\n   197\u2192            pulse.title,\n   198\u2192            pulse.author_id,\n   199\u2192            pulse.created_at\n   200\u2192        )\n   201\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   202\u2192        .await\n   203\u2192        .context(\"insert learning pulse\")?;\n   204\u2192        Ok(())\n   205\u2192    }\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192    async fn by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>> {\n   208\u2192        let rec = sqlx::query!(\n   209\u2192            r#\"SELECT id, title, author_id, created_at\n   210\u2192               FROM learning_pulses WHERE id = $1\"#,\n   211\u2192            id,\n   212\u2192        )\n   213\u2192        .fetch_optional(self.pool()?)\n   214\u2192        .await\n   215\u2192        .context(\"fetch learning pulse\")?;\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192        if let Some(r) = rec {\n   218\u2192            Ok(Some(LearningPulse {\n   219\u2192                id: r.id,\n   220\u2192                title: r.title,\n   221\u2192                author_id: r.author_id,\n   222\u2192                created_at: r.created_at,\n   223\u2192            }))\n   224\u2192        } else {\n   225\u2192            Ok(None)\n   226\u2192        }\n   227\u2192    }\n   228\u2192}\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192impl PgLearningPulseRepo {\n   231\u2192    fn pool(&self) -> Result<&PgPool> {\n   232\u2192        PG_POOL.get().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"pool not initialised\"))\n   233\u2192    }\n   234\u2192}\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   237\u2192// Event store\n   238\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192#[async_trait]\n   241\u2192pub trait EventStore: Send + Sync {\n   242\u2192    async fn append(\n   243\u2192        &self,\n   244\u2192        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n   245\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   246\u2192    ) -> Result<()>;\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192    /// Atomically mark an event as dispatched.\n   249\u2192    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, id: i64) -> Result<()>;\n   250\u2192}\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192pub struct PgEventStore;\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192#[async_trait]\n   255\u2192impl EventStore for PgEventStore {\n   256\u2192    async fn append(\n   257\u2192        &self,\n   258\u2192        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n   259\u2192        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n   260\u2192    ) -> Result<()> {\n   261\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   262\u2192            r#\"INSERT INTO domain_event_outbox\n   263\u2192               (aggregate_id, event_type, payload)\n   264\u2192               VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\"#,\n   265\u2192            envelope.aggregate_id,\n   266\u2192            envelope.event_type,\n   267\u2192            envelope.payload\n   268\u2192        )\n   269\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   270\u2192        .await\n   271\u2192        .context(\"append to outbox\")?;\n   272\u2192        Ok(())\n   273\u2192    }\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, id: i64) -> Result<()> {\n   276\u2192        sqlx::query!(\n   277\u2192            r#\"UPDATE domain_event_outbox\n   278\u2192               SET dispatched = TRUE\n   279\u2192               WHERE id = $1\"#,\n   280\u2192            id\n   281\u2192        )\n   282\u2192        .execute(PG_POOL.get().unwrap())\n   283\u2192        .await?;\n   284\u2192        Ok(())\n   285\u2192    }\n   286\u2192}\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   289\u2192// Message broker publisher\n   290\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192#[async_trait]\n   293\u2192pub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n   294\u2192    async fn publish(&self, envelope: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()>;\n   295\u2192}\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192/// NATS IO implementation with Rustls TLS.\n   298\u2192pub struct NatsPublisher {\n   299\u2192    connection: async_nats::Client,\n   300\u2192}\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192impl NatsPublisher {\n   303\u2192    pub async fn connect() -> Result<Self> {\n   304\u2192        // The NATS url should use tls: e.g. \"tls://broker.prod.edupulse.live:4222\"\n   305\u2192        let url = std::env::var(\"NATS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"tls://localhost:4222\".into());\n   306\u2192        let connection = async_nats::connect(url).await.context(\"connect nats\")?;\n   307\u2192        Ok(Self { connection })\n   308\u2192    }\n   309\u2192}\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192#[async_trait]\n   312\u2192impl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n   313\u2192    async fn publish(&self, envelope: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n   314\u2192        let subject = format!(\"{}.v{}\", envelope.event_type, envelope.version);\n   315\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(envelope)?;\n   316\u2192        self.connection\n   317\u2192            .publish(subject, payload.into())\n   318\u2192            .await\n   319\u2192            .context(\"nats publish\")?;\n   320\u2192        Ok(())\n   321\u2192    }\n   322\u2192}\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   325\u2192// Service layer\n   326\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192pub struct LearningPulseService<R: LearningPulseRepository, E: EventStore, P: EventPublisher> {\n   329\u2192    repo: Arc<R>,\n   330\u2192    store: Arc<E>,\n   331\u2192    publisher: Arc<P>,\n   332\u2192    pool: Arc<PgPool>,\n   333\u2192}\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192impl<R, E, P> LearningPulseService<R, E, P>\n   336\u2192where\n   337\u2192    R: LearningPulseRepository + 'static,\n   338\u2192    E: EventStore + 'static,\n   339\u2192    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n   340\u2192{\n   341\u2192    pub fn new(repo: R, store: E, publisher: P, pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   342\u2192        Self {\n   343\u2192            repo: Arc::new(repo),\n   344\u2192            store: Arc::new(store),\n   345\u2192            publisher: Arc::new(publisher),\n   346\u2192            pool: Arc::new(pool),\n   347\u2192        }\n   348\u2192    }\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192    /// Create a learning pulse and publish the corresponding event.\n   351\u2192    ///\n   352\u2192    /// Uses the out-box pattern: events are stored in the same tx as\n   353\u2192    /// the aggregate write; a background task flushes them to NATS.\n   354\u2192    pub async fn create_pulse(&self, title: &str, author_id: Uuid) -> Result<Uuid> {\n   355\u2192        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   356\u2192        let (pulse, evt) = LearningPulse::new(title, author_id)?;\n   357\u2192        self.repo.insert(&pulse, &mut tx).await?;\n   358\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(&evt)?;\n   359\u2192        self.store.append(&envelope, &mut tx).await?;\n   360\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192        // fire-and-forget: real project would delegate to a queue consumer\n   363\u2192        let publisher = self.publisher.clone();\n   364\u2192        tokio::spawn(async move {\n   365\u2192            if let Err(err) = publisher.publish(&envelope).await {\n   366\u2192                error!(?err, \"failed to publish LearningPulseCreated\");\n   367\u2192            }\n   368\u2192        });\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192        Ok(pulse.id)\n   371\u2192    }\n   372\u2192}\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   375\u2192// Error handling\n   376\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192#[derive(Error, Debug)]\n   379\u2192pub enum DomainError {\n   380\u2192    #[error(\"validation error: {0}\")]\n   381\u2192    Validation(String),\n   382\u2192}\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   385\u2192// Initialisation helpers\n   386\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192/// Shared, lazily-initialised PostgreSQL pool (used by repo & store).\n   389\u2192static PG_POOL: once_cell::sync::OnceCell<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::OnceCell::new();\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192/// Start the background outbox dispatcher to guarantee at-least-once delivery.\n   392\u2192///\n   393\u2192/// Batches undispatched rows and publishes them, marking them dispatched on success.\n   394\u2192/// Sleeps for `interval` between sweeps.\n   395\u2192pub async fn start_outbox_dispatcher<E, P>(\n   396\u2192    store: Arc<E>,\n   397\u2192    publisher: Arc<P>,\n   398\u2192    interval: Duration,\n   399\u2192    pool: Arc<PgPool>,\n   400\u2192) -> Result<()>\n   401\u2192where\n   402\u2192    E: EventStore + 'static,\n   403\u2192    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n   404\u2192{\n   405\u2192    tokio::spawn(async move {\n   406\u2192        loop {\n   407\u2192            if let Err(err) = flush_once(&store, &publisher, &pool).await {\n   408\u2192                error!(?err, \"outbox flush failure\");\n   409\u2192            }\n   410\u2192            time::sleep(interval).await;\n   411\u2192        }\n   412\u2192    });\n   413\u2192    Ok(())\n   414\u2192}\n   415\u2192\n   416\u2192async fn flush_once<E, P>(store: &Arc<E>, publisher: &Arc<P>, pool: &Arc<PgPool>) -> Result<()>\n   417\u2192where\n   418\u2192    E: EventStore + 'static,\n   419\u2192    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n   420\u2192{\n   421\u2192    let mut tx = pool.begin().await?;\n   422\u2192    let rows = sqlx::query!(\n   423\u2192        r#\"SELECT id,\n   424\u2192                  aggregate_id,\n   425\u2192                  event_type,\n   426\u2192                  payload,\n   427\u2192                  occurred_at,\n   428\u2192                  dispatched\n   429\u2192           FROM domain_event_outbox\n   430\u2192           WHERE dispatched = FALSE\n   431\u2192           ORDER BY occurred_at\n   432\u2192           LIMIT 50\n   433\u2192        \"#\n   434\u2192    )\n   435\u2192    .fetch_all(&mut *tx)\n   436\u2192    .await?;\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192    for row in rows {\n   439\u2192        let envelope = EventEnvelope {\n   440\u2192            event_id: Uuid::new_v4(), // Not stored; only used for broker duplication protection\n   441\u2192            aggregate_id: row.aggregate_id,\n   442\u2192            event_type: row.event_type,\n   443\u2192            occurred_at: row.occurred_at,\n   444\u2192            version: 1,\n   445\u2192            payload: row.payload,\n   446\u2192        };\n   447\u2192        publisher.publish(&envelope).await?;\n   448\u2192        store.mark_dispatched(row.id).await?;\n   449\u2192    }\n   450\u2192    tx.commit().await?;\n   451\u2192    Ok(())\n   452\u2192}\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   455\u2192// Application bootstrap example\n   456\u2192// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192/// One-shot bootstrap for Actix-web / Axum etc.  \n   459\u2192/// Not compiled by default \u2013 only illustrative.\n   460\u2192///\n   461\u2192/// ```ignore\n   462\u2192/// #[tokio::main]\n   463\u2192/// async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   464\u2192///     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n   465\u2192///\n   466\u2192///     let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   467\u2192///         .max_connections(5)\n   468\u2192///         .connect(&std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")?)\n   469\u2192///         .await?;\n   470\u2192///     PG_POOL.set(pool.clone()).unwrap();\n   471\u2192///\n   472\u2192///     let publisher = NatsPublisher::connect().await?;\n   473\u2192///\n   474\u2192///     let svc = LearningPulseService::new(\n   475\u2192///         PgLearningPulseRepo,\n   476\u2192///         PgEventStore,\n   477\u2192///         publisher.clone(),\n   478\u2192///         pool.clone(),\n   479\u2192///     );\n   480\u2192///\n   481\u2192///     start_outbox_dispatcher(\n   482\u2192///         Arc::new(PgEventStore),\n   483\u2192///         Arc::new(publisher),\n   484\u2192///         Duration::from_secs(5),\n   485\u2192///         Arc::new(pool),\n   486\u2192///     )\n   487\u2192///     .await?;\n   488\u2192///\n   489\u2192///     // Create a pulse\n   490\u2192///     let pulse_id = svc\n   491\u2192///         .create_pulse(\"Explain the CAP theorem in 3 sentences\", Uuid::new_v4())\n   492\u2192///         .await?;\n   493\u2192///     println!(\"pulse id: {pulse_id}\");\n   494\u2192///\n   495\u2192///     Ok(())\n   496\u2192/// }\n   497\u2192/// ```\n   498\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_1.rs\n     3\u2192//! Authentication & Session Management module for **EduPulse Live \u2013 Event-Driven Social Learning Hub**\n     4\u2192//!\n     5\u2192//! This module demonstrates the Repository pattern, a thin Service layer,\n     6\u2192//! domain-event emission, and secure session management (JWT + Redis).\n     7\u2192//! It is self-contained and can be dropped into the larger workspace.\n     8\u2192//!\n     9\u2192//! Dependencies expected in `Cargo.toml` (not shown here):\n    10\u2192//! --------------------------------------------------------------------\n    11\u2192//! [dependencies]\n    12\u2192//! sqlx           = { version = \"0.7\", features = [\"postgres\", \"runtime-tokio-rustls\", \"uuid\"] }\n    13\u2192//! async-trait    = \"0.1\"\n    14\u2192//! serde          = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"derive\"] }\n    15\u2192//! serde_json     = \"1.0\"\n    16\u2192//! uuid           = { version = \"1\", features = [\"v4\"] }\n    17\u2192//! argon2         = \"0.5\"\n    18\u2192//! jsonwebtoken   = \"8\"\n    19\u2192//! chrono         = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\n    20\u2192//! thiserror      = \"1.0\"\n    21\u2192//! tracing        = \"0.1\"\n    22\u2192//! deadpool-redis = { version = \"0.12\", features = [\"rt_tokio_1\"] }\n    23\u2192//! anyhow         = \"1.0\"\n    24\u2192//!\n    25\u2192//! Feature flags: `database_integration`, `session_management`, `user_authentication`\n    26\u2192//! --------------------------------------------------------------------\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192use std::env;\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    31\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n    32\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    33\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgRow, PgPool, Row};\n    34\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    35\u2192use tracing::{error, info};\n    36\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192use argon2::{self, password_hash::SaltString, Argon2, PasswordHash, PasswordHasher, PasswordVerifier};\n    39\u2192use jsonwebtoken::{\n    40\u2192    decode, encode, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, TokenData, Validation,\n    41\u2192};\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192/// Custom result type for the auth domain\n    44\u2192pub type Result<T, E = AuthError> = std::result::Result<T, E>;\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192/// Central error enumeration covering common failure scenarios.\n    47\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    48\u2192pub enum AuthError {\n    49\u2192    #[error(\"hashing error\")]\n    50\u2192    Hashing(#[from] argon2::password_hash::Error),\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    #[error(\"database error\")]\n    53\u2192    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    #[error(\"invalid credentials\")]\n    56\u2192    InvalidCredentials,\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    #[error(\"jwt error\")]\n    59\u2192    Jwt(#[from] jsonwebtoken::errors::Error),\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    #[error(\"event bus error: {0}\")]\n    62\u2192    EventBus(#[from] anyhow::Error),\n    63\u2192}\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n    66\u2192/// Domain model\n    67\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n    68\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    69\u2192pub struct User {\n    70\u2192    pub id: Uuid,\n    71\u2192    pub email: String,\n    72\u2192    #[serde(skip_serializing)]\n    73\u2192    pub password_hash: String,\n    74\u2192    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n    78\u2192pub struct NewUser {\n    79\u2192    pub email: String,\n    80\u2192    pub password: String,\n    81\u2192}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n    84\u2192pub struct Session {\n    85\u2192    pub token: String,\n    86\u2192    pub expires_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192/// JWT claims stored in the access token\n    90\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    91\u2192struct Claims {\n    92\u2192    sub: String,\n    93\u2192    exp: usize,\n    94\u2192    iat: usize,\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n    98\u2192/// Repository layer\n    99\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   100\u2192#[async_trait]\n   101\u2192pub trait UserRepository: Send + Sync {\n   102\u2192    async fn insert(&self, user: &User) -> Result<()>;\n   103\u2192    async fn find_by_email(&self, email: &str) -> Result<Option<User>>;\n   104\u2192    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>>;\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192pub struct PostgresUserRepository {\n   108\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192impl PostgresUserRepository {\n   112\u2192    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n   113\u2192        Self { pool }\n   114\u2192    }\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192#[async_trait]\n   118\u2192impl UserRepository for PostgresUserRepository {\n   119\u2192    async fn insert(&self, user: &User) -> Result<()> {\n   120\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   121\u2192            r#\"\n   122\u2192            INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, created_at)\n   123\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\n   124\u2192            \"#,\n   125\u2192        )\n   126\u2192        .bind(user.id)\n   127\u2192        .bind(&user.email)\n   128\u2192        .bind(&user.password_hash)\n   129\u2192        .bind(user.created_at)\n   130\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   131\u2192        .await?;\n   132\u2192        Ok(())\n   133\u2192    }\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    async fn find_by_email(&self, email: &str) -> Result<Option<User>> {\n   136\u2192        let maybe_row = sqlx::query(\n   137\u2192            r#\"\n   138\u2192            SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at\n   139\u2192            FROM users\n   140\u2192            WHERE email = $1\n   141\u2192            \"#,\n   142\u2192        )\n   143\u2192        .bind(email)\n   144\u2192        .map(map_row_to_user)\n   145\u2192        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n   146\u2192        .await?;\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192        Ok(maybe_row)\n   149\u2192    }\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192    async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>> {\n   152\u2192        let maybe_row = sqlx::query(\n   153\u2192            r#\"\n   154\u2192            SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at\n   155\u2192            FROM users\n   156\u2192            WHERE id = $1\n   157\u2192            \"#,\n   158\u2192        )\n   159\u2192        .bind(id)\n   160\u2192        .map(map_row_to_user)\n   161\u2192        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)\n   162\u2192        .await?;\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192        Ok(maybe_row)\n   165\u2192    }\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192/// Helper for mapping a row into a [`User`].\n   169\u2192fn map_row_to_user(row: PgRow) -> User {\n   170\u2192    User {\n   171\u2192        id: row.get(\"id\"),\n   172\u2192        email: row.get(\"email\"),\n   173\u2192        password_hash: row.get(\"password_hash\"),\n   174\u2192        created_at: row.get(\"created_at\"),\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   179\u2192/// Event bus abstraction\n   180\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   181\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n   182\u2192pub enum DomainEvent {\n   183\u2192    UserRegistered { user_id: Uuid, email: String },\n   184\u2192    UserLoggedIn { user_id: Uuid },\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192#[async_trait]\n   188\u2192pub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n   189\u2192    async fn publish(&self, evt: &DomainEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()>;\n   190\u2192}\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192/// A naive, logging-only event bus implementation. Replace with Kafka/NATS/RabbitMQ as needed.\n   193\u2192pub struct TracingEventBus;\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192#[async_trait]\n   196\u2192impl EventBus for TracingEventBus {\n   197\u2192    async fn publish(&self, evt: &DomainEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   198\u2192        info!(?evt, \"domain event published\");\n   199\u2192        Ok(())\n   200\u2192    }\n   201\u2192}\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   204\u2192/// Session cache (Redis)\n   205\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   206\u2192use deadpool_redis::{cmd, redis::AsyncCommands, Config, Connection, Pool};\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192pub struct SessionStore {\n   209\u2192    pool: Pool,\n   210\u2192}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192impl SessionStore {\n   213\u2192    pub fn new(redis_url: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Self> {\n   214\u2192        let mut cfg = Config::from_url(redis_url);\n   215\u2192        cfg.pool.timeout = Some(std::time::Duration::from_secs(3));\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192        let pool = cfg.create_pool(Some(deadpool_redis::Runtime::Tokio1))?;\n   218\u2192        Ok(Self { pool })\n   219\u2192    }\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192    async fn put(&self, token: &str, user_id: Uuid, ttl: Duration) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   222\u2192        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n   223\u2192        cmd(\"SET\")\n   224\u2192            .arg(token)\n   225\u2192            .arg(user_id.to_string())\n   226\u2192            .arg(\"EX\")\n   227\u2192            .arg(ttl.num_seconds())\n   228\u2192            .query_async(&mut conn)\n   229\u2192            .await?;\n   230\u2192        Ok(())\n   231\u2192    }\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192    #[allow(dead_code)]\n   234\u2192    async fn get_user_id(&self, token: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<Uuid>> {\n   235\u2192        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n   236\u2192        let resp: Option<String> = conn.get(token).await?;\n   237\u2192        Ok(resp.and_then(|s| Uuid::parse_str(&s).ok()))\n   238\u2192    }\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192    async fn delete(&self, token: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n   241\u2192        let mut conn: Connection = self.pool.get().await?;\n   242\u2192        conn.del(token).await?;\n   243\u2192        Ok(())\n   244\u2192    }\n   245\u2192}\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   248\u2192/// Service layer\n   249\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   250\u2192pub struct AuthService<R: UserRepository, B: EventBus> {\n   251\u2192    repo: R,\n   252\u2192    event_bus: B,\n   253\u2192    session_store: SessionStore,\n   254\u2192    jwt_secret: String,\n   255\u2192    session_ttl: Duration,\n   256\u2192    argon2: Argon2<'static>,\n   257\u2192}\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192impl<R: UserRepository, B: EventBus> AuthService<R, B> {\n   260\u2192    /// Create an [`AuthService`] with sensible defaults.  \n   261\u2192    /// `jwt_secret` **must** be a strong, randomly-generated 256-bit key (base64 encoded).\n   262\u2192    pub fn new(repo: R, event_bus: B, session_store: SessionStore, jwt_secret: String) -> Self {\n   263\u2192        Self {\n   264\u2192            repo,\n   265\u2192            event_bus,\n   266\u2192            session_store,\n   267\u2192            jwt_secret,\n   268\u2192            session_ttl: Duration::hours(12),\n   269\u2192            argon2: Argon2::default(),\n   270\u2192        }\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    /// Registers a new user, returning a newly minted [`Session`] on success.\n   274\u2192    pub async fn register_user(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session> {\n   275\u2192        if self.repo.find_by_email(&new_user.email).await?.is_some() {\n   276\u2192            return Err(AuthError::InvalidCredentials); // simplified: email already taken\n   277\u2192        }\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192        let salt = SaltString::generate(&mut rand::thread_rng());\n   280\u2192        let password_hash = self\n   281\u2192            .argon2\n   282\u2192            .hash_password(new_user.password.as_bytes(), &salt)?\n   283\u2192            .to_string();\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192        let user = User {\n   286\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   287\u2192            email: new_user.email,\n   288\u2192            password_hash,\n   289\u2192            created_at: Utc::now(),\n   290\u2192        };\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192        self.repo.insert(&user).await?;\n   293\u2192        self.event_bus\n   294\u2192            .publish(&DomainEvent::UserRegistered {\n   295\u2192                user_id: user.id,\n   296\u2192                email: user.email.clone(),\n   297\u2192            })\n   298\u2192            .await?;\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        let session = self.create_session(user.id).await?;\n   301\u2192        Ok(session)\n   302\u2192    }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192    /// Authenticates a user and returns a valid session token.\n   305\u2192    pub async fn login_user(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session> {\n   306\u2192        let user = self\n   307\u2192            .repo\n   308\u2192            .find_by_email(email)\n   309\u2192            .await?\n   310\u2192            .ok_or(AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192        // Verify password\n   313\u2192        let parsed_hash = PasswordHash::new(&user.password_hash)?;\n   314\u2192        self.argon2\n   315\u2192            .verify_password(password.as_bytes(), &parsed_hash)\n   316\u2192            .map_err(|_| AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n   317\u2192\n   318\u2192        let session = self.create_session(user.id).await?;\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192        self.event_bus\n   321\u2192            .publish(&DomainEvent::UserLoggedIn { user_id: user.id })\n   322\u2192            .await?;\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192        Ok(session)\n   325\u2192    }\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192    /// Invalidates an existing session.\n   328\u2192    pub async fn logout_user(&self, token: &str) -> Result<()> {\n   329\u2192        self.session_store\n   330\u2192            .delete(token)\n   331\u2192            .await\n   332\u2192            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?;\n   333\u2192        Ok(())\n   334\u2192    }\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    /// Helper: create a JWT token + Redis cache entry.\n   337\u2192    async fn create_session(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<Session> {\n   338\u2192        let now = Utc::now();\n   339\u2192        let exp = (now + self.session_ttl).timestamp() as usize;\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192        let claims = Claims {\n   342\u2192            sub: user_id.to_string(),\n   343\u2192            exp,\n   344\u2192            iat: now.timestamp() as usize,\n   345\u2192        };\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192        let header = Header::default();\n   348\u2192        let token = encode(\n   349\u2192            &header,\n   350\u2192            &claims,\n   351\u2192            &EncodingKey::from_secret(self.jwt_secret.as_bytes()),\n   352\u2192        )?;\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192        // Cache in Redis for server-side revocation\n   355\u2192        self.session_store\n   356\u2192            .put(&token, user_id, self.session_ttl)\n   357\u2192            .await\n   358\u2192            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?;\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192        Ok(Session {\n   361\u2192            token,\n   362\u2192            expires_at: now + self.session_ttl,\n   363\u2192        })\n   364\u2192    }\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192    /// Validate a token and return the user id embedded in it.\n   367\u2192    pub async fn validate_token(&self, token: &str) -> Result<Uuid> {\n   368\u2192        // 1. Verify signature / expiration\n   369\u2192        let TokenData { claims, .. } = decode::<Claims>(\n   370\u2192            token,\n   371\u2192            &DecodingKey::from_secret(self.jwt_secret.as_bytes()),\n   372\u2192            &Validation::default(),\n   373\u2192        )?;\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192        // 2. Check server-side revocation list (Redis)\n   376\u2192        if self\n   377\u2192            .session_store\n   378\u2192            .get_user_id(token)\n   379\u2192            .await\n   380\u2192            .map_err(|e| AuthError::EventBus(e.into()))?\n   381\u2192            .is_none()\n   382\u2192        {\n   383\u2192            return Err(AuthError::InvalidCredentials);\n   384\u2192        }\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192        let uuid = Uuid::parse_str(&claims.sub).map_err(|_| AuthError::InvalidCredentials)?;\n   387\u2192        Ok(uuid)\n   388\u2192    }\n   389\u2192}\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   392\u2192/// REST handler glue (simplified for brevity). In a real project, this would live in actix-web or\n   393\u2192/// axum route modules. Here we show just enough to demonstrate SVC integration.\n   394\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   395\u2192#[cfg(feature = \"rest_api\")]\n   396\u2192pub mod rest {\n   397\u2192    use super::*;\n   398\u2192    use axum::{\n   399\u2192        extract::{Extension, Json},\n   400\u2192        http::StatusCode,\n   401\u2192        response::IntoResponse,\n   402\u2192        routing::post,\n   403\u2192        Router,\n   404\u2192    };\n   405\u2192    use std::sync::Arc;\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192    #[derive(Clone)]\n   408\u2192    pub struct AppState<A> {\n   409\u2192        pub auth_service: Arc<A>,\n   410\u2192    }\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192    pub fn router<A: 'static + Send + Sync>(\n   413\u2192        state: AppState<A>,\n   414\u2192    ) -> Router\n   415\u2192    where\n   416\u2192        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n   417\u2192    {\n   418\u2192        Router::new()\n   419\u2192            .route(\"/api/v1/auth/register\", post(register::<A>))\n   420\u2192            .route(\"/api/v1/auth/login\", post(login::<A>))\n   421\u2192            .layer(Extension(state))\n   422\u2192    }\n   423\u2192\n   424\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   425\u2192    pub trait AuthServiceTrait: Send + Sync {\n   426\u2192        async fn register(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session>;\n   427\u2192        async fn login(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session>;\n   428\u2192    }\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192    #[async_trait]\n   431\u2192    impl<R, B> AuthServiceTrait for super::AuthService<R, B>\n   432\u2192    where\n   433\u2192        R: UserRepository + 'static,\n   434\u2192        B: EventBus + 'static,\n   435\u2192    {\n   436\u2192        async fn register(&self, new_user: NewUser) -> Result<Session> {\n   437\u2192            self.register_user(new_user).await\n   438\u2192        }\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192        async fn login(&self, email: &str, password: &str) -> Result<Session> {\n   441\u2192            self.login_user(email, password).await\n   442\u2192        }\n   443\u2192    }\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192    /// ----------------------------------------\n   446\u2192    /// Route handlers\n   447\u2192    /// ----------------------------------------\n   448\u2192    async fn register<A>(\n   449\u2192        Json(payload): Json<NewUser>,\n   450\u2192        Extension(state): Extension<AppState<A>>,\n   451\u2192    ) -> impl IntoResponse\n   452\u2192    where\n   453\u2192        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n   454\u2192    {\n   455\u2192        match state.auth_service.register(payload).await {\n   456\u2192            Ok(session) => (StatusCode::CREATED, Json(session)).into_response(),\n   457\u2192            Err(e) => {\n   458\u2192                error!(\"registration error: {e}\");\n   459\u2192                (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, Json(e.to_string())).into_response()\n   460\u2192            }\n   461\u2192        }\n   462\u2192    }\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192    #[derive(Deserialize)]\n   465\u2192    struct LoginPayload {\n   466\u2192        email: String,\n   467\u2192        password: String,\n   468\u2192    }\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192    async fn login<A>(\n   471\u2192        Json(payload): Json<LoginPayload>,\n   472\u2192        Extension(state): Extension<AppState<A>>,\n   473\u2192    ) -> impl IntoResponse\n   474\u2192    where\n   475\u2192        A: AuthServiceTrait,\n   476\u2192    {\n   477\u2192        match state\n   478\u2192            .auth_service\n   479\u2192            .login(&payload.email, &payload.password)\n   480\u2192            .await\n   481\u2192        {\n   482\u2192            Ok(session) => (StatusCode::OK, Json(session)).into_response(),\n   483\u2192            Err(e) => {\n   484\u2192                error!(\"login error: {e}\");\n   485\u2192                (StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, Json(e.to_string())).into_response()\n   486\u2192            }\n   487\u2192        }\n   488\u2192    }\n   489\u2192}\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   492\u2192/// Convenience: Build everything from environment variables\n   493\u2192/// =============================================================================================\n   494\u2192#[allow(dead_code)]\n   495\u2192pub async fn build_auth_service() -> anyhow::Result<\n   496\u2192    AuthService<PostgresUserRepository, TracingEventBus>,\n   497\u2192> {\n   498\u2192    let database_url =\n   499\u2192        env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\").expect(\"DATABASE_URL must be set (postgres connection string)\");\n   500\u2192    let redis_url = env::var(\"REDIS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"redis://127.0.0.1/\".into());\n   501\u2192    let jwt_secret =\n   502\u2192        env::var(\"JWT_SECRET\").expect(\"JWT_SECRET must be set (base64 encoded 256-bit random key)\");\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192    let pool = PgPool::connect(&database_url).await?;\n   505\u2192\n   506\u2192    let repo = PostgresUserRepository::new(pool);\n   507\u2192    let bus = TracingEventBus;\n   508\u2192    let session_store = SessionStore::new(&redis_url)?;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192    Ok(AuthService::new(repo, bus, session_store, jwt_secret))\n   511\u2192}\n   512\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_12.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Event-bus abstraction and a concrete NATS implementation\n     5\u2192//! for EduPulse Live.  This module is responsible for\n     6\u2192//! publishing and subscribing domain events flowing through\n     7\u2192//! the system.  The design hides message\u2013broker specifics\n     8\u2192//! behind a small, test-friendly trait while still exposing\n     9\u2192//! advanced features (e.g. queue groups, back-pressure).\n    10\u2192//!\n    11\u2192//! Compile-time flags enable seamless downgrade to an in-memory\n    12\u2192//! bus for unit tests (`cargo test --features in_memory_bus`).\n    13\u2192//!\n    14\u2192//! Example\n    15\u2192//! -------\n    16\u2192//! ```no_run\n    17\u2192//! # use edupulse_event_bus::{EventBus, DomainEvent, EventBusConfig};\n    18\u2192//! # #[tokio::main]\n    19\u2192//! # async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    20\u2192//! let config = EventBusConfig::from_env()?;\n    21\u2192//! let bus = EventBus::connect(config).await?;\n    22\u2192//!\n    23\u2192//! bus.publish(DomainEvent::lesson_published(\"lesson-id\".into(), \"alice\".into()))\n    24\u2192//!     .await?;\n    25\u2192//!\n    26\u2192//! let mut sub = bus.subscribe(\"quiz.submitted\".into()).await?;\n    27\u2192//! while let Some(event) = sub.recv().await {\n    28\u2192//!     println!(\"Incoming event: {event:#?}\");\n    29\u2192//! }\n    30\u2192//! # Ok(()) }\n    31\u2192//! ```\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192#![deny(clippy::all)]\n    34\u2192#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192use std::{\n    37\u2192    fmt,\n    38\u2192    sync::Arc,\n    39\u2192    time::{Duration, SystemTime},\n    40\u2192};\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    43\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n    44\u2192use thiserror::Error;\n    45\u2192use tokio::{\n    46\u2192    select,\n    47\u2192    sync::{broadcast, mpsc},\n    48\u2192};\n    49\u2192use tracing::{debug, error, info, instrument, trace};\n    50\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192#[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n    53\u2192use async_nats::Client as NatsClient;\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192/// Top-level error type returned by public APIs.\n    56\u2192#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n    57\u2192pub enum BusError {\n    58\u2192    #[error(\"nats client error: {0}\")]\n    59\u2192    Nats(#[from] async_nats::Error),\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    /// Returned when a subscriber misses messages due to a full buffer.\n    62\u2192    #[error(\"subscriber lagged behind and missed messages\")]\n    63\u2192    Lagged,\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192    /// Serialization / deserialization failures.\n    66\u2192    #[error(\"serde json error: {0}\")]\n    67\u2192    Serde(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    /// Environment variable or config errors.\n    70\u2192    #[error(\"configuration error: {0}\")]\n    71\u2192    Config(String),\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    #[error(\"channel closed\")]\n    74\u2192    Closed,\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192/// An immutable domain event.\n    78\u2192///\n    79\u2192/// Persisted for auditing and sent over the event-bus.\n    80\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    81\u2192pub struct DomainEvent {\n    82\u2192    pub id:            Uuid,\n    83\u2192    pub occurred_at:   SystemTime,\n    84\u2192    pub event_type:    String,\n    85\u2192    pub correlation_id: Option<Uuid>,\n    86\u2192    #[serde(flatten)]\n    87\u2192    pub data:          EventData,\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192impl DomainEvent {\n    91\u2192    pub fn lesson_published(lesson_id: String, author: String) -> Self {\n    92\u2192        Self {\n    93\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n    94\u2192            occurred_at: SystemTime::now(),\n    95\u2192            event_type: \"lesson.published\".into(),\n    96\u2192            correlation_id: None,\n    97\u2192            data: EventData::LessonPublished { lesson_id, author },\n    98\u2192        }\n    99\u2192    }\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192    pub fn quiz_submitted(quiz_id: String, student_id: String, score: u8) -> Self {\n   102\u2192        Self {\n   103\u2192            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n   104\u2192            occurred_at: SystemTime::now(),\n   105\u2192            event_type: \"quiz.submitted\".into(),\n   106\u2192            correlation_id: None,\n   107\u2192            data: EventData::QuizSubmitted {\n   108\u2192                quiz_id,\n   109\u2192                student_id,\n   110\u2192                score,\n   111\u2192            },\n   112\u2192        }\n   113\u2192    }\n   114\u2192}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192/// Strongly-typed payloads for `DomainEvent`.\n   117\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   118\u2192#[serde(tag = \"kind\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n   119\u2192pub enum EventData {\n   120\u2192    LessonPublished { lesson_id: String, author: String },\n   121\u2192    QuizSubmitted { quiz_id: String, student_id: String, score: u8 },\n   122\u2192    LearningPulseResponded { pulse_id: String, responder: String },\n   123\u2192    //  \u2191 Add more variants as the domain evolves\n   124\u2192}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192/// A configuration object used to build [`EventBus`] instances.\n   127\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   128\u2192pub struct EventBusConfig {\n   129\u2192    /// Comma-separated list of NATS endpoints.\n   130\u2192    pub nats_url: String,\n   131\u2192    /// Optional credentials file for NATS authentication.\n   132\u2192    pub creds: Option<String>,\n   133\u2192    /// Subject prefix (e.g. `\"edupulse\"` \u2192 `\"edupulse.lesson.published\"`).\n   134\u2192    pub prefix: String,\n   135\u2192    /// Size of the bounded channel for fan-out to internal subscribers.\n   136\u2192    pub internal_buffer: usize,\n   137\u2192}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192impl fmt::Debug for EventBusConfig {\n   140\u2192    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n   141\u2192        f.debug_struct(\"EventBusConfig\")\n   142\u2192            .field(\"nats_url\", &self.nats_url)\n   143\u2192            .field(\"creds\", &self.creds.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"<none>\"))\n   144\u2192            .field(\"prefix\", &self.prefix)\n   145\u2192            .field(\"internal_buffer\", &self.internal_buffer)\n   146\u2192            .finish()\n   147\u2192    }\n   148\u2192}\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192impl Default for EventBusConfig {\n   151\u2192    fn default() -> Self {\n   152\u2192        Self {\n   153\u2192            nats_url: \"localhost:4222\".into(),\n   154\u2192            creds: None,\n   155\u2192            prefix: \"edupulse\".into(),\n   156\u2192            internal_buffer: 1024,\n   157\u2192        }\n   158\u2192    }\n   159\u2192}\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192impl EventBusConfig {\n   162\u2192    /// Attempt to build a configuration from environment variables.\n   163\u2192    pub fn from_env() -> Result<Self, BusError> {\n   164\u2192        let nats_url = std::env::var(\"NATS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| Self::default().nats_url);\n   165\u2192        let creds = std::env::var(\"NATS_CREDS\").ok();\n   166\u2192        let prefix = std::env::var(\"EVENT_SUBJECT_PREFIX\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"edupulse\".into());\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   169\u2192            nats_url,\n   170\u2192            creds,\n   171\u2192            prefix,\n   172\u2192            internal_buffer: 1024,\n   173\u2192        })\n   174\u2192    }\n   175\u2192}\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192/// Tokio-aware streaming receiver produced by [`EventBus::subscribe`].\n   178\u2192pub struct EventStream {\n   179\u2192    rx: broadcast::Receiver<Arc<DomainEvent>>,\n   180\u2192}\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192impl EventStream {\n   183\u2192    /// Await the next event.  `None` is returned only when the bus is shutting\n   184\u2192    /// down or the sender dropped, signalling the consumer to exit.\n   185\u2192    pub async fn recv(&mut self) -> Option<Arc<DomainEvent>> {\n   186\u2192        loop {\n   187\u2192            match self.rx.recv().await {\n   188\u2192                Ok(ev) => return Some(ev),\n   189\u2192                Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Lagged(_)) => {\n   190\u2192                    error!(\"subscriber lagged behind event stream\");\n   191\u2192                    return None;\n   192\u2192                }\n   193\u2192                Err(broadcast::error::RecvError::Closed) => return None,\n   194\u2192            }\n   195\u2192        }\n   196\u2192    }\n   197\u2192}\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192#[async_trait]\n   200\u2192pub trait EventBus: Send + Sync {\n   201\u2192    /// Publish a single event to the broker.\n   202\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) -> Result<(), BusError>;\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192    /// Subscribe to a beginning of subject pattern.  Wild-cards supported\n   205\u2192    /// (`*` for token, `>` for all remaining).\n   206\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self, subject: String) -> Result<EventStream, BusError>;\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192    /// Gracefully close all connections / tasks.\n   209\u2192    async fn close(&self) -> Result<(), BusError>;\n   210\u2192}\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192/// Concrete NATS-backed implementation.\n   213\u2192///\n   214\u2192/// When the `in_memory_bus` feature is enabled, a stub is compiled instead\u2014\n   215\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   216\u2192pub struct NatsEventBus {\n   217\u2192    #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   218\u2192    nats: NatsClient,\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192    /// Internal fan-out so multiple components inside the same service can\n   221\u2192    /// subscribe without hitting the broker again.\n   222\u2192    tx: broadcast::Sender<Arc<DomainEvent>>,\n   223\u2192    cfg: Arc<EventBusConfig>,\n   224\u2192}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192impl fmt::Debug for NatsEventBus {\n   227\u2192    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n   228\u2192        f.debug_struct(\"NatsEventBus\")\n   229\u2192            .field(\"cfg\", &self.cfg)\n   230\u2192            .finish()\n   231\u2192    }\n   232\u2192}\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192impl NatsEventBus {\n   235\u2192    /// Establish connection and spin up the inbound pump.\n   236\u2192    pub async fn connect(cfg: EventBusConfig) -> Result<Arc<Self>, BusError> {\n   237\u2192        let cfg = Arc::new(cfg);\n   238\u2192        let (tx, _) = broadcast::channel(cfg.internal_buffer);\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192        #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   241\u2192        let nats = {\n   242\u2192            use async_nats::ConnectOptions;\n   243\u2192            let mut opts = ConnectOptions::new();\n   244\u2192            if let Some(creds) = &cfg.creds {\n   245\u2192                opts = opts.authenticate(async_nats::auth::Credentials::from_file(creds));\n   246\u2192            }\n   247\u2192            let client = opts.name(\"edupulse-event-bus\").connect(&cfg.nats_url).await?;\n   248\u2192            client.flush().await?;\n   249\u2192            client\n   250\u2192        };\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192        #[cfg(feature = \"in_memory_bus\")]\n   253\u2192        let nats = ();\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192        let bus = Arc::new(Self { nats, tx, cfg });\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192        // Spawn inbound pump only when real NATS is used.\n   258\u2192        #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   259\u2192        {\n   260\u2192            let bus_clone = Arc::clone(&bus);\n   261\u2192            tokio::spawn(async move {\n   262\u2192                if let Err(e) = bus_clone.run_inbound_pump().await {\n   263\u2192                    error!(\"Inbound pump terminated: {e}\");\n   264\u2192                }\n   265\u2192            });\n   266\u2192        }\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192        Ok(bus)\n   269\u2192    }\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192    /// Long-running task that forwards NATS messages to [`broadcast::Sender`].\n   272\u2192    #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   273\u2192    async fn run_inbound_pump(self: Arc<Self>) -> Result<(), BusError> {\n   274\u2192        let subject = format!(\"{}.*\", self.cfg.prefix);\n   275\u2192        let mut sub = self.nats.subscribe(subject).await?;\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192        loop {\n   278\u2192            select! {\n   279\u2192                msg = sub.next() => {\n   280\u2192                    match msg {\n   281\u2192                        Some(m) => self.forward_inbound(m).await?,\n   282\u2192                        None => break,\n   283\u2192                    }\n   284\u2192                }\n   285\u2192                // Add graceful shutdown signal here if needed\n   286\u2192            }\n   287\u2192        }\n   288\u2192        Ok(())\n   289\u2192    }\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192    #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   292\u2192    async fn forward_inbound(&self, msg: async_nats::Message) -> Result<(), BusError> {\n   293\u2192        let ev: DomainEvent = serde_json::from_slice(&msg.payload)?;\n   294\u2192        trace!(target: \"event_bus\", ?ev, \"inbound event\");\n   295\u2192        let _ = self.tx.send(Arc::new(ev));\n   296\u2192        Ok(())\n   297\u2192    }\n   298\u2192}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192#[async_trait]\n   301\u2192impl EventBus for NatsEventBus {\n   302\u2192    #[instrument(skip(self, event))]\n   303\u2192    async fn publish(&self, event: DomainEvent) -> Result<(), BusError> {\n   304\u2192        let subject = format!(\"{}.{}\", self.cfg.prefix, event.event_type);\n   305\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(&event)?;\n   306\u2192        #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   307\u2192        self.nats.publish(subject, payload.into()).await?;\n   308\u2192        #[cfg(feature = \"in_memory_bus\")]\n   309\u2192        {\n   310\u2192            let _ = self.tx.send(Arc::new(event.clone()));\n   311\u2192            trace!(?event, \"loopback publish (in-memory)\");\n   312\u2192        }\n   313\u2192        Ok(())\n   314\u2192    }\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    async fn subscribe(&self, subject: String) -> Result<EventStream, BusError> {\n   317\u2192        let pattern = if subject.starts_with(&self.cfg.prefix) {\n   318\u2192            subject\n   319\u2192        } else {\n   320\u2192            format!(\"{}.{}\", self.cfg.prefix, subject)\n   321\u2192        };\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192        #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   324\u2192        {\n   325\u2192            // Ensure NATS has a subscription so events get delivered even if\n   326\u2192            // no internal consumers are listening yet.\n   327\u2192            let _sub = self.nats.subscribe(pattern).await?;\n   328\u2192            // Discard messages; we'll receive them via inbound pump.\n   329\u2192        }\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192        let rx = self.tx.subscribe();\n   332\u2192        Ok(EventStream { rx })\n   333\u2192    }\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192    async fn close(&self) -> Result<(), BusError> {\n   336\u2192        // Closing broadcast::Sender drops all receivers.\n   337\u2192        self.tx.close();\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192        #[cfg(not(feature = \"in_memory_bus\"))]\n   340\u2192        self.nats.close();\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192        Ok(())\n   343\u2192    }\n   344\u2192}\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192// Optional re-export for consumers of this library.\n   347\u2192pub use NatsEventBus as DefaultEventBus;\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   350\u2192mod tests {\n   351\u2192    use super::*;\n   352\u2192    use tokio::time::timeout;\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192    // Use in-memory bus to avoid needing a running NATS server.\n   355\u2192    #[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\", worker_threads = 2)]\n   356\u2192    async fn roundtrip_publish_subscribe() -> Result<(), BusError> {\n   357\u2192        let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()\n   358\u2192            .with_test_writer()\n   359\u2192            .with_env_filter(\"debug\")\n   360\u2192            .try_init();\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192        let bus = NatsEventBus::connect(EventBusConfig::default()).await?;\n   363\u2192        let mut stream = bus.subscribe(\"*\".into()).await?;\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192        let lesson = DomainEvent::lesson_published(\"123\".into(), \"alice\".into());\n   366\u2192        bus.publish(lesson.clone()).await?;\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192        let received = timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), stream.recv()).await?;\n   369\u2192        assert_eq!(received.unwrap().id, lesson.id);\n   370\u2192        bus.close().await?;\n   371\u2192        Ok(())\n   372\u2192    }\n   373\u2192}\n   374\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192```rust\n     2\u2192//! src/module_34.rs\n     3\u2192//!\n     4\u2192//! Audit trail & GDPR-erasure support for EduPulse Live.\n     5\u2192//!\n     6\u2192//! This module listens to domain events that flow through the message broker,\n     7\u2192//! persists an immutable audit trail in Postgres (via `sqlx`), and optionally\n     8\u2192//! indexes the events in Meilisearch for fast compliance searches.\n     9\u2192//!\n    10\u2192//! It provides helpers to:\n    11\u2192//!  1. Persist any `DomainEvent` that implements `serde::Serialize`.\n    12\u2192//!  2. Query the audit log by user, event type, or date range.\n    13\u2192//!  3. Trigger GDPR \u201cright-to-be-forgotten\u201d procedures while preserving a\n    14\u2192//!     cryptographic tombstone of the erasure request.\n    15\u2192//!\n    16\u2192//! The code below is \u201cproduction-grade\u201d: non-blocking, structured logging,\n    17\u2192//! proper error bubbling, and feature-gated search integration.\n    18\u2192//!\n    19\u2192//! NOTE: \u2026\n    20\u2192#![allow(dead_code)]\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192use std::sync::Arc;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192use anyhow::{Context, Result};\n    25\u2192use async_trait::async_trait;\n    26\u2192use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\n    27\u2192use futures::TryStreamExt;\n    28\u2192use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Deserialize, Serialize};\n    29\u2192use sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Row};\n    30\u2192use tokio::sync::RwLock;\n    31\u2192use tracing::{error, info, instrument};\n    32\u2192use uuid::Uuid;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192#[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n    35\u2192use meilisearch_sdk::{\n    36\u2192    client::*,\n    37\u2192    indexes::Index,\n    38\u2192    document::Document,\n    39\u2192};\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/// Unique wrapper around a `Uuid` for stronger typing.\n    42\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    43\u2192#[serde(transparent)]\n    44\u2192pub struct EventId(Uuid);\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192impl EventId {\n    47\u2192    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    48\u2192        Self(Uuid::new_v4())\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192/// Tag that every domain event must expose.\n    53\u2192#[async_trait]\n    54\u2192pub trait DomainEvent: Serialize + Send + Sync {\n    55\u2192    /// Name of the event, e.g. `\"LessonPublished\"`.\n    56\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    /// The principal user (if any) related to this event.\n    59\u2192    fn user_id(&self) -> Option<Uuid>;\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    /// Occurrence in UTC.\n    62\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n    63\u2192}\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192/// Concrete audit-trail record stored in Postgres.\n    66\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n    67\u2192pub struct AuditLogEntry {\n    68\u2192    pub id:          EventId,\n    69\u2192    pub event_type:  String,\n    70\u2192    pub payload:     serde_json::Value,\n    71\u2192    pub user_id:     Option<Uuid>,\n    72\u2192    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192/// Repository abstraction (Repository pattern).\n    76\u2192#[async_trait]\n    77\u2192pub trait AuditLogRepository: Send + Sync {\n    78\u2192    async fn persist<E: DomainEvent>(&self, event: &E) -> Result<()>;\n    79\u2192    async fn fetch_by_user(\n    80\u2192        &self,\n    81\u2192        user_id: Uuid,\n    82\u2192        limit: i64,\n    83\u2192        offset: i64,\n    84\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditLogEntry>>;\n    85\u2192    async fn forget_user(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<()>;\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192/// PostgreSQL implementation.\n    89\u2192pub struct PostgresAuditRepo {\n    90\u2192    pool: PgPool,\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    #[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n    93\u2192    index: Option<Index>,\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192impl PostgresAuditRepo {\n    97\u2192    /// Establish a new repository.\n    98\u2192    ///\n    99\u2192    /// # Parameters\n   100\u2192    ///\n   101\u2192    /// * `pg_dsn` \u2013 Postgres connection string.\n   102\u2192    /// * `max_conns` \u2013 Maximum connection pool size.\n   103\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"info\", skip_all)]\n   104\u2192    pub async fn new(pg_dsn: &str, max_conns: u32) -> Result<Self> {\n   105\u2192        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n   106\u2192            .max_connections(max_conns)\n   107\u2192            .connect(pg_dsn)\n   108\u2192            .await\n   109\u2192            .context(\"cannot connect to Postgres\")?;\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192        // Ensure table exists; in real code migrations run separately.\n   112\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   113\u2192            r#\"\n   114\u2192            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (\n   115\u2192                id          UUID PRIMARY KEY,\n   116\u2192                event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n   117\u2192                payload     JSONB NOT NULL,\n   118\u2192                user_id     UUID,\n   119\u2192                occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL\n   120\u2192            );\n   121\u2192            \"#,\n   122\u2192        )\n   123\u2192        .execute(&pool)\n   124\u2192        .await?;\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192        #[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n   127\u2192        let index = {\n   128\u2192            let client = Client::new(\"http://localhost:7700\", \"masterKey\");\n   129\u2192            let idx = client.get_or_create(\"audit_log\").await?;\n   130\u2192            idx.set_primary_key(\"id\").await?;\n   131\u2192            Some(idx)\n   132\u2192        };\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192        Ok(Self {\n   135\u2192            pool,\n   136\u2192            #[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n   137\u2192            index,\n   138\u2192        })\n   139\u2192    }\n   140\u2192}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192#[async_trait]\n   143\u2192impl AuditLogRepository for PostgresAuditRepo {\n   144\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip_all, err)]\n   145\u2192    async fn persist<E: DomainEvent>(&self, event: &E) -> Result<()> {\n   146\u2192        // Serialize payload now to avoid borrowing issues & corruption later.\n   147\u2192        let payload = serde_json::to_value(event).context(\"serialize event payload\")?;\n   148\u2192        let id = EventId::new();\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   151\u2192            r#\"\n   152\u2192            INSERT INTO audit_log (id, event_type, payload, user_id, occurred_at)\n   153\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n   154\u2192            \"#,\n   155\u2192        )\n   156\u2192        .bind(id.0)\n   157\u2192        .bind(event.event_type())\n   158\u2192        .bind(&payload)\n   159\u2192        .bind(event.user_id())\n   160\u2192        .bind(event.occurred_at())\n   161\u2192        .execute(&self.pool)\n   162\u2192        .await\n   163\u2192        .context(\"insert audit record\")?;\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192        #[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n   166\u2192        if let Some(index) = &self.index {\n   167\u2192            // We implement Document manually instead of deriving to avoid trait bound collision.\n   168\u2192            #[derive(Serialize)]\n   169\u2192            struct SearchDoc<'a> {\n   170\u2192                id: Uuid,\n   171\u2192                event_type: &'a str,\n   172\u2192                user_id: Option<Uuid>,\n   173\u2192                occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   174\u2192            }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192            let doc = SearchDoc {\n   177\u2192                id: id.0,\n   178\u2192                event_type: event.event_type(),\n   179\u2192                user_id: event.user_id(),\n   180\u2192                occurred_at: event.occurred_at(),\n   181\u2192            };\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192            // Non-blocking; Meilisearch handles indexing eventually.\n   184\u2192            let _ = index.add_documents(&[doc], None).await;\n   185\u2192        }\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192        Ok(())\n   188\u2192    }\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(self))]\n   191\u2192    async fn fetch_by_user(\n   192\u2192        &self,\n   193\u2192        user_id: Uuid,\n   194\u2192        limit: i64,\n   195\u2192        offset: i64,\n   196\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditLogEntry>> {\n   197\u2192        let mut cursor = sqlx::query(\n   198\u2192            r#\"\n   199\u2192            SELECT id, event_type, payload, user_id, occurred_at\n   200\u2192            FROM audit_log\n   201\u2192            WHERE user_id = $1\n   202\u2192            ORDER BY occurred_at DESC\n   203\u2192            LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3\n   204\u2192            \"#,\n   205\u2192        )\n   206\u2192        .bind(user_id)\n   207\u2192        .bind(limit)\n   208\u2192        .bind(offset)\n   209\u2192        .fetch(&self.pool);\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192        let mut out = Vec::new();\n   212\u2192        while let Some(row) = cursor.try_next().await? {\n   213\u2192            out.push(AuditLogEntry {\n   214\u2192                id: EventId(row.try_get::<Uuid, _>(\"id\")?),\n   215\u2192                event_type: row.try_get(\"event_type\")?,\n   216\u2192                payload: row.try_get::<serde_json::Value, _>(\"payload\")?,\n   217\u2192                user_id: row.try_get(\"user_id\")?,\n   218\u2192                occurred_at: row.try_get(\"occurred_at\")?,\n   219\u2192            });\n   220\u2192        }\n   221\u2192        Ok(out)\n   222\u2192    }\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192    /// GDPR \u201cright-to-erasure\u201d.\n   225\u2192    ///\n   226\u2192    /// We do *soft* deletion: PII is wiped, but an audit tombstone remains.\n   227\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"warn\", skip(self))]\n   228\u2192    async fn forget_user(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<()> {\n   229\u2192        let tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192        // 1. Wipe personal data (example table).\n   232\u2192        sqlx::query(\"UPDATE users SET email = NULL, name = NULL WHERE id = $1\")\n   233\u2192            .bind(user_id)\n   234\u2192            .execute(&mut *tx)\n   235\u2192            .await?;\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192        // 2. Insert a tombstone audit.\n   238\u2192        let tombstone = serde_json::json!({\n   239\u2192            \"reason\": \"gdpr_forget_user\",\n   240\u2192            \"user_id\": user_id,\n   241\u2192            \"ts\": Utc::now(),\n   242\u2192        });\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192        sqlx::query(\n   245\u2192            r#\"\n   246\u2192            INSERT INTO audit_log (id, event_type, payload, user_id, occurred_at)\n   247\u2192            VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)\n   248\u2192            \"#,\n   249\u2192        )\n   250\u2192        .bind(Uuid::new_v4())\n   251\u2192        .bind(\"UserErased\")\n   252\u2192        .bind(&tombstone)\n   253\u2192        .bind(user_id)\n   254\u2192        .bind(Utc::now())\n   255\u2192        .execute(&mut *tx)\n   256\u2192        .await?;\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192        tx.commit().await?;\n   259\u2192\n   260\u2192        #[cfg(feature = \"search\")]\n   261\u2192        if let Some(index) = &self.index {\n   262\u2192            let _ = index.delete_documents(&[user_id.to_string()]).await;\n   263\u2192        }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192        Ok(())\n   266\u2192    }\n   267\u2192}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192/// High-level fa\u00e7ade (Service Layer).\n   270\u2192#[derive(Clone)]\n   271\u2192pub struct AuditService {\n   272\u2192    repo: Arc<dyn AuditLogRepository>,\n   273\u2192    /// Cached config with live reload (demonstration).\n   274\u2192    cfg:  Arc<RwLock<ServiceConfig>>,\n   275\u2192}\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192impl AuditService {\n   278\u2192    pub fn new(repo: Arc<dyn AuditLogRepository>, cfg: ServiceConfig) -> Self {\n   279\u2192        Self {\n   280\u2192            repo,\n   281\u2192            cfg: Arc::new(RwLock::new(cfg)),\n   282\u2192        }\n   283\u2192    }\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192    pub async fn update_config(&self, new_cfg: ServiceConfig) {\n   286\u2192        let mut guard = self.cfg.write().await;\n   287\u2192        *guard = new_cfg;\n   288\u2192        info!(?*guard, \"audit service config updated\");\n   289\u2192    }\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192    /// Persist an event; if capturing fails we *log & bubble*.\n   292\u2192    #[instrument(level = \"info\", skip(self, event), fields(ev = event.event_type()))]\n   293\u2192    pub async fn record<E: DomainEvent>(&self, event: &E) -> Result<()> {\n   294\u2192        // Toggle via config at runtime (ex: maintenance window).\n   295\u2192        if !self.cfg.read().await.enabled {\n   296\u2192            info!(\"audit service disabled, skipping persist\");\n   297\u2192            return Ok(());\n   298\u2192        }\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        if let Err(err) = self.repo.persist(event).await {\n   301\u2192            error!(error = %err, \"failed to persist audit event\");\n   302\u2192            return Err(err);\n   303\u2192        }\n   304\u2192        Ok(())\n   305\u2192    }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    pub async fn events_for_user(\n   308\u2192        &self,\n   309\u2192        user_id: Uuid,\n   310\u2192        limit: i64,\n   311\u2192        offset: i64,\n   312\u2192    ) -> Result<Vec<AuditLogEntry>> {\n   313\u2192        self.repo.fetch_by_user(user_id, limit, offset).await\n   314\u2192    }\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    pub async fn gdpr_forget_user(&self, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<()> {\n   317\u2192        self.repo.forget_user(user_id).await\n   318\u2192    }\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192/// Runtime-switchable service config.\n   322\u2192#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   323\u2192pub struct ServiceConfig {\n   324\u2192    pub enabled: bool,\n   325\u2192}\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192impl Default for ServiceConfig {\n   328\u2192    fn default() -> Self {\n   329\u2192        Self { enabled: true }\n   330\u2192    }\n   331\u2192}\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192/* ==== Example domain events ================================================= */\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n   336\u2192pub struct LessonPublished {\n   337\u2192    pub lesson_id:  Uuid,\n   338\u2192    pub course_id:  Uuid,\n   339\u2192    pub author_id:  Uuid,\n   340\u2192    pub published_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n   341\u2192}\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192#[async_trait]\n   344\u2192impl DomainEvent for LessonPublished {\n   345\u2192    fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n   346\u2192        \"LessonPublished\"\n   347\u2192    }\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192    fn user_id(&self) -> Option<Uuid> {\n   350\u2192        Some(self.author_id)\n   351\u2192    }\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n   354\u2192        self.published_at\n   355\u2192    }\n   356\u2192}\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192/* ==== Unit tests (tokio) ==================================================== */\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192#[cfg(test)]\n   361\u2192mod tests {\n   362\u2192    use super::*;\n   363\u2192    use once_cell::sync::Lazy;\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192    static RT_LOG: Lazy<()> = Lazy::new(|| {\n   366\u2192        let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()\n   367\u2192            .with_test_writer()\n   368\u2192            .try_init();\n   369\u2192    });\n   370\u2192\n   371\u2192    async fn init_repo() -> PostgresAuditRepo {\n   372\u2192        let _ = *RT_LOG; // init logging once\n   373\u2192        // For tests we spin a transient database (use `docker-compose` CI) or\n   374\u2192        // connect to `$TEST_DATABASE_URL`.\n   375\u2192        let dsn = std::env::var(\"TEST_DATABASE_URL\")\n   376\u2192            .expect(\"TEST_DATABASE_URL must be set for integration tests\");\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192        PostgresAuditRepo::new(&dsn, 5)\n   379\u2192            .await\n   380\u2192            .expect(\"cannot create repo\")\n   381\u2192    }\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192    #[tokio::test]\n   384\u2192    async fn record_and_fetch() {\n   385\u2192        let repo = init_repo().await;\n   386\u2192        let svc  = AuditService::new(Arc::new(repo), ServiceConfig::default());\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192        let ev = LessonPublished {\n   389\u2192            lesson_id:  Uuid::new_v4(),\n   390\u2192            course_id:  Uuid::new_v4(),\n   391\u2192            author_id:  Uuid::new_v4(),\n   392\u2192            published_at: Utc::now(),\n   393\u2192        };\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192        svc.record(&ev).await.expect(\"record failed\");\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192        let entries = svc.events_for_user(ev.author_id, 10, 0)\n   398\u2192            .await\n   399\u2192            .expect(\"fetch failed\");\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192        assert!(!entries.is_empty());\n   402\u2192        assert_eq!(entries[0].event_type, \"LessonPublished\");\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192}\n   405\u2192```\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
