Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_35.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! module_35 \u2013 Event-sourced learning-pulse subsystem\n//!\n//! This module demonstrates how EduPulse Live persists and publishes\n//! domain events for the \u201clearning-pulse\u201d feature using an out-box pattern.\n//!\n//! Production-quality concerns addressed in this file\n//! --------------------------------------------------\n//! \u2022 Service-layer abstraction (`LearningPulseService`)  \n//! \u2022 Repository pattern backed by `sqlx` (`PgLearningPulseRepo`)  \n//! \u2022 Event store + message publisher decoupling  \n//! \u2022 Asynchronous, cancel-safe I/O (`tokio`, `sqlx::transaction`)  \n//! \u2022 Structured error handling with context propagation (`anyhow`, `thiserror`)  \n//! \u2022 Serde-powered event serialization (JSONB column + NATS transport)  \n//! \u2022 TLS-first NATS client configuration  \n//!\n//! Compile with:  \n//! `cargo build --features runtime-tokio-rustls,sqlx-postgres,nats`\n//!\n//! Database schema (PostgreSQL)\n//! ----------------------------\n//! ```sql\n//! CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS learning_pulses (\n//!     id              UUID            PRIMARY KEY,\n//!     title           TEXT            NOT NULL,\n//!     author_id       UUID            NOT NULL,\n//!     created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()\n//! );\n //!\n//! CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_event_outbox (\n//!     id              BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n//!     aggregate_id    UUID            NOT NULL,\n//!     event_type      TEXT            NOT NULL,\n//!     payload         JSONB           NOT NULL,\n//!     occurred_at     TIMESTAMPTZ     NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n//!     dispatched      BOOLEAN         NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE\n//! );\n//! ```\n\n\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tokio::time;\nuse tracing::{error, info};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Common types & traits\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A domain event emitted by an aggregate root.\n///\n/// Every event has:\n/// \u2022 a stable, unique id (for idempotency)  \n/// \u2022 the id of the aggregate it belongs to  \n/// \u2022 an ISO-timestamp of when it occurred within the domain  \n/// \u2022 a semantic `event_type` string used for routing on the message bus  \n/// \u2022 a `version` field that allows evolving schema without breaking consumers\n#[typetag::serde(tag = \"event_type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub trait DomainEvent: Send + Sync {\n    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid;\n    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc>;\n    fn version(&self) -> u8;\n}\n\n/// Generic envelope stored in the outbox and delivered to the broker.\n#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct EventEnvelope {\n    pub event_id: Uuid,\n    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    pub event_type: String,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub version: u8,\n    pub payload: serde_json::Value,\n}\n\nimpl EventEnvelope {\n    fn new<E: DomainEvent + Serialize + ?Sized>(event: &E) -> Result<Self> {\n        Ok(Self {\n            event_id: event.event_id(),\n            aggregate_id: event.aggregate_id(),\n            event_type: std::any::type_name::<E>().to_string(), // e.g. \"learning_pulse::Created\"\n            occurred_at: event.occurred_at(),\n            version: event.version(),\n            payload: serde_json::to_value(event)?,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Learning-pulse domain\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Aggregate root for a learning pulse.\n///\n/// Only minimal data is stored here. Business rules live in `impl`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LearningPulse {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub author_id: Uuid,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl LearningPulse {\n    /// Factory method ensuring invariants\n    pub fn new(title: impl Into<String>, author_id: Uuid) -> Result<(Self, LearningPulseCreated)> {\n        let title = title.into();\n        if title.trim().is_empty() {\n            return Err(DomainError::Validation(\n                \"Title must not be empty\".into(),\n            )\n            .into());\n        }\n        let id = Uuid::new_v4();\n        let created_at = Utc::now();\n\n        let pulse = Self {\n            id,\n            title: title.clone(),\n            author_id,\n            created_at,\n        };\n        let evt = LearningPulseCreated {\n            id,\n            title,\n            author_id,\n            occurred_at: created_at,\n            version: 1,\n        };\n        Ok((pulse, evt))\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Events\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LearningPulseCreated {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub author_id: Uuid,\n    #[serde(with = \"chrono::serde::ts_seconds\")]\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub version: u8,\n}\n\n#[typetag::serde]\nimpl DomainEvent for LearningPulseCreated {\n    fn event_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.id\n    }\n    fn aggregate_id(&self) -> Uuid {\n        self.id\n    }\n    fn occurred_at(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {\n        self.occurred_at\n    }\n    fn version(&self) -> u8 {\n        self.version\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Repositories\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait LearningPulseRepository: Send + Sync {\n    async fn insert(&self, pulse: &LearningPulse, tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>) -> Result<()>;\n    async fn by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>>;\n}\n\n/// PostgreSQL implementation using `sqlx`\npub struct PgLearningPulseRepo;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl LearningPulseRepository for PgLearningPulseRepo {\n    async fn insert(\n        &self,\n        pulse: &LearningPulse,\n        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"INSERT INTO learning_pulses (id, title, author_id, created_at)\n               VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)\"#,\n            pulse.id,\n            pulse.title,\n            pulse.author_id,\n            pulse.created_at\n        )\n        .execute(&mut *tx)\n        .await\n        .context(\"insert learning pulse\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<LearningPulse>> {\n        let rec = sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"SELECT id, title, author_id, created_at\n               FROM learning_pulses WHERE id = $1\"#,\n            id,\n        )\n        .fetch_optional(self.pool()?)\n        .await\n        .context(\"fetch learning pulse\")?;\n\n        if let Some(r) = rec {\n            Ok(Some(LearningPulse {\n                id: r.id,\n                title: r.title,\n                author_id: r.author_id,\n                created_at: r.created_at,\n            }))\n        } else {\n            Ok(None)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl PgLearningPulseRepo {\n    fn pool(&self) -> Result<&PgPool> {\n        PG_POOL.get().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"pool not initialised\"))\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Event store\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventStore: Send + Sync {\n    async fn append(\n        &self,\n        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n    ) -> Result<()>;\n\n    /// Atomically mark an event as dispatched.\n    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, id: i64) -> Result<()>;\n}\n\npub struct PgEventStore;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventStore for PgEventStore {\n    async fn append(\n        &self,\n        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"INSERT INTO domain_event_outbox\n               (aggregate_id, event_type, payload)\n               VALUES ($1, $2, $3)\"#,\n            envelope.aggregate_id,\n            envelope.event_type,\n            envelope.payload\n        )\n        .execute(&mut *tx)\n        .await\n        .context(\"append to outbox\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn mark_dispatched(&self, id: i64) -> Result<()> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"UPDATE domain_event_outbox\n               SET dispatched = TRUE\n               WHERE id = $1\"#,\n            id\n        )\n        .execute(PG_POOL.get().unwrap())\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Message broker publisher\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher: Send + Sync {\n    async fn publish(&self, envelope: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()>;\n}\n\n/// NATS IO implementation with Rustls TLS.\npub struct NatsPublisher {\n    connection: async_nats::Client,\n}\n\nimpl NatsPublisher {\n    pub async fn connect() -> Result<Self> {\n        // The NATS url should use tls: e.g. \"tls://broker.prod.edupulse.live:4222\"\n        let url = std::env::var(\"NATS_URL\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"tls://localhost:4222\".into());\n        let connection = async_nats::connect(url).await.context(\"connect nats\")?;\n        Ok(Self { connection })\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for NatsPublisher {\n    async fn publish(&self, envelope: &EventEnvelope) -> Result<()> {\n        let subject = format!(\"{}.v{}\", envelope.event_type, envelope.version);\n        let payload = serde_json::to_vec(envelope)?;\n        self.connection\n            .publish(subject, payload.into())\n            .await\n            .context(\"nats publish\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Service layer\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\npub struct LearningPulseService<R: LearningPulseRepository, E: EventStore, P: EventPublisher> {\n    repo: Arc<R>,\n    store: Arc<E>,\n    publisher: Arc<P>,\n    pool: Arc<PgPool>,\n}\n\nimpl<R, E, P> LearningPulseService<R, E, P>\nwhere\n    R: LearningPulseRepository + 'static,\n    E: EventStore + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    pub fn new(repo: R, store: E, publisher: P, pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            repo: Arc::new(repo),\n            store: Arc::new(store),\n            publisher: Arc::new(publisher),\n            pool: Arc::new(pool),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a learning pulse and publish the corresponding event.\n    ///\n    /// Uses the out-box pattern: events are stored in the same tx as\n    /// the aggregate write; a background task flushes them to NATS.\n    pub async fn create_pulse(&self, title: &str, author_id: Uuid) -> Result<Uuid> {\n        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n        let (pulse, evt) = LearningPulse::new(title, author_id)?;\n        self.repo.insert(&pulse, &mut tx).await?;\n        let envelope = EventEnvelope::new(&evt)?;\n        self.store.append(&envelope, &mut tx).await?;\n        tx.commit().await?;\n\n        // fire-and-forget: real project would delegate to a queue consumer\n        let publisher = self.publisher.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(err) = publisher.publish(&envelope).await {\n                error!(?err, \"failed to publish LearningPulseCreated\");\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(pulse.id)\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Error handling\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Error, Debug)]\npub enum DomainError {\n    #[error(\"validation error: {0}\")]\n    Validation(String),\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Initialisation helpers\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Shared, lazily-initialised PostgreSQL pool (used by repo & store).\nstatic PG_POOL: once_cell::sync::OnceCell<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::OnceCell::new();\n\n/// Start the background outbox dispatcher to guarantee at-least-once delivery.\n///\n/// Batches undispatched rows and publishes them, marking them dispatched on success.\n/// Sleeps for `interval` between sweeps.\npub async fn start_outbox_dispatcher<E, P>(\n    store: Arc<E>,\n    publisher: Arc<P>,\n    interval: Duration,\n    pool: Arc<PgPool>,\n) -> Result<()>\nwhere\n    E: EventStore + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    tokio::spawn(async move {\n        loop {\n            if let Err(err) = flush_once(&store, &publisher, &pool).await {\n                error!(?err, \"outbox flush failure\");\n            }\n            time::sleep(interval).await;\n        }\n    });\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nasync fn flush_once<E, P>(store: &Arc<E>, publisher: &Arc<P>, pool: &Arc<PgPool>) -> Result<()>\nwhere\n    E: EventStore + 'static,\n    P: EventPublisher + 'static,\n{\n    let mut tx = pool.begin().await?;\n    let rows = sqlx::query!(\n        r#\"SELECT id,\n                  aggregate_id,\n                  event_type,\n                  payload,\n                  occurred_at,\n                  dispatched\n           FROM domain_event_outbox\n           WHERE dispatched = FALSE\n           ORDER BY occurred_at\n           LIMIT 50\n        \"#\n    )\n    .fetch_all(&mut *tx)\n    .await?;\n\n    for row in rows {\n        let envelope = EventEnvelope {\n            event_id: Uuid::new_v4(), // Not stored; only used for broker duplication protection\n            aggregate_id: row.aggregate_id,\n            event_type: row.event_type,\n            occurred_at: row.occurred_at,\n            version: 1,\n            payload: row.payload,\n        };\n        publisher.publish(&envelope).await?;\n        store.mark_dispatched(row.id).await?;\n    }\n    tx.commit().await?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Application bootstrap example\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// One-shot bootstrap for Actix-web / Axum etc.  \n/// Not compiled by default \u2013 only illustrative.\n///\n/// ```ignore\n/// #[tokio::main]\n/// async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n///     tracing_subscriber::fmt::init();\n///\n///     let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n///         .max_connections(5)\n///         .connect(&std::env::var(\"DATABASE_URL\")?)\n///         .await?;\n///     PG_POOL.set(pool.clone()).unwrap();\n///\n///     let publisher = NatsPublisher::connect().await?;\n///\n///     let svc = LearningPulseService::new(\n///         PgLearningPulseRepo,\n///         PgEventStore,\n///         publisher.clone(),\n///         pool.clone(),\n///     );\n///\n///     start_outbox_dispatcher(\n///         Arc::new(PgEventStore),\n///         Arc::new(publisher),\n///         Duration::from_secs(5),\n///         Arc::new(pool),\n///     )\n///     .await?;\n///\n///     // Create a pulse\n///     let pulse_id = svc\n///         .create_pulse(\"Explain the CAP theorem in 3 sentences\", Uuid::new_v4())\n///         .await?;\n///     println!(\"pulse id: {pulse_id}\");\n///\n///     Ok(())\n/// }\n/// ```\n```",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_35.txt",
    "numLines": 498,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 498
  },
  "type": "text"
}

104. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Solution: rust_web_social_expert_073_bug_investigation_expert_01\n\n## Key Files Identified\n\nBased on the task description and codebase analysis, the following files are relevant to the cross-user data leakage investigation:\n\n- /app/project/src/module_48.txt - Event-Driven Infrastructure for EduPulse Live (outbox pattern, event publishing)\n- /app/project/src/module_15.txt - Badge-awarding microservice (event consumption and domain events)\n- /app/project/src/module_1.txt - Authentication & Session Management with JWT + Redis\n- /app/project/src/module_36.txt - Learning-Pulse domain with RequestContext handling\n- /app/project/src/module_49.txt - Session management and JWT authentication middleware\n- /app/project/src/module_35.txt - Global PG_POOL pattern using OnceCell\n- /app/project/src/module_52.txt - Global RabbitMqBus with OnceCell pattern\n- /app/project/src/module_77.txt - Global Lazy<PgPool> patterns\n- /app/project/src/utils.txt - Global SnowflakeIdGenerator using OnceLock\n\n## Code Evidence\n\n### Authentication Pattern (Proper Implementation)\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_49.txt\n// Lines: 200-252\n/// Extractor used in handlers to pull out the authenticated user.\nstruct AuthenticatedUser(Uuid);\n\nimpl FromRequest for AuthenticatedUser {\n    type Error = Error;\n    type Future = Ready<Result<Self, Self::Error>>;\n\n    fn from_request(req: &ServiceRequest, _payload: &mut actix_http::Payload) -> Self::Future {\n        // Read `Authorization: Bearer ...` header and verify JWT. In a\n        // production system you would cache decoding keys and handle\n        // algorithm negotiation.\n        let auth_hdr = req\n            .headers()\n            .get(header::AUTHORIZATION)\n            .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        if !auth_hdr.starts_with(\"Bearer \") {\n            error!(\"Missing bearer token\");\n            return ready(Err(ServiceError::Unauthorized.into()));\n        }\n\n        let token = &auth_hdr[7..];\n        let key = jwt::DecodingKey::from_secret(b\"super-secret-change-me\"); // TODO: rotate\n        let validation = jwt::Validation::new(JWT_ALGO);\n\n        match jwt::decode::<Claims>(token, &key, &validation) {\n            Ok(data) => ready(Ok(AuthenticatedUser(data.claims.sub))),\n            Err(err) => {\n                error!(\"JWT validation error: {:?}\", err);\n                ready(Err(ServiceError::Unauthorized.into()))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Request Context Pattern (Proper Implementation)\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_36.txt\n// Lines: 66-73\n/// Additional request context extracted by auth/session middleware.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RequestContext {\n    pub user_id: UserId,\n    /// Trace ID injected by instrumentation middleware so logs can be\n    /// correlated end-to-end.\n    pub trace_id: Uuid,\n}\n```\n\n### Global State Patterns Found\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_35.txt\n// Lines: 388-389\n/// Shared, lazily-initialised PostgreSQL pool (used by repo & store).\nstatic PG_POOL: once_cell::sync::OnceCell<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::OnceCell::new();\n\n// File: /app/project/src/module_52.txt\n// Lines: 170-180\n/// Lazily initialised, globally shared instance.\npub fn global() -> Result<Arc<Self>> {\n    static INSTANCE: OnceCell<Arc<RabbitMqBus>> = OnceCell::new();\n    INSTANCE\n        .get_or_try_init(|| {\n            let cfg = AmqpConfig::from_env()\n                .context(\"Failed to read EDUPULSE_AMQP_* environment variables\")?;\n            Self::new(cfg)\n        })\n        .cloned()\n}\n\n// File: /app/project/src/module_77.txt\n// Lines: 358, 378\nstatic POOL: once_cell::sync::Lazy<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::Lazy::new(|| {\n    // ... pool initialization\n});\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Investigation Methodology\n\nI conducted a systematic investigation following these steps:\n\n1. **Searched for shared mutable state patterns**: Looked for `static`, `lazy_static!`, `Arc<Mutex>`, `Arc<RwLock>`, `OnceCell`, and `OnceLock` patterns across all source files.\n\n2. **Analyzed module_48.txt and module_15.txt**: These were identified as primary suspects. Module_48 handles event publishing infrastructure, and module_15 handles badge awarding. Neither contains user-context-specific global state.\n\n3. **Traced the request lifecycle**: Examined authentication middleware in module_1.txt and module_49.txt to understand how user identity flows through requests.\n\n4. **Searched for anti-patterns**: Looked for patterns like `CURRENT_USER`, `USER_CTX`, `ACTIVE_SESSION`, `REQUEST_INFO` that would indicate global per-request state storage.\n\n### Expected Vulnerability Pattern (Race Condition)\n\nThe bug described in the task would occur with code like this:\n\n```rust\n// ANTI-PATTERN (NOT FOUND IN THIS CODEBASE)\nstatic CURRENT_USER_CTX: Arc<Mutex<Option<UserContext>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));\n\n// Middleware sets user context globally\nasync fn auth_middleware(req: Request) -> Result<()> {\n    let user_id = extract_from_jwt(&req)?;\n    let mut guard = CURRENT_USER_CTX.lock().await;\n    *guard = Some(UserContext { user_id });  // WRITE to global state\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n// Handler reads from global state\nasync fn fetch_dashboard() -> Dashboard {\n    let guard = CURRENT_USER_CTX.lock().await;  // READ from global state\n    let user = guard.as_ref().unwrap();\n    // Fetch user's dashboard data\n}\n```\n\n**Race Condition Sequence:**\n1. Request A (User A) enters middleware, acquires lock, writes User A's context\n2. Request A releases lock, yields to async runtime\n3. Request B (User B) enters middleware, acquires lock, writes User B's context (overwrites!)\n4. Request B releases lock\n5. Request A resumes, acquires lock, reads from global state\n6. **BUG**: Request A now sees User B's context, not User A's\n\n### What Was Actually Found\n\nThe codebase follows **correct patterns** for request-scoped user context:\n\n1. **Per-request extractors** (module_49.txt): `AuthenticatedUser` is extracted fresh from JWT headers for each request\n2. **Explicit context passing** (module_36.txt): `RequestContext` is passed as a function parameter, not stored globally\n3. **Safe global state**: Global statics like `PG_POOL` store only shared infrastructure (connection pools), not user-specific data\n\n### Global State That IS Present (Safe Usage)\n\nThe codebase has several global static patterns, but they are used correctly:\n\n| File | Pattern | Purpose | Safety |\n|------|---------|---------|--------|\n| module_35.txt:389 | `OnceCell<PgPool>` | Database connection pool | Safe - no user data |\n| module_52.txt:172 | `OnceCell<Arc<RabbitMqBus>>` | Message broker | Safe - no user data |\n| module_77.txt:358 | `Lazy<PgPool>` | Database pool | Safe - no user data |\n| utils.txt:311 | `OnceLock<SnowflakeIdGenerator>` | ID generation | Safe - no user data |\n| constants.txt:70 | `Lazy<RunMode>` | App configuration | Safe - no user data |\n\n## Implementation\n\nThis is a bug investigation task - no code modifications were required.\n\n## Summary\n\n- **Primary finding**: The described cross-user data leakage bug pattern (storing user context in global mutable state) was **not found** in the examined codebase. The application uses proper Rust async patterns where user identity is either extracted per-request from JWT headers or passed explicitly through function parameters.\n\n- **Key components**:\n  - module_49.txt - Proper `AuthenticatedUser` per-request extractor\n  - module_36.txt - Proper `RequestContext` parameter passing\n  - module_1.txt - JWT-based session with Redis backing (per-token, not global)\n\n- **Architectural pattern**: The codebase follows correct patterns for async web services:\n  1. Request-scoped extractors for authentication\n  2. Explicit context passing through function parameters\n  3. Global state only for stateless infrastructure (connection pools, message brokers)\n\n- **Recommendations**:\n  1. The described bug pattern would require a global `Arc<Mutex<UserContext>>` or similar being written to by middleware and read by handlers\n  2. If such code exists in modules not examined, it should be refactored to use per-request extractors or explicit parameter passing\n  3. Use `tokio::task_local!` if truly request-scoped storage is needed in async contexts\n\n### Note on Task Expectations\n\nThe task description indicated that module_48.txt and module_15.txt would contain the bug. After thorough examination:\n- module_48.txt contains event publishing infrastructure with no user-context storage\n- module_15.txt contains badge awarding logic with no global user-context storage\n\nThe vulnerability pattern described (global Arc<Mutex> storing per-request user data) was not found in the available codebase. The authentication and session management code follows correct patterns for concurrent async web applications.\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Solution: rust_web_social_expert_073_bug_investigation_expert_01\n\n## Key Files Identified\n\nBased on the task description and codebase analysis, the following files are relevant to the cross-user data leakage investigation:\n\n- /app/project/src/module_48.txt - Event-Driven Infrastructure for EduPulse Live (outbox pattern, event publishing)\n- /app/project/src/module_15.txt - Badge-awarding microservice (event consumption and domain events)\n- /app/project/src/module_1.txt - Authentication & Session Management with JWT + Redis\n- /app/project/src/module_36.txt - Learning-Pulse domain with RequestContext handling\n- /app/project/src/module_49.txt - Session management and JWT authentication middleware\n- /app/project/src/module_35.txt - Global PG_POOL pattern using OnceCell\n- /app/project/src/module_52.txt - Global RabbitMqBus with OnceCell pattern\n- /app/project/src/module_77.txt - Global Lazy<PgPool> patterns\n- /app/project/src/utils.txt - Global SnowflakeIdGenerator using OnceLock\n\n## Code Evidence\n\n### Authentication Pattern (Proper Implementation)\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_49.txt\n// Lines: 200-252\n/// Extractor used in handlers to pull out the authenticated user.\nstruct AuthenticatedUser(Uuid);\n\nimpl FromRequest for AuthenticatedUser {\n    type Error = Error;\n    type Future = Ready<Result<Self, Self::Error>>;\n\n    fn from_request(req: &ServiceRequest, _payload: &mut actix_http::Payload) -> Self::Future {\n        // Read `Authorization: Bearer ...` header and verify JWT. In a\n        // production system you would cache decoding keys and handle\n        // algorithm negotiation.\n        let auth_hdr = req\n            .headers()\n            .get(header::AUTHORIZATION)\n            .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        if !auth_hdr.starts_with(\"Bearer \") {\n            error!(\"Missing bearer token\");\n            return ready(Err(ServiceError::Unauthorized.into()));\n        }\n\n        let token = &auth_hdr[7..];\n        let key = jwt::DecodingKey::from_secret(b\"super-secret-change-me\"); // TODO: rotate\n        let validation = jwt::Validation::new(JWT_ALGO);\n\n        match jwt::decode::<Claims>(token, &key, &validation) {\n            Ok(data) => ready(Ok(AuthenticatedUser(data.claims.sub))),\n            Err(err) => {\n                error!(\"JWT validation error: {:?}\", err);\n                ready(Err(ServiceError::Unauthorized.into()))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Request Context Pattern (Proper Implementation)\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_36.txt\n// Lines: 66-73\n/// Additional request context extracted by auth/session middleware.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct RequestContext {\n    pub user_id: UserId,\n    /// Trace ID injected by instrumentation middleware so logs can be\n    /// correlated end-to-end.\n    pub trace_id: Uuid,\n}\n```\n\n### Global State Patterns Found\n\n```rust\n// File: /app/project/src/module_35.txt\n// Lines: 388-389\n/// Shared, lazily-initialised PostgreSQL pool (used by repo & store).\nstatic PG_POOL: once_cell::sync::OnceCell<PgPool> = once_cell::sync::OnceCell::new();\n\n// File: /app/project/src/module_52.txt\n// Lines: 170-180\n/// Lazily initialised, globally shared instance.\npub fn global() -> Result<Arc<Self>> {\n    static INSTANCE: OnceCell<Arc<RabbitMqBus>> = OnceCell::new();\n    INSTANCE\n        .get_or_try_init(|| {\n            let cfg = AmqpConfig::from_env()\n                .context(\"Failed to read EDUPULSE_AMQP_* environment variables\")?;\n patterns**: Looked for `static`, `lazy_static!`, `Arc<Mutex>`, `Arc<RwLock>`, `OnceCell`, and `OnceLock` patterns across all source files.\n\n2. **Analyzed module_48.txt and module_15.txt**: These were identified as primary suspects. Module_48 handles event publishing infrastructure, and module_15 handles badge awarding. Neither contains user-context-specific global state.\n\n3. **Traced the request lifecycle**: Examined authentication middleware in module_1.txt and module_49.txt to understand how user identity flows through requests.\n\n4. **Searched for anti-patterns**: Looked for patterns like `CURRENT_USER`, `USER_CTX`, `ACTIVE_SESSION`, `REQUEST_INFO` that would indicate global per-request state storage.\n\n### Expected Vulnerability Pattern (Race Condition)\n\nThe bug described in the task would occur with code like this:\n\n```rust\n// ANTI-PATTERN (NOT FOUND IN THIS CODEBASE)\nstatic CURRENT_USER_CTX: Arc<Mutex<Option<UserContext>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));\n\n// Middleware sets user context globally\nasync fn auth_middleware(req: Request) -> Result<()> {\n    let user_id = extract_from_jwt(&req)?;\n    let mut guard = CURRENT_USER_CTX.lock().await;\n    *guard = Some(UserContext { user_id });  // WRITE to global state\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n// Handler reads from global state\nasync fn fetch_dashboard() -> Dashboard {\n    let guard = CURRENT_USER_CTX.lock().await;  // READ from global state\n    let user = guard.as_ref().unwrap();\n    // Fetch user's dashboard data\n}\n```\n\n**Race Condition Sequence:**\n1. Request B (User B) enters middleware, acquires lock, writes User B's context (overwrites!)\n4. Request B releases lock\n5. The application uses proper Rust async patterns where user identity is either extracted per-request from JWT headers or passed explicitly through function parameters.\n\n- **Key components**:\n  - module_49.txt - Proper `AuthenticatedUser` per-request extractor\n  - module_36.txt - Proper `RequestContext` parameter passing\n  - module_1.txt - JWT-based session with Redis backing (per-token, not global)\n\n- **Architectural pattern**: The codebase follows correct patterns for async web services:\n  1. Request-scoped extractors for authentication\n  2. Explicit context passing through function parameters\n  3. Global state only for stateless infrastructure (connection pools, message brokers)\n\n- **Recommendations**:\n  1. The described bug pattern would require a global `Arc<Mutex<UserContext>>` or similar being written to by middleware and read by handlers\n  2. If such code exists in modules not examined, it should be refactored to use per-request extractors or explicit parameter passing\n  3. After thorough examination:\n- module_48.txt contains event publishing infrastructure with no user-context storage\n- module_15.txt contains badge awarding logic with no global user-context storage\n\nThe vulnerability pattern described (global Arc<Mutex> storing per-request user data) was not found in the available codebase. store\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt:306:                .write()\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-307-                .map_err(|_| RepositoryError::Io(\"poisoned lock\".into()))?;\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-308-\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-309-            if guard.contains_key(&pulse.id) {\n--\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-433-            .expect(\"pulse creation should succeed\");\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-434-\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-435-        // Verify the pulse is stored.\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt:436:        let store_guard = repo.store.read().unwrap();\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-437-        assert!(store_guard.contains_key(&pulse.id));\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-438-\n/app/project/src/module_70.txt-439-        // Dispatcher writes to tracing; we assert via `traced_test`   "content": "/app/project/src/module_70.txt:398:        // callers need transactional guarantees.\n/app/project/src/module_80.txt:177:        // Insert into audit log without blocking caller on I/O latency.\n/app/project/src/module_58.txt:254:        // Fire-and-forget tracking of the ack so we don\u2019t block callers.\n/app/project/src/module_31.txt:238:        // Publish event \u2013 failures bubble up; caller may choose to retry.\n/app/project/src/module_19.txt:254:    /// channel.  The caller is expected to cancel the task when done.\n/app/project/src/module_59.txt:36:// Re-export so callers do not have to pull the crate again.\n/app/project/src/module_59.txt:252:    /// Subscribe to all events. Caller receives a [`BusReceiver`].\n/app/project/src/module_20.txt:125:/// Convenience alias used by callers that don't care about the concrete error.\n/app/project/src/module_41.txt:142:///   back-off to shield the caller from transient issues.  \n/app/project/src/module_47.txt:129:/// Database-level error that is intentionally *boring*: callers are not\n/app/project/src/module_71.txt:197:/// callers use the same API regardless of backend.\n/app/project/src/module_7.txt:105:        F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> futures::future::BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n/app/project/src/module_7.txt:163:            F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n/app/project/src/module_7.txt:247:            F: Fn(EventEnvelope<E>) -> BoxFuture<'static, Result<(), EventBusError>>\n/app/project/src/module_48.txt:111:    const EVENT_TYPE: &'static str = \"user.erase_requested.v1\";\n/app/project/src/module_14.txt:156:    ///   \u2022 At least once delivery (caller must handle idempotency)\n/app/project/src/module_14.txt:157:    ///   \u2022 Caller will be notified of any _synchronous_ failures\n/app/project/src/module_1.txt:433:        R: UserRepository + 'static,\n/app/project/src/module_18.txt:288:        // Fire-and-forget publish; failure bubbles back so caller can retry.\n/app/project/src/module_6.txt:89:/// in callers easier and metrics aggregation cleaner.\n/app/project/src/module_6.txt:146:    /// is not supplied.  Callers can also pass a pre-initialised `PgPool`\n/app/project/src/module_22.txt:103:/// caller code can use `?` without additional noise.\n/app/project/src/module_22.txt:297:        // Fire-and-forget the event; let caller decide whether to await or spawn.\n/app/project/src/module_83.txt:275:        // after spawn), so the caller knows if publication failed.\n/app/project/src/module_28.txt:145:    /// caller to manually stop a subscriber at runtime.\n/app/project/src/module_51.txt:197:    async fn get_connection() -> Result<&'static Connection, EventBusError> {\n/app/project/src/module_45.txt:285://     session: CurrentUser,         // auth middleware\n/app/project/src/module_33.txt:258:    /// **not** rolled back (best-effort), but the caller is informed through\n/app/project/src/module_65.txt:342:/// Keeps the public API clean while avoiding `Any` imports in caller code.\n/app/project/src/module_3.txt:122:/// caller to swap implementations (e.g. for tests or for sharding).\n/app/project/src/module_85.txt:267:///  \u2022 Broker failure    => event *already* persisted, caller should retry\n/app/project/src/module_85.txt:270:/// For brevity we just propagate the error back to the caller.\n/app/project/src/module_15.txt:353:    /// so callers can `.await` graceful shutdown.\n/app/project/src/config.txt:17://! driven_; callers only need to care about the resulting strongly\n/app/project/src/module_69.txt:59:/// The concrete payload is stored in a `serde_json::Value`, therefore callers\n/app/project/src/module_69.txt:319:    // Store sender on dispatcher so caller can gracefully stop it (simplified)\n/app/project/src/module_9.txt:255:    /// This method is *synchronous* with respect to the caller\u2019s DB\n/app/project/src/module_9.txt:329:                // Log and backoff; caller decides global error handling.\n/app/project/src/module_11.txt:101:/// Must be invoked within the caller's SQL transaction.\n/app/project/src/module_11.txt:174:    /// Caller decides the sleep interval between attempts.\n/app/project/src/module_36.txt:59:/// Helper DTO used by service callers (presentation layer) to submit a reply.\n/app/project/src/module_44.txt:246:        // Fire-and-forget caching (we still propagate error to caller).",

109. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_45.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```rust\n//! src/module_45.rs\n//! -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n//! GDPR\u2013Ready Event\u2013Sourced Erasure Service\n//!\n//! This module lives inside EduPulse Live\u2019s core and is responsible for:\n//!   1. Persisting domain events in a Postgres event-store.\n//!   2. Publishing the events to the message broker for downstream processing.\n//!   3. Handling GDPR-related commands (`EraseUser`) by producing *soft-delete*\n//!      events that cascading services understand (search index pruning,\n//!      cache eviction, file storage tomb-stones, etc.).\n//!\n//! The implementation follows the Repository + Service pattern so that the\n//! application layer can depend on abstractions, making it easy to embed in an\n//! Actix-Web REST handler or a gRPC fa\u00e7ade.\n//!\n//! Note: the code is written for illustrative purposes, focusing on correctness\n//! and idiomatic Rust rather than compile-time completeness in every edge case.\n\n#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]\n\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sqlx::{postgres::PgPoolOptions, PgPool, Postgres, Transaction};\nuse thiserror::Error;\nuse tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};\nuse tracing::{error, info, instrument};\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Domain API\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// High-level commands that can be issued to the GDPR service.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum GdprCommand {\n    /// Permanently & irreversibly remove PII for the given user account.\n    EraseUser {\n        requesting_admin_id: Uuid,\n        target_user_id: Uuid,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Top-level domain events.\n///\n/// All events SHOULD be append-only; compensating actions must be expressed as\n/// new events, never by mutating existing records.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(tag = \"type\", rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum DomainEvent {\n    /// An irreversible erasure was requested and queued.\n    UserErasureRequested {\n        target_user_id: Uuid,\n        requesting_admin_id: Uuid,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    /// All PII for a user is now anonymised.\n    UserErased {\n        target_user_id: Uuid,\n        erased_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    },\n    // \u2026 other domain events live elsewhere\n}\n\n/// Metadata wrapper around a DomainEvent, useful for tracing, ordering, etc.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct EventEnvelope {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub sequence: i64,\n    pub aggregate_id: Uuid,\n    pub aggregate_type: String,\n    pub event: DomainEvent,\n    pub occurred_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Error Types\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\npub enum GdprError {\n    #[error(\"database error: {0}\")]\n    Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),\n    #[error(\"event publish error: {0}\")]\n    Publish(#[from] PublishError),\n    #[error(\"command timed out after {0:?}\")]\n    Timeout(Duration),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\n#[error(\"failed to publish event\")]\npub struct PublishError;\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Abstractions\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Event store responsible for persisting `EventEnvelope`s in DB.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventStore {\n    async fn append(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error>;\n}\n\n/// Publisher responsible for pushing events to the broker.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait EventPublisher {\n    async fn publish(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), PublishError>;\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Concrete Implementations\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Postgres-backed event store.\npub struct PostgresEventStore {\n    pool: PgPool,\n}\n\nimpl PostgresEventStore {\n    pub fn new(pool: PgPool) -> Self {\n        Self { pool }\n    }\n\n    async fn append_single(\n        tx: &mut Transaction<'_, Postgres>,\n        envelope: &EventEnvelope,\n    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n        sqlx::query!(\n            r#\"\n            INSERT INTO event_store (\n                id,\n                sequence,\n                aggregate_id,\n                aggregate_type,\n                event,\n                occurred_at\n            ) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)\n            \"#,\n            envelope.id,\n            envelope.sequence,\n            envelope.aggregate_id,\n            envelope.aggregate_type,\n            serde_json::to_value(&envelope.event).expect(\"serialisation never fails\"),\n            envelope.occurred_at\n        )\n        .execute(&mut *tx)\n        .await?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventStore for PostgresEventStore {\n    #[instrument(skip_all, name = \"event_store.append\")]\n    async fn append(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {\n        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;\n        for ev in events {\n            Self::append_single(&mut tx, ev).await?;\n        }\n        tx.commit().await\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dummy publisher; swap with NATS / RabbitMQ / Kafka implementation.\npub struct LogPublisher;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl EventPublisher for LogPublisher {\n    #[instrument(skip_all, level = \"debug\")]\n    async fn publish(&self, events: &[EventEnvelope]) -> Result<(), PublishError> {\n        for ev in events {\n            info!(event_id = %ev.id, \"published event to broker\");\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Service Layer\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\npub struct GdprService<S, P>\nwhere\n    S: EventStore + Send + Sync,\n    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync,\n{\n    store: S,\n    publisher: P,\n}\n\nimpl<S, P> GdprService<S, P>\nwhere\n    S: EventStore + Send + Sync,\n    P: EventPublisher + Send + Sync,\n{\n    pub fn new(store: S, publisher: P) -> Self {\n        Self { store, publisher }\n    }\n\n    /// Dispatch a GDPR command, storing and emitting the resulting events.\n    #[instrument(skip(self, cmd))]\n    pub async fn handle(&self, cmd: GdprCommand) -> Result<(), GdprError> {\n        // Step 1 \u2013 Translate command \u2192 events\n        let events = self.to_events(cmd);\n\n        // Step 2 \u2013 Persist events inside a DB transaction\n        self.store.append(&events).await?;\n\n        // Step 3 \u2013 Broadcast the events to the broker\n        //          (so that search/pruning/etc. can react asynchronously)\n        self.publisher.publish(&events).await?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn to_events(&self, cmd: GdprCommand) -> Vec<EventEnvelope> {\n        match cmd {\n            GdprCommand::EraseUser {\n                requesting_admin_id,\n                target_user_id,\n                reason,\n            } => {\n                let base_seq = self.sequence_seed();\n                vec![self.envelope(\n                    base_seq,\n                    target_user_id,\n                    DomainEvent::UserErasureRequested {\n                        target_user_id,\n                        requesting_admin_id,\n                        reason,\n                    },\n                )]\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sequence_seed(&self) -> i64 {\n        // In production this should come from a monotonic sequence (e.g. PG sequence).\n        // For illustration we just use a randomish number.\n        (Utc::now().timestamp_micros() & 0x7FFF_FFFF) as i64\n    }\n\n    fn envelope(&self, seq: i64, aggregate_id: Uuid, ev: DomainEvent) -> EventEnvelope {\n        EventEnvelope {\n            id: Uuid::new_v4(),\n            sequence: seq,\n            aggregate_id,\n            aggregate_type: \"user\".to_string(),\n            event: ev,\n            occurred_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Bootstrap helpers (e.g. called from main.rs or integration tests)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Convenience builder that spins up a connection pool and returns\n/// a GDPR service ready for injection.\npub async fn bootstrap_gdpr_service(\n    database_url: &str,\n) -> Result<GdprService<PostgresEventStore, LogPublisher>, sqlx::Error> {\n    let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()\n        .max_connections(5)\n        .connect(database_url)\n        .await?;\n\n    Ok(GdprService::new(\n        PostgresEventStore::new(pool),\n        LogPublisher,\n    ))\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Example (when used from an Actix-Web handler or anywhere else)\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n//\n// #[post(\"/admin/erase_user/{user_id}\")]\n// async fn erase_user(\n//     svc: web::Data<AppCtx>,       // contains GdprService behind Arc\n//     path: web::Path<Uuid>,\n//     session: CurrentUser,         // auth middleware\n// ) -> impl Responder {\n//     let cmd = GdprCommand::EraseUser {\n//         requesting_admin_id: session.user_id,\n//         target_user_id: *path,\n//         reason: \"User requested account deletion\".into(),\n//     };\n//\n//     // Guard against runaway futures; we never want to hang.\n//     match timeout(Duration::from_secs(3), svc.gdpr.handle(cmd)).await {\n//         Ok(Ok(())) => HttpResponse::Accepted().finish(),\n//         Ok(Err(e)) => {\n//             error!(error = %e, \"GDPR command failed\");\n//             HttpResponse::InternalServerError().finish()\n//         }\n//         Err(_) => HttpResponse::GatewayTimeout().finish(),\n//     }\n// }\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// End of module\n```",
